contents: Hi there........whatever you do, be sure to consult with your physician, ok? You don't want to stop something that could be helping you, only to have to start all over with the treatment, now THAT would be expensive... Also, I really hope everyone who takes the alternatives tell their physicians. The docs don't always learn about these products and are not sure about recommending them. The more people who report their experiences to their personal urologists, the better our understanding of them will be! Have you called the CystoProtek people about your questions? They are very very good with patients over the phone.

contents: Hi Christin, Just a thought: Have you checked the calorie content of what you're eating? It's easy to think we're eating healthier because of reducing colorants, preservatives, etc, but I know I'm eating way more calories because of cutting out fruits, diet sodas, soy, salads, etc. I was pretty shocked when I logged in my food onto a calorie tracker & discovered how many calories were in what I considered a "healthy" day I used www.sparkpeople.com - it's free & easy to use. That said, I have no idea about the supplements (sorry!), I hope someone can come along who knows more...

contents: From what I can find on the ICN (or other places), D Mannose does not cure IC nor UTIs. Nor does the ICN represent that it will. It seems to help prevent UTIs from forming by binding with the e coli that causes most UTIs and flushing them out of the body in the urine. It is important that you remember when purchasing anything anywhere that not everyone is helped by everything. What works or helps one person may not work or help someone else. If only that were not the case. *sigh*

contents: Enteric coatings are put on medications to insure that its not digested until it reaches the small intestines...it keeps the stomach lining from becoming irritated. I take the generic brand of fish oil capsules purchased from Sams Club. Aside from them being huge, I've had no issues with them. Wishing you well... Diana

contents: me too! I take the enteric coat fish tablets because I had read that the regular ones that open in your stomach can cause you to "burp" up the oils. I absolutely hate fish or any sort of seafood so I buy the enteric coated ones so I don't have to worry about any fishy tastes in my mouth. I use Vitasmart brand that I find at K-mart. Hope this helps.

I LOVE the pictures of your kids!!!! I too use the generic Costco brand fish oil capsules without any problem. The lemon flavoring is likely to mask the fishy burp if you get that. The enteric coating is also to make it dissolve in the intestines instead of the stomach, probably for the same reason. I haven't heard of anyone having problems with the coating. It is used on many medications. Reading those labels can make all of us crazy....hang in there, you are doing the right thing. :-)

contents: I've bought the Walgreen's version of uristat and I noticed it has die's in it. That won't make the flare worse? The other thing I don't get is why my doctors didn't even mention drugs like pyridium. I've been to two different urologists and they just let me go through these flares with no help. I've been on Elmiron for almost four months now and waiting for it to work. I take 5m of valium which my primary doctor prescribed to help me sleep and I found it helps the flare, but I'd rather not take it. I'm afraid of getting addicted. This website has so much information I'd be lost without it.

I have used over-the-counter Pyridium (and the generic script for it which is just the main ingredient--Phenazopyridine) for short term emergencies and find it to be a very helpful drug, and very fast acting--like within hours. I have not heard of anyone reacting to the dye. If you've never used it don't be alarmed --the color of your urine will be intense. I have a script for Urelle which I use as needed during flares. It seems to take a day or two to start helping, but it is recommended for long-term use, while Pyridium generally is not. Urelle is my go-to med. It stains blue from the antiseptic in it instead of orange, and is less freaky I think. I too am waiting for the benefits of Elmiron to kick in (only one month on that.) so I really depend on Urelle. I would call the uro or your primary care doc and ask about these drugs--they can be really helpful in relieving UTI-like symptoms. Edited: Aw, Bri, I must have posted same time!
2 words for anyone using Urelle, Prosed or any incarnation of Pyridium: BLACK PANTIES!
uristat Thank's so much! These drugs sound great. I've been in a flare for a few days now and will give uristat a try. When I see the doctor in Feb. I will ask about Urelle.

It helped! I tired the Uristat today and I feel less pressure. You are right about the brown color. It stained the tolet at work.I was panicking until I noticed some toilet cleaner and it came off. I can't imagine what the janitor or people at work would think. I might have to buy some more cleaner to keep in stock. It seemed like the first time I went the color was the darkest. I hope the flare goes away now. Everytime I have one I worry that it will never go away. I'm not sure if it was stress, calcium pills or spinach that caused it. I just have to learn to take one day at a time.
The only problem is the pressure came back pretty bad last night around 6:00. I had to take a valium to help sleep. I guess it doesn't last all day. I don't feel as bad today.

Glad you are feeling better. You can take it up to three times a day for a short period of time if you need to. The effect does wear off within several hours, so if I am having a bad flare one dose a day doesn't do it for me.

Thank You! I didn't even read the directions, since you can only take it for a couple of days I thought it was so strong you could only take it once a day. Now I know. That's why this site is so wonderful there are people to help.
thanks

I am wondering, I am on month 3 of Cystoprotek and it is working a bit. What I I am wondering is, does it just get better and better like Elmiron does? I mean, as time goes by does it work even better? I know it is said that Elmiron can take 3 mos to a year to begin to work. Is Cystoprotek the same way, or is it that once you hit the 3-4 mo. mark, that is at good as it is going to get?
signature: As I think, so I shall be.... I have had IC for 4.5 years now. I also have fibro, sjogren's syndrome, and vulvadynia. I live in immense pain. I often daydream of life before this demon . When I feel good, I embrace it with all of my heart! I pray that someday I will feel 100% again, we all deserve it! Medications: Vicodin (as needed, so daily!) Percocet (morning & night) Klonopin (.5 mg at night) Zanaflex (2 mg each night) Thyroid med for low thyroid (hypo) leuko-stim Vitamin D (4000) Diagnosis: IC, CFS, Fibro (it is really bad it has taken so much from me and changed my life), Anxiety, Depression from it all, & Hypothyroid.

contents: Sorry. No answer here but I did get a month free but have yet to start taking it. How long did you take it before it worked?

hmm, that's a good question and one i've wondered about myself. i've been on the cystoprotek since febuary and it has helped some. about 2 months on the elmiron i started having some obvious good days and thought holy cow, that worked fast. i haven't really felt any better since then though. but a few weeks ago i decided to cut out the cystoprotek....BAD IDEA!!!! the next day, i had the worst flare EVER. it wasn't even that bad before i was diagnosed. i waited it out for a few days to make sure it wasn't just bad timing, but couldn't take it anymore. about 2 days later i started feeling better again. i'm not brave enough to try it again to see if it's the same. SO, either cysotprotek does get better and better with time, or the elmiron is starting to help some. time will tell. good luck though and keep in touch with how you are doing. i'm very interested to see what other's have to say about the whole thing
signature: Current: -pelvic floor therapy, -weekly uracyst instills

contents: I think about a month and a half into it Originally posted by Michelle in AR Sorry. No answer here but I did get a month free but have yet to start taking it. How long did you take it before it worked? It started to work. I think of my life about 3-4 mos ago and I was crying almost everyday and urinating like crazy. It is hard to say which medicine is making me feel better. I am sure it is a combo of the Cystoprotek, DS Aloe Vera, Lyrica, and Tofranil. At times I wish I did know which one is making the difference so I could cut down on the others for financial reasons, but I am not willing to not take any of them. I think they all work synergistically together. I know for sure that my frequency has dropped tremendously so I don't know if I thank the Cystoprotek for that, or the tofranil. I will take this time to thank both. My main problem now is pain. Lyrica worked wondeful at first, and still does, but not like it did at first. I am sure it is most likely time to up the dosage, but I am not willing to do that. I will continue the Cystoprotek. p.s. I get the Cystoprotek through Amazon and am on an autoship program so I get free shipping, and the cost is only $28.00 per mo. for a bottle.

contents: Hey guys. I'm probably putting this in the wrong forum, so I apologize to any admins if I'm doofing this one up. I know a number of our members suffer Fibro/CFS/what have you in addition to their IC. Recently, after a bout with some type of infection, possibly EBV, I've had Fibro symptoms flaring up on me. (Although, my IC calmed down a bit, oddly.) Dr. Theo seems convinced that Algonot and the others in that same family can assist with Fibro pain, probably because he has a belief that mast cells play a part in the condition. Has anyone here tried using Algonot or any of the others in that 'family' of meds for Fibro symptoms? Any effect? I was going to try Cystoprotek for my IC symptoms already, but it'd be nice if it overlapped and did something for my Fibro symptoms as well. Thanks guys.
It is definitely possible that it could help your fibro, however, I don't have any personal experience with it. It did help my daughter's eczema and migraines pre-menstrally!

contents: It's Desert Harvest Aloe Vera It's not the liquid form or the raw form. It is in capsules and they remove the part of the Aloe that causes diarrhea. I've been taking it for about two years now and it has allowed me to get off Elmiron. It takes about a week or two for me to feel the effects. I initially took 2 caps/3 x day and now take 2 caps 2xday. Great stuff!
its great stuff, i noticed a difference straight away. please pm me if you need anymore details.

contents: Is there any sort of "prelief" to neutralize spicy food? Silly question, I know...

Yes, it's called Prelief, & is over the counter. My local drug store ordered it for me & I also purchased it on this website. My dr. has me taking it w/ meals & snacks. Hope you can find it in England.
I thought that prelief neutralized only acid foods... does it work also for caffeine and spicy food? Thanks!

Prelief doesn't work for caffeine. I've not tried it with spicy foods because I've found that I feel best if I stick to the IC diet & just use Prelief for restaurant foods that may have small amounts of acidic preservatives (like breads or hamburger buns).

contents: Originally posted by Briza I have heard/read that Valerian root has relaxing properties...as a muscle relaxer I don't know how well it would work or what kind of muscle it works on, but I have some friends who have used it to help them relax so they can sleep. I have taken it a few times but didn't notice any effect one way or another. Side effects for me: none. Prescription muscle relaxers are a whole nother animal....they can be extremely sedating....have had them knock me out for up to 14 hrs even on a low dose. Flexeril that Angeles mentioned and also Zanaflex are two that hit me particularly hard. I prefer not to take them....and I really don't know if they are working b/c I am usually knocked out after I take them. Baclofen is another Rx muscle relaxer that I tried, and it did not seem to be as sedating from what I can remember, but it didn't work for my symptoms. Low dose diazepam like Angeles also mentioned I much prefer...diazepam is valium and usually a low dose is considered 2-5 mg. At that dose I have never had the knock me out side effects, really felt nothing at all except good relief for my pelvic floor spasms and vaginal spasms. I took the 2 mg dose 3 x day with much success very quickly, and then only as needed...for a few months. Never had any side effects from that. I think you mentioned Tangerine that you have a trip or vacation coming up? I know you asked about natural/alternative muscle relaxers... possibly valerian root could help, I just don't know how well, but most definitely without the extreme initial side effects that rx MRs can cause for MANY people. If you do get a prescription muscle relaxer, I would use it with extreme caution, especially if you are not at home to stay in bed to see how it effects you, or have obligations or things you want to do and see if you are going on a trip. I say this b/c MRs do tend to initially be extremely sedating for many people, for me moreso than even sleeping pills. If you do go the rx route, then if it were me, low dose valium is much more tolerable. It also depends on what kind of muscles need relaxing....valium seems to be good for say muscluar pain in lower back or muscle spasms in the pelvic floor/vagina. If it is smooth muscle, like bladder spasms, that you want relief for...Pyridium, Pyridium Plus, Urelle, or Prosed DS might be more the way to go and easier to get an Rx for.....I know you were looking for meds that you could take longer term....if this is the case then URelle or Prosed DS would be much better choices than Pyridium or Pyridium Plus, as the Pyridiums are intended more for short term, emergency use while Urelle and Prosed DS I have read are safer or long term use. Also for bladder spasm relief, Hycosamine, an ingredient I believe that is in Urelle and Prosed DS, can be Rxd by itself and many report excellent symptom relief w/out side effects for bladder spasms with just that. Anyway, just sharing my experiences....best to talk to your dr about what would be the best route to go...alll natural or prescription. And if you do go the Rx route, PLEASE keep in mind what I said about the extremely sedatin effects that muscle relaxers can cause. If it is for PFD you need relief for, my choice personally is 2mg valium. Whether your dr will rx that to you is another issue, also whether or not it would work for you is also very indiviual. Best wishes,Bri Thank you this e-mail is a keeper for when I get back and think about what I am going to ask my Dr. for. I definately cannot risk being put out by strong drugs. I might call him tomorrow though just to see if he can prescribed me some valium until I get to my new Dr. Luckily the PFD muscle pain has decreased over the last 2 days pretty good. It must have been just too much going on and thinking about everything, my mom coming out, preparing TG dinner then another party on Saturday and getting excited about my vacation. I feel alot better. I went to yoga twice last week and did some of that deep breathing when I was feeling real bad. I'm trying to catch myself more when I feel my body tensing up. I'm pretty prepared for my vacation. I do have some 200mg pyrdium, prelief, baking soda, antibiotics, aleve, all my instill stuff and the prescriptions to go along with it. I've never had to pack so much meds in my life Anyway thank you again.

contents: Has anybody tried oral hyaluronic acid for their IC? I see that it is a component of cystoprotek, the theory being that it is another substance that may provide bladder coating. Currently, I'm doing daily heparin instills for that purpose, but the price of heparin has really shot up this year. I know there is some concern about the poor absorption of hyaluronic acid when given orally, so companies have made low-weight products which are better absorbed, but I'm not sure if these have proven to be as effective as the larger molecule when directly instilled. I tried oral and instilled Elmiron but had side-effects to both routes. Appreciate any feedback.

contents: first of all, i havent noticed any releif from any supplements meant for inflammation, which would make sense because i think i have mostly nerve pain. so i've alwasy been looking for supplements that suppsedly work like the antidepressants (the kind that releive nerve pain). the meds work for me but cause too many side effects, so i've been looking for an herbal equivilent. well i decided to research St. John's wort, and started coming up with articles that have used this herb for yes, IC. it was only used in animals so far, and it was injected rather than swallowed, but it did have good results. if you type in St. Johns wort bladder, you may see the same articles i did. keep in mind, SJW aslo can react to current antidepressents so there can be problems there, and i think it can mess with birth control as well. i'm just hoping this herb will give me some releif but not the bad side effects associated with the prescriptions. this was the info i saw: there were also longer articles on the same study. St. John's Wort Could Ease Bladder Woes 2006-May-24 By -- Robert Preidt WEDNESDAY, May 24 (HealthDay News) -- The herbal supplement St. John's wort may help control pain linked to hypersensitive bladder disorders such as interstitial cystitis (IC), according to a new study involving rats. Bladder hyperactivity -- characterized by frequent contractions -- is a significant cause of bladder irritation and pain. In the study, researchers at the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center injected a formulation of St. John's wort called DP015 into the abdomens of rats with bladder inflammation. They found that the treatment resulted in longer intervals between bladder contractions. The findings were expected to be presented Tuesday at the American Urological Association annual meeting, in Atlanta. Prior research found that neural control of the lower urinary tract is controlled by serotonin- and norepinephrine-emitting neurons. These are similar to neurons that play a role in anxiety and depression. "St. John's Wort is an herbal supplement that has been used for years to treat symptoms of mild depression, while urologists often use antidepressants to treat interstitial cysts," researcher Dr. Michael B. Chancellor, a professor of urology and gynecology, said in a prepared statement. "Given that the supplement and the drug work on the same systems, it makes sense that St. John's wort could help treat this painful disease," Chancellor said.

signature: I got IC in 1970! I was not diagnosed until 1991. I've recently have been trying to solve my connective issue problems in general. I look to diet and herbs mostly unless it gets really bad. I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: Ask the pharmacist. I get mine either at CVS, GNC or wherever I am. Sometimes I walk right by it and feel like an idiot, but the someone behind the pharmacy counter always helps me.

General questions about Glucosamine/MSM Basically on a whim I bought some of this at Whole Foods. It's Glucosamine Sulfate 1500mg plus MSM 1000mg. The bottle suggests 3 tablets per day, but I assume this suggested dosage is aimed more at those who take it for arthritis or joint pain. I am not allergic to shellfish or anything else. I am a fairly cautious person when it comes to OTC supplements since I believe that they are not well regulated or tested, but I thought I would give this a trial since I am currently managing my bladder issues with only Urelle as needed and a fairly strict IC diet. Uro, gyn and primary care think I most likely have IC but all admit they are not experts, so I am waiting for my insurance to approve a Uro-gyn that is out-of-plan. The uro suggested I go on Elmiron but I want another more experienced opinion before I commit to that long-term therapy. For those of you who find this supplement helpful for IC what symptoms seem to be helped by it and what dosage do you take? Are there any side effects? Thanks!
i take one cap per day 500mg msm/500mg glucosamine in one. i think its supposed to coat your bladder similar to elmiron. i dont have any side effects.

has anyone gained weight on quercetin. I am 5 1.5" and a the beginning of august I weighed 112 lbs. I began taking querceting (which seems to help) and even excercising (2 miles fast walking with weights 5 times per week). That was when I think the weight gain began. I was gaining about 3-4 lbs a week and it has not stopped. I am up to 25 lbs and counting. They even increased my synthroid 5 weeks ago to see if that would help and I am still gaining. I am getting very nervous. My hormones etx... are all ok. Has this ever happened to anyone? I also take Gluc/msm, omega3, calcium/vit D magnesium, green tea extract, and recently added zyrtec and potasium citrate about a week or so ago. Anyone have any suggestions? I am desperate and absolutely have no idea what is causing this weight gain. My eating habits have not changed much, not even enough for an overall 5lbs gain let alone 25. So now not only do I feel awfull and not sleep I look like crap. This just keeps getting worse. Please advise.

Get to your doctor and demand answers now. There are many other possible causes besides quercetin. You can stop taking the quercetin, but my first thought was thyroid - then realized that you are being treated for hypothyroid condition. Bottom line is, 25 lbs in that short a time is not normal, and should be a warning flag to a physician. I am taking quercetin and did not have that reaction - but I don't have a thyroid condition, either. I found these comments online that indicate quercetin is actually good for thyroid issues: BIOFLAVONOIDS, RUTIN, QUERCETIN. Many people with thyroid disease, particularly hyperthyroidism, have bleeding gums, a condition which doesn’t respond to the usual vitamin C therapy that most health books recommend. Other nutrients often recommended for bleeding gums include bioflavonoids, rutin, and quercetin. It’s quite possible that these facilitate collagen formation and may be very important for copper utilization. If this is true then they may be very important in correcting thyroid conditions and care should be taken to eat a high percentage of raw foods and to supplement these nutrients. HYPERS: Take amounts as directed on bottle. HYPOS: Same. and from the Oprah show: While the above recommendations are general, individuals can always tailor nutrient support for their specific metabolic needs. For example, the “thyroid type” also needs anti-inflammatory nutrients (like quercetin) and minerals (especially calcium and magnesium) to buffer stress. We found that quercetin was the single most helpful nutrient for lowering elevated thyroid autoantibodies, most likely due to its ability to modulate adrenaline-driven friction as well as stabilize immune cells from hyper-reacting. link: http://www.naturalnews.com/022155.html So if you think quercetin is responsible, stop taking it and see if the weight gain stops - but really, would be better to also see a doc and have updated testing as well....I put on 15 lbs overnight when I was first treated for IC, cause then they use Prozac to dampen pelvic nerves - and it seriously screwed with my appetite buttons. I could only keep the weight off by strict Weight Watchers, no fat at all - regardless of the amount of exercise. When I finally switched to Detrol 4 years ago, my appetite cravings changed within 4 weeks, and I can now maintain my weight easily again. So follow through, it is bad enough to feel crappy, without feeling like you look crappy as well... terry, with only the usual middle aged spread to fight

contents: Ever since a posting on another thread suggesting that you might save some dollars and simply down calcium instead of Prelief I have been wondering what it actually is. Clearly it is not worth taking as an efficient source of calcium. I couldn't find any good description of the glycerophosphate component--like what does it actually do? But Prelief tablets are small and powdery, so they must break down in the stomach very fast to interact with whatever else is coming down the pipe. Isn't a phosphate a bubbly thing? Didn't our grannies drink phosphates on their stools at the soda fountain?
I was personally hoping it was a not a high mg of calcium in the prelief. I was taking two a night. You night high calcium= kidney stone. Vicky Thanks Mary

signature: Diagnosed with IC sept. 2008, treatment then was hydrodistension, heparin instills for about 4 months and IC diet; symptoms lasted until sept 2009. Then only a couple of relapses overworking, stress etc. Then completely in remission until Jan. 2016 now pretty much symptom free as of mid august 2016. Symptoms: overall body aches, dryness feeling at vaginal opening, sitting makes it worse, standing makes it worse, stress makes it worse, no frequency or urgency, worse symptom is at night with panging of uretha/bladder. - went on diet, started Cysto Renew 3/31/16 and yoga at least twice a week. - tried uribel but really not for my symptoms since it seems it is for frequency and painful urination which I don't have - Ativan when needed to relax. - Elmiron only took for a couple of days; didn't like it - took atarax 1/2 10mg atarax pill at night really helped but gave me nightmares - tramadol worked great for back aches - generic claritin D worked best for me for calming bladder down and my panging - still taking cysto renew 4x a day and believe this and diet helped the most. - took dosage of cysto renew down to 2x a day and still ok

contents: Take aloe with caution, it works for some people and not at all for others. I took aloe because other IC'ers said it helped them but it made me flare. And I know that for sure because I fallow a strick diet of foods I know will not flare me, and keep a journal also very helpful when trying new alternatives. If you are looking to try alternatives try them one at a time and write down how you feel, always try them for over a week unless they hurt you right away! Good luck to you. Things I have tried: CornSilk Tea: nothing changed Marshmellow Root Tea: Helped me with pain when urinating Hyland's Bladder tablets: nothing changed Aloe pills: made me flare! L-Arginine pills (free form): still no improvement (studies show no improvment untill after 6 months) Activated Quercetin: still no improvement

contents: anyone tried this? I have a friend in florida with IC and her Dr. prescribes it to lots of ICers. It is supposed to alkanize the urine. I wake up every morning around 4:30-5 with a constant burning in my bladder and and even increased frequency than during the rest of the night. I have read that your urine is most acidic early in the AM. It sounds like it might be worth a try but I was wondering if anyone had ever tried it.

I have tried it but I stopped taking it before I had truely got a handle on my flares from food. I didn't notice too much of difference on it. What has really helped is the Prelief. Most mornings when I got up my bladder would burn and in the am it is usually worse but I find taking some prelief helped alot. I feel better now that I take that reguarly. I didn't notice a difference when I took Urocit K. Hope it works for you.

I saw a new urologist yesterday and he says that since I am now having to take 75 or more mgs of Pamelor and still am in a flare that has lasted 15 weeks, that I have tolerance to the med now. His approach for me is: Stop Elmiron, replace with CystoProtek Taper off the Pamelor over several weeks, and use Ultram ER while doing this. Then he wants me to start Lyrica. Has anyone had succes with CystoProtek? Their website seems to be full of great information, but frankly, I am scared. I'd like to know of both sides of the coin on this one. Helped...didn't help? Anyone who can share their experience, I would really appreciate it. Thanks.

signature: 30 year old Mother, Midwife, Birth Doula, Herbalist, and Massage Therapist with sudden onset of IC approx. 2/16/05 (after a PAP smear and routine gynecological exam). I tried a HUGE list of natural and alternative treatments (including Cystoprotek, Desert Harvest Aloe, Aloe gel, Glucosamine/Chondroitin, L-Argenine, Quercitin/Bromelain, Marshmallow Root...etc) for the first 9 months of having IC, without much relief, until I finally researched and started Elmiron. My experience: ~Previously took Elmiron 100mg three times a day (9/29/05-7/06) ~Experimented with dosage (1-2 Elmiron a day recapped into a plain gelcap) and did well with occasional mild flares. (7/06-9/09) ~Stopped all together at the 1 year mark (9/29/06) for 10 days. Still felt good, with low-level symptoms. ~Decided to go back on it (10/16/06) to see if another year on it will bring me into full (zero symptoms) remission. Currently taking 1-2 pills a day, recapped into a plain gelcap. ~ 6/08...Tried to go off Elmiron again, had huge flare at the 3 week mark, went back on 100mg once a day for maintenance. -I went off Elmiron for 2 months due to pregnancy and had a complete relapse of IC symptoms to pre-diagnosis levels of pain/frequency. I then went back on Elmiron at 10-12 weeks, but was unable to take it further due to severe morning sickness. The 2nd/3rd trimesters were MUCH better IC-wise, and postpartum is the best I have felt in years. I am no longer on Elmiron for now. -20 month postpartum remission from 4/17/09 to 12/10/10. -Out of remission after a pap smear on 12/10/10. Pap smears are obviously the main IC trigger for me. Currently trying to cope with loss of the remission. Also doing: ~Sugar-free/gluten free and strict IC diet (With an emphasis on organic, alkaline and whole foods) ~Prenatal DHA/Fish oil supplement ~Vitamin D supplement ~Culturelle probiotic

contents: Thanks for the input ladies....I think the dr. suggested me going off Elmiron becuase I actually don't know if it works for me or not. I was put on it straight away, and I never noticed whether or not it helps, and I've never missed a dose. Changing my diet in the beginning really improved my symptoms. Didn't take them away, but made them so much better. I've never noticed that with anything else except the TCAs Elavil and Pamelor. And now the Pamelor doesn't seem to be working for me. For instance, I've been in a horrible flare for the past 15 weeks brought about by taking Flagyl for BV. I have not been able to get this under control even when taking 50 mg of Pamelor in the morning and another 50 mg at night. When I was first put on it, 25 mg at night was enough to cool the burning for 24 hours. Now, 50 mg isn't enough to cool the burning at all. It takes a dent out of it, but I still feel it loud and clear. I knew I was building resistence to this drug. Anyway, I think I've decided to stay on the Elmiron. I don't have side effects from it anymore except occasional nausea, but whose to say that is Elmiron. I'm on so many drugs, it could be the combo that does it sometimes. I am going to try the CystoProtek and the Ultram ER while I try to taper off of Pamelor. My gyno suggested I try the Ultram ER for a week while I cut my Pamelor to 50mg. She said if my symptoms increase then I may want to wait to change courses until after my 1st anniversary trip to Mexico in less than 6 weeks. See, that is where I am....do I take the chance now of making things worse, or do I try to stay on the course I was on....because things were getting better, just not better enough yet.

contents: If you're taking quercetin with some success, how long did it take to kick in? Although I know we're all different, I'd love to hear how long it took to benefit you. Thanks!

quercetin update Well, I noticed an impact from the quercetin the day after I had my first dose of the supplement (500mg quercetin + ester c + bromelain). The normal pain I have has reduced significantly. Quercetin is observed to bind estrogen in clinical studies. This means that if high estrogen levels during your cycle act as a pain trigger, the quercetin will bind to the estrogen receptors on the IC mast cells so that they cannot be activated. I can vouch for this, as I'm in a time in my cycle when estrogen is high -- and for once, I'm feeling no pain. Having had estrogen-negative breast cancer 10 years ago, I have a call in to my oncologist to ask if quercetin is safe to continue taking. It sounds like many of you are wondering if quercetin causes cancer. I'll ask him about this, although if you look at all the studies back to back, there is much more evidence supporting quercetin as an anti-cancer agent than a cancer-causing agent. I'll see what he says about all this and let you know. I also submitted a question on Dr Weil's message board about this -- if anyone knows, it would be him! I'll let you know if I receive an answer.
Thank you for the information. It will be interesting to hear what the doctor says.

contents: My oncologist got back to me and let me know that quercetin is safe to use. He said that the main risk with quercetin is that it interferes with antibiotics. So if you're taking antibiotics, do not take quercetin. Quercetin also interferes with an enzyme that breaks down drugs in the body. This just means that it will increase the serum levels of the drugs you are currently taking -- perhaps causing the effects to last longer or to be more intense. I take quercetin in the day and Atarax at night, and haven't noticed any ill effects. It's always best to go through your meds list with your doctor to confirm that no harm will come from having increased serum levels of any meds you're taking. Some sources (including Dr. Weil's site) say that taking quercetin on an empty stomach will make it more effective, although some of the quercetin bottles advise taking it with food. I noticed some improvement within a day of taking my first dose on an empty stomach. I think there's been some success with it because it does so many things: it's an anti-inflammatory, it prevents estrogen from activating mast cells, and it's a natural antihistamine that doesn't cause drowsiness. (No, I don't sell it -- I'm just a medical writer by day Good luck to everyone.

Zyrtek used to make me drowsy and miserable, since I stopped I feel more energy! If it works for rhinitis, I have no reason to doubt that it works for the histamines released in the bladder too. contents: I keep reading that prelief can be very effective in preventing flares with problem foods. I have just recently been starting IC treatment as well as the diet and am having trouble eating without having flares, so, I bought some this afternoon. The prelief worked and I have been able to go the entire day without symtoms (yay!). However, I was wondering will continueing to eat 'problem foods' and taking prelief as a preventative further damage my bladder? I know that it is suppose to take acid out of food, but I don't want to just mask my symptoms while doing further damage to my bladder.
signature: 37mg Eavil each night 300 Elmiron a day Hydrocodiene and Pyridium as needed

contents: hi there, yay.....glad you've found some relief using prelief. As far as I am aware (I am no doctor) prelief takes the acid out of the food & doesn't tamper with the natural acids etc within our bodies. Prelief is ONLY an antacid. So I would say if you get away with eating acidic foods using prelief then that's great, just watch out for troublesome additives, preservatives etc that wont be helped by using prelief. Not everyone is food sensitive & it might just be that your just acid sensitive. Just be careful & take your time trying new foods would be my advice. Big healing hugs xx
Prelief is simply calcium.....you can save money by buying straight calcium. Don't ever buy an anacid.........it's just calcium.

I took CystoProtek for about a week and both my urine and my feces developed a terrible smell, my feces turned greenish and shape changed dramatically--(read no further if you are easily grossed out)--kind of longish clumps that looked like they were coated in oil, sort of like bread dough when you oil it. The part that disturbs me is that I stopped taking the CystoProtek but my bowel movements have not normalized in shape altho the smell is gone. I'm interested in hearing from anyone who has had change in bowel movements with CystoProtek whether they resemble what I have described or not. I am trying to decide if perhaps CystoProtek did not cause the change in shape, since shape did not return to normal when I stopped taking it, altho it seemed obvious at the time because the change was quite sudden and coincidental with starting to take CP and coincidental with the bad smell as well.

hum, that sounds like a tricky problem...my urine does sometimes smell "grassy" or almost "musty" but i didn't think to link it to they cystoprotek. as for the color of your bm's, cystoprotek goes in green so that sorta makes that it comes out green but that doesn't explain the other problem...hopefully someone else will chime in. good luck!!!

contents: Cowdean I am glad you have found some relief with aloe and at the same time taking it in a form/brand that may be less expensive and just as effective as other advertised brands I hope it continues to work for your symptoms I, for one, have an adversion to aloe even tho I have tons of aloe growing in my yard and I know it is used successfully for many conditions....from burns, inflammation, stings, bites, etc. Unfortunately I have very bad memories of getting the taste of fresh aloe in my mouth ICK whenever my grandmother would use it for whatever calamity or ailment I was suffering from when I was a kid! But regardless, I do believe it has some special inflammatory properties to it, and if I didn't say that I would probably be shot dead since I live in the #1 aloe growing region in the country Seriously, though, I think for some it is worth a try and may very well help. Just b/c I have a long aversion to it's taste doesn't mean it wouldn't be helpful for many ailments, including IC! Best wishes,Bri

contents: When I took it, it helped so I wasn't constipated from the meds I was taking. It has olive oil and I think another kind of oil so that could be why.

Does anyone use Move Free? I have been on it for about 2.5 moths. I amout of it right now and am waiting for Walgreens to have another buy ine get one free sale. Has this worked for anyone? I do notive my knees and ankles hurt less, but I wonder if it's all in my mind. THanks, Michelle.

If I am correct (and I may not be) I believe this is a gluc/chron/msm supplement. Many ICers have found relief with some combination of this. I use it myself, but cannot tolerate the chrondroitin do I take Glucosamine/msm, I think I have had some improvement with it but it takes usually about a month to see results for sure. A few things to be careful of 1. The chrondroitin makes alot of people flare 2. make sure it is not the sulfate version - also flares alot of people. good luck

After trying Cystoprotek, and determining that when I took it, I flared, but after quitting it for a week or two, I stayed flare free even when I shouldn't have been, I investigated the individual components. Had Move Free in the pantry for some reason, so tried it for the glucosamine/chonitidrin. No luck, flared, turns out I can't handle the chonitidrin - but glucosamine sulfate along works great - and no, you aren't imagining the joint improvement, Move Free kept my mother mobile for a long time despite extra weight, no exercise and a bad knee. Nice to get a two for one - joints and bladder, isn't it? terry

I hope someone can answer my question (and that I am posting it on the proper board.) As I was drying my hair tonight, I noticed that it seems that gray and white hairs have just EXPLODED on my head out of nowhere! However, even stranger was that when I started pulling some out, I noticed that most of them are in fact multicolored strands! What I mean is that they are gray/white at the roots and even a few inches down (like maybe 2-3 inches), then they are brownish-black for a few inches, then gray/white again, and then back to the brown. What the heck is up with this? I have never seen anything like this before! I thought when hair started turing gray or white that it stayed that way and dying it was the only thing to do if you didnt like the change, (so, of course I will be heading out to buy something tomorrow!) However, I wondering now if maybe a med could cause this or a diet or mineral deficiency. I mean, I dont know what else could account for my hair changing colors like this. Anyway, I was telling a friend tonite about this, and she said she thought she remembered reading something somewhere about a vitamin or mineral deficiency that can cause this, but she couldnt remember which one it was. So, I was wondering....1) Has anyone else ever experianced this? 2) If so, was yours med related or vit/mineral related? 3) Does anyone know of a vitamin/mineral deficancy that causes this? I hope I havent offended any of our gray/white haired friends. (I know lots of women who are quite beautiful with gray and/or white hair, however I am not one that could pull it off. I am doing good just look okayish with brownish-black hair. But, I SURE dont have the kind of face/looks that could pull off gray hair/white hair!) Thanks in advance to anyone who can help!

contents: Hi: I've had IC for l0 years and have a lot of chronic related conditions. I've been dealing with nausea for several months now and my internist has suggested that I start taking ALIGN DAILY PROBIOTICS.....has anyone heard of it taken it? It's fairly new to the market and is a good bacteria that supposedly make your digestive track better. I'm a little nervous to start taking them because I don't know if they will cause a bladder flare. I would appreciate any advice that you can offer. Thanks! lindie

don't know! I don't know of that brand in particular. There are tons of probiotics on the market and it can be overwhelming. My accupuncturist directed me to take Megadophilus by Natren. This can be found at whole foods markets. I also sometimes used Femdophilus by Jarred. I could only get that on-line. Good luck to you.

My gastronterologist recomended VSL#3, it is also used in hospitals....very expensive, but I believe it is the only one clinically studied, you can find it online and sometimes at Walgreens (at the pharmacy). I take the unflavored version. Jarrow is pretty reliable too, Femdophilus is good. I used Threelac for a while, it has lemon flavor and I think vitamin C. I stopped after a few months b/c I wasn't sure if it was flaring me. I don't know about other brands but I'd suggest you a refrigerated one. If you can handle Kefir or Yogurth that's a good way to go too, but it can be acidic.

contents: YES~ Judy~ PLEASE let us kow what the pan difference is. Also, what dosage you take. We fibro/IC'ers must help each other all we can. Michelle.

Magnesium helps other vitamins get absorbed into our systems. I have been doing alot of research on it since I heard Oprah's gyno talking about it. I have been taking it as a supplement for years to help me with my bowels and with the immune system. My prior doctor suggested I take it several years ago. Now, I have discovered that it helps with our muscular system. I don't have Fibro, but I am sure it would be helpful. It also helps with our stress levels.
This is interesting. I take magnesium to keep me regular and to balance out my calcium. I hope this helps your pain. Please let us know.

Magnesium I have been taking Magnesium Citrate (powder form)...built up to 300 mg at night......150 mg in the morning.....150 mg at lunch. I can curb an IC flare up by taking more. I also am totally reducing my sodium intake to let my bladder heal. Trying to keep between 100-200 mg per meal. Even drinking bottled water only because my water supply contains sodium too. I no longer have any pain and am worlds better than I was a few months ago! I feel like I can function and enjoy life again! I still have the "need to go" feeling some days, but the magnesium seems to really help that. I hope it helps you too!!

I am glad that you have been able to find improvement in your symptoms. Hopefully your success story will be a benefit for others. I know that taking the supplements that my prior doctor who is a pelvic pain specialist works very hard at natural ways of managing IC symptoms. She also realizes that we all can't get total relief, but with her suggested supplements and both her knowledge and now my new doctor's help, I am finally on a great combo of meds that have allowed me to decrease many of my meds and even stop a couple of them all together. I still deal with some pain (very low on the pain scale) on a daily basis, but coming from where I was 10 years ago when I was diagnosed with severe IC and pain meds where required 24/7, I am amazed how far I have actually come. I very rarely have flares, spasms or even urg and freq anymore. And I pray everyday that I will continue in that same direction.

contents: Hello ladies... my friend's mom was just recently diagnosed with IC and as we all know, she has LOTS of questions. One thing I was not able to answer for her is if she can take soy protein??? she says she takes this because she doesn't eat much meat at all. she's also a big vitamin supplement person... anyone out there that is familiar with reactions to soy protein and natural vitamin supplements??? thanks and God Bless

Hi Huntermommy, Yes, both soy and some vitamins can create problems for someone with an IC bladder. I can't have even the smallest amount of soy. As far as vitamins are concerned, most IC patients cannot tolerate extra B vitamins or C. Some IC patients can tolerate Ester-C or non-acid Vitamin C, but that is individual. I use whey protein powder to make shakes and smoothies. It is very good. :-)

contents: I was on this for I think 2 years. It helped out a lot with different things. It helped to sooth the bladder some, it helped so I didn't ache all over anymore and it helped so I wanst' constipated. I finally had to give it up because I couldn't afford it anymore. The last few days I have been trying to figure out how I could start buying it again to take it. One of the few things that really helped me out.

I Love Cystoprotek I started taking Cystoprotek and ALoe Vera from Desert Harvest in Jan. 2007 and I have been essentially getting better and largely fine since then. Dr. Theoharides recommended them both together, says they work better together. It took a month or two to get better and then I just kept getting better and better, and have been eating mostly what I want (not tomato sauce or tomato based soups or foods, but lots of "forbidden" foods and drinks, and about 1/2 glass wine very slowly pretty often. I've been drinking Teechino mixed with a large tablespoon of Puroast low acid cofffee, and occasionally a quarter cup of good coffee on the run. About 4 months ago, I cut way back on the aloe vera and about 3 months ago, I ran out of money and couldn't get the Cystoprotek, and seemed fine for a few weeks. One day I ate a sandwich with avocado and bacon, and that pushed me over the edge, and I began taking the two or 3 products in the Cystoprotek, quercetin and MSM, but they did not have the effect of the Cystoprotek, even in pretty high doses. I ordered the Cystoprotek Immediately and it did its magic within a few days or a week, and I have been felt much better. At this point in my recovery, I would definitely chose Cystoprotek over the aloe vera, but when starting out, I would do both. Cystoprotek is a fantastic supplement, and it is a crime that medical doctors do not embrace it as treatment of first resort for people with fairly mild symptoms. How fantastic that your doctor has recommended it!!!! It is a miracle. My doctor called me today and she suggests trying cystoprotek - has anyone had a lot of success with it? Bladder OneTree: Your problem with not feeling like you are empty could be due to a pelvic floor problem. For anyone who has not had a PFD work up it sounds ridiculous to suggest, but trust me a LOT of IC symptoms from burning urethra to frequency are tied to pelvic floor spasms, and pelvic floor nerve damage. I would check this out, what do you have to lose? Sammi

contents: Cystoprotek I have found the Cystoprotek very helpful in alleviating my symptoms in conjunction with other meds. I guess you could say I have mild IC. I have read many stories of those who are much worse off than me and my heart goes out to you. I feel so selfish for feeling sorry for myself at times! Here's my story... Symptoms: more or less constant mild to moderate frequency and urgency since childhood - I'm now 37 (how many of you went to the doctor as kids and were told it was all in your head because they couldn't find anything wrong with you) constant pressure in the urethra for the last few years(feels like my bladder is falling out) sensation of leakage but no actual leakage for the last few years bladder pain since January 08 I was finally diagnosed with IC this past February. (I think the only reason I got the right diagnosis was because I finally experienced bladder pain. Previous uros told me that if I didn't have pain then I couldn't have IC!). My uro prescribed Elavil 20mg/day,hydroxizine (Atarax) 75 mg/day, and a urinary alkinizer. Those got the intense urgency and pain under control within weeks, but I still had constant moderate urgency. He also prescribed Elmiron, which I was reluctant to take because I was afraid of the hair loss side effect (how vain!). I began taking Cystaprotek and Desert Harvest Aloe Vera, in late March instead of the Elmiron. After about a month, the pain was gone (as long as I stayed on the IC diet) and I noticed a further decrease in urgency/frequency. I also pursuaded my doctor to prescribe Singulair 10mg/daily. I read a study that suggested it may possibly help IC patients. He didn't think it would work, but I have found that it relieves the intense pressure I get in my urethra. By the beginning of May I was feeling much much better: could eat a few forbidden things from the IC diet without pain, had mild to no pressure in the urethra; decrease in urgency/frequency and short periods when I acutally did not feel the urge to pee! Sometimes I even felt "done" after urinating. That's the worst part for me, never feeling done and spending so much time in the bathroom! How do you get up off the toilet and leave the room when you still feel like you have to pee? The brain does not compute! In mid May I had an ovarian cyst removed. I requested a pediatric catheter because I read somewhere that since it it smaller it is less irritating. Hear me loud and clear: NEVER DO THIS! The thing was too small to handle the urine output and backed up. Apparently the nurse who kept bugging me to drink water to increase my urine output never bothered to check if the cath was working! At shift change, the new smarter nurse figured it out and spent 1/2 hour working the cath to empty my bladder, which was so full it hurt (even with intravenous pain meds from the surgery!) The cath then had to be replaced with a full size one. Between this and getting pushed around during the surgery, my bladder was a mess and the urgency and frequency returned with a vengance! By mid June I was feeling a little better but I went back to the doc to try something new. I am not a patient person. I had to stop the elavil because I was having trouble getting up in the morning but would probably feel better if I went back on it. He prescribed Vesicare 5mg/day, which is really helping with the urgency/frequency. I finally had the script for the Elmiron 100mg/3x day filled at the beginning of July (my doc said the hair loss is rather rare and I no longer care - just want to get better). I don't expect to notice any difference for a few months. But the Cystoprotek does not seem to be working as quickly as it did the first time. Maybe its because I stopped the Desert Harvest Aloe Vera (I ran out and decided to hold off while I try the Elmiron.) If you're not taking a urinary alkinizer, ask your doctor for one. I stopped taking mine for a week "just so see" and started having pain so I went back on it. Good luck! Never stop searching for answers. Trust your instincts. Fight for your health.

What is a urinary alkinizer ? Is that similar to Prelief? I have been taking Elmiron for several months now and the only side effect I notice was only one bloody stool (hope that's not too personal) and that was only one time. No hair loss at all. The only problem with Elmiron is the expense. It is so costly and my insurance has such a high deductable. But it seems everything associated with this illness is expensive. I am thinking of trying the Desert Harvest Aloe Vera. I am going to a IC Forum in Tulsa OK the first weekend in August and Desert Harvest in one on the sponsors. I am hoping to get some more info on it there. I guess it's all about trying things until we find out what works best for us. I was doing so great until I quit taking Atarax because it made me so tired, and now I am hurting again. I had almost forgotten what it felt like then so quickly it all came back again. Darn!!!! contents: urinary alkilizer First off - I spelled it wrong, sorry - its "alkalizer" - makes the urine less acidic and irritating to the bladder. I take potassium citrate (generic for Urocit-K).My uro tested my urine and said it was very acidic. Sodium bicarb (good old baking soda) does the same thing but has a high salt content so you can't use too much and shouldn't use if you have certain medical problems - esp high blood pressure. Always best to ask the doc first. I use Prelief when I want to eat something that is acidic. It has calcium and magnesium in it. Seems to work. I'm not sure if it helps to take it daily or how much you would need to take - can't find any info on this. One thing to be careful of when using any calcium supplements (or multivitamins) is not to take them right before or with your other meds because it can alter absorption. I'm not sure how long you're really supposed to wait after taking your meds but I would give it at least an hour - that's how long you have to wait to eat after taking Elmiron. But it might be longer for certain supplements. Another thing not to take with meds is grapefruit juice (not that any of us ICers should be drinking it anyway!) because it can also interfere with meds. When I tried the Aloe Vera I was also taking the Cystaprotek so I can't say if it works as well alone. I did take more Aloe for flares and it seemed to calm them. I just decided to cut back my hydroxizine from 25mg/3xday to twice because I am sick of being an airhead!!!

I, too, am using Cysto-Protek and it has worked wonders for me. I was dealing with the pain by using quite a bit of Fioricet (migraine med, but it worked here too), which was not so good for my liver. The CP has been wonderful. I do 4 a day, 2 with breakfast and 2 with dinner. If I'm feeling flair-ish, I'll up it to 6 a day. My doctor even asked me for the information so she could recommend it to 2 of her other IC patients.
signature: Stuff I'm taking... Climara estrogen patch - 0.1mg/day Amitriptyline - 10 mg/day for pain Elmiron - 100 mg 3xday Phenazopyridine plus - 1 4xday as needed Fiorcet - up to 6xday for IC pain and migraine

contents: aloe vera juice I know that this thread is a little old, but I will share what I know... ALoe vera juice can be made with preservatives (inparticularly in combination with Vit C) which has the chance of causing a flare in many ICers. I think that these preparations also have the laxative affect, so it may no tbe the best combination. I guess what is so unique about the Desert Harvest brand is the concentration of aloe and the lack of other preservatives and "side effects" (laxative effect). It doesn't appear that you can get that concentration of aloe with any other brand. I learned a lot by reading the lectures that are available through this site. I agree that it is expensive! Good Luck!

contents: I have been feeling better over the last week and few days on my traditional allopathic regime (Elimiron, Vistaril). So much so that the MD doesn't want to see me for 2 months. So that is good news, but I still deal with day to day "stuff," "fatigue" in my pelvic region in the evening, pressure and urgency occassionally when I am stressed. Overall not too bad considering everything else that I've heard I don't know whether or not to leave well enough alone or give a supplement a try. And at that, which one? With Cystaprotek being one of the only ones offering a free sample, I hate to sink another $40... and supplements are a big trigger for me (I have never tried any of the IC supplements before, but this whole thing started with taking vitamins and I was having general discomfort on another supplement I tried). I have also been reading the IC puzzle by Armit- has anyone tried that with success? I know that some of you will recommed talking to my MD, he doesn't seem to know too much about the supplements (and it was very difficult to even find someone to treat my IC,so I would have difficulty switching). He is a firm believer in the diet, but after reading Armit- it seems that even the diet can be complicated. Sometimes I wished I lived in a metro area where holistic practitioners were more readily available! Thanks for listening and any advice is welcome.

contents: This is an FYI for anyone trying the Cystoprotek and not having success with it. I have had IC 10 years now (it was a 40th bday present ) but with regular bc pills, heparin/lidocaine/sodium bicarb instillations every three months and Detrol LA (switched from Prozac at 8 years) I was symptom free most of the time - until I hit 48 and the instillations lasted shorter and shorter. Long story short, figured out that at the exact point in the bc pill pack every month, 2nd week 2nd day, my estrogen level drops or the progestin peaks - and flareup occurs. OB/Gyn offered stronger BC, tried it, no diff, went to a dose of Premarin the day before Dday, Dday and after, which worked for a year, this January, that wasn't working, I was getting mild flare. So - ordered Cystoprotek. Started on lower dose, was already in mild flare so no difference. Flare corrected itself, After about a week on it, I looked at my pill pack, and thought, I should be hurting - huh? Must be the Cystoprotek - which I had not been taking for 7 days prior. So natch, swallowed a dose that night, and - instant flare (well within a couple of hours). Tried again a week later, still instant bladder change. So that wouldn't work. Decided to take the ingredients separately, one at a time, because something in the mix was helping, and was able to build up in my system enough that a week without it still had results - I just couldn't tolerate the whole list of ingredients, and wasn't willing to have flare for one week on, one week off due to supplements. Started with Glucosamine Sulfate and Chlonditrin (sp) since I had some in the house, flare within 2 hours. Tried twice, no go, so went and got just Glucosamine Sulfate after reading on this board what had set others off. And guess what. I can be starting a flare, and if I take the glucosamine, it calms down. I made it through the D-day and week off pills with mild or no discomfort.If I miss the glucosamine dose after D-day and before the new pack, I will notice, but taking a dose gets results by the next morning. I am going to go through one more cycle using this to see if results are consistent, then I will try adding the Quercetin. My real goal would be to allow me to stop bc pills and allow menopause to happen, my mother was just diagnosed with uterine cancer at 80, which is casued by essentially too much estrogen, so I can't really afford to stay on bcs past the next 2-3 years. Hopefully the change my doc wants to make to elmiron in the instill instead of heparin won't ruin the progess I have made....will let you know the progress in case it can help anyone else... terry

Hi Terry Someone said to me before here on the boards that we often have to be our own detectives to determine exactly WHAT is causing symptoms. I had to do the same with my vitamins and supplements, because I know I need them. Taking them all at the same time after lunch I finally realized was causing me my everyday afternoon pain, after feeling just fine before an IC safe lunch. So I tried taking just one vitamin each by itself after lunch for several days, no pain. Just to make sure, I tried them all together again, pain. Then I tried taking just one of each vitamin/supplement after each meal or a good snack spread out throughout the day, and yea, no pain! So now I can take all my vitamins and supplements after eating something, as long as I don't take them all at one time. I'm so happy you found what worked for you and were able to weed out what was aggravating your symptoms...I've read here that others also have had much luck with glucosamine...I'm in remission right now so haven't tried it, but it is one thing I DO plan to try if my symptoms come back. I hope you continue to feel better and better....thanks for sharing your success story...always good to hear what has really helped someone! Best wishes Bri

hi everyone...i'm not sure why exactly but have been feeling very discouraged the last few days and just am feeling so sorry for myself. i know that you all have a shoulder i can cry on and just whine to i've been taking DH aloe and cystoprotek for about 3 months now (i don't remember exactly but right around there) i have found some releif....i rarely have pain anymore but the feeling like i have to pee all the time and the pressure are stretching me to my max!! i feel like if the cystoprotek doesn't work for me then there is no hope! and since i still don't feel good and its been the 3 months, that cytoprotek isn't working. i was so hopeful for the stories that i've read, that i would get to say i am symptom free like others have! and i can't and i'm just so discouraged! should i quit taking and i try something else? well, thank you all for listening!!! hugs to everyone!

contents: ditropan? Have you tried ditropan yet? I took the generic of it, and it helped break that constant urge to pee feeling for me. I had to start with high doses (like 3 or 4 times a day) after about 2 weeks things started to calm down. I was eventually able to wean back to 1 a day, and now don't take it at all. I know how maddening that feeling is. I would pee every 10 minutes round the clock for days at a time. Sleep medications did not help either. I also take cystoprotek. You can take it at the same time you are on bladder meds (Although you should ask your doctor first). I found the aloe vera made me flare for some reason. Whenever I try it, my frequency goes way up and so does my burning. You may want to experiment and try not taking it for a few days to see if that helps. Hope this helps.

Hi, I am so sorry your not feeling well. Do you have a uro you can talk to about some other options for the pressure and feeling of having to go all the time? I never tried the meds you are on. I would talk to your doctor about it though and see what they suggest. I hope you feel better. Lisa

contents: Finally I got my dx of IC...after too many tests and going through 2 potassium tests (the first a delayed reaction and the 2nd positive), in-office cysto and hydrodistention w/biopsy which was negative for mast cells, a PUF score of above 20, food sensitivities, etc. I tried Elmiron in the past, but my hair started to fall out in chunks...not attractive especially since I have to meet with clients, etc. for my work and after reading lots on Elmiron and how not much of it actually gets into your bladder, I am not jumping to try it again. My dr wants me to just try PT for PFD, but I have been going weekly since early March and the pain is getting worse, not better. I already take hydroxyzine (25mg) and do the IC diet....so I am looking to add something else to the mix. I was thinking about trying the Desert Harvest Aloe and Cystoprotek, but was wondring if I should start with the aloe and then add the cysto if the aloe doesn't take care of all the pain, or start them together, etc. Anyone have any advice? It would be much appreciated.

My main advise is not to start them both at the same time....you want to know what helped if anything did and if something bothered you what did. Which should be first is up to you I prefer Aloe for myself but that was because of my own experiences. Good luck I hope you get to a point of relief!

contents: I was taking MSM/Glucosamine from GNC and I feel that I am having a weird side effect from it. Still trying to figure it out thought.....I normally run 3 miles every day and never had a pain on my knee before, I mean never. But recently with a bladder flare up, at the same time, taking MSM/Glucosamine, Elmiron, DH Aloe Vera........I started having immense, excruciating pain in both knees. I could not kneel anymore, the pain was so bad. I have since stopped running, stopped MSM and the pain is very slowly going away. I know the running did not cause it, as I have been doing that for a long time and never had the pain there. I do get muscular pains from exercise, but never had it in the knee. I was thinking, maybe it is coming from the Glucosomine/MSM - the sulphate. Now, if this is the case, then would I also react to Cystoprotek? Does that contain the same sulphate that Glucosomine/MSM contains? Does anyone know. Please tell me. I want to try Cystoprotek before quitting Elmiron. I am so afraid of hair loss, as it is, I was already losing so much hair due to perimenupause. And now this. And also do they have a refund policy for Cystoprotek? does anyone know? Thanks.

They do have a refund policy, although they ask that you first order some samples to try, so that you can see if you will have any stomach problems (which is the main side effect and 99% of the time with people with IBS or other types of stomach sensitivities). I'm curious about your other question as well because I also take it, and I'm hoping to start MSM. I emailed the CystoProtek rep and she is going to ask Dr. Theoharides who was one of the researchers who developed the product. I'll keep you posted!

contents: can i take Elmiron & CystoProtek? i started CystoProtek 2 days ago. i'm taking 2 twice daily. i am still taking Elmiron. it has been my savior since 2006. however, i have had enough bladder pain lately that i had to restart hydroxyzine, pyridium, and kadian as needed. i figured CystoProtek sounded worth a try since apparently Elmiron is not working as effectively as it has been in the past. CystoProtek actually costs less than Elmiron too. is it safe to keep taking Elmiron with the CystoProtek? i have SO much pain without Elmiron if i even go over a day without it. Cystoprotek states it takes 2-3 mos. to work effectively (same as it took me with Elmiron). do you think i could continue Elmiron during that 2-3 mos? has anyone done this? i am hoping to "convert" and stop taking Elmiron altogether. i also had a very bad reaction to hydrodistension and DMSO procedure in 2006. i was in such bad pain i wanted to die. i had to stay in bed all the time that i was not in the bathroom. it lasted over two mos. thank God for a pain specialist and a great physiotherapist! i am happy to try anything not so invasive and potentially harmful. contents: I am going to give it a try. After 33 years of IC, it would be great if I could get just a little more relief. Most days I do pretty good, but I am always aware of my bladder. I do take Hydroxyzine also and hope this will be a winning combination for me. I am easing into it to make sure I don't react. I took one tablet last night with supper. My biggest problem is that I spend a lot of time traveling to clients homes for work and I can't be having a lot of frequency when I have to be out on the road. So hopefully it was just a fluke and the little flare won't come back. I am sure after having IC for as long as I have that it will take time to heal. I just pray that this works!

contents: Hi Dreamy, I had a very positive response to Cystoprotek. I suffered from IC 9 years ago for appox 5 months (was never diagnosed and went into remission for 9 year.) My IC returned this summer with a vengence and I was finally diagnosed. I would encourage anyone to try "natural" or complementary therapies however......here is my opinion. I believe the key to getting control over IC is to "sooth the bladder when symptoms are your worse, and only when you feel a significant improvement, to try "natural " therapies. During my worst months I was doing instillations, starting Elmeron, drinking baking soda, taking pain meds, Detrol etc. etc. and felt worse and worse. This is what I am trying to explain... I believe that CystoProtek helped me "control my urgency and freqency" because my bladder was ready for this type of treatment. I feel I was successful at "soothing" my bladder months prior by the IC Diet, improving my voiding habits (I hold my urine because it stings!), drinking more water, avoiding diet soda, caffine and trigger foods, self-directed relaxation techniques, slowing down and resting when needed, getting good pain management. Slowly my bladder began to respond. I am doing so much better and my bladder is having more good days than bad. This is the time that I started CystoProtek . When you think your bladder can handle something "new" give it a try and go very slowly. I still only take 1-2 caps a day (others can take the prescribed 4). If you have any more questions feel free to PM me. Warm Regards, Goldengirl
Does Cystoproteck relieve all the IC symptoms or has it lessen them?

(she supposedly works with the lady's group promoting the juice) and she told me the PH in the juice actually reduces the acid in the stomach and she feels this juice has more antihistimines than Aloe Vera and will work faster to heal the bladder. No luck with Elmiron I tried Elmiron for about 6 months and had little success - this was after hidrodistension surgery. I was diagnosed about 7 years ago, but only flared up for a month. I was in remission (which I didn't know until know) until a little over 2 years ago. Since then, the only time I was pain free was the 2 1/2 months after I broke up with my boyfriend ( when I wasn't having sex). Sex makes the symptoms unbearable - we got back together and within a week I got a UTI and was back on pain meds. I have been struggling for over 2 years - as former drug addict (I have Hep C, too), pain meds are not good for me I start abusing them. It seems that the only choice I have is to not have any sex at all. My boyfriend and I got in a huge fight over the weekend - because it always comes back to the same thing. For over 2 years, he has put up with it - saying he will cut back on sex but to him that's going 2 or 3 days. I don't know what to do anymore, but at this point, celibacy doesn't seem that bad anymore. It's better than being in pain and strung out on pain pills not able to function or handle my 5 year old. I wrecked my car a couple of weeks ago driving on vicodin. I upped my anti-depressants to deal with this. Ok, by everyone - glad there are people out there that can relate to how bad IC is and can get.

The person who posted above me is correct - And, yes, it does take time to work. Most say 3-4 months to see any improvement. I've been on it for 3 weeks so far, and I feel the same so far. I do have an occasional day where I'd feel okay, but it's probably not due to the Cystoprotek. I am on many other medications as well in addition to Cystoprotek. (Elmiron, Elavil, Ditropan XL, Desert Harvest Freeze-Dried Aloe Vera, MSM/Glucosamine, and L-Arginatine...I think that's all of 'em!) I started all of these about 3 weeks ago, except the Elmiron - I started that 2 and half months ago when the IC became debilatating.

I started Cystoprotek about 7 months ago. I was taking it and still wasn't feeling too great and then near the 3 month period I started to feel a lot better. Of course, I was trying to figure out what I was doing that made me feel better and then I realized I had hit the 3 month mark for Cystoprotek. It takes about 3 months for Cystoprotek to start working. For me, it really works. I am taking 2 in the morning and 2 at supper. I also take Vistaril and Enablex. I was taking Elavil but my blood pressure was elevated and I gained 10 pounds. Stopped the Elavil and had no increase in IC symptoms. Still have the 10 pounds but I just joined a health club. Hopefully, I will either firm up or loose the 10 pounds!!!!

I stopped taking Elimiron and developed ulcers again. Started taking again and will have to wait 4 months for effectives. I now understand that my IC will not go away and I will have to take Elmiron for the rest of my life.

contents: Yes, Cystoprotek is for IC. I stopped taking Elmiron after being on the Aloe Vera for a couple of months. Had a flare up, and after that started taking the Cystoprotek. I'm still drinking lots of water and limiting the amount of acidic foods and drinks, but it's amazing how well it's helped.

Nothing but good news for me with CystoProtek!! The company that manufactures it is located in the same city as I live, so I met with the director of marketing for CystoProtek. She says that the majority of people who try it for long periods of time (6-9 months) and 4-6 capsules a day usually find some relief with it. Many find a lot of relief. That's the feedback that she gets.

Address is 6720 Old McLean Village Drive, McLean,print up the paperwork, take the urine test from home then fed ex next day. sometimes they find yeast. when i spoke with the lady on the phone and she said yes pretty much all the tests come back with some sort of bacteria showing. good luck

contents: hey nicole, actually I have communicated with Dr. Theoharides and the company who distributes CystoProtek and according to the research that he has done, it does in fact also coat the bladder wall. But you're right, it also has ingredients that serve as anti-inflammatories. It uses ingredients that are make up the bladder wall cells. Jill has also included three great transcripts on this website explaining all about it. I know it's working well for me, and my IC story sounds similar to yours. I hope it does as well for you!
signature: Karen

contents: what i meant to say was that Cystoprotek is a lot different in what it uses to coat the bladder. Glucosamine and sodium hylauranate make up the bladder wall so the idea is that you can increase the lining with supplementation. but elmiron is a total different chemical all together so you shouldnt get the same bad side effects.

contents: Here's the answer from the product manager at Alaven (who distributes CystoProtek): According to the manufacturer, the capsules should not be kept in the freezer all the time as it could damage the integrity of the product. It’s not going to cause any adverse episodes, but the product may not be as effective. Putting only the capsules you plan to take within the next 30 minutes in the freezer is fine. For those with sensitive stomachs, it may help avoid mild nausea. Generally speaking, the capsules should be kept in a cool, dry place, away from children.

contents: Seafood allergy I have severe seafood allergies and was nervous about trying Cystoprotek or Glucosamine Chondroitin for that reason. I was accidentally "tricked" into trying Glucosamine Chondriotin by a guy selling Mona Vie, who told me it was "just fruit juice." After my initial stunned reaction when I read the list of ingredients (too late!) I waited and had no bad reaction. A few days later I rubbed half a Glucosamine Chondriotin tablet on my tongue and waited. Still no reaction, so I started taking Cystoprotek. So far the only thing I notice is perhaps some itchy eye symptoms (which might be seasonal allergies too) but I don't think I'm having any other side effects. If anyone is allergic to seafood, I would advise to test very carefully and proceed with caution, but I was told that Glucosamine Chondriotin is make with the shells, not the meat of the seafood and is generally safe for many people with seafood allergies. Your milage may vary, so test carefully!
The word I got was that if you're allergic to shell fish, you shouldn't take Cystoprotek. Donna

I have been lucky enough to see Dr Hanno, the top IC specialist in PA who has told me due to the fact that I have not responded to any "standard" treatments that I have a severe, end stage case of IC with a horrible quality of life (didn't have to tell me that last part!) Proud wifey of Shane, mommy to Griffin, and step-mom to Logan and Gage Also proud mom to the best Bullmastiff on earth, Claus

contents: Tried Cysta-q I have tried Cysta-q when I had a mild flare to add to my elmiron. I did eventually get better and it did not seem to bother me . I took it for about a month. The only thing that concerns me is that it has cranberry powder, and an enzyme from pineapple, and these things are supposed to be bad for IC. Next time I may try cysta protek. At least the ingredients in this make sense for ic patients. Both have an antioxident called querciten which is supposed to have anti-inflammatory effects, but you can buy querciten at your health food store for a fraction of the cost of these pricey supplements.

I like Cysta Q. I haven't noticed a difference changing from Cystoprotek to Cysta Q. I've switched a month ago or so. I stopped Cystoprotek b/c it is rough on the stomach and I have a sensitive stomach. I used to freeze them but if on a given day I had to take a strong pain med like 2 Darvocet pills, I had to stop taking Cystoprotek b/c it was too much on my tummy. Cysta Q is fine with me. You could get a sampl but you need to get it through your uro.

contents: i TOOK the free sample bottle and really liked it. I felt better from taking it I think. I am on a lot of other meds so it's hard to be sure it was that helping me, but I think it was. I need to get more. I tried the Cysto-portek and it hasn't helped me as much.

Hard to tell what is helping. I think most of us are on multiple meds so that it is often difficult to know which one has made the difference when we do feel better. The other thing is that healing a damaged bladder is a very long process. Most medications that actually help heal the bladder take a minimum of 3-4 months to work. During that course in time, it is actually possible that the bladder was going to heal itself anyway. I am trying cysto-protek. It doesn't bother me so I will do the suggested 4 month course and report back. So far so good.

I received my 1 bottle today. I have had a sample bottle before but only took it one month. This time, I am going to use it for the 3 months specified. I had called Farr Labs the manufacturer. The cranberry powder used is acid free. I have never heard of that before. I am in desperate need of relief since I have tried many things already without relief.
