contents: I took the bottle of Cysta Q. Slowly, over time, of course. It seemed to help some. Then, I bought the Cystoprotek to compare and I don't think it's doing a lot for me. I will try to take it more regularly, but I like the Cysta Q better. It's so hard to tell though if that was what helped me or not. I still have pain every day, but am not quite as miserable as I was before. My urethra feels much better than before, but I have tons of bladder pain.

Since none of the uros I have gone to, nor primary care doctors or gyno know what Cysta Q is. Does anyone know if Cysta Q can be taken while on nortriptyline, DMSO instills, Urelle as needed and Vicadin as needed? I should get my period tomorrow. I am in so much pain right now with burning. It is 1am and I am still up.

contents: Bunny, Don't giwe up. How is DMSO working for you? Does it help? Remmber there are other treatments for IC: Botox, Cytotec, spirulina, Aloe Vera, so if you haven't tried those yet, you still have some things to try without throwing the towel ( I don't think we should throw the towell anyways) try, try, and try. We are here for you if you need support. It'll get better. Are you doing something to take care of the emotional part? This illness can wear you down and you need to do all you can to keep your mind in tune since it is very connected to the immune system and to the ability to help the body heal. Best wishes. SMR

contents: Have you tried Urelle I have recently started Urelle four times a day. It has helped me alot with the burning and does not seem to bother my stomach like pyridium. I also had no luck with DMSO. My uro who sees a lot of ic does not use it any more, and told me he thinks a lot of uros are abandoning it because of poor response rate. Have you tried more frequent instills like several times a week? Many patients here are doing several times a week or even daily with Xylocaine and heparin. You would probably need to learn to do them yourself but that might be worth a try. Good Luck

When I was having heparin instills, it was only once a month since back in mid 2004, I was only diagnosed with mild ic. It seems as if my bladder is always in a flare. My co-worker today asked if it could be all in my head. From this remark, I know she truly doesn't understand what IC is nor seems to have listened when I have told her. I try not to complain too much to her, but I need someone to listen. I go in this Wednesday to the uro and ask him what else can be tried. When I was getting DMSO every week, I seemed to have a constant flare. UGH said the bug.

I just have to let it go. It actually makes me feel better not to talk to them about it. Sorry you are having such a hard time. I'm there too. This disease is so hard to manage. I take pain killers and then they make me want to sleep and have a number of other side effects.
I would rather have something wrong in which I can take a pill and all my symptoms go away rather than trying different things and nothing works.

contents: Hi everyone, I've been having problems with straight Quercetin, which helped me a whole lot for a while (and now it gives me really, really bad heartburn that interferes with my sleep and also seems to make my symptoms worse). Someone recommended Cystoprotek as being better for digestion issues (and the Cystoprotek web pages says only 1% of people tested had problems), so I'm thinking about trying it. I usually like to do lots of research before I put anything into my body, though. But, even though I've done some reading up on this, I still have a few questions . . . And I'd really appreciate any answers you all might have concerning some or all of them . . . 1. The information from the company says its for pain, but I'm wondering if it supposed to help with frequency? 2. The ingredients listed include soy, which I normally stay away from because I've heard that it's bad for IC symptoms. Is there anyone out there who doesn't normally tolerate soy, but takes Cystoprotek and is ok with it? 3. I can't find studies that discuss specific success rates beyond saying that Cystoprotek is "well tolerated" and "effective" (the only one I've seen mentions success in 1/3 of patients who took it intervesically, but I think that means that the meds were injected directly into the bladder). Does anyone know where I can find studies with specific success rates? 4. I've read that it will take 4-6 months to work. I'm not taking any other medicine right now --and am pretty much going nuts because my symptoms have been so bad for the last couple of months. So, I'm kind of reluctant to be sitting around (and shelling out $300-$500 for 4-6 pills a day) while I'm waiting for something that may or may not work (especially if I can't find any studies with success rates out there). Have any of you seem some improvement before the 4-6 month mark? I guess I'm looking for something to hold on to besides just blind faith, so I'm asking if symptoms start to get a little better bit by bit between months 0-6 or if nothing happens until month 4-6? 5. What are the side effects of Cystoprotek? I've read that some people can't really can't tolerate it because of digestive issues, but are there any other side effects? 6. Does taking aloe with Cystoprotek help with possible digestive issues or does the aloe help in other ways? Once again, any information you all have about any or all of this--or any experiences you have to share would be greatly appreciated. Best wishes, little bear

Hey Littlebear, I don't blame you for wanting to ask before you fork up that kind of money, I am pretty much going through the same thing. I haven't tried this product, but I'll answer it from what I've seen and someone else can always correct me if I'm wrong. 1. When inflammation goes down, the frequency might go down. I think it's how it works. From what, I've read, I see people say they experience decrease in voiding. 2. I can't answer this, I don't really know but my guess is it could be some form that doesn't effect the bladder or in a small quantity and unable to do much harm. 3. http://www.ic-network.com/guestlectu...cript0503.html <<< Have you checked that out? There is a study on here: http://algonot.com/articles_details.php?ID=33 I haven't heard of intervesically using it. 4. I think it's gradual improvement and some don't seem to feel much, I guess it's how damaged your bladder is. I would suggest buying one and seeing if you're able to take it, like the issues with the stomach and stuff. If you're tolerating it well for a week, to order the second bottle and third and go through it for three months. Three to four months seems to be the time period, to see improvement in this product it seems. Since, you're not taking anything else, you would be able to tell if it helps, since most of us are on many things at once. I know I am. What are your symptoms? If you're in pain, I would suggest pyridium, control bladder spasms, but it's short term treatment. Most medication, take awhile to get into your symptom and show some improvement, just like Cystoprotek. It's a gamble with this illness, what works and what doesn't. I think you could take Prelief now, to help with acid level in the urine. That could help pain and frequency and it's pretty quick to see results, it's only for when you eat something problematic. 5. There doesn't seem to be other side effects, besides those stomach issues, because of the capsule cover? 6. To me that sounds like a good idea but I'm not sure if that's a good combination. I've read someone take honey to coat the stomach after taking Elmiron, since it's pretty strong. Hope the above helps. Vampireness

Hi! Thanks so much for your reply. I really appreciate it. Thanks for finding the second study too. I hope that all is well with you and that you have a wonderful weekend. Thanks again and best wishes, little bear
You're welcome. Glad you found it useful. Let us know, when you've tried the product, I am thinking of trying it sometime. Vampireness

contents: I take MSM 1500mg twice a day + Glucosomine with MSM tablets, 2 once a day. I think the MSM really helps. When I don't take it, I can tell. I ran out of my vitamin E and Fish Oil last week. I really noticed a difference not taking it. Once I started taking it again I felt better. I'm not trying to confuse you, but these are the alternatives that help me. I take these along with the Elmiron. I have read that Chondritin makes people flare so be careful with that one. I just added Quertcin to the mix this week. That is supposed to reduce inflammation and that is my main complaint - menstrual cramp pain like you.

Thank you for replying. Yes I've heard bad things about Chondrotin too but then Dr. Moldwin recommends it in his book so I figure I'm going to try. I have also heard mixed things about the MSM flaring some people. I guess I've got to take it one supplement at a time!

signature: Dianne My bellydance "sisters" , our dogs, and me.

contents: I've also just been diagnosed with osteoporosis, and prescribed Fosamax, but I'm researching it before trying it. What I've found so far hasn't been good. I think this is potentially a dangerous drug (well, all drugs have side -effects) but there are lawsuits currently against the makers of Fosamax for jaw bone necrosis. I just dont like the whole gastro irritation angle of these biophosphate type drugs like this or Actonel. I'm not saying I won't ever take it, but I'm taking a wait and see approach about it, particularly with the IC. Of course osteoporosis is not a good thing, but as with any diagnosis, I think we should all be informed patients, consumers and advocates before jumping into a medication. I'd be interested to know if anyone else has problems with it and IC, etc.
Fosamax I take Actonel once a week and it does not bother me.

contents: Algonot info Well, the first thing I would do is click on the Patient Handbook link at the top of this page. You will find articles on Cystoprotek and articles and interviews with its inventor; Dr. T. This should give you a good idea of what Cystoprotek is about. Then, I would go to www.algonot.com the website of Cystoprotek and read their studies and information. cystoprotek can be taken with Elmiron. There is also quite a lot of posting on another link about taking Glucosamine, Chondrotin and MSM. A lot of the posters are buying the glucosamine over the counter, and if you read the articles on the Algonot website and IC patient handbook; you will see that all chondroitin products are not equal or even pharmacy grade. Some of the chondroitin is made from European cow trachea. I certainly would not take a product made from cows from the land of one of the biggest Mad Cow outbreaks in the Western world. Algonots is made from shark cartilage. It is also good for Arthritis, as well as reduces mast cells. If you want to add MSM, get pharmacy grade. Algonot makes a pharmacy grade product with no other additives which is why it is more expensive. If you want to try some, you should get the three pack and take it for 3 months 4 a day if on Elmiron, 6 a day if not. I still take mine with my Elmiron. Sammi

Try it and hope for the best It's funny how many of these supplements contain ingredients that we have been told shold be avoided, for instance cysta-Q has cranberry powder, and an enzyme, yet many people have been helped by it. I think the best thing to do is start slowly with a small dosage, and if things go ok then gradually step it up. I have been taking cystoprotek for a few weeks now with no difficulty. It does not even bother my stomach. I'm not sure how long it takes to start to improve your IC, but I am hoping it will be sooner rather than later. In my many years of dealing with this disease, I have learned that although things can deteriorate rather suddenly, the journey back is always a slow process which is often marked with quite a few setbacks. It is difficult to to keep a positive attitude during such trying times, but patience often pays off. Do not abandon any possible treatment until you have given it a full trial.

Things are getting better again. The only other thing I'm on is Green Magma and UltraClear pH -- which neutralize acidity. IC is the kind of thing that takes time to heal, so I don't plan on rushing into any other treatment (dmso) unless I'm feeling more desparate. Have even thought of getting acupuncture to see if that would help. Anything but having an invasive treatment! What are your symptoms? Mine is frequency, sometimes urgency, and some abdominal pain. contents: I think I remember reading it can take 4 months to work.

In most cases it takes 3 to 4 months to see an improvement in symptoms; however, some patients see improvement in a shorter period of time. Additionally, if your symptoms are caused by Pelvic Floor Dysfunction, CystoProtek will most likely not help to resolve those symptoms (which can mimic IC symptoms). Your treatment regimen may also need to be adjusted; for example, if you are living in an area where allergy season is at its peak, then your healthcare provider may increase or add antihistamine to your treatment regimen. These drugs and dietary supplements work synergistically together to help you get better faster. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you. Only your healthcare provider can determine if the above suggestions are appropriate treatment options for you; these are just some suggestions that you can discuss with him/her. The contents in this post are provided for informational purposes only. The contents are not intended in any way to be a substitute for professional medical advice. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. Best wishes, ICB

contents: Hi! My doctor just told me today about using Cystoprotek but he told me not to use Cysta Q and Cystoprotek at the same time. He also told me about Dessert Harvest Freeze Dried Aloe Vera. differences Hi there, They have different ingredients, but the main way I separate them in my mind is that Cysta Q has a couple of ingredients in it that help relax you, while CystoProteck seems to focus on reducing inflammation, and coating/rebuilding the bladder lining. Aloe works differently....it may work to decrease inflammation, but it also has healing properties. That is very simplistic, but you can read more about the products either in the ICN store or at the different products' websites.

contents: My uro. said it would be fine to take these all at the same time. I really haven't had much success with the Elmiron (2x a day, out of capsule) I know it takes a while to get results but I have been doing this for several months. They don't work against each other I hope. I just can't seem to get past the frequency and urgency feeling. The Pelvic Floor therapy with the PT helps with actiave bladder control. But it would be so nice not to have to feel like I constantly have to pee. Susan

susan, I wish I had an answer as i have the same problem. I constantly have to pee as well. One thing that has made a bit of a difference for me is Atarax. I take 10mg at bedtime and it helps with the waking up to pee all night. Also, I have a lot of pain and the neurontin has also helped with the frequency. mabe it's dulling the nerves enough where I don't notice the feeling of having to pee so much. anyway, i go about every 45 minutes to an hour on a good day now! way better than ever 15-25 on a good day. It might be worth looking into. hang in there friend. you are not alone. much love, laura

Laura's treatment regimen sounds very similar to what the average IC patient receives as a first line treatment option. My only suggestion is that you discuss increasing the dose of Atarax with your healthcare provider; he or she can determine if this may be helpful in reducing your frequency and pain. Moreover, you may want to ask about bladder retraining to train your bladder to send "pee" signals to your brain less often. Taking all of the medications and dietary supplements (i.e. CystoProtek, Desert Harvest Aloe Vera & Elmiron) may help to improve/control your symptoms faster; there are no reports of contraindications with these prescription medication and dietary supplements. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you. Only your healthcare provider can determine if the above suggestions are appropriate treatment options for you; these are just some suggestions that you can discuss with him/her. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. Good luck to you both!!! contents: The typical effective dosage for Elmiron is 300mg a day (which is three pills a day). 200mg might not be enough to be effective, I would talk to your doctor about incresing your dosage to the recommended 300mg. Also I take both cystoprotek and Elmiron and I have found that for me they work well together. I have read studies which suggest that they do indeed work together to improve the lining of the bladder and cystoprotek also has some antiinflamatory properties which may aid in protecting the bladder as well. Erin

After about 2 weeks of taking Cystoprotek all my symptoms were gone. Then I replaced some of my amalgam fillings and some symptoms returned. Now after about 6 weeks I still have some symptoms but it is much better than it was before I started. I beginning I thought that Cystoprotek was working wonders, but now I would say it works for me but I still have to be careful with food. The manufacturer recommends at least 3 months trial. All the listed ingredients are good and I’ll continue to take it for at least 6 months. I haven’t had any experience with CystaQ. I shouldn’t hurt anyone so it is worth trying. Alan

I really like cystoprotek. I was in a huge flair and after taking it for about four days things started to feel better. I have been on it now for about 3 months and I am able to eat a more "normal" diet again and my symptoms are not as noticable. Now the only thing that really gets to me is stress. I really recommend it. Erin

Hi everyone, I'm wondering about Cysta-Q. I've been taking it and it seems to help some at double doses (2 capsules 2 times a day). But, it is so darned expensive . . So, I'm trying to save money (and maybe even to find something that works even a little better). Has any one tried just plain Quercetin and had it work? If so, how much did you take and what brand did you buy? I know that Quercetin with Bromelain might be better as far as helping with the body's absorbing the Quercetin, but I tried Now's brand and it seemed to cause problems the first day. So, I'm also wondering if anyone has tried Quercetin with Bromelain successfully (how much did you take? what brand did you buy?). Also, if anyone has any other suggestions, knowledge, or experiences they'd like to share, I'd love to hear them! Best wishes, little bear

Bromelain sent me into a flare. I cannot take it. Quercetin (non-citrus) has been a major help, though. I take 500 miligrams twice a day. The brand I take is Natures-Way and I was getting it at our Whole Health store. They recently stopped carrying it so I have found it on the internet. I have also read that quercetin is a powerful anti-oxident and may help lessen bladder infections. You might also add glucosamine-chondroitin to your regimen. It is also very helpful with pain relief.

Hi Bittersweet Rain, Thanks so much for you post. Would you be able to post some information about where you found the Quercetin on the web (or pm me?)--I've tried looking this up, but must be doing something wrong because I haven't found anything yet . . . Thanks again! Best wishes, littlebear

I'm wondering if anyone else has experienced a change in their hair with the Cystoprotek? I hadn't at first, and then I stopped using it for a week, and then went back on. When I went back on, my hair fall-out increased by a ton! I know that Elmiron causes problems with hair loss in some people, but I hadn't heard of CP causing it. And maybe it's something else altogether that is causing the recent exodus from my scalp, but I was wondering if anyone else has noticed changes in their hair while on CystoProtek.

Hi Redfaced! I've been taking Cystoprotek since January & have not had any hair loss from it. HOWEVER, I do have Grave's Disease (overactive thyroid) and that has caused my hair to fall out. I don't think you're loosing hair because of Cystoprotek - If you're not taking some other medication that might cause hair loss, you may want to have some blood tests done & make sure you don't have something else going on. Good luck! I know how awful it is when you're experiencing hair loss.
signature: Sandi Wherever you go ..... go with all your heart

contents: Thanks! I suspect my hormones are out of whack. Nobody else in my family has lost hair. It's really depressing. I have half the hair I did 10 years ago. There seems to be no end to losing it!

contents: Hey there! I have been taking the Cystoprotek for awhile now and have had no problems with it. It works as a coating agent, and has anti-inflammatory properties to it as well. It's glucosamine and chondroitin in a very body-usuable form, in a base of olive oil to help it get to where it needs to be more effectively. The Cystoprotek has a couple different ingredients in it from the Algonot Plus, but both worked fine for me. Here's a link to a Q & A session on the site here, with the doc who developed it. It's very helpful and informative. They also have a website you can check out for more information on the differences between the products! Hope this helps: http://www.ic-network.com/guestlectu...rides1205.html Rose You really should check out this Sticky by Tigger_gal on the Stim, it's very informative and can maybe give you some insights into it. It's on the board "Nerve Stimulation: Considering It?" There is loads of information over there you should check out. My understanding is it is NOT for pain, and if a doc tells you it is you might want another doc . But read Tigger's sticky, it will help you out!!! http://www.ic-network.com/forum/showthread.php?t=

contents: I am not 100% pain free, but it is MUCH more bearable than it was in the beginning. I still flare if I eat something that my bladder doesn't like, and stress which I'm under a lot these days is a killer too, but it definitely helped bring my symptoms down to a level where I could sleep. I now sleep through the night without pain meds, although I wake up with a very painful bladder due to holding it all night I guess, but for me that's huge. I still have frequency, urgency, and hesitancy. Did you try Elavil yet? A lot of people get relief from pain with Elavil. My uro told me that they give it to young teens who still wet the bed because of it's direct effects on the bladder. It also can relieve pain. You can check out my post under antidepressants where some members have replied to my questions.

Thanks glass. I'm glad it is making things better for you! I can't take Elavil b/c it is a tryciclyc (sp?) antidepresant and that make my dry eye condition worse. I also tried Lyrica and gave me headaches too and eye pain. Not worthy. I only hope I can tolerate Cystoprotek. The people that can tolerate medicines are very lucky...

contents: I forgot to tell you to freeze the capsules. Just put the whole bottle in the freezer. The formula releases slower that way, and so do the chances of stomach upset etc...Follow the directions exactly how they tell you too as well. I had to start with 1 then 2 etc...Sometimes we are in a hurry (like I was) to get relief (no kidding).

I was having an awful flair due to stress from a new job. I was already taking Elmiron and Evail, and decided to try Cysta Q. I have been taking it for two weeks now and I feel lots better. I am almost pain free when not at work and the pain is much more bearable when it does strike. I hope that it keeps working cause I really like living a "normal" life. Good luck to any of you who try it. Erin

Okay, I've been taking Cystoprotek for about 2 weeks and have to say that it really does help a lot. But I also have noticed that if I wake up in the middle of the night, I do have a little bit of a headache. I don't normally get headaches, so wondered if maybe it was because of taking the Cystoprotek.

Right now I only take one in the morning and one in the afternoon. I've been taking since May I think but I had problems with headaches at first so I never went to 3 a day or 4. I've been stuck at one with breakfast and one with dinner. Now I'm afraid to take more. I'm afraid of stomach upset since I seem to have IBS symptoms as well. I also have one pill that part of the outer coating is chipped off. Should I toss it or take it anyway? I've been taking them frozen but what happens if I can't take them frozen (i'm away from home or whatever). I really think they help my IC but I'm so chicken when it comes to taking meds! Tracy

Try taking 3 a day instead of trying to take two at one time. I took 6 a day for the first 3 months and then went down to 4. It didn't seem to cause any problems for me. I understand you feeling chicken about trying it. I'm the same way with pills. Hope you do well with it.

I take one pill two times a day now. When should I take the third pill. Right now I take one with brakfast and one with dinner. Do you think taking more would mess up my GI system. I seem to be having strange GI symptoms off and on now anyway. Thanks! Tracy

Hi Tracey, I started off with the normal dosage 3 at breakfast and 3 at dinner. Now I take two at breakfast and two at dinner. I eat half of my meal (breakfast or supper), and then take the pills and continue eating. That way I have food in my stomach before and after the pills. If you want to try to increase the dose to see how you do. Take 1 at breakfast, 1 at lunch, and 1 at supper. I don't think there is anything to worry about with the pill you have left. It will dissolve in your gut anyway. Cystoprotek has helped me quite a bit.

This helped me a lot, but it can take a couple of months to see the benefits so you have to be patient (like Elmiron). It is definitely worth a try. You can buy it on this site in the ICN shop or you can order it online at algonot.com. I hope this helped.

Hi! I'm an estatic user of cystroprotect! Our ICN support leader suggested this over cytro-Q. Within days my pain went from a 7-8 (while on pain killers and IC diet) to a 1-3 each day! My pain started out off the scale for over a month! It took an extra month to get my strength back. Then I started slowly exercising and learning how slowly breathe to relax my pelvic floor muscles and bladder when it starts spasming.I do rest a lot during the day and I make sure I have quiet time morning and evening.I also follow the IC diet and eat bran cereal in the morning to stave off constipation,which makes me worse. Last week I stopped my pain killers and have been doing great! Pain is still 1-3 with occasional spikes,but no pain meds is a thrill. I ran out of cytroprotect over the weekend and started hurting again and it put me back to bed.But, I'm back on it and just had to share the sucess I'm having so far! My doctor said it can take the bladder up to 3 months to start healing and I've been on it for about 3 months,so I think it's healing my bladder and that's why I can drop the pain meds. I just hope this lasts,but I know God has a major hand in how I'm doing! I know this company sends samples of cystroprotect and a really cool information packet with a CD that is very informative for free!
Good for you! Glad to hear tht an IC sufferer is getting relief! It gives us all a little hope.

contents: Hi, Can anyone give some good feedback on these alternatives v. Elmiron? I've had IC for 16 years. I did EVERYTHING. Finally agreed to try elmiron and it seems to be helping (WOW!), but I'm shedding like a dog in spring - my hair was thin BEFORE I started. I tried prelief and had so much stomach upset, it just wasn't worth it. What about the products from algot? Are they comparable to elmiron? has anyone tried them? And, if I stop the elmiron, when will my hair stop falling out? THANKS!

http://www.remedyfind.com/newsletter_archives/Jan alternative start for IC in that thread there are also links to polls on alternatives. ok what Elmiron does is put a protective coating on the bladder...allowing it to heal. Quercetin I think is something that helps you build your gag layer back so it is slightly different on how it works but same concept. I think the other alternatives of this category do more of a similar thing of making their own protective layer. Treating the Gag layer of the bladder has different possibilities. Some alternatives are of things naturally found in the gag layer. Other alternatives in this category create their own layer that helps to coat the bladder and protect it. • Desert Harvest Aloe Vera • Licorice Root (Soothing and coating) • Quercetin (may reduce the effectiveness of some forms of estrogen) • Glucosamine Sulfate • Mullein (protecting) • Slippery Elm (coating, don’t take near Elmiron) Also besides alternatives Instills such as Heparin are bladder coats.

contents: Algonot Products, such as Algonot Plus and CystoProtek has helped many IC patients control their IC symptoms. These products may help to rebuild the lining of the bladder and reduce inflammation at a somewhat faster rate than Elmiron. Only 3% of Elmiron gets to your bladder; with CystoProtek 15% of the product gets to your bladder. The absorption rate allows for faster healing/protection of the lining of the bladder. Some healthcare providers prescribe both Elmiron and CystoProtek to help the patient achieve faster symptom control and then wean them off of one of the products. The following ingredients are in CystoProtek: Actually, Quercetin is a natural compound found in plants and seeds that helps reduce inflammation. Olive kernel oil is a powerful anti-oxidant that helps the body absorb chondroitin sulfate and may help guard against cancer. Glucosamine sulfate is present in the soft material between bones and joints and has mild anti-inflammatory abilities. Chondroitin forms the connective tissue that holds muscles, nerves, and blood vessels together. Chondroitin also inhibits the release of enzymes that trigger joint destruction. Sodium hyaluronate, something the GAG layer already produces, works in harmony with glucosamine and chondroitin to replenish that protective GAG layer and reduce the inflammation associated with its deterioration. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you. Only your healthcare provider can determine if the above suggestions are appropriate treatment options for you; these are just some suggestions that you can discuss with him/her. The contents in this post are provided for informational purposes only. The contents are not intended in any way to be a substitute for professional medical advice. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. Best wishes, ICB

contents: Is Cystoprotek okay to take if you haven't been diagnosed with IC and you're not sure you have it (no more symptoms) but you just want to be safe and promote a healthy bladder? Same for the Desert Harvest Aloe Vera gels? Any potential downside in taking these supplements if you're not an ICer? I'm just so worried and paranoid. Gah.

Kieren, Have you been taking cystoprotek and now are wondering if it's ok to quit it? If you have & you're now symptom free, I don't see why you couldn't continue taking it - maybe at a reduced dosage. If you've never taken it at all & you don't have any symptoms .... well, I'm not sure on that one. My understanding of cystoprotek is that it works to repair the bladder. What I would do is call Alganot & ask them. They're very helpful with any questions you have. They could tell you for sure if there's any downside to taking it, I'm sure.

contents: Hi Sandi - Thanks for the response! No, I haven't taken cystoprotek before. I would have to say that for the last few days, I've been almost symptom free. I've doubled/tripled my water in-take in the last week so my frequency has gone up. But at this point, my nerves are so shot that whenever I have to go to bathroom, I think, "AHHH, could this be IC?" I was hoping maybe Cystoprotek or Aloe Vera would be a good preventative measure and trying to keep my bladder healthy.

Maybe you should see the doctor for a diagnosis? How long have you had symptoms? Did you have a UTI? There shouldn't be any drawbacks to taking it, but it is best to ask a doctor and find out if it is IC, before taking any form of medication. Sometimes doctors diagnose you on the basis of symptoms only so you may no need the hydrodistention. Have you tried the IC diet? Are your flare-ups to due with certain food or drinks? Didn't think mine were for a year, but now can def. see the connection almost an hour afterwards I flare!! I would recommend seeing a specialist- I used to have frequency when in teens, but it would come and go after alcohol etc, then the frequency got a little much and then finally to the point where I went to see the doctor because I was having UTI symptoms. The sooner you seek medical advice, the better. You many not even have IC?

contents: Cystoprotek The ingredients in Cystoprotek is excellent for Arthritis and the Glucosamine and Chondrotin is used to rebuild cartillige in the knees. Weather you use it for the bladder or for arthritis, it will not harm you. Some skin doctors also recommend it for collagen build up in the skin. I took Cystoprotek for 6 months to help OB symptoms before I even saw a Uro for a diagnosis. I now take both Elmiron and Cystoprotek. Sammie

I have always taken umbrage when any alternative company suggests that their product can prevent the onset of IC. Fortunately, the Algonot company would never do this because we still don't understand why IC happens to some people. That said, the ingredients in that product are common in a variety of OTC supplements. The MOST IMPORTANT preventative IMHO is not injuring your bladder through diet. Thus, if you're serious about it, I'd say LOSE all coffees and sodas. That would have a far more beneficial effect at helping to prevent irritating in the bladder. JMHO. Jill

contents: Caffeine It is caffeine for me - shortly after I drink something with caffeine my bladder lets me know it. I drink soy milk and I have not noticed that it bothers me.

caffeine and soy sauce Coffee and other caffeinated drinks are diuretics and remove much needed water from our systems. Soy sauce is so salty that it does the same. Anything that is extremely salty like soy sauce or worchestishire can delete water from our systems. This is just my opinion only. I 've noticed that when it is hot my symptoms can flare more quickly than in cooler weather when I sweat much less.

I've been on Cystoprotek since Feb this year. I had a couple of small breaks from it because I didn't order it on time. I'm still flaring. I have been whatching my diet etc... Anyone have any idea when I should just stop this product? I'm still taking 3 tabs 2x'x daily. Any input would be appreciated. I do realize that it can take time for it to work.

I'm sorry it hasn't seemed to help you yet. I started taking it around the middle of Jan., and I started noticing some relief within a couple of weeks. I can't say I'm in remission, though, as I still get flares and some days are pretty good, and some are worse. I do think that overall, though it has helped, and I plan to keep taking it. I haven't started on Elmiron or Elavil or the typical drugs that others take for IC, as I want to give the alternative plus diet route a chance to work first, and my uro is fine with that. I'm also doing HRT right now with my gyno and seeing if that helps. Too soon to see a difference yet. Hope you get better soon. I know that others have said that with Elmiron it sometimes takes 6 months or even a year to notice a difference, so CystoProtek may take that long as well.

Glass, you're getting close. Are you doing a voiding diary at all?? Has your frequency levels changed??? Getting up less at night or during the day??? Also... are you being PERFECT at avoiding those top five forbidden foods.,.. cause even one cup of coffee a day is going to defeat any chance of cystoprotek or any therapy working well for you. jill

Hey Hi Glass, Are you relatively new to the IC diet? I know when I first started I wasn't avoiding as much stuff as I should have been. You gotta be careful and think about additives and preservatives in everything. Especially when you eat out. For example, in my early IC diet days I'd be like....oh "plain" grilled chicken sandwich I can eat that. But the problem is most restaurants and fast food joints marinate their chicken breasts in stuff that contains MSG or citric acid or other no no ingredients.....even if it tastes plain it might not be. So just be very careful with your diet. I take Cystoprotek too and I have found that it helps but it certainly doesn't make me totally pain free or never get flares. However, I think that it helps me enough to warrant my spending money on it. So, if you have seen some improvement, you might just wanna evaluate if you think its worth your money. Also, you might wanna add something else to the mix because most ICers find that they do best on multiple meds. If your trying to go the natural route...maybe some of those aloe vera capsules they sell in the ICN store? Kim

contents: Hi Holly, I had a problem too, but I order directly from them. When checking out from the order screen online I asked for two day shipping and it didn't arrive. I was so mad because I paid 27.99 for the faster shipping, and I still only got it in 5 days. I called Algonot and they got back to me, and sent it overnight. I ordered Thursday evening and did not get it until Tuesday morning. They said that it is better to call them directly if you need faster service. Online they do not receive the orders right away, and that the two day shipping applys only after they receive the order. (a little misleading to the consumer). Anyhow my advice: I thought I heard there was an autoship plan available on this site, or plan way ahead. I ordered the four bottle package this time so I wouldn't run out. Hope you feel better soon!! Glass - you hit the conundrum that all store owners deal with. Each postal carrier and postal offices have their own unique deadlines each day for express mail deliveries. For example, here in California, it's 11am. So, it's very rare that we can actually get anything out that day and, throw in a holiday, weekend or a location where express mail isn't available overnight, then we're all screwed. Your advice about having an extra bottle on hand is perfect. That way, noone will go out and there will be plenty of time to get orders out. - Jill Last edited by icnmgrjill; 06-17-2006, 07:43 AM.

Holly, Posting about an order in the message boards doesn't work because our order center manager doesn't read the message boards. It usually takes about a week for us to process the order and then however long USPS takes to deliver it. It's always best to call and place your order by phone because those are processed daily where as our web based orders are batched every two or three days. Please send an email to my sister Janice ([email protected]), who manages our mail order store. Her son has been very ill this week, including a brief hospitalization, so she wasn't able to work for several days. It's been a rough two weeks here at the ICN and because I care for my elderly parents, I couldn't go up and fill in at the store. Mark, my 21 year old (and very hunky) nephew, has a very contagious form of mono that caused his throat to swell nearly shut. He wasn't able to swallow for days and my sis was frantic. And, we both agreed that I couldn't risk bring that back to my parents. Glad to hear you're doing the Vistaril/hydroxyzine. It was so helpful for me personally that I took it for eight years and really credit it with stabilizing and then reducing my IC. But, it took just a bit to really kick in. So, don't give up too soon! The symptom that you're having of feeling like you're going to pee when you walk sounds like you're having a really bad pelvic floor muscle flare right now. That's really not typical of IC... but when those muscles are tight, then it's the movement of walking that makes them feel worse. Or, if you're leaking, it could be that the muscles are just a bit weak. If I were you, I'd ask for a pelvic floor assessment immediately. The good news about muscles is that they are treatable... but it takes some physical therapy to do it. Take a moment and read some of the guest lecture transcripts on our site about pelvic floor work.. particularly Kotarinos and Anderson. http://www.ic-network.com/guestlectures/ Also, if you're feeling desperate, as we all have on occasion, please make sure that you find someone that you trust to talk with. Whether it's a family member, friend, doctor or therapist, it's important that you not do this alone. You can also call a suicide hotline for help - 1-800-SUICIDE I try to listen to a lot of music and write in a journal when I'm feeling particularly down.... and it helps me to just get those worries out. Also, every day, I try to break the isolation that I've often felt. Call your friends and invite them over for a video!!! Order in food! Have a good ole fashioned girl gathering. Big hugs! Jill Last edited by icnmgrjill; 06-17-2006, 07:39 AM.

contents: Lori,how is the new med working for you. I just thought I would check in with you to see how you are doing.

I'm doing just great - I have a thread where I post most days to say how I'm doing, if you'd like to follow it... I feel actually better than I have in many many years (decades) energy-wise and just in general health. I have a small amount of bladder pain still at night, which is handled easily with Ultram. I am told that I should start to see results from this drug on my bladder in about one month after starting it. I told you that already, right? That it won't affect my bladder much until about 4 weeks into treatment? This is one of those drugs like Elmiron that take awhile to work. My doc said I would see some results in about 4 weeks and the best results in about 4 months. So anyway, I'm on day 11 right now, and already my general health is improving because of CyA. Apparently this autoimmune disease has sapped my strength and health for a long time - but no more! Now I just have to wait for the little tiny bit of bladder pain that is left, to go away. I figure that might happen in another 2-3 weeks or a few months at the most Blessings, Lori P.S. My bladder is definately better than usual...it's just not quite 100% yet....
I am so happy that you are doing better. You have been through so much.

contents: CystoProtek is recommend for 3 months for best results. I have spoken with patients that did not start seeing symptom improvement until the second or third month. Moreover, many patients need a multi-modal (more than one) treatment regimen. If you are not addressing the three major components of IC, you may not get as well as you can be with just CystoProtek alone. Discuss your treatment options with your healthcare providers; together you can decide what is the best plan of action for the immediate future. Best wishes, ICB Last edited by ICB; 07-06-2006, 09:48 AM.

contents: Originally posted by elsie3205 ICB, are you a spokesperson for Algonot? No. The company headquarters is in Sarasota, FL. I run an IC support group in Tampa, Florida. Several years ago, I asked their VP of Customer Support and Education to please send me information packages for the support group meetings. She came to the support group meeting and brought me the requested information. We spoke after the meeting and I asked if we could have Dr. Theoharides speak at one of my support group meetings when he came to Sarasota. The meeting was arranged for the following year and I decided to try the product myself. I've now been on the product for the past 4 years and it's really helped to control my symptoms. I was a National Patient Advocate for 5+ years and am currently an IC Education Advocate and Support Group Leader. I provide information to IC patients and healthcare providers; however, I am not a medical professional. I'm just a patient that has done a lot of reading, and have had the priviledge to learn from some of the best IC minds (clinicians, researchers) in the country. Both Algonot, LLC and Desert Harvest Aloe Vera refer patients to me for support and education. Both companies can answer many of your questions pertaining to their products, however they usually recommend the ICN and the ICA for comprehensive IC information. If the patient wants to speak to a support group leader - they usually give them my contact information. Best regards, ICB Last edited by ICB; 06-12-2006, 05:09 PM.

Originally posted by peaceplease I have a question for ICB, what are the 3 major components of IC you are referring to? I am doing diet and I assume that is one of them. Just curious. I will try to give the Cystoprotek some more of a chance I guess. The three major components of IC is a simplistic view that refers to the overall causes of our symptoms; they include: 1. A breakdown of the protective lining of the bladder. The lining (glycosaminoglycans, or GAG layer) is composed of a coating of mucus, which protects the bladder wall from bacteria and irritating substances in urine. 2. Mast cells - many IC patients have high levels of histamine release in the walls of their bladders. Histamine can stimulate the inflammatory process. Mast cells - originate from immature cells in the bone marrow. Once released into the circulation, they develop into one of three types of mature cells; connective tissue mast cells, mucosal mast cells in the gastrointestinal tract and bladder, and brain mast cells. Mast cells communicate information from the immune system to tissues surrounding the mast cell. When environmental allergens attach to immune cells, these cells produce and release an antibody (IgE) that binds to mast cells. Once activated by IgE, mast cells can selectively release a variety of chemicals to act on nearby cells. These chemicals also can attract other cells of the immune defense system. Actions of some of these chemicals also could explain some of the clinical features of IC, such as pain and inflammation. Mast cells also can be activated directly by the nervous system. . . Moreover, the effects of stress on mast cells also are not restricted to the bladder; similar results have been found in brain, intestine, and skin. (By: Tony Buffington) 3. When the bladder becomes inflamed, sensory awareness increases, and less distension is needed before it is registered as painful. This may be a defensive response first; the body is trying to eliminate any bacteria or objects that may be the cause of the inflammation. This is most likely how the initial pain of IC starts; an inflammatory process may set the stage. We believe that you can start with your bladder as the source of your pain. Pain is transmitted through your central nervous system on a chronic basis, and in the process, some of these nerves may undergo a change called “upregulation.” When these nerves become up-regulated, they can send out pain information all on their own. They don’t need the bladder anymore. There is also Pelvic Floor Dysfunction, Trigger Points and Tender Points that affect 70% of IC patients. I consider this a sub-component of IC. If left untreated, your IC symptoms could be totally under control; however, you may feel pain, frequency, burning, urgency, etc, due to PFD. You may want to check out the following website for more information www.roadtoslainte.blogspot.com. If you would like a PFD Fact Sheet, send your request to [email protected]. If the above IC symptoms/causes are treated early, the chances of significant symptom control are above 80%. The key is to look at the big picture, the multimodal approach, looking at all the things that are going wrong and then correcting them. Diet, Self-Help Techniques, Physical Therapy, Prescription Medications, and Dietary Supplements may be part of your treatment regimen. The majority of IC patients respond best to a multimodal (more than one) treatment regimen. Consult with your healthcare provider to find the appropriate treatment options/regimen for you. Best wishes, ICB Last edited by ICB; 07-06-2006, 09:39 AM.

contents: Tracey: The recommended dose for CystoProtek is 2 capsules, three times per day for a total of 6 capsules per day; for the first 4 months. After that, they recommend 2 capsules, twice a day for a total of 4 capsules a day. From your list of medications that you are taking, it seems that you are taking more than the recommended dose. Are you taking 8 capsules a day now? If so, have you checked with the company for their recommendations and most importantly with your healthcare providers? I don't know if taking a large amount of CystoProtek can cause problems; check with the company and your doctors to make sure. I may have completely misunderstood your post; but I wanted to make sure you're treatement regimen is safe for you. Best wishes, ICB

contents: Originally posted by elsie3205 I didn't freeze the first one I took last Thurs. It caused me to have SEVERE stomach pain and my stomach is still trying to recover almost a week later. What in the world is "olive seed oil"? That is what algonot claims is the reason for the stomach upset. I thought it was just plain ole olive oil and I use that in cooking all the time. I'm not sure if I will try one frozen or send them back. I'm very sorry that you had such a bad experience. The olive oil is a very concentrated olive oil that comes from the pit of the olive. The reason that they use this particular olive oil is because it actually helps the body to absorb chondroitin and quercetin; thus increasing the absorption rate into the bladder. I too would be shy about trying something that caused me such distress. It bothers my stomach, if I don't freeze the capsules. If I take the capsules frozen, I have no problems. However, everyone is different. I've spoken to several patients that have sent the product back to the company; the majority have not had a problem receiving a refund. However, make sure that you send in your request within 4 months of purchasing the product. Best wishes, ICB

What would we take for component number 3 bladder inflammation?

Originally posted by ICB The three major components of IC is a simplistic view that refers to the overall causes of our symptoms; they include: 1. A breakdown of the protective lining of the bladder. The lining (glycosaminoglycans, or GAG layer) is composed of a coating of mucus, which protects the bladder wall from bacteria and irritating substances in urine. 2. Mast cells - many IC patients have high levels of histamine release in the walls of their bladders. Histamine can stimulate the inflammatory process. Mast cells - originate from immature cells in the bone marrow. Once released into the circulation, they develop into one of three types of mature cells; connective tissue mast cells, mucosal mast cells in the gastrointestinal tract and bladder, and brain mast cells. Mast cells communicate information from the immune system to tissues surrounding the mast cell. When environmental allergens attach to immune cells, these cells produce and release an antibody (IgE) that binds to mast cells. Once activated by IgE, mast cells can selectively release a variety of chemicals to act on nearby cells. These chemicals also can attract other cells of the immune defense system. Actions of some of these chemicals also could explain some of the clinical features of IC, such as pain and inflammation. Mast cells also can be activated directly by the nervous system. . . Moreover, the effects of stress on mast cells also are not restricted to the bladder; similar results have been found in brain, intestine, and skin. (By: Tony Buffington) 3. When the bladder becomes inflamed, sensory awareness increases, and less distension is needed before it is registered as painful. This may be a defensive response first; the body is trying to eliminate any bacteria or objects that may be the cause of the inflammation. This is most likely how the initial pain of IC starts; an inflammatory process may set the stage. We believe that you can start with your bladder as the source of your pain. Pain is transmitted through your central nervous system on a chronic basis, and in the process, some of these nerves may undergo a change called “upregulation.” When these nerves become up-regulated, they can send out pain information all on their own. They don’t need the bladder anymore. There is also Pelvic Floor Dysfunction, Trigger Points and Tender Points that affect 70% of IC patients. I consider this a sub-component of IC. If left untreated, your IC symptoms could be totally under control; however, you may feel pain, frequency, burning, urgency, etc, due to PFD. You may want to check out the following website for more information www.roadtoslainte.blogspot.com. If you would like a PFD Fact Sheet, send your request to [email protected]. If the above IC symptoms/causes are treated early, the chances of significant symptom control are above 80%. The key is to look at the big picture, the multimodal approach, looking at all the things that are going wrong and then correcting them. Diet, Self-Help Techniques, Physical Therapy, Prescription Medications, and Dietary Supplements may be part of your treatment regimen. The majority of IC patients respond best to a multimodal (more than one) treatment regimen. Best wishes, ICB Are the chances of getting 80% symptom control only if treated early? I have had IC for 15 years. Does that mean that I will not be able to achieve 80% symptom control?

contents: Originally posted by HollyS So, one component would be to take something for the GAG layer such as elmiron or cystoprotek or both, the second component is an antihistamine for mast cells. Is that correct? What would we take for component number 3 bladder inflammation? In my simplistic view, the third component of IC is nerve damage. Neuropathic pain refers to pain, burning, frequency, and urgency generated by nerve damage. Although it is not known for sure, pain in IC, especially later in the disease, is thought to be neuropathic. IC experts think that after some time of getting pain, burning, and other signals from the bladder, nerves change, and they start firing off pain messages on their own, even if the bladder is not irritated. That may explain why pain can persist in some patients after the bladder is removed. Although I am not aware of any scientific data on the subject, the theory is that if you have a mast cell next to a sensory nerve, the sensory nerve releases the chemicals and activates the mast cells. Then the inflammation stimulates the sensory nerve again, and the whole process repeats itself; it becomes a repetitive circle. The most widely prescribed drugs for this "third component" are Elavil or other tricyclic antidepressants, Effexor, Neurontin, Lyrica, Cymbalta, and Topamax. Nevertheless, your healthcare provider may prescribe something else based on your health history. If you believe that any of the information in this email would be helpful for you, share it with your healthcare providers and ask it they believe it would be an appropriate treatment option for you. Never disregard your physician’s advice or delay in seeking it because of something you have read in this email. You are correct in the treatment of the first two components. Share the information with your healthcare provider; he/she will evaluate your condition and make appropriate recommendations for you. This email has been sent without being proof read to expedite delivery. Best regards, ICB

Originally posted by HollyS Are the chances of getting 80% symptom control only if treated early? I have had IC for 15 years. Does that mean that I will not be able to achieve 80% symptom control? No. However, in most cases, it is easier to achieve symptom control if your symptoms are treated early; it usually takes a lower dose and less treatment options to control IC symptoms. Many patients find successful treatment regimens after having IC for many years. Nevertheless, because we've had the disease for so long, it may take longer to find the right treatment regimen to control our symptoms; it may also take higher doses of prescription medications and additional modalities. The key to finding a successful treatment regimen is research, tenacity, and a team of great healthcare providers willing to work with you to find the appropriate options for you. Regardless of when treatment begins, numerous patients have achieved complete symptom control with medication and other modalities. If you believe that any of the information in this email would be helpful for you, share it with your healthcare providers and ask it they believe it would be an appropriate treatment option for you. Never disregard your physician’s advice or delay in seeking it because of something you have read in this email. This email has been sent without being proof read to expedite delivery. Best wishes, ICB

Here's the specs, according to the literature from Algonot: Algonot Plus: Chondroitin Sulfate (Urothelium GAG component, Mast Cell Inhibitor ) - 900 mg Glucosamine Sulfate (Urothelium GAG component Building Block) - 900 mg Quercetin Dihydrate (Anti-Inflammatory, Mast Cell Inhibitor) - 900 mg Olive Kernal Oil (Anti- Inflammatory, Anti-Oxident, Cytoprotective, Increases the absorption of ingredients) - 3300 mg Cystoprotek: Chondroitin Sulfate (Urothelium GAG component, Mast Cell Inhibitor ) - 900 mg Glucosamine Sulfate (Urothelium GAG component Building Block) - 840 mg Sodium Hyaluronate (Urothelium GAG component Building Block) - 60 mg Quercetin Dihydrate (Anti-Inflammatory, Mast Cell Inhibitor) - 780 mg Rutin Glycoside (Anti-Inflammatory) - 120 mg Olive Kernal Oil (Anti- Inflammatory, Anti-Oxident, Cytoprotective, Increases the absorption of ingredients) - 3300 mg Hope this helps, I got it right from their literature that they send with the product!! Hugs!

contents: Hi, I just ordered some cystoprotek for my horrible urinary urgency I have with my IC. I also had started Elmiron 3 months ago. I was wondering if the cystoprotek also helps with vulvodynia. Thanks.

Originally posted by HollyS Hi, I just ordered some cystoprotek for my horrible urinary urgency I have with my IC. I also had started Elmiron 3 months ago. I was wondering if the cystoprotek also helps with vulvodynia. Thanks. Hello: CystoProtek has helped my IC, Vulvodynia and Fibromyalgia. I have a few friends that only suffer from Vulvodynia, two of them are currently taking CystoProtek for their Vulvodynia and are doing wonderfully ~ they can't believe that it's helped them so much. One other vulvodynia friend tried it, unfortunately, it did not help her. I hope that you find relief with CystoProtek. But remember, that the best treatment options for IC is multi-modal treatment regimen. Remember to discuss all treatment options with your healthcare provider. Best wishes, ICB Last edited by ICB; 07-06-2006, 05:05 AM.

contents: I was able to get a sample by calling them a few months back, maybe get another operator, or if their contact us thing on their site works, didnt a while back for me, try that?

This sounds weird. Sending out a sample doesn't require a decision.... it's just a matter of availability. The problem is that the only samples available are for the old formulation of cystoprotek which is no longer available. Also, we rarely have samples on hand. We do have some now, I think. You can call Janice at the order center, leave a message if needed, and ask for a sample to be sent to you.... but, again, it depends upon if we have been given some by the company. Please leave your name and address and phone! We cannot accept returns for any opened supplements EXCEPT the algonot products. This is because the manufacturer has recently agreed to refund the cost of those bottles to us. But, if you've bought CystaQ, Aloe or any of the other products and opened it, it's yours. If you make the Algonot/Cystoprotek return within two weeks, we may give you a refund for a single opened bottle, as well as unopened bottles also included in that purchase (i.e. a 3 pack) provided that they are in good shape. If, for example, they have been kept in a hot environment, the capsules may have melted. Thus, we reserve the right to check the bottles to make sure that they are resellable before we issue a refund. We are, though, a small business and we can't afford to give a refund for three months of product like they do. We also need you to promptly respond to us if we have any questions. It's VERY FRUSTRATING when someone calls and says they have a problem with an order, but they then don't respond to any of my emails or phone calls. What's worse is when it's returned two months later... well after our return date... and they get made at me. I mean, sheesh!!! We've had, for example, people purchase books... then photocopy them to the point that the book spine is damaged from being flattened in the copier... and then ask for a refund for the damaged book. We've had people return chair cushions covered in cat or dog fur or, worse, smelling horribly of cigarette smoke. Hmmm... the worst are packages sent to England... only to have them returned on the slow boat (a 2 month journey) because they weren't picked up by the customer... only to discover that the postal reps in England NEVER told the customer that the package had arrived. This has happened MANY times in the past year. So... it's a daily process of adjustments... If you have any issues at all, I encourage you to contact me directly with your questions. Jill

signature: 30 year old Mother, Midwife, Birth Doula, Herbalist, and Massage Therapist with sudden onset of IC approx. 2/16/05 (after a PAP smear and routine gynecological exam). I tried a HUGE list of natural and alternative treatments (including Cystoprotek, Desert Harvest Aloe, Aloe gel, Glucosamine/Chondroitin, L-Argenine, Quercitin/Bromelain, Marshmallow Root...etc) for the first 9 months of having IC, without much relief, until I finally researched and started Elmiron. My experience: ~Previously took Elmiron 100mg three times a day (9/29/05-7/06) ~Experimented with dosage (1-2 Elmiron a day recapped into a plain gelcap) and did well with occasional mild flares. (7/06-9/09) ~Stopped all together at the 1 year mark (9/29/06) for 10 days. Still felt good, with low-level symptoms. ~Decided to go back on it (10/16/06) to see if another year on it will bring me into full (zero symptoms) remission. Currently taking 1-2 pills a day, recapped into a plain gelcap. ~ 6/08...Tried to go off Elmiron again, had huge flare at the 3 week mark, went back on 100mg once a day for maintenance. -I went off Elmiron for 2 months due to pregnancy and had a complete relapse of IC symptoms to pre-diagnosis levels of pain/frequency. I then went back on Elmiron at 10-12 weeks, but was unable to take it further due to severe morning sickness. The 2nd/3rd trimesters were MUCH better IC-wise, and postpartum is the best I have felt in years. I am no longer on Elmiron for now. -20 month postpartum remission from 4/17/09 to 12/10/10. -Out of remission after a pap smear on 12/10/10. Pap smears are obviously the main IC trigger for me. Currently trying to cope with loss of the remission. Also doing: ~Sugar-free/gluten free and strict IC diet (With an emphasis on organic, alkaline and whole foods) ~Prenatal DHA/Fish oil supplement ~Vitamin D supplement ~Culturelle probiotic

contents: Hi all, I had high hopes for Cystoprotek, as an additional treatment with Elmiron, Elavil, and Urised, which I have been taking for 7 months. My main problem is pain, but lately have been having a lot more urgency too! Yuck. Literally 20 minutes after taking one Cystoprotek, my bladder felt like I had instilled acid in there and I was in a whopper flare for 3 days after. Now, do I dare try just the Algonot? Or just glucosamine and chondroitin? Has anyone reacted to the Cystoprotek but been fine with either Algonot or a glucosamine/chondroitin supplement? Thanks for any input!
signature: IC dx June 2003 Pregnant with IC 2004-05....off all meds Elmiron 200 mg bid Elavil 25 mg nightly Ovcon 35 BCP IC Diet Tried but dc'ed: Neurontin, Lyrica, Claritin, Cystoprotek Dreaming of a grande non-fat mocha with extra shot!

contents: I did not do well with Cystoprotek but did fine with plain glucosamine and chondroitin. Good luck!
signature: 30 year old Mother, Midwife, Birth Doula, Herbalist, and Massage Therapist with sudden onset of IC approx. 2/16/05 (after a PAP smear and routine gynecological exam). I tried a HUGE list of natural and alternative treatments (including Cystoprotek, Desert Harvest Aloe, Aloe gel, Glucosamine/Chondroitin, L-Argenine, Quercitin/Bromelain, Marshmallow Root...etc) for the first 9 months of having IC, without much relief, until I finally researched and started Elmiron. My experience: ~Previously took Elmiron 100mg three times a day (9/29/05-7/06) ~Experimented with dosage (1-2 Elmiron a day recapped into a plain gelcap) and did well with occasional mild flares. (7/06-9/09) ~Stopped all together at the 1 year mark (9/29/06) for 10 days. Still felt good, with low-level symptoms. ~Decided to go back on it (10/16/06) to see if another year on it will bring me into full (zero symptoms) remission. Currently taking 1-2 pills a day, recapped into a plain gelcap. ~ 6/08...Tried to go off Elmiron again, had huge flare at the 3 week mark, went back on 100mg once a day for maintenance. -I went off Elmiron for 2 months due to pregnancy and had a complete relapse of IC symptoms to pre-diagnosis levels of pain/frequency. I then went back on Elmiron at 10-12 weeks, but was unable to take it further due to severe morning sickness. The 2nd/3rd trimesters were MUCH better IC-wise, and postpartum is the best I have felt in years. I am no longer on Elmiron for now. -20 month postpartum remission from 4/17/09 to 12/10/10. -Out of remission after a pap smear on 12/10/10. Pap smears are obviously the main IC trigger for me. Currently trying to cope with loss of the remission. Also doing: ~Sugar-free/gluten free and strict IC diet (With an emphasis on organic, alkaline and whole foods) ~Prenatal DHA/Fish oil supplement ~Vitamin D supplement ~Culturelle probiotic

contents: All I can take is N-acetyl glucosamine. Glucosamine sulfate killed me and so did chrondroitin. I couldn't take Algonot or Desert Harvest aloe vera so I was afraid to try the others.
signature: Dianne My bellydance "sisters" , our dogs, and me.

contents: I couldn't handle Cystoprotek or DH Aloe either. But I tried NAG and Glucosamine/Chondrotin sulfate and was fine. So I think that is was the quercitin and the aloe that got me!

I evidently did not do my research before I ordered the Cystoprotek. After doing a search on the internet, I found that the Sodium Hyaluronate may be causing my problems. That's it for me - I am really allergic to feathers. That may be your problem as well. I read the product information that came with the Cystoprotek and it did not say anything about the above. I ordered three bottles from the ICNetwork, so I hope they will let me return the two unopened bottles.
I think the hyaluronic acid (spelling?) is derived from chicken combs, if you can believe it. Maybe that's the problem. Diana.

Hello Everyone: I started taking Algonot Plus about 4 1/2 years ago; then switched to CystoProtek when the company came out with this new product. I've done fabulously on both products; except for stomach upset, which went away with freezing the capsules. I started getting migrane headaches about a year ago and from what my neurologist had to say, it seems very common with IC and fibromyalgia patients. I used to take Elavil and that helped with the IC pain, frequency and burning. However when the migranes got so bad, the neurologist decided to take me off of Elavil (I was so scared) and start me on a very low dose of Topamax. It was a GREAT decision, by a great doctor!!! The migranes went away, the IC symptoms are still under control and the best part is that I lost 30 pounds. :woohoo: But to get back to CystoProtek - those who cannot take a product with sodium hyaluronate, you can try their Algonot Plus formula (it really works well for many IC patients too). The Algonot Plus formula is everything CystoProtek is, minus the sodium hyaluronate. If any of you are allergic to fish, the Chondroitin in both Algonot Plus and CystoProtek comes from shark cartilage and may cause some of you problems. Overall, I agree with those of you who are going off of the product and waiting until you feel better to try it again to see what happens. I would call the company and report what you are experiencing; if it happens again return the product. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you. The contents in this post are provided for informational purposes only. The contents are not intended in any way to be a substitute for professional medical advice. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. Good luck to everyone. Sorry to hear that you are having problems with Cystotoprek. I started it about a week ago, and am doing fine with it so far. I guess the allergy thing has a lot to do with the adverse reactions.

Regarding Cystoprotek Hi everyone, I had some adverse reactions to Cystoprotek, mainly gastritis. I started it on 1/21 and stopped it on 1/24. I also started taking prozac 20mg qd on 1/21. I was also taking an herbal/homeopathy tincture called UTI-Clear from nativeremedies.com (which I've been using for over a year to prevent UTI). I started getting stomach pain in the evenings following start of Cystoprotek, and then the night of 1/23 I woke up with a slight burning sensation on my face and arms, so of course I stopped the supplements, but kept taking the prozac. On 1/25 I started to feel better and the burning sensation on my skin stopped, and my IC symptoms lessened as well. I e-mailed both ICNetwork and nativeremedies regarding this situation 1/26, but no one has yet to answer back. Until then I'm staying off the cystoprotek until I get some more answers. Anybody else with these symptoms? Last edited by __KATIE__; 01-27-2006, 08:36 AM. Reason: mis-spelling

contents: I tried Cystoprotek twice. The second night, I felt prickly all over my skin, without rash or hives. It wasn't really an itch, but a prickly sensation. It was rather disconcerting, so I stopped the Cystoprotek. I also have a history of allergy to fish, so it made me nervous. Diana.

Okay, I had the same reaction to the Cystoprotek. It was a tingling sensation in my arms, hands, face and neck. Not to mention the fact that I couldn't get to sleep the two nights that I took it. I sent an email to Algonot from their website and also put a call in. I received a call back on Sunday (the following day) and was told that no one had ever reported the symptoms that I was experiencing and was asked to send a list of the meds I am cuurently taking and the amount of Cystoprotek that I had taken. When I told the rep from Algonot the amount that I had taken (following the recommended dosage on the bottle) she said that there should of been an instruction sheet included with my order stating to start with a lower dosage. I then told her that I had ordered from the ICN Shop (that's another story). She told me to stop the new supplements (I had also ordered Aloe Vera) and to wait a couple of days and then start with only one of the supplements and only one pill a day and then increase it, but as soon as I took the Cystoprotek again I had the same reaction, so I have quit both until I here back from the rep. Evidently the Dr. who invented the supplement is in Greece and she was going to be talking to or corresponding with him and getting back to me. I haven't heard anything back yet, but was glad to see that I am not the only one having a reaction to the supplement. Please let me know if you here anything and I will do the same. I was not able to take Elmiron due to an allergic reaction so had alot of hope for the Cystoprotek. P.S. I am allergic to Sulfa but not shellfish.

reaction to cystoprotek I'm glad I'm not the only one in the boat about this one!! The weird thing is I'm not allergic to either shellfish or sulfa, the only thing I notice as far as any shellfish reactions go is when lobster juice touches dry skin it itches but no rash. Thank you BigFig and Diana for your input on your experiences, please write back about your outcome with your query to the rep! I guess I'll be asking my doctors lots of questions in the next few days. Last night was startling, I woke up and my nerves were on fire, and had a heated tingly prickley sensation all over. I felt ill to my stomach and my IBS started acting up, it took about a half hour for the sensation to stop. What's up with that? I received a call last night by my gastroenterologist telling me that the gall bladder u/s revealed "sludge" crystals. I had that u/s done 01/09/06! It was originally ordered because of nagging pain in my upper rt. quadrant and stomach on 12/23/05. Then four days later I had a colonoscopy due to finding blood, so the previous problem took the back burner and so did my bladder, I was having a flare up through this whole ordeal. I'm glad everybody here knows how IC flare ups hamper your ability to funtion, because none of my doctors understand anything about IC except for that it's painful and there may be a correlation between flare ups and endocrine release. I'm waiting to see a urogyn in Mt. Auburn Hosp., Cambridge, MA she is the only one my urogyn knows that treats IC patient, but she's been on maternity leave since 10/05 and doesn't return till 04/06. So I'm just hopeful while persevering through the flare ups. Sorry I just divuldged all my probs on here, after all this is about cystoprotek, but i find peace knowing everybody here understands what I'm going through with getting a doctor with knowledge how to treat IC. Here's part of the response from Jill Osborn : Thanks for your letter. Unfortunately, I don't have an answer for you about specific interactions and you really should email the company and ask Dr. Theoharides for his response as well. You can find their website at: http://www.algonot.com. However, I can offer that sometimes the olive kernal seed extract can be difficult to tolerate if it's warm... and thus it has been suggested that if it's kept chilled in the fridge that it can help. Also... the homeopathic tincture is suspicious to me.... and since IC isn't infection anyway... these are usually a waste of time and money IMHO. I'm really not sure about the side effects of prozac. Good to talk with your pharmacist about that.

signature: 30 year old Mother, Midwife, Birth Doula, Herbalist, and Massage Therapist with sudden onset of IC approx. 2/16/05 (after a PAP smear and routine gynecological exam). I tried a HUGE list of natural and alternative treatments (including Cystoprotek, Desert Harvest Aloe, Aloe gel, Glucosamine/Chondroitin, L-Argenine, Quercitin/Bromelain, Marshmallow Root...etc) for the first 9 months of having IC, without much relief, until I finally researched and started Elmiron. My experience: ~Previously took Elmiron 100mg three times a day (9/29/05-7/06) ~Experimented with dosage (1-2 Elmiron a day recapped into a plain gelcap) and did well with occasional mild flares. (7/06-9/09) ~Stopped all together at the 1 year mark (9/29/06) for 10 days. Still felt good, with low-level symptoms. ~Decided to go back on it (10/16/06) to see if another year on it will bring me into full (zero symptoms) remission. Currently taking 1-2 pills a day, recapped into a plain gelcap. ~ 6/08...Tried to go off Elmiron again, had huge flare at the 3 week mark, went back on 100mg once a day for maintenance. -I went off Elmiron for 2 months due to pregnancy and had a complete relapse of IC symptoms to pre-diagnosis levels of pain/frequency. I then went back on Elmiron at 10-12 weeks, but was unable to take it further due to severe morning sickness. The 2nd/3rd trimesters were MUCH better IC-wise, and postpartum is the best I have felt in years. I am no longer on Elmiron for now. -20 month postpartum remission from 4/17/09 to 12/10/10. -Out of remission after a pap smear on 12/10/10. Pap smears are obviously the main IC trigger for me. Currently trying to cope with loss of the remission. Also doing: ~Sugar-free/gluten free and strict IC diet (With an emphasis on organic, alkaline and whole foods) ~Prenatal DHA/Fish oil supplement ~Vitamin D supplement ~Culturelle probiotic

I have not heard of this side-effect associated with CystoProtek. I've spoken to many IC patients that take CystoProtek and the most common side-effect is "burping up" the taste of the pill; which usually goes away when you freeze the capsules. Have you started any other medication or dietary supplement at the same time? If not, call the company 1 800 254 6668 and ask if others have reported this side-effect. Make sure that you keep yourself hydrated; at least a sip of water every 5 minutes. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you.
