contents: Re: 1st Botox treatment -please help Jamie, Rereading your posts, I see that pt did not help you either. Hmm, I can only go by my 1 experience with Botox injections. It took nearly 3 weeks for me to really notice a decrease in pelvic pain..and the first week after the procedure was quite pain filled. We are all different, so perhaps your doctor expects your treatment to kick in sooner. By the way, the nerve block they gave me to do the procedure did not give me any relief from pain. I was so surprised that as soon as I woke up the pelvic pain was much worse...I didn’t expect that! Hang in there and keep your chin up, Jamie. You will find something that works for you..and it just may be these injections.
signature: Sending positive thoughts and prayers, Sue68

contents: Re: 1st Botox treatment -please help Hello everyone, I am just giving an update on my pelvic floor botox injections since there is not many recent threads I wanted to make sure I let everyone know how it is going. It has been 2 months since my injections. I have gotten no side effects from the injections. It doesn't make your body feel numb or anything, your sensations are still the same as before. For me, they did not make a difference on my pain. But don't let my update make you depressed because I have chronic hip pain that also has a great impact on my overall pain. So I started my 2nd around of pelvic floor PT about a month ago. We are not doing much internal and mostly external but that is because of all my hip issues. I can not have anyone touch my hamstring/hip because of the pain or lay on my stomach or sides so we are very limited to what we can do at PT. My PT thinks that the hip pain is coming from my core muscles and pelvic floor (I already had full r/o of hip pathologies). She said my muscles from my pubic bone to diaphragm are very tight and knotted up together and that is pulling on my back'hip, causing some of the back pain. At my appointments, she does mostly visceral mobilization and internal works on the obturator internus ( for the hamstring pain). Also my dr has even given me diazepam (valium) to use as a suppository every morning and orally once at night before bed(and we stopped the clonazepam). He said that will help with the pelvic floor spasms. Another great thing is that my PT and dr work closely with each other, which is different than what I did in the past and I think it makes a big difference for your PT to keep active communication with your Dr. So far this time around in PT I make sure to let my PT know everything and if I know something will cause me a lingering flare I let her know ahead of time (which in the past I would not do). So for the 1st time I am not leaving PT with flares! I also have lidocaine gel to put on vaginally and that helps a lot too. So far I actually got seconds of improvement in my pain, which is a lot for me since I have pain at around a 7-8 level for 5yrs nonstop with not one thing giving a dot of relief, until recently! (And I have had loads of PT/injections/ you name it). So I would say I am headed in the right direction!!!! to encourage you, I did 2 rounds of regular PT and now my 2nd round of Pelvic floor PT and who would think anything would be different by the 4th time? Well it is! Healing takes time and involves a lot of trial and error, sometimes you need to revisit some situations and start again, Get a provider that has a different mind set and views things in a different way. Most important is to keep communication with your Drs and PTs! If something makes you flare don't do it, find another way to approach the problem. You can only fail if you give up, so never give up! PS- so what I do now is message out my abdominal muscles every day and sometimes my sister does it for me because its hard to do by yourself. My PT told me to do that everyday. If you are seeing a PT you should ask them to start doing it more and/or teach you how to do it on your own. I find it to be a big source of my issues, as I believe whatever impacts your core, impacts the rest of your body. Also I do breathing exercises everyday, psychologists have told me that diaphragmatic breathing is the best one for people with pain you can look up different types. Your fascia is one continuous layer of tissue covering all the muscles in your body !!!! So if your find yourself having minor pains/sore spots in other areas of your body it may be from the fascia at one part pulling on another. Which is why I think it is so important to make a routine of all of these things and can take less than 10 mins out of your day and make big impacts over time! Jamie Last edited by Jamie123abc; 03-09-2018, 05:04 PM.

contents: Re: 1st Botox treatment -please help Sorry the botox did not help. I am sure you are on top of it all as usual. I was hoping this treatment course would give you some relief. Having IC and hip pain sucks....pardon my language.

I had my fourth set of Botox injections the day before yesterday, and I thought it would be a good time to check in. I haven't been around here much lately - my job and my life are crazy busy, and my IC has been overall well behaved. I've generally been getting five months between Botox injections, although this time because of scheduling limitations I only went four months. In between Botox injections, I've been keeping up with meds (primarily Elmiron) and daily instillations (Marcaine, heparin, gentamicin, and sodium bicarbonate). And with all that, I've mostly been feeling great. The last few weeks of a five-month period can bring some increased pain, and urgency is still an on-again, off-again problem, but compared with where I had been, it's all very good. I do have to cath regularly because of retention issues brought on by the Botox, but that's become so routine (and so pain-free) that I hardly think about it anymore. Apart from the instillations and catheters, most days I could just about forget that I have IC. So, while my bladder has been happy, my liver has been continuing to give me conniptions. I was supposed to have a cholangioscopy for my PSC back in August, but all they ended up doing was an ERCP (sheesh, all these acronyms!) and they weren't able to dilate my strictured right hepatic duct. I've fought with extreme fatigue and itching, and recently it's gotten much worse. I called my gastroenterologist earlier this week when I couldn't deal with the itching any longer, and he added naltrexone to the Rifampin and cholestyramine I already take. I just started it, so it's too early to tell if it's going to help. On top of that, my normal low-level depression has blossomed (yet again) into full-blown clinical depression. I've been seeing a psychologist since August, but I'm a little underwhelmed by him - I last saw him nearly a month ago, at which time he promised to call me back to make a follow-up appointment for the following week, and to refer me to another doctor for further evaluation. After a week and a half of not hearing from him, I called his office and they sent in the referral, but still no call back and no follow-up appointment. Yeah, THAT helps my depression. On the good side of things, work has been mostly steady, and often pretty busy with lots of fun projects. My kids are a continual source of joy. And to top it off, a friend of mine is going to nursing school, and lent me his hot air balloon for the next couple or three years, so I've got a good excuse to go out and fly a little more often than I had been in my own old, leaky balloon. There have to be a very limited number of friends in this world who, when someone says, "Can I borrow your hot air balloon," they say, "Sure!" I count myself fortunate that I have three of these friends, and one who's willing to make it a long-term loan. Dave

Re: Long term Botox update Glad to hear that the botox is still working. Have you ever been able to give up the instillations all together while you have the botox? Sorry to hear about your liver trouble. Having itchy skin all the time doesn't sound like fun at all. I bet there are many wonderful hot air balloon rides in the future though by the sounds of it. Glad to touch base again. I hope your health improves on the new medication. Many blessings!
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Re: Long term Botox update After my first and second Botox injections I stopped the instills completely for a while, and picked them back up as the Botox started wearing off. The third time I had Botox, I decided to keep up the instills - I'm already cathing anyway, so it's very easy to do an instill in the morning before I leave the house. I've found that they really level out the you'd and down of my bladder, even with the Botox. If I start every day with an instill, every day is a good one for my bladder. That's hard to beat. I've only been on the new med for two days, but I've slept better the past two nights than I've slept in years. Hopefully that keeps up - it's a nice side effect! I'm going out tomorrow to crew for a friend who's ballooning in the morning. If the weather is nice, there will be 75-100 balloons up in the morning. Reminds me why I like Albuquerque so much.

Re: Long term Botox update I am considering keeping up the instillations for a while as it really seems to help with my pain. However, the instillations cost 320 per month and I was really hoping to put that money on my loan.

contents: Re: Long term Botox update What are the ingredients in your instills? Mine aren't that much, and ironically the most expensive part is the sodium bicarbonate, which is just baking soda in sterile water. The marcaine, gentamicin, and heparin aren't all that costly. Do you do your own instills, or is it a doctor visit each time you need one? Having to choose between meds and bills sucks.
Re: Long term Botox update Mine treatments are 8cc of lidocaine, 4cc of sodium bicarbonate, and 2cc of heparin. The heparin costs the most 170 for 25ml. I do instillations 4 times a week at home.

You would think they would want to go the cheaper route and cover home treatment, but insurance companies can be strange. Donna

contents: Re: Long term Botox update Donna and Wizbe Donna the rules for this stuff are very different in Canada. You should check the caths mine are covered, I called about 6 months ago asking about them cutting out paying for blood sugar test strips. I was told they were no longer covering theses, so I asked about the caths and they said they would still be covered. The list of stuff the has become OTC grows all the time. I get B12 RXed for injections, you can buy this OTC after you have had it written as a script at least once. IF you have a script it is covered if not you pay, they will not pay for the needles to inject it with. It makes no sense where I live for a B 12 shot it costs $150.00 to our health insurance if I go get the shot at the clinic. If I give it to my self the B 12 is covered, but the sryinge and needle to get her visit about a dollar, they won't pay for that. When I checked into Urastat (sp), they would not cover any of it, the medication or caths to administer it. So yes it is available in Canada but I sure don't have the $500- $800 per month to pay for it. I am not too sure if the price it has been quite a while since I checked into this.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Long term Botox update Wow. I hadn't known just how different the rules are in Canada. That's a big expense to have to beat on your own. All my catheters and instill meds are covered at 100%. The only thing that they won't cover is the syringes and needles, but I've got it down to needing just one catheter-tipped syringe with Luer slip adapter and one needle to mix the whole cocktail, so my out of pocket is around a dollar a day. I'm very fortunate that my wife works for a hospital. If we didn't have insurance through her job, I'd have to change jobs because my employer offers no benefits of any kind. Actually, that's not true - just no official benefits. I have a series of appointments that started today, general anesthesia three days a week for at least a month, then one day a week for at least another month. My boss drove me to my appointment and had a coworker pick me up afterwards, since I can't drive at all on my procedure days, and he's offered to do that for the whole two months worth of treatment. That's the way he runs his company and takes care of his employees, but it's a tiny firm that can't afford things like paid vacation, sick leave, and health insurance.

X Maria I have never tried botox, but I learned self catheterization years ago. The first few times were a little touchy, but no problem after that. If you have concerns, I suggest you ask your doctor about learning how should you need to know --- much easier than having the trauma and expense of an emergency room visit. I think knowing how is a good idea for any individual with IC even if it's never needed. Hugs, Donna

contents: Re: Long term Botox update Originally posted by Mariamagnolia Hi there May I ask how much botox in the bladder I should ask for when its for pain? 50,100 or 200 units? Im really scared of having to self cath...is it hard? Have had IC for 3 year (only pain) and nothing has helped yet.. Thanks. X Maria It's very, very easy. Like Donna said, the first few times may be a little tricky, but I've been cathing multiple times a day for a couple of years now and I hardly think about it. I'm told that the hardest part for women is getting the catheter into the urethral opening (I'm male, so that part's no challenge at all). Catheter manufacturers make various mirrors that are specifically designed to help you see what you're doing to make it easier, and your doctor should be able to help you figure out what works well for you. It is somewhat uncomfortable at first, just because the urethra is rather sensitive. That goes away quickly, especially if you use slender catheters - ask your doctor for the smallest size available (often 8 French). I'm assuming from your screen name that you're female, so be sure to ask for female-length catheters, which are shorter and much easier to handle because they're not as long and floppy as male-length catheters. Also, be sure to ask for hydrophilic catheters, which you hydrate with water or saline (usually from a pouch contained in the catheter package) and are fully lubricated. They're much easier and more convenient than using a tube of lubricating jelly. Some of them even have the water already on the catheter, so you just take it out of the package and use it. I started out with red rubber catheters and lidocaine jelly, which was messy (although not hard) to insert. The lidocaine helped with numbing, but I ended up not needing it once I switched to hydrophilic. That said, if it' uncomfortable at first, you can ask for lidocaine to numb your urethra. Your doctor can prescribe it in a Urojet dispenser, which is specifically designed to squirt the jelly into the urethra. It burns slightly as it goes in, but if you give it a few minute it does a good job of numbing your urethra. Feel free to ask any questions you have. There are many people here who have learned to self-cath, so you'll find lots of tribal knowledge to address any of your concerns.

I'm getting my first botox in a little over a week and I'm scared, excited and nervous. I have had IC for over 20 years and have tried instillations, medications, hydro distention and now I'm trying botox. I'm on several medications still and hoping botox helps. Can anyone give me tips, advice or anything to help with before and after the procedure?
Re: Getting Botox I will be sure to write tips for you tomorrow. Thinking about you and hoping it is successful for you!
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Re: Getting Botox Thanks so much anything would be helpful. I know Dr.'s give out standard stuff for procedures but I think getting tips from people that have had it done is more helpful.

Re: Getting Botox 1. It can take up to two weeks before it starts to work. 2. Make sure you know how to self-catheterize just in case you have the side effect of retention. 3. Fatigue can be a side effect. 4. Bring a frozen bottle of water for after the procedure. I have found urethral pain to be more after botox procedures than hydrodistenstion. 5. If you are currently doing instillations ask your doctor whether you should continue with instillations or not. 6. It does feel a little funny when it first starts working - I describe it as a sort of tug of war between my bladder and the botox.

I would think you should go discuss this with your doctor.

I'm scheduled to have this done the end of March and would like any info or personal experience info I can get. Thanks!!

Re: Needing input on Botox Hi Jessro! From December 2015 to February 2016, there were three of us sharing our journeys with botox on this forum. All three of us had botox in the bladder wall, not in the pelvic floor. Each of us had a lot of success with botox at that time. We each had a reduction in pain symptoms, our frequency was greatly reduced, and so was the urgency. 2 of us out of the three did have retention. I had to catheterize 3 to 4 times a day for 2.5 months. The man who had it done catheterize up to 5 or 6 times a day for about the same time. The other lady did not have retention at all. We could all still pee on our own but we were not emptying it all out. All of us went on to have the procedure again. Both of them had success again, I did not. With that being said I am having botox done again in 12 sleeps at a higher dosage. With the dosage amount they are doing with me there is a real chance I may not be able to pee on my own for a couple of months. I am okay with that. When the botox starts to kick in 1- 2 weeks after the procedure, it can be more difficult to pee. But as time moves forward that wears off and you are able to pee more easily. I would have to say my first botox experience was amazing and it helped me to function somewhat normally - I even took a trip. Actually all three of us did at different times. If you search botox my experience wizbe in Google, you can read about my experience and you will see the other two commenting throughout. It helped me that much that this is why I want to try it again even though I had little success last time. Pain after the procedure - as the botox starts to settle in it will feel weird. Both times I have not enjoyed that feeling. After it settles it is fine. I found my urethra hurt more after each botox procedure than after the 6 hydrodistentions that I have had done. Bring a bottle of frozen water with you to your procedure. Hope that is helpful. Many blessings

contents: Hello all, I just had Botox today. I'm wondering how long most of you waited to have sex afterward. I was told 2 weeks. That seems a bit much for something so simple. I'm wondering if that restriction is a blanket restriction that applies to bladder procedures in general. What have you done/been told? Tara

Well today marks two months since I had my botox procedure and one month since my return to work. Thankfully the Botox has REALLY helped the frequency. It has definitely given me my life back in this regard. I do wish the pain was better. I feel like I live on hydromorphone and this week I have added pyridium (which causes retention for me), and diazepam because my pelvic floor is out of whack. Sitting is very difficult at times. I presently have to sit on two bladder friendly cushions to feel any sense of comfort when sitting. Hard chairs are my enemies. Thankfully I have a good pelvic floor therapist. Being back to work is a gift. There were times in January that I wondered whether I would be able to work again because all of it combined was never ending pain and frequency. It had come to the point where I had lost most of my passions. Going on leave for nearly 3 months helped greatly. It helped me to heal not only physically but mentally and spiritually. Taking the time off has cleared my head and refreshes me. Before going off on leave I was a mess in every way especially physically. I also came to the point where I began to feel that I couldn't live out my dreams due to this condition. Thankfully God really spoke into my heart during my time off. Now I have decided to not cancel my dreams because of this condition. I applied to a dream job that I always wanted. I was offered the job and I accepted. Before I would have declined. I know this may seem crazy that I would do this but I feel completely peaceful about my decision. They have made accommodations for me at my new work place. They are also paying for my move. I will also be way closer to my specialist and I will have more treatment options available. I feel it will be way better than where I am currently living - a fairly isolated place. I can't wait to see what is in store. Moving forward I plan to have this procedure done every 5 to 6 months. I will not be waiting any longer than this. I also realize the importance of rest. I do very little activities after my normal work hours. If I do I immediately see a difference in my health. I plan on continuing the VERY limited diet I am on that a naturopath has put me on. I have not seen much difference on this diet yet but it has only been 2.5 months. After 7.5 years on the IC diet I had food allergy testing done that showed allergies to some of the foods I was using to help 'ease' off flares. Ironic, right? I am hopeful that I will see a difference. I am also continuing to do the installations as per my doctor's orders. I do 4 a week. It does seem to help. On days that I do installations I do feel much better bladder wise. Overall, I am very thankful. I am thankful to have my IC much more manageable and stable. Although I need boundaries to help me keep this condition in check, I will not let this disease dictate my life. I will fight it. I can't wait for my new job to start in September and I can't wait to see how God blessed it. Many blessings and prayers to each of us.

contents: Re: Botox Today So I went to my family doctor. I was done. I had had it and when he said, 'I am not sure what I can do and you know if I had a magic pill I would definitely give it to you', I looked back at him and said, 'please dont make me cry.'. You see on Monday I emailed my urologist office stating what was happening since the procedure. I received a response immediately that told me to go to the hospital because he felt that I had blood clots that was blocking the urethra but wasn't coming out with my size 10 female catheters. He wanted them to give me a size 20 catheter and put it in a couple of times. When I got to the hospital they did not do what he asked. They said there was no blood in my urine and thus that was not possible. However, two hours before I went to the hospital there was tons of bright red blood and lots of it to the point I left work. So of course the hospital loaded me up with hydromorphone and Diazepam and sent me on my way. Tuesday I was a complete fog and still having to catheterize every second pee - which meant I used over 20 catheters on Tuesday and the same on Wednesday. By Thursday I was done and completely drained and unable to sleep and in significant pain and the list could go on. LOL. Three days after the procedure your bladder can NOT be paralyzed.... two weeks in the procedure should start to work - that will be on Tuesday. My doctor decided to have my urine sample cultured. It is a long weekend here so I might hear by Tuesday. He also gave me sleeping pills which is helpful - somewhat. And I am trying compounded belladonna morphine suppositories which seem to relax my bladder. So it is a bit better than the week days but I also tested all of Friday night, Saturday and i will rest today and Monday. Praying that things start to improve soon.

contents: Re: Botox Today I am so sorry this is happening to you. Maybe next time get your uro to call your local ER and tell them directly what he would like them to do. It is so hard when you have to deal with other docs that don't really know what is going on. I am not a doctor but maybe if you do have a large blood clot that is not able come out with a 10, maybe the clot was blocking your urethra so the blood could not come out, this could be why there was no blood when you went in, but was blood prior. When I am having problems I have a supplie of urine sample bottles at home, I collect the sample when I am having problems, just incase I have to go in about it. So many things can effect what the urine is like. Example how much water you are drinking, some meds. My bladder used to shed its lining lots, I would have big pieces of tissue in it, other times it would be small pieces or none at all, I would collect the sample when there was lots and then take it in. I also used to get lots of blood in my urine after running, by the time I would go in it would be gone. I find docs don't tend to believe what they can't see. I am sure you are very careful, but please be extra careful with catching so often, with the retention and frequency increases your chance of getting a UTI, you don't want that on top of things. I assume this is different than the last time you had Botox, can I ask what is different, or what was your expirence before and now, are you documenting your symptoms. I am glad you are able to rest over the long weekend, and that you have lots of medications to make it a bit easier. I am finished a month if antibiotic's, sinus infections feels a lot better, but now I ache head to toe so bad I can hardly move, but I also can't lie still, it hurts too much. I still have a temp and having been feeling a bit worse each day. I saw my doc last Wenesday, he did blood work and exrays, I go back to see him Tuesday. I hurt so bad I can not sleep, my bladder is starting to niggle at me which is not uncommon for me if I get sick with something else. I am so tired of being sick, this has been going on for months now. My husband is working all weekend so I can spend my time resting. We were going to go to our son's for thanks giving but not going now. This means I will be cooking turkey dinner tomorrow. I have made things that I can make a head of time already, so tomorrow I can rest on the couch and just get up and down to put things in the oven etc. I am going to cut up veggie's today and put them in cold water till tomorrow. This way I can pace what I have to do tomorrow and still get a fair amount of rest. I really hope after the weekend and resting you are feeling better.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today wizbe has the Botox helped? You mentioned in the past it has? How long did it take and how long did it help for?

Re: Botox Today Originally posted by ABliske wizbe has the Botox helped? You mentioned in the past it has? How long did it take and how long did it help for? The botox has helped the last time. By now I had already started to see significant changes. Right now, I am not seeing any change in frequency. At least today I can pee more without having to catheterize so much. Catheterizing is a pain as you have to make sure your hands are clean, you are clean, the catheter doesn't touch anything else and with all the times I am having to do it I fear for an infection. Last time it helped me for about 6 months then I started to see a huge change downhill.

contents: Re: Botox Today I think 6 months would be worth it. It if was only helping for a couple months it might be a different story because of all the hassle and pain and cost of the surgery. Of course, at times, like today I'd do almost anything for just 1 hour of relief. I have a few more questions if you don't mind. 1. How hard was it to learn to cath yourself? 2. Did you learn it on your own at home or in a doctor's office? (I got all the supplies once for home instills but was too scared to try it. Hurts my urethra.) 3. How long after getting the injections until you can get around a walk normally? I'm thinking how long until I could work. 4. Does insurance cover the cost of the injections? They used to not be FDA approved for the bladder wall. 5. How many injections did you get? 6. Was it the same # as the last time? Hope you feel much better soon!! So happy to hear you found something which works!!!!!!!!!

Re: Botox Today I learned to catheterize with a nurse who specializes in treating IC patients. It took maybe 10 minutes to learn. My doctor had tried to teach me but was a little clueless about how so I could never do it until I went to this nurse. It is so much easier to catheterize while sitting on a toilet than it is to do an instillation on my bed. After the procedure it takes two days before I feel good to walk around. I usually take three days off from work. This time I should have taken four days. I do not live in the States. My drug insurance covered the cost of the Botox completely with a special letter from my doctor stating it was for therapeutic purposes. The amount of injections vary. My doctor does it in a grid shape. The dose for IC is 100 units. Hope this helps you :-)

contents: Re: Botox Today Best wishes, Mothergoose as you head for surgery today. I hope that it goes well. I also hope that the antibiotics really work this time. Yesterday was a very painful day and the frequency stayed the same. I did go to my osteopath yesterday. We chatted about possible causes. He believes I should see an Immunologist. He thinks auto-immune is my problem. As he stretched my pelvic floor, he once again told me of all the scarring and adhesions. My bladder is attached places it shouldn't be and my cervix is attached to the vaginal wall in ways it shouldn't be. I can't help but wonder why it seems that urologists only treat the bladder and not everything else surrounding it - like the ligament from my belly button to bladder, this is where my main problem is. Seriously, if there was a surgery to have that ligament removed, I would do it.

Maybe b/c it has cut down on number of infections, my IC always bothers me when I get sick. It also really cut down on the number of UTI I get. There is some basic testing they can do before sending you to an immuno.

contents: Re: Botox Today MY UPDATE: October 22, 2016 Yesterday afternoon I went to my urologist for a follow-up. I was hopeful for this appointment as I thought he may be able to help me. Let's back up a bit. On Monday night I had three urinations where there was nothing but bright red blood in the toilet which later turned to pink after. I also was going back and forth to the washroom way more on Monday so I figured once I saw the blood, maybe I may have an infection. On Tuesday I went to my doctor, told him about my bright red pee and he decided to take an urine sample and have it cultured (makes sense!). Dip test showed a significant amount of blood and puss. He put me on Macrobid, two times a day. So naturally, I think I have an infection. Fast forward to Friday. Here is what takes place at the appointment: Urologist: How has your symptoms been since the botox procedure on the 27th? Me: 'it may have improved by 5 or 10 percent perhaps. But I have an infection now, so I am back to square one. I know I haven't had an infection this whole time as I have had my urine cultured 3 times now. One time at the hospital, and two times at my doctor's office. The first two times came back negative, I haven't seen this culture but I haven't heard from my doctor either so I am assuming it is positive. I;ve been on Macrobid since Tuesday morning.' Urologist: 'How far is your town from here?' Me: I have to take a plane to get here. It is 14 hours to drive. Urologist: So nothing is going to be convenient for you. I can't diagnose or decide what to do unless I see those reports or I have you come back next week once all the antibiotics are gone. Me: I can't afford to fly back - it's anywhere from 4 to 6 hundred return. Urologist: Well, I need to see the reports that were cultured. Can you get them to me now? Call you doctor and have them fax the reports. Me. Sure (Meanwhile, I'm thinking my doctor doesn't work on Fridays and his office is not open. So guess what because I know him outside of being a doctor, I call his cell phone - praise God that I knew it!). - - - - They send me out of the office and he makes me do a urodynamic test to see if the botox is causing retention and to see how long it takes me to empty. I nearly laugh - I know it has done that but I had catheterized because of the pain an hour before my appointment. So what do you think happens when the nurse then scans my bladder? Of course, I had emptied my bladder. Next I phone my family doctor. Me: Hi there. I am sorry to cross boundaries but if I do not get a report about the urine cultures on the 6 and 7th of October as well as the last one, I need to come back next week and I can't afford it. Was there an infection in the last culture? Doctor: No, it came back NEGATIVE! Me: That one, came back negative as well? Doctor: Yes. But I will send the reports right away. I'll go over to the office and send them. Me: Thanks! Doctor: Best of luck! All the best and I wish you well. - - - - - The reports are sent by my doctor and received by my urologist. I am called back into the exam room. Urologist: Both reports came back negative so unfortunately this is the interstitial cystitis. There are many studies that talk about how botox can work many times but there's little research to tell you why someone who responds to botox really well the first time, does not respond well the second time. Unfortunately, this is the way this condition is. One person may have success many years with one treatment and suddenly it not work after that.' (I'm thinking, I know! I've been down this road many times!) Me: so what's the plan? Urologist: Come back in two months and we will see how you are doing then. Me: What?? Urologist: Come back in two months. Me: I'm sorry but this is really affecting my work. I can't do my job proper. I am having to leave counseling sessions. I can't sit through meetings with parents, with colleagues, etc. I have cried at least five times in public this month because of this condition and I'm not a crier - especially not in public. Urologist: Do you participate in step aerobics? Me (Completely stunned): I can't even walk my dog at the moment and working out like that, puts me in a flare. Urologist: Well, there's some research that says if you get your heart rate up to 140 beats per minutes, for 40 minutes, 3 times a week either by running, biking or swimming, it can help. It helps to bring down your cortisol levels Me: (Completely stunned and not cluing in says) 'I can't even walk my dog at the moment but I like to walk. Does walking work? Urologist: No, walking isn't good enough. Me: Well, I know that this flares me. I can't even sit on a chair at the moment. Urologist: Well, it's a bit of a catch 22 but it can work. What kind of counselor are you? Me: I work with little kids (Then I get it - my brain kicks in! He's thinks I am crying in public because I'm stressed!). Urologist: Well, you might want to see a counselor so you can learn some coping skills. Me: I know coping skills BUT I may need to talk to a counselor about having to work like this for the next two months!. Urologist: Getting yourself de-stressed from the condition, will help the condition. Urologist gets up and leaves. Writes me prescriptions where everyone else is (nurses, staff, patients). I do not get a chance to ask anymore questions. _ - - - - - By the way, I am not in denial either - I am truly not stressed. I take care of myself. I have good self-care. I almost panicked the other day because I could not get to a washroom BUT I was able to take charge and take control. I practice coping techniques with kids all day long and I use these skills myself. Am I concerned about what work may think? Yes, I am. Would I like to talk to a counselor? Yes, mainly to vent about this moment. Will it help improve my symptoms? HA! If it were that easy, I should be the healthiest IC patient in the world! I practice all of these things on a daily basis. I am VERY self-aware! I am aware that this appointment made me feel very discouraged. On the inside I truly wanted to stay home last night but I knew that I needed to have some people around - so I went out with a couple of friends who know I will be back and forth to the washroom numerous times. I am also aware that I need to develop a plan of action. Part of me feels that my body needs to rest perhaps which may help with the frequency and hopefully the pain as well. I am considering taking a couple of weeks off from work. However, I have never had success getting the frequency down except for hydrodistentions (which no longer works), botox (which did not work this time!). Overactive bladder drugs do not work either. Who knows? What do you think? I am seeing my osteopath on Thursday. I look forward to that appointment and I am hopeful. I would love to have this to a point that it becomes manageable before Christmas. I keep feeling hopeful though I know I have tried just about everything. What's next? Currently I am taking Elmiron 3 times a day, 3600 mg of gabapentin, 600 mg of Cimetidine, hydromorphone as needed, pyridium as needed, Quetipine to sleep night time, belladonna morphine suppositories as needed. I do instillations - but can't do that right now because with the botox it causes severe retention - I am tempted to try again tomorrow. I use heating pad, ice. I am seeing a physiotherapist/osteopath for pelvic floor dysfunctiom.

[/I]

contents: Re: Botox Today "Do you participate in step aerobics?"- is he crazy?Thankfully,when I tell my urologist that I am unable to get off of the sofa without pain during a flare,she is empathetic.I am so sorry this happened to you-& at no small expense for traveling.Hoping that the next intervention-whatever it may be-is soothing and helpful.You have been very strong through all of this.

Re: Botox Today I am so sorry your appointment went like that, I thought maybe he would try more Botox. It is so disappointing to go all that way to be basically patted on your hand. Sounds like you were not really being heard. I really wonder where he got his info about the exercise, some people can go back to that hard of exercise after they have things under control. If he wants your cortisol levels down I can message you a supplement I take for that, and where I order it from. I know docs do it all the time but to put you off two months when your like this doesn't seem right to me. Have you tried Dexadrine that was a game changer for me, I took 10 Ml's in am and 10 most in the afternoon, it didn't take it later in the day because I wouldn't sleep. Dexadrine, short acting, not Adderal or other ones that are combos or one pill a day. I could tell when it wore off but I was happy to have inorovement for 2/3 of the day. In a couple of weeks can your GP call him to discuss what else you might try? I am so sorry I was so hopeful for you that the Botox would work. Sending you a hug

contents: Re: Botox Today I know it is very hard to deal with all this and the pain, but it us also very hard to keep being positive. I really believe positive thoughts and attitude go a long way in healing, and dealing with pain etc. I am sure it is very disapointing for the Botox not wrk the same this time round. Just a thought when I used Botox in my forehead for migraines my doc thought I should get it before the previous shot wore off, so he scheduled me for every three months for a treatment. You will over come this too, when I got to my lowest point, I agreed to try a cocktail of meds, pain meds, clonasapam, Dexadrine, and topamax, along with struck diet. It took time a couple if years, but I did get my bladder down to a dull roar, after I was stable for a year or more I started to wean off theses meds. The Dexadrine and topamax is not mainstream treatment for IC, but it worked for me, I am not saying they are the answer for everyone. About the time I started taking Dexadrine a girl here started it took, after a week or two she felt much better, she took it 6 days a week, took Sunday's off. I assume she continued to do well, she stopped coming to the site. Once you not in need you stop coming to the site. I try to hang around to repay those that helped me so much in my dark days. I try to do at least one random act of kindness a day, even if it us just holding a door open for someone. I believe giving our selves is therapeutic for us. I rally feel for you, keep us updated after you see your doc on Thursday
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today Ok . . . So . . . I went to my osteopath today. The appointment was all internal work. Parts of it I told him i couldn't handle so he stopped. Parts of his work helped me to sit well after but the frequency stayed the same at about every 15 to 20 minutes. It has truly been one of those days - week actually. I then went to my doctor's appointment. He emphasized with me about my horrendous specialist appointment. He has me off work for the next 10 or more days. We are debating putting a catheter in for a few days to give me a break. I need to phone Pharmasave tomorrow and figure that out. I have booked two counselling sessions for next week. I am concerned about my work as I am finding it very difficult to work like this. Next week I will just be resting and I will not be working from home.

contents: Re: Botox Today Hello Everyone! So here's the deal! I have been resting ALL week. I have been able to get the pain down a couple of points but I still can't get the frequency down. I am doing instillations every second day and that's helping for pain but not for frequency. I have started trying to do some bladder re-training but because my pain is still too high I am finding that to be a problem. If I could get the pain down a bit more, I may be able to do it. I have been playing flute in the morning as a distraction for pain and frequency. I have been able to stay consistently at 30 minutes when playing flute. My brain is able to focus solely on the music. It is helpful as a form of distraction. I did have a counseling session on Monday to try to learn some pain management techniques. I have ordered a book called, 'Dissolving Pain' - it should be here tomorrow. If I could use some technique to help me be able to work through the pain in order to stay in a counseling session I may be able to work successfully. I am hopeful. I have looked into short term disability and I think I could still pay for all of my bills on it. Right now I am trying some things and if they don't work, short term disability is what it will be.

contents: Re: Botox Today I went back to work on Monday. It has been a painful experience from sitting for my job as a counselor. My frequency is still up rather high as well. However, I am aiming to be positive! On Monday and Tuesday, I felt very discouraged. The 'chatter' that was going on in my head was crazy. I thought things such as, 'I am going to quit my job!', 'I am inadequate because I can't do my job proper!', and 'I can see why people end it.' DON'T WORRY . . . these were momentary feelings, that I was very aware needed to be in check. On Tuesday night, I journalled lots and got a hold of some of those feelings. I was able to turn them around to have a more positive mindset. On Wednesday I was still in lots of pain and the frequency was still high, but now I took time if I needed it. Lunch time I went in my office, turned off the lights, lid over a chair, and put on contemporary Christian music. I spent most of my lunch reflecting. Today, I am going home for lunch. I will also spend that lunch time relaxing. This morning I also did a special awareness brain training exercise before coming to school. What am I doing? 1) Pelvic Floor Therapy (Internal Work) - I do exercises at home too. 2) Bladder Instillations 3) Psychotherapy (I am also reading a book on brain-training exercises for chronic pain). I need to be able to work! 4) I decided to order URACYST as I tried this when I was first diagnosed. My specialist at the time did not think it was working successfully, but I definitely thought it worked. Therefore, I am going to try it again, along with my other treatment protocol. That's a wrap for now. Recess is over. Back to work! Wizbe
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Re: Botox Today You go girl I really believe positive attitude can go a long way. You are doing what you need to do to coop and to try to improve your situation. You can do this, shirt week this week with the holiday tomorrow. I find writing stuff down dies help me put it in preservative. Sorry about all the typos in above post, I had just gotten out of the ER from flu/dehydration, my eyes suddenly got very blurry and I was seeing double, so I had to go to ER, even after getting IV fluids I still could not see right, it is better today, but still not right. Last edited by Mothergoose; 11-11-2016, 04:46 AM.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today Proud of you!

Re: Botox Today Here's another update! First of all I had an epiphany last night that I was less than a month away from seeing my specialist which MEANS less time of feeling this way (hopefully). I almost did a happy dance when I realized this! It's the little things that make me excited. After getting over the disappointment of my urologist saying that there was nothing else he could do for me and taking 9 days off in a row to regroup, I was able to have a more positive mindset. It has taken a couple of weeks to turn that around. There has been improvement My pain levels have decreased. My osteopath/physiotherapist was able to pen point the electrical current in my pelvis. Internally, he has been able to push and stretch that pelvic floor muscle which has helped some of my pain when sitting. The electrical current pain literally went away within minutes of him pushing and stretching this muscle. I am very happy about that! He also said that my pudendal nerve is inflamed and irritated. We will continue to work to bring the pelvic floor pain down. Bladder pain is still present. Decreased, but definitely present. Frequency - let's not talk about the frequency. I cannot get the frequency under control at all! On instillation days the frequency is better but not by much. I do a lidocaine cocktail Sunday, Monday and Wednesday. Friday, I am doing Uracyst. Now Uracyst is very expensive but I am not getting the same relief from uracyst as I get from the lidocaine cocktail. Tomorrow morning I am going to Uracyst, Lidocaine, and Heparin together. Therapy - I am going to a counselor. It has been helpful to talk about some of my feelings about this condition. It is relieving to get some of it out in the open and off my chest. It has been very insightful. We have been starting to pen point when I first started feeling stress in my pelvis. I pen pointed it back as far as when I was 14. My mom always wondered why I sat somewhat one-sided at the piano - looking back, I think it was because I was already having feelings of a tight pelvis. I have also started doing Open Focus brain training before I go to work in the mornings. It seems to open up my head space. IC DIET - Not sure where I am at with this. I may need to do an elimination diet again. I think I may have new trigger foods. Saturday I will start a week of chicken breast, rice, and carrots and go from there. Urologist appointment on the 21st of December. I am hoping he may try the botox again and see if it works this time. I think it will - I'm hopeful.

Urologist appointment on the 21st of December. I am hoping he may try the botox again and see if it works this time. I think it will - I'm hopeful. Finally saw my specialist today!!! It has been a long time coming. He took time with me today and was friendly, unlike last time. After reviewing my symptoms and discovering that despite the fact that my pain level has improved to some extent, my other symptoms have not. I have been working hard to help this - to make it improve but I am stuck now and cannot get it any better. It has been nightmarish some days though I remain positive and power through. My urologist explained that out of all my years with this condition the only thing that was like hitting a 'homerun' was the botox in January 2016. So although the botox of September 2016 was unsuccessful, he knows it was extremely successful the time before. Therefore, he would like to do the procedure again but this time with 200units of botox instead of 100units. I may not be able to pee on my own after but that would still be better than being in the washroom anywhere from 36 to 44 times a day, plus 6 to 15 times at night. I hope it works this time. Now to wait for a date!

Re: Botox Today Hi wizbe When I read your posts, it makes me feel extremely thankful that my IC is relatively mild in comparison to many here. I was reading your medication list and feeling so desperately sad for you as I only need to take Elmiron and it removes my symptoms completely. I have only had one flare in 3 years since being on Elmiron and only because I reduced the dose. I hope you get some relief. I also hope that you can live a fulfilling life despite your substantial issues with IC. Every day is a new day and perhaps you will reach the stage where your life feels normal again.
signature: Elmiron 2 x 100mg daily for IC Relpax 40mg as needed for migraine Vitamin D - 1 tablet per day Vitamin B - 1 tablet per day Iron/Vitamin C Tab - As needed, usually during my period. Previous Medications: Topamax - 2 x 50mg daily (migraine)

I also hope that you can live a fulfilling life despite your substantial issues with IC. Every day is a new day and perhaps you will reach the stage where your life feels normal again. Thank you for your response. It is nice to know that people care and have hearts of compassion! Thank you :-) Yes, it has been a difficult road. I hope to get to a form of normalcy or a 'new normal' after having the procedure done again. I am praying that it will be successful! Despite the pain, urgency, and frequency . . . I try to 'live' anyway. It looks different but I do it. I was happy with myself this week when at my specialist appointment in Vancouver. I did go shopping on Robson Street. I was concerned about washrooms the whole time but I went shopping anyway. I used my Medical Alert call to get into washrooms when I needed it. It was a little stressful at times but I did it! I have learned how to be assertive lately when it comes to using the washroom. So important! It has really helped me to get out even when I'm not well. Hopefully in a few weeks, whenever the botox is, I will function better! That's my hope and prayer! Thank you everyone! I will keep you posted how the procedure goes. Many blessings :-)

contents: Re: Botox Today I have been reading on 200units of Botox being injected into the bladder. I am debating whether I should do it. Quite honestly I don't think I have any other option but to do it. I have IC that is refractory to other treatments. My only concern about 200 units is that it could cause me to not be able to urinate on my own. I don't mind self catheterization but I am concerned that my bladder may paralyze and the pain stay the same. I guess then I would need a permanent catheter put in place so I wouldn't have to catheterize multiple times a day. This last Botox procedure that did not work for about a week I was in excruciating pain and having to catheterize every second pee which meant I was using a catheter 20 plus times a day. It was a nightmare.

If you look up the procedure on ichelp.org you will find more info on the procedure and it does mention hydrodistention as part of the procedure but I am certain not all doctors do it that way.

contents: Re: Botox Today Originally posted by Mothergoose Just my opinion, more Botox might be what you need. Your bladder is a much larger area than my forehead, I used to get 100 units in my forehead for migraines. I agree with your concerns though. Yes, I agree. However, some research studies have showed that 100 units is just as effective as 200 units. This is why the recommended dose for IC and overactive bladder is 100 units. Now where I am struggling so much he feels 200 units means my bladder will paralyze more - the more the better right now, I guess.

contents: Re: Botox Today That makes sense to me more of the bladder would be paralyzed. Maybe that is why the last treatment didn't work, is you needed more of your bladder paralyzed to reduce pain etc. So much of IC treatment has not been research enough to know for sure what is right or wrong. I also think each person case is individual and needs to be treated that way. So for you maybe 200 is what is needed to get good enough coverage. I sure at this point it us all just a guess. You mention you would have to self cath, did you have to after previous treatments? If do for his long? Your ability to void comes back as the botoxs wears off, right? I guess it us a trade off, if you get relief but gave to self cath all the time. I understand your concern. I opted not to have Botox fir a few reason, mostly b/c the uro wanting to do it had never done it, she had only read about it. She was also sure with my lack of bladder function I would have to self cath all the time. I am sure I don't know what us right or wrong for you. It sounds like you are doing you research to make informed decisions. I just really wish for you that you could find something that helps you. It almost seems cruel for you to get relief from the Botox, know what that's like, then to have that taken away from you. Happy New a Year, my prayers are with you that you find something to help you. I know you path theses last months has not been an easy one. Hugs MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today Thanks Mothergoose :-) I always appreciate your input :-) When I had my first round of botox in January 2016 I was able to pee on my own. With this being said, six weeks in I started getting pretty severe pain. That's when I discovered that I had retention. The doctor had me use the washroom and when I came back to the exam room, they scanned my bladder (which hurt like heck), and it was discovered that after urinating I still had 336ml of urine - so I was having retention. At that point I was instructed to catheterize 4 times a day for two to three months. I ended up catheterizing 4 times a days for about 2.5 months. Sometimes after that I was still need to catheterize when I felt the retention was greater. The second round of botox in September 2016, I had a horrible time - my bladder felt like it was paralyzing, yet I was having vicious pain (unlike the pain from last time), and I ended up having to catheterize every second pee for about 10 days. After I got over that hurdle, I catheterized only in the morning and night for a couple of weeks. Now I only catheterize when I know I am having retention - like today! Two totally different experiences. If this next round (Round 3) fully paralyzes my bladder I will be one happy lady. I don't mind having to catheterize 6 times a day, I am just concerned that the pain won't go away which makes me want to urinate and then it will be difficult. I really believe it is going to work this time! I am feeling positive about it but still doing some research just in case - I want a complete picture to make the best informed decision!

contents: Re: Botox Today Now on another note . . . I ended up in EMERGENCY today which is my list favourite place to go. The truth is I was having pain that I had only experienced a handful of times at this extreme level before, which happened in my biggest flare in November and December 2015. I was really concerned about what was happening and phoned a friend who is a registered nurse. She said to go to emergency right away so I had someone pick me up, as I couldn't drive, to take me to emerge. Let me tell you about the pain! On a scale of 1 to 10 - when urinating it was a 10, when moving it was a 10, lying in a fetal position it was a 6 - 7. As we all know from reading this thread I have been struggling but today has been the worst! This morning was not that bad but at 1:30pm this afternoon I got up off my couch to go pee. When I stood up I held my sweater closer to my body to feel its warmth and in the process I brushed over my belly button. Ummm . . . the next minute I had the worse stabbing pain from my belly button to my urethra. It brought me to my knees in pain. I crawled to my bathroom to pee where I screamed in pain. Then I got off the toilet and stayed curled up on the floor. Anytime I moved it felt like something was ripping inside of me and that the ligament between my belly button and bladder was being ripped off. It also felt like someone had taken sand paper, placed on a needle and started grinding it inside of my belly button and made its ways to my urethra. The pain was horrible. After 45 minutes I phoned my registered nurse friend. Then as she arranged to get someone to take me to the hospital I cried in pain on the phone to another friend as I crawled around my house. Sometimes I would try to pee and only drops would come out, yet I felt like I needed to go. Barely being able to walk to the car - bent over in excruciating pain, I asked my friend to grab me bottles of water as we were going out the door. I took my regular medication plus a hydromorphone after I got in my friend's car. I then started chugging back the water hoping to dilute, dilute and dilute. Has anyone experienced this type of pain? I always have navel pain. I sometimes have shooting pain from my belly button to urethra. However, this was extreme pain, bring you to your knees pain. My bladder pain sometimes does that too but NOT like this! This was horrible! At the Hospital The nurse seemed clueless about interstitial cystitis. Then asked me if Tylenol helped! I almost laughed in her face. Then I told that I felt I had retention but was afraid to self-catheterize because I felt something was inside of my urethra and that I needed a bigger catheter to do that if that was what it was. She looked at me with ten heads and then said, 'You know how to catheterize?' Then she asked what antibiotics I was on. I nearly had a fit! After an hour nobody came to catheterize me. I had done a pee sample and could only get 10ml out - that was it. Then my pain kept building. I peed tiny amount throughout the hour in excruciating pain. Then I had enough of it (couldn't handle the pressure of my bladder) and went to catheterize myself which was relieving for the bladder pressure but not the excruciating pain so much. Then the doctor came in. He asked if I was on antibiotics for the cystitis. I once again almost laughed and then said, I have severe interstitial cystitis. Antibiotics do not do anything for me except for when I have an infection. Then he asked me what medications I was taking. I then told him I do bladder instillations at home. At which point he asked how I instill the liquid into the bladder. 'How do you instill the medication?' In my head I was saying, 'Are you serious?' Then he says to me, 'Your case is too complex for me and it really needs a specialist. I really do not know what to do for you. This may sound rude but it is not intended to sound rude, what would you like me to do for you?' 'Well, I would like for you to check for an infection. Second, could this be some sort of stone trying to pass. Third, is this just the interstitial cystitis at a new level.' He said it wasn't an infection - nothing showed up. I asked for it to be cultured. Then he said that it could not be a stone because it was in the wrong location. Then he said it was likely the flare for the interstitial cystitis which I'm not totally sure that he even knew what it was.' He did not examine me. Did not ask anything about the pain - how it felt, where it was located, what had helped or wasn't helping. Then he suggested that I take a preventative antibiotic because I catheterize for instillations and because I was having to catheterize more often today sent me home with COTRIMOXAZOLE. I'm still not sure why. I don't want to take the antibiotic because (a) there was no sign of infection, (b) antibiotics sometimes makes me flare, and (c) I don't want to take antibiotics needlessly. Now, if it gets worse, and I cannot get it under control in the next day, I will consider taking antibiotics - but I will take one that will not cause me to flare. Honestly when I left the room with the medication in hand, I couldn't even ask questions or express my concern but I was still in agony and using the washroom every 10 minutes at that point because I had been diluting with water. What helped to bring down the pain: alternating between heat and ice over my bladder, navel and urethra; diluting with water; 4 mg of hydromorphone every 3.5 hours, curled up in fetal position on my couch, and I took some pyridium just then. So it has been quite the afternoon and day! I hope tomorrow I can walk less like an old lady and more like a 34 year old like I am but honestly when I move I feel that tug feeling that may cause me to go back on my knees. Yikes! I know that this too shall pass.

contents: Re: Botox Today So the procedure is officially booked for February 28th. That's 54 days away. I am pretty sure I am going to end up in the loonie bin before this is over!!! Now I think I will be going on short term disability. I am officially done!

contents: Re: Botox Today You are trying so hard to be positive,take good care of yourself and get the best medical care....but your body seems to be demanding more! Most people would have gone on short term disability earlier-you have really suffered. Hoping that you feel better before Feb 28th! It will at least be a relief to not have to fit in your urgent needs around work. Wishing you healing and sending you hugs! Shorty

Re: Botox Today Wizbe, My heart goes out to you, I feel so bad that this is happening to you at only 34 years old. I hope everything will go well for you and I hope you that you will recover from this ASAP. That Emergency Room Dr. sounds so ignorant, he should definitely make it his business to educate himself on Interstitial Cystitis. Wishing you all the best for a speedy recovery.

contents: Re: Botox Today Wizbe I am so sore this is happening to you. I agree about giving a seminar to docs, we have 3 docs 2 know me know all about Ic and how to help me. The other is clueless. In the future I suggest you ask for antispasmodic IV med, IV fluids, IV pain med to get your pain under control, and to be cathed right away, even with one that they leave on while you are there. I have had that ripping pain from time to time, for me it goes away after a day or so but can return in a day or week or months later, I Never have figure out what it is cause from. What are you booked for in couple of months ? More Botox? I do think it is time to take the time off, you have been a trooper and worked through lots, I would think you need the rest. It sounds like it is taking all you have to deal with this. I will have new phone numbers in the next couple of days, I will pm you my number, so if you want next time you can call me. I really hope today gets better for you. I will say a prayer for you. This too shall past.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today Do you live alone? Do you have someone that come and help you. I spend lots of time on my own, it is not nice when you are suffering that bad.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Botox Today Hi Mothergoose, You are correct when you say that it's not a good feeling to be alone with this pain. When my bladder pain started I was soo SCARED and I was afraid of being alone. I reached out to my husband since we had been separated and I asked him to come see me and I just hugged him and cried hysterically and he embraced me and told me that I would be okay. We are now living together again and I could not imagine living alone with my 2 kids with this bladder agony. This pain is so scary that I couldn't face it alone any more. I literally have videos that I made of myself crying in pain because it used to hurt soo much. I am still very uncomfortable and have a lot of bloating, cramps like menstruation, feeling like my urethra is burning and that I have urine lingering in my bladder,and mild consistent bladder pain. But I know that at least I have a shoulder to cry on. God bless all of us and may we heal from this pain as soon as possible.

contents: Re: Botox Today Originally posted by wizbe So the procedure is officially booked for February 28th. That's 54 days away. I am pretty sure I am going to end up in the loonie bin before this is over!!! Now I think I will be going on short term disability. I am officially done! Actually I think short term disability could be the best route for you to take --- that way you can have some time to just concentrate on getting better --- and once you improve, you could look at going back to working. I also suggest letting your doctor know that if there's an opening, you could change your date to an earlier one. I hope this works for you. Donna

contents: Re: Botox Today Originally posted by ICNDonna Actually I think short term disability could be the best route for you to take --- that way you can have some time to just concentrate on getting better --- and once you improve, you could look at going back to working. I also suggest letting your doctor know that if there's an opening, you could change your date to an earlier one. I hope this works for you. Donna Thanks Donna for your response. Yesterday, I stayed off work yet again, and phoned about short term disability. I have an application package coming my way. My plan is to go see my doctor this coming week and discuss this with him. I feel like right now my primary concentration is trying to get through work when I'm there which in turn is not helping me physically. So not working would give me the time to just rest and allow myself to heal. I figure if I stay off work from now until the beginning of April (if the procedure works), I should be all set, hopefully. Although, it takes about 3 to 4 weeks for the paperwork to be processed for short term disability with my work, I think I have enough money to get me through the first month. They will back pay to the day I lose all my sick days (which will be Monday). So I don't have to be approved to start it. I also asked if this is a once in a lifetime benefit with my company. I was told NO that I could have this claim for the next two months and another claim in two months after that. So I was very happy to hear that! As for my procedure, I have asked that if there is an opening for them to call me. There is only one difficulty about that - I live in a rather isolated location so the cost of last minute flights can sometimes be outrageous. My family doctor has actually written a letter to my urologist asking for there to be consideration given for the procedure to be moved up closer.

contents: Re: Botox Today Yes they gave anti spazmotic meds that are IV they work very fast, also when in that kind of pain it us not unreasonable to expect pain meds, to get the pain back under control. With pain the more out of control it gets that more meds you need to get it manageable again. I went into PG ER in total retention, I was in tears and could not stand up right, the check in nurse asked when was the last time I voided I told her and she said then you can't be in that much trouble yet, well that depends on how much you have drank and how much you already had retained. It was not nice. I am glad you are going to try Botox again, are you going you get double the amount. So another trip to Van? You could ask your uro that treats you IC to write up you out what the ER should do incase of future flared. I am glad you have a good support system we all need that.

contents: Re: Botox Today Week One of being on short term disability has ended. I am very thankful for this time to rest. I think what I have noticed most since being off is that I am exhausted. From months of just pushing through I am purely exhausted. This week I rested and slept when I could. I also went for a massage and did another round of acupuncture. I haven't really spotted a difference yet. The massage was for relaxation primarily. The acupuncture was to address the bladder. I have also spent time trying to figure out my subtype. I wonder if the Hunner's lesions have come back. I know until I had them fulgurated off last time I was in lots of pain. Always worth the investigation.

contents: Re: Botox Today February has arrived and I am elated. This is botox procedure month. I am over the moon. 27 sleeps!! This week has been a bit rough. I am always on top of renewing and refilling my prescriptions especially my instillations. Two weeks before I run out I place my order only to discover my prescription had expired. The pharmacy sent the request to my doctor. It took him a whole 9 days to sign the prescription. Needless to say, I ended up waiting for 5 days without an instillation. Ugh! Thankfully today they arrived in the mail and now with it instilled, I can do a happy dance. So relieving. Tomorrow is my day for appointments. I start with a massage, onward to a Naturopath appointment, and then an appointment with my doctor. I pray that it all goes as planned and that the naturopath can give me some ideas for a detox. I have just ordered a couple of bottles of cystoprotek. I am continuing with acupuncture and osteopathy/pelvic floor physiotherapy. My pelvic floor is out of whack. I cannot sit for more than a couple minutes without the spasms happening. I do feel less exhausted this week. Thankfully! However, sleeping is still not going too well. Monday night I slept for 1.5 hours, last night I slept for 3 hours. Tonight I will probably be exhausted and likely I will sleep more. I am positive. Be blessed everyone. I will update again soon.

contents: Re: Botox Today A quicker update than I thought: As I posted two days ago, I had three appointments today. One was a very relaxing massage - so great! NATUROPATH I want to share about the Naturopath appointment. It cost me close to 630 dollars by the time I left there but I figure that's a small amount considering in 2015 I spent $11,000 in medication and travel to specialist appointments for IC. I also have benefits that cover $1000 of naturopath. First of all, I was thankful that she took the time to understand the complete picture. She wanted to know exactly when these symptoms started to try to pinpoint root causes, etc. I was very thorough in my answers. She was attentive and listened with empathy. It was amazing to be given 90 minutes to go through everything thoroughly. (I wish my specialist didn't that!) I told her I was there because conventional medicine seems to be failing except for the gabapentin and instillations - and let's be honest, they're no miracle but they are helpful for pain. She talked about food allergy/sensitivity testing. That's another reason I went to a Naturopath. When she mentioned this, I immediately agreed to doing the testing. I will have the results of this in two weeks. She also indicated that we should only try one herb or supplement at a time. When she said this, I felt like she did know about IC and flares. Then I knew I could trust her. Our steps of action: food allergy testing, I started on an intensive probiotic (30 capsules for $60), once the results come back from food testing she will help me with a detox, and then she listed five recommendations that we will try one at a time - though I am researching each one before I proceed. These are a) cornsilk - I am ify about this one since it is a diuretic, b) N-AG N-acetylglucosamine, c) Berben's formula - I cannot find any information about this, d) Perineural injection therapy - I am most interested in this. It would be injected close to the pudendal nerve which would help the pelvic floor dysfunction (I am all over this as I can't sleep tonight due to intense spasms like I am wearing a belt of pain). Overall, it gave me a sense of hope that maybe there are other ways of getting this under control. I have a follow up in two weeks. MEDICAL DOCTOR APPOINTMENT I am also thankful for a doctor who is helpful and listens. He was in with me for 45 minutes. First of all last week I requested a copy of all of my specialist reports. What I found interesting was the fact I had Hunner's lesions resected and fulgurated three times. I was only aware of once. It also shows throughout the reports that my bladder keeps looking worse instead of better. I still wish my second urologist was still working because I had the most success with him but I do have faith in my current urologist. Secondly, I asked my doctor for diazepam. He seemed very reluctant to give it to me though it has worked with the pelvic floor spasms in the past. He did agree after I explained my reasoning. Thirdly, I asked about birth control. I am unsure if I want to try this or not. I notice my symptoms get worse from ovulation right until my period. Thus I wondered if birth control would help. I also have very painful periods so I thought maybe this could kill two birds with one stone. However, I want to be cautious about this because when I was on birth control before when I wasn't diagnosed but experiencing symptoms, I went off bc because I thought it may be aggravating whatever was going on so I am very cautious about this. I am thinking this one through. All in all with each of these things we may not be able to move forward with any of this until after the botox is completed and I see how that takes. If my bladder paralyzes with the botox and it helps actually blocks the pain signals I will not know how things are hindering me or helping me so then I will hold off on some of the above. One step at a time but I have a new found hope in carrying on in this journey. Blessings!

contents: Re: Botox Today I am pleased that you have found a direction to go in. When my son was very sick with digestive issues I took him to a natural path in PG. She did the allergy testing on him, I felt it was well worth it, he had so food that he could eat, but it turned out I was making him worse by feeding him foods he was allergic too everyday. We had tried three gastro's, a homeopath, and some other doctors. The natural oath could not diagnose him but she helped with his symptoms more than anyone else. I am glad your doc is will to work with you on this. I hope it all goes well

contents: Re: Botox Today First of all, 10 sleeps until I fly to Vancouver for my procedure. 12 sleeps until the botox procedure. I am excited! I am waiting with hope and expectancy. So I went back to the Naturopath for my food allergy testing results today. I have major allergies to the following: casein, whey, cottage cheese, cow milk, goats milk, egg whites and a moderate allergy to egg yolk and various cheese. The test also indicated that I have a moderate level of Candida in my body - this is probably why my IBS is amped up lately. There are other things as well that I don't think I would have ever caught without this testing. So diary will be completely eliminated from my diet. I am also off all refined sugars as well in four months I can add natural sugars back into my diet such as honey or maple syrup. I will not be adding honey as it makes me flare. For the next four months only I cannot have anything with yeast but that can be added. I have to completely eliminate eggs and egg products. Make no wonder I have started to see a difference in the last two weeks. I have ate very little diary as I have been doing an elimination diet. I basically eat chicken and veggies for lunch and supper. I also eat Royal Gala apples and blueberries. I did try popcorn again (I popped myself) and I am pretty sure it causes burning. I have also discovered that crimini mushrooms are a problem for the IBS. I need to figure out what I can eat for breakfast. I bought some oats today and stevie (not sure about this) and I will try this for breakfasts. I have coconut milk bought too. I will eat just chicken/fish and veggies for the rest of my life if I have to. Red meat seems to make me feel sluggish so I try to stay away. That's it for now. Many blessings Wizbe
