[/I]

contents: Re: Starting Botox I am in Dallas right now, having flown to Orlando on Friday morning and driven my new truck to Pensacola to spend the night, then driven all day yesterday to get here. I will drive back to Albuquerque tomorrow. My bladder has mostly behaved itself. I am very short on sleep and have been drinking lots of caffeinated stuff including coffee and sodas, and that has let to some bladder pain. But the fact that I can drink any of that says a lot. Other than a little pain, I've had no trouble. The flight here was six hours including the stop in Denver, and I only had to use the restroom once the whole time. I can't say enough good thing about the Botox. I love how I'm feeling.

Re: Starting Botox Another update: I continue to feel great. The pain I had on the trip was minor and settled in a couple of days once I quit drinking all the wrong stuff, but I've been able to carefully add some coffee back in without a problem. I'm limiting it to one cup a day, but I'm doing fine with it so far. I'm not using a low-acid coffee or anything, just plain ol' joe. If I start to flare it'll be the first thing to go, but for now it's nice to have some of my old morning routine back. My big news, though, is that my retention volume is slowly decreasing. I'd been at 225-250 mL for quite a while, but the past week it's been 125-175 mL most of the time. I took the time to measure it because it felt like I've been getting more out when I've urinated before putting in the catheter, and it turns out I was right. I don't know yet if it's going to plateau here for a while like it did at 225-250 mL, or if it'll just gradually drop until I can quit having to cath, but it helps me to realize that there will come a time that I don't have to cath at all. I just hope that that point doesn't come at the same time as the good effects of the Botox wearing off. I couldn't be happier with where my bladder is, and barring something completely unforeseen, I'll be pushing my urologist to give me another round of Botox when this one does wear off. The few days of pain and the urinary retention I've experienced are so, so much better than the pain, urgency, and frequency. The rest of my health continues to be so-so. I am continuing to have abdominal pain, both in my bile duct area and my stomach area, and I've had a lot of trouble with fatigue and muscle/joint/skin pain. Some days I take my temperature over and over because it feels like I'm running a fever, but my temperature is always normal. I've got a rheumatologist appointment in April to evaluate fibromyalgia, but I don't know if that's what's going on at all. It's frustrating to have this get bad right when my bladder is finally feeling so good, but whatever is causing this is just another obstacle that I'll overcome one way or another. I'm blessed to have a supportive wife and an understanding employer.
Re: Starting Botox Are you saying it took your bladder pain away? I will do anything to take this bladder pain away. It is destroying my life. Please let me know. I'm desperate.

Re: Starting Botox Hi all, I thought it was time I checked in. I am almost at the three month mark since my Botox injections. Everything was great until about 10 days ago. I'm having a lot of lower pelvic pain and my pelvic floor has definitely reared its ugly head. I don't think any of it is my bladder but can't be sure. Pain from the pelvic floor has many of the same symptoms. All my usual self help tricks have not worked. Will call the doctor tomorrow and see if I can get in for an instillation. I was very fortunate that I did not have any retention. I was so worried I would. I'm thrilled that you two are doing well. Isn't it a blessing to live a somewhat normal life. Itapilot, I'm sorry to hear you are still having digestive problems. I hope the doctors will be able to find the causes of your symptoms. Be well and carry on.

Re: Starting Botox Botox didn't completely eliminate my pain, but it came close. I'm starting to have a little more pain off and on, buy still much better than before the Botox. I've been having pretty bad urgency the past couple of weeks, but without frequency and without much pain. It seems like I don't get any signal to empty my bladder until all of a sudden I have to go RIGHT NOW. Then it's a mad dash to the bathroom. My retention is usually worst right after these episodes - I'll still have 150-250 ml in my bladder, versus 50-150 ml other times. I'm hopeful that I'll be done cathing soon - if I can get past the urgency and resulting retention, I think I'll be able to stop having to cath. I'm worried that the urgency and the slight pain increase in having mean it's time for another round of Botox. I don't want to go to my doctor and ask for more Botox when I'm still having to cath myself, since I think she'll say no. In truth, I'd cath six times a day for the rest of my life if it keeps the pain, frequency, urgency, and accidents in check. We shall see what happens.
Re: Starting Botox I am having similar with the urgency. When I have to go I have to go. It is quite sudden and no build up

Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Re: Starting Botox I'm scheduled for more Botox on June 22. The past three weeks my urgency has gotten worse and the frequency is picking up, along with increasing pain and the constant feeling of pressure in my bladder. Sort of back to where I was before the Botox I had in December, except that my frequency isn't nearly as bad and my bladder capacity is still much, much better. I can sometimes go as long as four hours between times I pee (one to two is more typical, but especially in the afternoon I do better), and when I do go it's often 350-500 ml, and occasionally as much as 650 ml. I'm not looking forward to the procedure, although I expect it'll be better than last time. The last time I was the guinea pig patient for my urologist, and the cystoscopy took a really long time as a result. Now she's done a few of these (another patient is even scheduled on the same day as I am) so I expect the cysto to be quicker, along with the fact that my urethra is very used to having things inserted there so it won't be quite as traumatic on it. Last time I was basically incapacitated for two days and didn't start to feel the improvement for four or five days, so hopefully it'll be better. I'm expecting to have to go back to cathing six times a day for a while after the procedure. I've never completely quit, but the past month or so I've been using a catheter two or three times most days unless I feel like I have some significant retention going on. Not having to carry so many catheters with me is nice, but to be honest the cathing doesn't bother me - I've gotten so used to it that I hardly even think about it any more, except when I have to get my car keys out of my pocket and they get buried under three rolled up catheters. I'd cath six times a day for the rest of my life if it keeps me doing as well as I was doing through the winter and early spring. Nearly six months into my Botox experience, I can say with assurance that it was completely worth it for me. I'd do it again in a heartbeat, even if I knew up front the various problems I'd end up facing.

Re: Starting Botox Itapilot, 6 months seems to be the breaking point. I had my Botox in Feb. and my doctor said that I can schedule it again the end of July. I think if possible I will try to push it off another month. Right now I'm having some pain and more frequency but can control it with meds. I will be thinking about you on the 22 and will be anxious to hear it all went well.

Re: Starting Botox Thanks, signgal. I was surprised when I reread this thread that I've been having the urgency problems at least since late April. I probably should have seen my doctor a little sooner. Then again, it's been noticeably worse the last couple of weeks, with the urgency happening much more often than it has before, and the pain is just getting bad again, so maybe it wasn't time yet. My IC usually flares around this time of year, though. I don't know if it's the hot weather or what, but for the last four years I've started having increased pain and urgency.

Re: Starting Botox It's been wonderful to read about Botox from you all. Itapilot, thank you so much for your updates. I recently had Botox (31st May) after many years of finding excuses as to why Botox would make me worse. I have the most experienced urogynologist, who has been treating many of his patients with Botox. Not just for IC patients, but MS and wheelchair bound patients too. Anyway, he convinced me and I'm so glad to report I've had success. I know it's early days, but I no longer leak. I cried when I realised that. I don't have the urgency, but I do still have spasms in the bladder. But I can live with that!! Just before I walked into theatre, he asked me what I was afraid of. I told him infection as I usually get one after cystoscopy or urodynamic tests. He assured me I would not have an infection as he injects antibiotics during the procedure. And I didn't. The other worry was retention and as I've never experienced it before so I'm very thankful it has not been the case so far. I think if anyone is considering Botox, make sure it's with a specialist who has plenty of experience. I started walking 3 days after the Botox injections. I haven't done any exercise for years as the pressure on my bladder would make it impossible. I pray everyone else has a result similar. Will definitely go back and have it done again once it's worn off. This was my last option

Re: Starting Botox Have any of you tried Elavil? I started it last night, I hear many have good results. My bladder distention in Jan, only lasted for 4 months, what a relief it was! If this doesn't work maybe I'll try botox! Like so many of you, I'm miserable, praying for a break thru.
signature: Have had symptoms since March 2010 Have tried: Ditropan, Detrol, Aloe Vera, Uribel, Myrbetriq, Vesicare, others I can't remember Diagnosed Jan 2016, with cysto/hydro,(no hunners) worked great for 4 months, another hydro in July I just started drinking Aloe again and taking hemp oil which is getting great reviews!! 25mg hydroxyzine before bed, 25(was on 50) mg Elavil before bed, now on 400mg Elmiron, since about Sept. valium suppositories when needed Ambien before bed for a CURE,

contents: Re: Starting Botox Elavil helps me a lot, but leaves me to groggy to function. Ditto hydroxyzine. I had good success with hydrodistention, but kept reacting poorly to the anesthetic. Three out of the last four times I've had anesthesia for an outpatient procedure, I've ended up hospitalized overnight with nausea and vomiting. I think my urologic is leery of putting me through that again. The Botox has worked significantly better than the hydrodistention anyway, and it's just an office procedure. My urologist didn't have much experience (or any experience) with Botox, but she's such a good doctor that I was willing to be the test case. It's worked out well for me. Can't wait for the next round so I can quit having all the urgency, pressure, pain, and accidents!

Re: Starting Botox Had my second round of Botox yesterday afternoon. The procedure wasn't as unpleasant the second time around - it took less time, and the doctor was more confident in what she did, so I spent less time with the cystoscope inserted. My urethra is still pretty sore from all the maneuvering of the scope, but not as bad as last time. The injections themselves were a bit uncomfortable. I'm not sure the lidocaine they instilled in my bladder before the procedure had gotten everywhere. I should have rolled on my stomach for part of the time I was holding the lidocaine, but I didn't think about it. Yesterday evening wasn't much fun. My bladder was sore, my urethra was sore, and I had a lot of leakage. I did an instill with Marcaine and sodium bicarbonate, and that helped quite a bit. I also squirted lidocaine gel into my urethra several times, which would help for a bit. I ended up taking a Vicodin and a Xanax and sleeping through a lot of the evening. My wife is amazingly supportive, and made sure I had everything I wanted right there at hand so I didn't even have to get up. Today is better. I'm not on any major painkillers, just some Tylenol. My urethra is still sore, but not acutely painful. There's some retention, but that's something I expected since I had that last time around, and it's not particularly bad. Mostly I just feel a little beat up, and still slightly groggy from the meds yesterday. Last time it was a few days before I really felt good again, but given how I feel now I think I'll be back to my normal self sooner. I've already begged off a meeting tonight, but I may be able to make it after all. Time will tell whether I get the same wonderful response to the Botox this time as I did before. I'm keeping my fingers crossed, and I'll keep you all posted.

Re: Starting Botox Itapilot, so glad to hear your second round of Botox was a little easier. I hope you have as good or better results as the first round. We have a road trip planned the first part of August( I know, road trip! Ouch) so I scheduled my second Botox treatment for July 20th. As you may recall, my biggest fear with the Botox treatments was that I would have to self-cath. Fortunately that didn't happen. But once again it is a concern for me and since we would be leaving town and driving across the desert with few bathrooms, I was worried that if I did need to self-cath and/or had retention I would be in trouble. So now I am going to wait until we return. Hope I'm not sorry.

Re: Starting Botox One option that might help if you did need to self-cath without a bathroom nearby is an all-in-one kit. LoFric Hydro-Kit is one, but there are many others from various manufacturers. These have the catheter, often a self-lubricated hydrophilic type with its own sterile water packet, along with a collection bag already attached to the catheter. You never have to touch the catheter, and the urine stays contained in a bag that you can just discard when you're done. I've actually cathed a handful of times by the side of the road, and it's easier than you might think, even without one of the all-in-one kits - cover up with a blanket, slide my pants down far enough to cath, slip the catheter in and drain into a cup or bottle, slide the catheter out and pull up my pants when I'm done. I'm a man, so finding the urethral opening is easier for me, but I bet it wouldn't be all that hard for a woman either. I am not having much retention at this point, but it's probably too early for the Botox to have really taken effect yet. Still, I'm guardedly hopeful that I won't have to cath as long this time.

Re: Starting Botox Originally posted by ltapilot Had my second round of Botox yesterday afternoon. The procedure wasn't as unpleasant the second time around - it took less time, and the doctor was more confident in what she did, so I spent less time with the cystoscope inserted. My urethra is still pretty sore from all the maneuvering of the scope, but not as bad as last time. The injections themselves were a bit uncomfortable. I'm not sure the lidocaine they instilled in my bladder before the procedure had gotten everywhere. I should have rolled on my stomach for part of the time I was holding the lidocaine, but I didn't think about it. Yesterday evening wasn't much fun. My bladder was sore, my urethra was sore, and I had a lot of leakage. I did an instill with Marcaine and sodium bicarbonate, and that helped quite a bit. My urethra is still sore, but not acutely painful. There's some retention, but that's something I expected since I had that last time around, and it's not particularly bad. Mostly I just feel a little beat up, and still slightly groggy from the meds yesterday. Last time it was a few days before I really felt good again, but given how I feel now I think I'll be back to my normal self sooner. I've already begged off a meeting tonight, but I may be able to make it after all. Time will tell whether I get the same wonderful response to the Botox this time as I did before. I'm keeping my fingers crossed, and I'll keep you all posted. I'll cross my fingers for you too. For me, Marcaine seems to work a bit better than lidocaine, but I've never had botox so I don't know about how it might effect me. I hope this treatment helps even more than last time. Donna

contents: Re: Starting Botox It's been eight days since my second round of Botox, so I suppose it's time for an update. The pain didn't last as long this time as last time - I was at work the day after, and on a site visit with a customer the day after that. The urgency that's been plaguing me for a couple of months took longer than expected to calm down - today was my first day of having no sensation of bladder pressure or urgency. I remember last time that it took a while for the Botox to really reach its maximum effect, so probably not to surprising. The past few days have been pretty good, and today was really good. My retention is back, but that was something I'd expected after the issues I had with it last time. My retained volume has gone from 50-100 ml before the second round of Botox to 200 ml now, so I'm back to self cathing six or seven times a day. Not a big deal, though; the worst part of it for me is that having the catheters in my pocket makes it hard to get my car keys out. :-) Seriously, it amazes me how far I've come from a couple of years ago when I was literally in tears with dread of the pain of having a catheter put in for a VCUG. Now it's such a non-event that I hardly think about it any more. So, on the whole, I'm doing well and I'm very happy with round two of the Botox. It'll be interesting to see if it lasts longer this time.

Re: Starting Botox Good time for another update. My IC continues to be challenging. I've been having significant pain and pressure, along with retention volumes ranging from 150-500 ml, and occasional bouts of incontinence that seem to coincide with when the retention volumes are high. Instillations are helping some, but mostly I think I just need to wait out this flare. I'd hoped that the Botox would do more to shorten or minimize the flare. I'm waiting to hear back from my insurance on a preauthorization for more PTNS, and I'm probably going to ask my urologist to schedule a hydrodistension. I've also been having a lot of overall body aches, and particular pain in my hips and shoulders to the point that I can hardly climb a flight of stairs, and walking for more than about five minutes is excruciating. I had an appointment with a new rheumatologist today, which didn't go well - halfway through the appointment, before I'd had a chance to ask most of the questions I had, we were interrupted by a knock on the door. It was a psychologist that the nurse had requested because I'd mentioned in the pre-appointment questions that I've been struggling with depression, and I ended up basically getting frog-marched out of the exam room to talk to the psychologist. That was the end of the appointment - no chance to ask questions, no back-and-forth discussion of what's going on, just a vague suggestion that continuing to take my antidepressants would help with the pain. Never mind that the pain has been going on since before the bout of depression started, and I think the pain is one of the main contributors to the depression, not the other way around. I can schedule with another rheumatologist, but the next appointment I can get will be in December or January, so I guess I get to hurt for a while longer because the doctor I saw today figured I was a head case. I am frustrated beyond words with the brokenness of our healthcare system. The wait for appointments is horrendous, often four to six months, and when you do get an appointment it's too easy to get a doctor who doesn't want to deal with complex issues without a clearly-defined solution and just pushes you back to the beginning of the appointment line again. With my ongoing IC flare and other issues cropping up, it's hard to say whether the second round of Botox really did any good. I think it did - the flare started before the Botox, and the pain and urgency were far worse before the Botox than now, but I'm far from where I'd like to be. My IC is finally getting bad enough, along with my ever-worsening symptoms of liver disease, that I've started thinking seriously about disability. That's a scary road to start down, though, since I know the application process can drag on for many months or even years, and I don't know how I'll keep afloat financially for that long without an income. And the doctors have the gall to wonder why I'm depressed. :-(

Before Christmas when my IC was at its worst, I was headed in that direction. It brought me to an all time low. I am sorry that your appointment was rudely interrupted. I am glad they offered you help from a psychologist but it could have waited until after the appointment. I wonder what is causing so much retention?? Could there be too much botox? Or botox too soon? I am just wondering . . . Because my urologist didn't want to do it too soon. Thinking about you.
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Hello Everyone :-) I have decided to start this thread to give another account of one's experience with botox injections. From my research, I have not found very many accounts or experiences online of Interstitial Cystitis patients using botox. Therefore, I will write my experiences here on this thread as I feel maybe something I say about my experience may help someone else. YESTERDAY, January 4th, I had my botox injections into the bladder wall under general anesthesia. Before the surgery, I had a chance to talk with my doctor. I asked him whether this would work right away (you know, like how facial botox works LOL). I was very disappointed when he said that this would not start working for 2 weeks. TWO WEEKS!!! In my disappointed state, I immediately responded with that elbow gesture, "Oh, darn!" However, all I could do was laugh but quite frankly I really wanted to cry - seriously! I laughed it off and waited for them to wheel me to the operating room. The procedure is longer than having a hydrodistention done because it takes time to inject the botox throughout the bladder wall. I found differences from having this procedure done compared to the hydrodistention. The main one - A WHOLE LOT MORE PAIN IN MY URETHRA. My experience is this: I left the hospital at 3pm. The pain medication wore off about two hours later and then the urethral pain began. I have never experienced that much urethral pain after a procedure. It was to the point that even after taking my own medication, 16 mg of hydromorphone, 400mg of pyridium, I still could not get the pain down. Thus, I did not get to sleep until 6:30am this morning, and even then woke up every 30 minutes to pee. It was ridiculous! However, I relied on my faith and kept believing that there IS HOPE at the end of this tunnel. I woke up for good at 8:30am. A water bottle with frozen water was very helpful throughout the night! I alternated from placing it between my legs to putting it over the suprapubic area! So how am I feeling today? Hmmm . . . now that is a loaded question (haha). I feel like someone had a rigid machine up my urethra for 40 minutes! I am having more bladder pressure but the frequency is about the same (every 15 to 20 minutes). I am trying hard to not pee that much BUT today is not the day to play a MARTYR. Due to the fact that my pain was so high last night, I need to go to a walk in clinic in a few minutes to get a prescription for pain meds and something to help me sleep. I do not have everything I need. I flew here to have the procedure done so I need something to help me have a good flight back. It is not a long flight but it is long enough. So to take care of myself, I am heading to the walk-in clinic (where my former family doctor from when I lived in the Greater Vancouver Area is on duty). I know that he will be able to take care of my needs. So I hope something in this post will be helpful for someone considering BOTOX or who has experienced BOTOX. I will update you as I progress. Feeling hopeful, Wizbe

Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Wizbe, I'm glad you're feeling hopeful about your procedure. Your aftereffects seem similar to mine - my urethral pain was worst the first night, but still pretty bad for three days after. Then it subsided to a much more manageable level. Two weeks is the textbook answer on how long it takes to reach max effect from the Botox, but in less than a week I was having significant relief from my pain, urgency, and frequency. I hope you get fast pain relief, and I hope that you get quick results on the Botox working!

And Wizbe --- I'm sending a gentle hug your way. Donna

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Today is Thursday - it has been 72 hours (nearly) since I had the botox injections done under a general anesthetic. I flew back home last night. The trip to the airport was unbearable so I eventually got in a taxi and went the rest of the way. I decided to take some pretty strong pills to 'knock me out'/help me sleep on the plane. The pain yesterday made it difficult to walk without a strong urethral pain occurring. Thus, I took it slow. I was thankful that on the flight home there was nobody sitting next to me which meant I could stretch out. It made for a more comfortable ride. I also had a couple pick me up from the airport. I could stretch out in the back of my car (which I did). Today the urethral pain is minimal and I hope it stays that way after I do an instillation in a few minutes. We will see how that goes in a few minutes. I can walk normally today but still at a slower pace. The frequency seems to be less this morning so I am hoping that means that something is happening. As the weeks progress, I will fill you in more. I feel hopeful! Thanks itapilot and ICNDonna for your words of encouragement. Many blessings to everyone!

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience So this is day 5 since I had my botox injections. I am feeling hopeful. Today, at three different moments I was able to make it to 30 minutes before voiding. WOW! This is new for me. The last three months has been pure hell but today I saw some LIGHT and it made me excited! I am excited to see how this is going to work. I cannot explain how my bladder feels. It feels a little numb - not sure if this is the right word to describe it - but that is the only word that comes to mind. It is also harder to pee now. Not that I am struggling to pee but it is harder to feel the muscles that help to pee. I have always found it a bit difficult to start a stream but I can't even say it feels like that. It feels different in a positive way. So today I feel hopeful! I hope this is the start of getting my life back because the past year and 3 months I have been in a flare that never seems to end. I think the end may be in sight!

signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience That's so wonderful! I'm glad you're finally getting some relief. I have the same sort of numbness (I use the term "vagueness"), but it's so much better than pain and urgency. I hope you continue to improve - the effect of the Botox can take several weeks to reach its peak. Please continue to keep us updated on how you're doing!

Re: Botox - My Experience So here's another update: Saturday I began to notice significant improvement. I was cautiously excited! There were two times that I did not pee for 50 minutes, the rest of the times were 30 minutes. [B][/Sunday[B] morning was just as good. I did an instillation in the morning and that helped as well. But on Sunday afternoon I started peeing every 20 minutes again and I felt a bit discouraged. It also became increasingly harder to pee. It's almost as if you do not realize what are your bladder muscles and what are your rectum muscles. Then it seems like I can only feel my rectum muscles and not my bladder muscles. [B][/Monday [B]was my first day back to work. In the morning I felt only OK and by the afternoon I didn't really want to be there anymore. I just felt so tired. Though I was thankful that I was back to work . . . the first time in a long time. Tuesday has been a VERY GOOD day! I drove 45 minutes to work and although, I had a faint feeling like I needed to pee, I didn't have to stop anywhere. Lately, each time that I travelled to this work location I would have to pee before I went, pee 3 times on the journey, and as soon as I got through the door when I got there. It was horrible. Today I peed before I left, and I peed 10 minutes after I got there. YIPPEE! On the way home it was the same thing - I actually made it 1 hour and 7 minutes without peeing! Today I did have to take some pain pills and it brought my pain down to a MINIMAL level. It has been probably 8 years since I had pain at a minimal level. Tonight I walked my Airedale Terrier for 40 minutes. This a FIRST for a VERY LONG TIME!!!! I am so happy and so is she(my Airedale that is!)! My pain is slightly higher now . . . but it is slightly! The only DOWNFALL in having the botox injections is that there has been a few occasions in the last couple of days where I have had to use a catheter. I don't mind using a catheter at home but in the staff washroom at work is a bit of a pain. I find it hard to find the urethra and when I do I nearly make a mess each time! So I am hoping that I do not need to catheterize any more than that over a couple of days as the idea of infections makes me nervous. BUT . . . I would rather catheterize than to be dealing with what I have been dealing with - with no solution. No matter how many pills, pain meds, instillations, and hydrodistentions we have done. I had no other option but to try BOTOX and so far I am happy I did. Today was the first time in many days that I had a passion for my work! When you are sick, you lose your passions! Today I couldn't wait to read all kinds of counseling articles again and look up various trainings! So all-in-all I am thankful so far. I hope it continues in that direction. I almost sit here in disbelief of how wonderful it was today. There is still a ways to go but when you have been dealing with pain at a 6 - 7 level and peeing every 15 minutes, an average of 40 - 45 minutes without peeing feels pretty fantastic!

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience There are a few things I have noticed over the past couple of days: 1 - I am able to be pee again without struggling to pee. I hope when I am constipated that it stays like this too. The struggle was very hard in the first couple of weeks and I was really afraid that I was going to have to catheterize all the time. 2 - Without the botox, my instillations would only work for the pain (somewhat) and it wouldn't do anything for the frequency. Now - I am doing instillations every two days and when I do it, I notice a significant decrease in pain. I tried yesterday to not do an instillation BUT I was at a Professional Development day with the school district yesterday and we were sitting for the a very long time. Lunch time I came home and did an instillation and it made me have a lovely ending to the day. 3 - The bladder pressure feels different (Now, I ended up having an infection from the procedure so I am sure that this may have something to do with it. I will see some more what it feels like once the infection is cleared up). I have to be honest, for me when I have an infection I do not feel any difference. My flares feel much worse than having an infection. The only reason I suspected an infection was the amount of blood that was continuing to come after the procedure (for way more days than previous procedures). 4 - I feel more ALIVE and PASSIONATE since having the procedure. I am planning trips and believing that this is going to continue to improve. Last night I went to someone's house for supper I was there from 6 to 10. I used the washroom before I went at 5:50, used it again at 7:25, used it again at 8:35, and then used the washroom at 10:10. WOW! I still feel my bladder all the time but it HAS IMPROVED significantly. So in 4 hours an 20 minutes I used the washroom 4 times. On January 3rd if I would have visited their house (which I would not have), I would have peed at 12 - 16 times! And I would have been in crazy amounts of pain.
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. Bladder instillations (Lidocaine, Heparin, & Sodium Bicarbonate) I have tried but failed - lidocaine instillations, heparin instillations, oxybutynin by mouth also called ditropan, tylenol 3s with coedine, cyclobenzaphrine, Ativan, vesicare, Oxytrol patches worked for 3 to 4 years and then failed, I tried Uracyst and had some success but my urologist felt that it was not successful so we moved on to trying something else. . [/I]

Re: Botox - My Experience Thank you Wizbe for the update. My procedure is this Thursday (1/28.) of course, I am nervous but like so many of us the daily pain is greater than my fear. My personal doctor thinks I'm crazy to to this but what other choices do I really have? I have every treatment recommended up to this point without significant improvements. On my first hydrodistention my urologist lasered off 6 large Hunner's lesions of. My bladder. I felt great for about 3 months. It was heaven. I've been told this could help for 6-9 months. Wouldn't that be great. I have never had to cath myself so that also is adding an additional amount of fear.

Re: Botox - My Experience Hi Everyone :-) It has been two weeks since I updated. I feel the best I have felt in the last four years - this past week. My pain has gone down this week too. I am so happy about the result. It can be harder to pee - but wow, the difference from peeing 60 or more times a day, to peeing 15 to 20 (wow). I have slept through the night for the last two nights . . . so strange when I have spent nights awake - many nights in a row awake. To think the difference from January 4th until now! I am hoping this continues! So happy!

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Wizbe, that's wonderful news. Thanks for sharing it with us! I'm very, very happy for you. Signal, I hope you get wonderful results, too!

Re: Botox - My Experience Hello Everyone! I went for my follow-up yesterday. I told him that I seen increased levels of pain in recent days. Of course, sent me to pee and then scanned my bladder. Bladder showed 350ml of urine left. Oops . . . just thought it was my normal pain BUT the more she pressed on my bladder to scan it, the more I kept saying 'ouch'. So it seems I have more retention than I thought - I thought the retention had stopped. He is happy with how well I am doing and had assumed that I would have retention like this because of the pattern of injection he used which was required due to my pain levels before the procedure. So what's the next step? He wants me to catheterize 3 to 4 times a day. Morning, lunch, supper, and before bed. Thus, yesterday I went and bought 100 female catheters. Very easy to use and quick. Just have to figure out how to make this happen at work. I am still VERY happy with the results. Who couldn't be happy with going back to work, able to drive places, meet up with friends and play with my dog. Makes life special. Many blessings everyone!

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience I am one day out from surgery. Like others the pain afterwards was off the charts. I ended up in recovery longer that normal so they could get the pain under control. I came home and slept most of the day and night. I took pain pills to help. This morning the pain is very minimal. I haven't taken anything for pain except Tylenol. The doctor injected 200 units of Botox and lasered off a few more of the Hunner's lesions. I have been able to urinate with some discomfort. They didn't teach me how to self cath because he said if I urinate before I leave the hospital I should be fine. Having read your experience I doubt that will be true, but will see. Wizbe, I am glad you are doing so well. I look forward to the same.

Re: Botox - My Experience Signal, 200 units is a lot! That's how much they typically use in MS patients, while we IC folks usually get 100. The odds of having to start self catheterization is much higher with the higher dose, although by no means a sure thing. I hope you continue to have a smooth recovery and get good results from the Botox! Wizbe, does your insurance cover the catheters? It might be worth checking. Mine does, and that allows me to get the more expensive hydrophilic ones that are self lubricating and much more comfortable. Cathing at work gets easier as you get more used to the cathing process. I hardly think about it any more - pee what I can, pop the water packet to activate the lubricant layer, open the cath and take it out, put it in and drain the rest of the urine, take it out and slip it back in its package, and tuck it in my pocket to dispose of later. Takes about a minute and a half or two minutes.

Re: Botox - My Experience I know Itapilot, I thought 200 was a lot too. He originally wanted to do 300. Yesterday he said I really don't think 100 will be enough for you because your case is so complicated. I do not nor have I ever had any inconvenience so that is not an issuer for me. It is in there now so will hope for the best. I had wrapped my brain around having to self cath so if I do it will be fine. I just don't want to make a trip to the ER because I can't urinate. How are you doing?
Re: Botox - My Experience Itapilot, darn auto correct. Incontitence!

Re: Botox - My Experience I'm doing well. I keep watching for my symptoms to come back, but they haven't - I think I'm just having trouble wrapping my head around my bladder feeling this good. I'm flying to Florida next week and driving back, and its so nice to contemplate flying without stressing about when they'll turn off the seat belt sign, and when they'll turn it back on, and how badly I'll have to pee when we reach the gate. I'm not sure if I'll be able to take my usual catheters on the plane because of the water packets in them, but I've got enough regular catheters without water in them that I'll make it through the trip. I'm excited about the trip - I'm picking up a new-to-me hot air balloon and chase truck to replace my old balloon that's wearing out. I feel like I've gotten my life back - two months ago I would have had nothing but dread for the trip.

Re: Botox - My Experience Quite amazing difference, hey? I have some anxiety about symptoms returning too. I sometimes wonder when the rug is going to be pulled out from under my feet - world turned upside down because like you said it is hard to wrap my head around the Amazing difference. However, I am trusting that the botox will do the trick for a long while. I am trusting that if symptoms return it will be a gradual return of symptoms as the botox wears off. This is what I am hoping. I am so happy that you are doing well. Sounds exciting to be purchasing a balloon like that. Your wellness has made you be able to do just that and I went ahead a booked a trip... LOL. So happy for you. I think taking the liquid packs on the plane will not be a problem if the liquid packs are 100ml or less or used for medical purposes. I recently flew with many bottles of lidocaine which were more than the amount designated. Best wishes for you your trip.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Hello Everyone :-) It has been awhile since I shared about my BOTOX experience. I think it is important to share in order for other ICers to get a better idea of what the BOTOX can be like. First of all, this is how my botox experience is and it is different for everyone. My frequency is so much better! I was using the washroom 60 times a day, that doesn't include night time bathroom visits. I was going through 4 rolls of toilet paper a day (expensive when you live in a northern community)! It was exciting to see a 15 roll of toilet paper last nearly 2.5 weeks. WOW! Amazing, eh? This is exciting for sure. I do not need to leave counseling/ play therapy sessions with the children I work with. My bladder is not in a constant spasm. This part of the botox experience has been wonderful. I could probably go on forever talking about how happy I am about this part of my happiness of the botox treatment. But let's talk about PAIN. For me, my pain which was anywhere from a 6 - 10 (but mostly sat between a 7 to 8 on the pain scale), has only gone down to a 4 - 5 and sometimes shoots up to a 7 certain times. This is sometimes caused by retention. I can urinate on my own, but I do not necessarily get everything out. This afternoon for example, was horrible. I was in a lot of pain and it was rising to 7. I felt like I needed to pee all the time - I was ready to pull out my hair quite frankly and then I remembered - HMMM, I should use a catheter! Well, that made all of the difference. My pain level went down to a 4 with catheter use and moderate level narcotics. I still need to use narcotics to function some days. I know that some of my pain is deep pain - really deep pain, pelvic muscle pain. But I also have BLADDER pain. Before the botox, I couldn't really tell the difference between the types of pain. Everything was extremely painful 24/7 and like I said, my bladder was pretty much in a constant spasm. Thankfully, the botox has taken away the spasms, but it hasn't taken away the other pains. Now, I can tell the difference between the pain and quickly take what I know will work for me - work to a point (my pain never goes away). I can always feel pain in the pelvis. ALWAYS! I have not experienced a pain free day in 8.5 years. I am a very positive person though and only people who know me well, know the pain I experience - other people, are surprised when I tell them. I carry a thera-seat (bladder cushion) everywhere I go because I have GREAT difficulty sitting. I play piano sitting on my foot. I sit on church pews and hard chairs (even with my cushion) criss-cross apple sauce. I plan on starting internal physical therapy again soon to help with these levels of pain. I have mini-flares - my flares no longer last for days. When I go into a flare at the moment, it last for a day and then it is over with. I would say I have 3 good days and then a flare or maybe, I will have 3 excellent mornings and 3 not-so-good afternoons. We all know that IC flare patterns of pain doesn't always make sense and sometimes there is no rhyme or reason for them. I know when I walk faster than usual that sometimes causes me to have a mini flare in the afternoon. But even though I am not totally happy with how the botox has helped my pain levels, it HAS still helped them. I am VERY thankful for my BOTOX experience and I will do it again when this wears off. MOST definitely! This is the only thing that has helped and given me my life back in the last few years. SO I will do botox again. I am thankful that God sent me to this specialist and I pray that it will continue to be successful for awhile. Tomorrow should be interesting though - it will probably test the botox out a little bit. I am doing a colonoscopy prep - I am hoping that it doesn't cause me to flare but I am not certain that the liquid that you are required to drink will not affect me. Hopefully, it will be fine but I am preparing to use instillations, pyridium, and narcotics if I need to. For now, good night everyone! I am thankful that my severe interstitial cystitis has calmed down with botox treatment. Have a blessed evening.

I had a colonoscopy back in November and had no problem with the prep or test affecting my IC. I drank the Miralax and Frosh Gatorade. I will say that I drank it too fast (even faster than they said I had to), and I ended up throwing up once when I was done. There was no way I was going to drink more, so I just hoped and prayed I had gotten enough in me. I did, and got the "all clear" from the gastro.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience My IC is still doing well. I've had some pain and urgency the past few days, but I don't think it's because the botox is fading. I've been under tremendous stress, mostly at work but some at home - long hours, time-critical projects that were behind schedule, customers asking me to perform minor miracles, and a son who's having some trouble at school with not getting homework finished. I have been wondering if I've got a UTI on top of that - I saw a little blood in my urine on Saturday, and it's had a funny smell to it since Thursday or so. It's not a bad smell, just different. That by itself would explain some pain and urgency, and throw that in with being overworked and overstressed, and eating poorly to boot, and you come up with plenty of reasons that my bladder might not be very happy. I suppose I should go see my urologist about it, but if feels better today so I think I'll take the foolish route and wait another day to see if it's really there. I've also been dealing with a lot of body pain, and I've been referred to a rheumatologist for assessment of fibromyalgia. The symptoms are pretty consistent with that, but I won't know for another month - the soonest any of the rheumatologists in town could get me scheduled was two months out, and I'm only a month into that wait time. Frustrating. It varies day to day - Saturday, Sunday were good, and today has been pretty good so far, but Friday was pretty bad. Not like I need another diagnosis of something wrong with me, but at least if I know what it is I can get appropriate treatment. Other than that, life is good. I flew in a balloon rally over the weekend - Saturday was too windy to fly, but I made up for it Sunday with a two hour and fifteen minute flight over the Rio Grande with the editor of the local paper on board. It's hard to be down on life when I get to do that with my weekends.

I hope you don't have fibro, but if you do, at least you can get started on treatment. Please keep us updated. Warm healing thoughts, Donna

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Donna, it's not what I wanted to hear, but you're right about not waiting. I dropped by my urologist's office after lunch and they did a urinalysis. It came back clean, but given the symptoms the doctor still wants me to do a course of antibiotics. I'll pick those up on the way home from work.
Re: Botox - My Experience Wizbe, how did the colonoscooy prep go, and how was the procedure? Sending thoughts and prayers your way!

Re: Botox - My Experience Wisbe, fairly good suggestion- if you are at home, do not use toilet paper, but wash yourself with water and tap with some soft towel afterwards. I guess it can reduce irritation as well.

Re: Botox - My Experience The colonoscopy prep went well. It did make me urinate more than defecate at first. The liquid was gross to drink and to be honest I could only drink 2 of the 4 litres. I started to gag after that. I hope to never have to do that again. I was cleaned out though so that was good.
signature: Current Meds: 1 Elmiron 100mg 3x a day 1 Cimetidine 300mg 2x a day 4 Gabapentin 300mg 3x a day (3600mg) 1 - 2 Atarax 25mg at bedtime 1 baclofen 10mg 3x a day as needed 200mg of Pyridium 3x a day (usually do in cycles - on for a week or two as needed) - I am very careful about this. 2 belladonna and opium suppositories as needed for pain daily. [/I]

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Tonight I am having a bit of a flare. My pain levels are all over the map. I am trying to get comfortable to sleep but I am wide awake. Without the botox this would look different I would be back and forth to the washroom many many times than I was tonight. So I am keeping in my head that this is much better than before but the pain is not great. It switches from navel pain to urethra pain. I think an instillation is in my very very near future.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience You are lucky, you are from Canada, right? In Germany in forum people say that Botox is great, but only against frequency. If you do not start soon instillations, your bladder wall gets more and more broken, Botox will not help making it better. I very very much suggest Uracyst instillations that are available in Canada! But please understand, you need to make those instillations your whole life to protect bladder wall and second, you might not see results before some months (and do not do them every week as suggested, but leave at least 3-4 weeks between, otherwise it burns you so much that you give up). My bladder wall looked 2 years ago by NORMAL cysto absolutely inflamed and red, after 2 years Gepan (similar, bur weaker than Uracyst) there is no visible inflammation, though I still have symptoms and think about Botox. But I would NOT give up instillations. Please do it, ok?

Re: Botox - My Experience Hi Kris. I hope you are well. I did URACYST instillations from January 2010 until December 2013. I found they helped somewhat. My urologist tried URACYST on its own as well as a URACYST cocktail. The cocktail gave more results than uracyst alone. My urologist did not think they were very helpful for me so switched me to another cocktail. Currently, I do instillations four times a week. However, due to retention I have had to cut this down. The retention from the botox is way better now so I am doing instillations as needed. I do a heparin, lidocaine, sodium bicarbonate mixture. This is what I used to mix with the URACYST minus the sodium bicarbonate. I get more relief and results from the latter. What I have found interesting in the last year is how my bladder inflammation has gotten worst. My whole bladder is completely covered . . . I am currently doing an elimination diet to rule out even supposedly 'safe' food. In the meantime, botox is helping me function and I love it. In combination with instillations, I feel so much better than three months ago when I felt there was no hope.obviously, I see much hope now.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience I thought Botox is famous for smoothing out wrinkles on the face, but it has been approved by the FDA to treat chronic migraine headaches in adults. The injections are performed not only in the distribution of one's headache on one side, but also on the opposite side even if there is no pain. This treatment is extremely safe and it generally last from 3-4 months with the first few visits.I just read an article about this treatment...http://www.med-aesthetics.com/cosmetic-botox/

Re: Botox - My Experience I am a 49 year old man in the UK with a diagnosis of IC after 6 years of suffering. My symptoms began with pain and little change in frequency but after my first Urodynamics test I developed frequency as well. I have tried lots of oral medication with no succes and before I had my botox surgery I didnt really have much pain and frequency was the main problem. I had my procedure at the end of February 2016 and the pain has been horrendous for most of the time and done very little for the frequency. I must add though that Botox treatment may favour females much more than males,perhaps due to our different physiology. I can definitely anybody wanting to give it a try but its not something I will do again,personally.

Re: Botox - My Experience Vladilych, I'm sorry to hear that you haven't gotten relief with the Botox. I'm male, and my results have been wonderful, bordering on spectacular. But my symptoms have been almost the opposite of yours - my IC started with frequency and urgency, and the pain developed long after those two symptoms. My pain, while it's plenty bad, isn't nearly on the level of what some people here experience. I don't know if the clinical trial data support a difference in response to Botox, or if your disease and mine are different enough that the Botox worked for me and not for you. I'll do Botox again in a heartbeat when my symptoms return in full, and as long as I keep getting the same results I'll be happy with it. I was in a lot of pain for two days after the procedure, but since then the frequency has been almost nonexistent, the pain has been much improved, and the urgency has only recently begun to be bad again. I hope you're able to find something that gives you some pain relief!

Re: Botox - My Experience Itapilot,yes I was hoping for much better results too after pushing for this treatment for such a long time. Its surprising how symptoms tend to differ. I have virtually no bladder pain and my pain is almost all in my Urethra and I have always suspected my problem is more to do with this than my actual bladder but no doctor (and I've been through a good few!) seem to support my own instincts.) I also suffer from IBS quite severely and life stinks at the moment as there are very few treatments for this condition either here in the UK.

Re: Botox - My Experience Hi Wizbe I am also from Canada but the eastern side, Toronto. You mentioned that you mix uracyst with lidocaine and heparin. I have been using uracyst for several months and found that it helps somewhat. Now I have plateaued on it. I have also done the cocktail of lidocaine and heparin but have never mixed that with uracyst. Could you tell me how much lidocaine and heparin you added to the uracyst so I can run this by my doctor. Much appreciated.
Re: Botox - My Experience Hi there. My urologist did the vial of URACYST, 8ml of 2% lidocaine, and 2ml of heparin.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience Wizbe and Itapilot, glad to hear you are both doing well. My bladder is still better for the most part. My pelvic floor is another story. In the beginning both bladder and pelvic floor were both happy. I have also had some , new to me, stinging in various areas of my abdomen. I'm coming up on the 4th month. Really homing to get at least a couple more months from this. I have already been approved to have the procedure again. Looking at late July since I hope to go on vacation early Aug.
Re: Botox - My Experience Glad your bladder is still doing better. For me, it depends on the day - sometimes the hour LOL. I am doing well. I hope I get more time out of this too.

contents: Re: Botox - My Experience I had a second round of Botox done on 7/20. The pain after the procedure was much less than the first time. I did have several days of pain when I urinate, which I did not have the first time. It felt like a UTI, but tests came back negative. Dr. Says it could be from the Botox and said should get better soon. Pyridium seems to help. I'm also happy that I have not had any retention. I did have a couple more Hunner's lasered off. Those seem to grow back so quickly. We are taking a road trip across the southwest desert soon. Looking forward to a much more comfortable trip. Itapilot, did you do the Botox again? How are you?

Re: Botox - My Experience Signgal, I'm glad you're doing well with the second round of Botox. I've been having a rough time with things, but I don't think it's because of the Botox. It's been over a month since mine, and I was doing well for the first few weeks (some pressure and mild discomfort, but little urgency or pain), but around two and a half weeks ago I started feeling like I had a UTI. That was late on a Thursday and my urologist's office is closed on Fridays, so on Saturday I went to urgent care and they didn't find anything. I was in increasing pain, though, and having horrible bouts of urgency, so the next Monday I saw my urologist who gave me a month's worth of Cipro. I questioned why I needed it, and she said that it could be some prostatitis or various other things that wouldn't show up on a UA. She also suggested that I start on the instills again, and told me I was OK to take Azo as long as I need it. Things got a little better, but they've still been rough. Sometimes I'm pain free but still have a sensation of pressure unless my bladder is completely empty, and sometimes I'm in a lot of pain. The urgency comes and goes, and sometimes hits very suddenly. After the Botox I'd been routinely retaining about 250 ml of urine, but the past couple of weeks that's been as high as 600 ml and rarely below 500 ml - I often can't urinate at all until I put in a catheter. I don't know what's going on, but I don't think it's the Botox. My other medical problems are all acting up - I've got some pretty severe abdominal pain from my liver disease, my bowels are unhappy with me, and my shoulder and hip pain is back with a vengeance. I feel like my whole body is falling apart. Add in worsening depression, and I'm just kind of a mess right now.

Re: Botox - My Experience Itapilot, I am so sorry to hear you are having so many problems. It is difficult for people to understand what this silly disease is like. Most of us seem to have a variety of other ailments that ride the same train as IC. What does your Urologist think is going on? Don't give up, we are thinking of you. Will check in with you in a few days. Sending you positive thoughts.

Re: Botox - My Experience She thinks that it may be prostatitis, but the Cipro is something of a shotgun approach. We're also getting approval from the insurance for another round of PTNS (ankle stim), which seemed to help late last year before I had the Botox, and she's open to doing another hydrodistension as well. I've had good success with hydrodistensions before, but the past three times I've had anesthesia I've ended up hospitalized overnight because of my reaction. So I'm not in a hurry for that, and I'll hope for good results from PTNS. Meanwhile I'm trying to calm down my liver disease. My gastroenterologist referred me back in February for an endoscopy, but it's a rather specialized type that can only be done out of network. My insurance approved it, but the provider's scheduling people insisted that I had to schedule an office visit with their doctor before they could schedule the endoscopy. I had to wait four months for that appointment, and they still haven't scheduled the endoscopy - but now I find that they didn't need to schedule the office visit in the first place, and my insurance is refusing to pay for it because it wasn't part of what they'd pre-approved. Last week was very frustrating for me with the health care problems I've been having. Hopefully I'll get all this sorted out soon. I did get a call Saturday from my rheumatology clinic, who just hired a new doctor and scheduled me to see him in a week and a half. They'd told me Friday that they were scheduling for January. Maybe I've turned the corner on all this.

I been having Botox injections in my bladder for the last 7 years, they have stop working. Doctor told me it could be that my bladder got used to it, can someone help me! I have urge incontinence, I have tried Interstim and did not work, vesicle, little help and Botox was great, but I had the injections last monday and did not helped me. Can my body got used to it?
Re: Botox did not helped this time to the IC Network. Does your doctor have any idea why it isn't working this time? I haven't had Botox, but hope others who have will respond to your question. Donna

I wish I had some advice or help to give you. Thinking about you and whispering a prayer for you this morning.

contents: Re: Botox did not helped this time I talked to my doctor, he wants to do an hydrodistension. Since my problem is urge incontinence and frequency, can the hydro help increase the volume that my bladder can hold?

My urologist is suggesting botox after I try a third anti spasmatic (VESIcare) for one month. I am desperate so open to anything. I have been reading online, and I read some people get just the area numbed, get the injections and are on their way. Others have general anesthetic - talk about dramatically different. Some report a very complex recovery. Others seem to be out shopping the same day. My dr. said he could do it in his office, but prefers to go next door to the hospital to do it. How complex is this? Is it under whatever kind of anesthetic you and your doctor want - either a local, a twilight, or general? Does recovery just depend on how bad it is? And finally, I do have some pelvic floor issues that make sex painful quite often - but my gyno ruled out issues there. Does the bladder botox help with those symptoms as well? Added bonus. Do most people find your insurance does a pretty good job covering it? I hope I do not need it but I will give it a try if I need to. I just wish I could find a consistent report of what actually HAPPENS. My urologist made it seem like its pretty simple, like a lot less serious than when I had a uterine ablation which was done under twilight and my gyno was really detailed when she first explained it. My urologist was just like, "We can try botox if this does not work..." and when I asked if it was in patient in his office he said, "I could do it that way but I prefer to go next door and do it in the medical center just in case." Am I reading about different actual procedures? Thank you so much. Fingers crossed the VESIcare works - anyone have luck with it?

Re: Why are reports of botox so dramatically different? Location (office vs. hospital) and type of anesthesia seem to depend mostly on the doctor's preference, and hopefully on the patient's preference as well. My doctor offered to do it in the hospital, but since I'm something of an old hand with catheterization she recommended doing it in the office. Because I'd been cathing daily for several months, my urethra is desensitized and the cystoscopy wasn't all that bad. She used lidocaine gel in my urethra and lidocaine liquid in my bladder to make sure I wasn't in too much discomfort. If you're in the hospital, you'll probably get either conscious sedation or a general. If the doctor wants to just do a local, I think they'd push for an office procedure. The choice of general vs. conscious sedation (propafol or versed) is probably yours, but the doctor may have an opinion as well. Recovery probably depends on a number of factors. It doesn't seem to track with how bad the IC is, but rather with how difficult the cystoscopy is. My recovery was kind of hard (I was down for about three days) because the cysto took a long, long time - over an hour, if memory serves. I haven't noticed any difference in my pelvic floor with the botox. I think the botox mostly stays in the bladder muscles. I took Vesicare for a year and a half. It helped a lot, but I had consistent side effects, constipation being the worst of the batch. I had dry mouth for a while, but that subsided. It wasn't bad, but I'm happier with the botox.

Re: Why are reports of botox so dramatically different? Hi Sharno, my experience is a little different than Itapilot. My procedure was this past Friday 2/5/16. My doctor preferred doing it in the hospital under a general. I also have Hunner's lesions and he wanted to laser a few of the larger ones off at the same time. I had a hydrodistention with fulguration and 200 units of Botox done. I can't lie, after the prosecute was difficult, more pain that I've had with other procedures. All in all I was there about 9 hours. I needed pain meds that night and the next day but have not needed them since. I also have pelvic floor dysfunction and while the Botox is placed in the bladder I have found for me that if my bladder is happy my PFD is better as well. It is not surprising that each of us has a different story since IC seems to effect each of us a little differently. Of course, doctors across the globe have various opinions on treatment. My insurance paid for the procedure. I hope this helps answer some of your questions. I have never used Vesicare. Good luck.

Re: Why are reports of botox so dramatically different? Thank you both for your response. I saw my uro today and after Veiscare did not work, I am now trying Myrbetriq for a month. We talked again about botox and I asked him where he does it, he said in his office, just with lidocaine. I just don't like the idea. I had a hard enough time with my cyst. test where they take a look inside. So do I have to go to a different uro? I should have asked him - since he is next to a hospital he's affiliated with - if I can just say I want some conscious sedation (not general, but whatever they give for colonoscopy). And wow - an hour for the procedure? He was like "Oh it just takes a few minutes." Ugh. he is always pretty rushed with me. he also offered to send me to a uro-gyno to take another look...I may take him up on it. He basically is keen on just going through ALL the meds on the market to soothe the bladder (he said there were like 8 of three classes?) and he has samples of everything so the nice thing is I do not have to pay. I asked him if there was anything he can give me for relief for bad flares. The ones that make me not want to go in public (I almost had to leave a public event recently and could only get relief by taking walk and sitting in the bathroom in fear). I am becoming a shut-in and it's impacting my work. He said he did not want to give any medication like that. I have read about people taking everything from valium in the vagina to other pain meds but he shut that down right away (I did not mention anything specific). Pyridium does nothing for me. Do I need to look for, yet, another uro? This is my third doctor to look into this in two years. So frustrating.

I'd like to hear from anyone that's had botox injections for frequency and pain. I'm considering the procedure due to sleep deprivation; I get up 6-8 times a night. I've had IC for about 4 years, currently on Elmiron, have been on various meds including pain meds, have had several cystoscopies w/ hydro. Daytime pain is not too bad now but the night time sleep interuptions are killing me. Thanks, Sharon

Re: Botox I have not had the injections, but my doctors are always offering them as an option (spasms are my problem, not frequency, by the way). I wanted to share that the deciding factor for me, to not choose Botox, is that sometimes after the injection, the patient has to self-catheterize for a period of time until their bladder "remembers" how to just go on its own. I have seen this warning in commercials lately, and been told by my doctor that if I'm not comfortable with self-cathing as a possibility, then Botox isn't for me. Have you tried antihistamines?

contents: Re: Botox Im thinking of having botox, only option left, scared of having to cath myself though, putting me off.

Re: Botox That is one of many reasons I would not do botox. Once you have to start cathing then you will open the door to getting UTI's since cathing causes UTI's! Also Botox is an actual Toxin and I am just afraid of how my body would react to that. Once they inject it in you, you cannot get it out no matter what your reaction is. You have to wait it out and hope it will wear off.. To many risks in my opinion.
Re: Botox This is why I would like to hear from folks who have actually been treated with Botox injections. Let's hope we get some replies. Sharon
Re: Botox Joining this post. Hoping to see if someone who has been treated replies. My Doctor also recommended Botox as possible treatment but I don't like the idea either.
signature: Kris Diagnosed:Interstitial Cystitis 2010, Pelvic Floor Dysfunction 2013 Current Medications:Elmiron, Gelnique, Hydroxyzine Current Supplements:Prelief, Desert Harvest Aloe Past Medications: Amitriptyline, Uribel, Xanax Other: Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy, Tens Unit for Pain, Sensitive to medications

contents: Re: Botox Anyone have information on Botox used for pain management of severe cystitis? My doc is insisting on Botox treatment being used in conjuction with hunner's lesions treated with a steroid. I have problems with urinary hesitation and don't want Botox. Doc says it is required. Thoughts?

Re: Botox I have not had botox, but was offered it as well. From all that I have researched on it, it does not really work for pain, but more so for frequency. I did not feel comfortable with doing it because I am chemical sensitive and Botox is a toxin and can cause significant allergic reactions in some. My allergist was not really for it. Also, if you already have hesitancy issues they can get worse as botox can a lot of times cause urinary retention to a point where you have to catheterize yourself until it wears off( which can be months). Not everyone will experience the retention though, but it is more common from what I have heard. I think just the fact that it is not usually helpful for pain also helped me make my decision as well since pain is one of my biggest issues. Originally posted by iamcurious1970 Anyone have information on Botox used for pain management of severe cystitis? My doc is insisting on Botox treatment being used in conjuction with hunner's lesions treated with a steroid. I have problems with urinary hesitation and don't want Botox. Doc says it is required. Thoughts?

Re: Botox Hi everyone I am 45 year old female with IC and OAB-- I was going to the bathroom and having painful urination for the last 2 years nothing helped. I decided to try Botox-- as of yesterday I had my first injection and today I feel normal . I use to go to the bathroom every 1-2 hrs and I could never get a good night sleep because of this.... Well after just one day of treatment I slept for the first time in 2 years all night getting up only once and today I have been going to the bathroom every 4-5 hours-- so BOTOX seems to be a good choice for me and I wanted to share my experience with others. I was very nervous about this and I am now glad I had the treatment Relief finally
Re: Botox Hi, Are you still finding relief? Have you had days where you cannot pass water?

Re: Botox Yes I am still finding relief... I am able to urinate everyday and I still can hold my urine 3-4 hours without urgency and pain... Sometimes I do feel like I have to push harder to completely empty my bladder but other than that things are GOOD!

Re: Botox Originally posted by Lat0nia Yes I am still finding relief... I am able to urinate everyday and I still can hold my urine 3-4 hours without urgency and pain... Sometimes I do feel like I have to push harder to completely empty my bladder but other than that things are GOOD! I had Botox a week ago and I feel a LOT better, but I do have to push harder. Not much comes out considering what I've been drinking. I spoke to my doctor on the phone and he said that's normal. However, I'm going to go to my doctor's office on my lunch hour because I'm a little worried by how little is coming out. I definitely feel more comfortable for sure. Something inside me has calmed down. It did take a few days for the irritation from the scope they use to go away. As of now, I'm glad I did it.

Re: Botox My doctor has recommended Botox, and I think I'll go for it. I struggle with pain, urgency, frequency, and incontinence. The working theory is that I have IC overlaid with OAB, both of which can be helped by Botox. My main fear is the chance of urinary retention. I have a history of ending up with retention due to pain killers, anesthesia, and a few other things. The doctor warned me that it might be several months before I don't need to catch anymore. I'd rather deal with catheters than diapers, though, so I think it's a good way to go. Hopefully it'll be scheduled for the week after next, or the week after that.

Re: Botox Originally posted by ltapilot My doctor has recommended Botox, and I think I'll go for it. I struggle with pain, urgency, frequency, and incontinence. The working theory is that I have IC overlaid with OAB, both of which can be helped by Botox. My main fear is the chance of urinary retention. I have a history of ending up with retention due to pain killers, anesthesia, and a few other things. The doctor warned me that it might be several months before I don't need to catch anymore. I'd rather deal with catheters than diapers, though, so I think it's a good way to go. Hopefully it'll be scheduled for the week after next, or the week after that. You might want to ask your doctor about learning to self catheterize --- just in case you should ever have retention --- that way you won't have to worry about the possibility of not being able to urinate. Good luck! Donna

contents: Re: Botox Donna, I already do my own instillations, so self cathing is no big deal. In fact, that was something the doctor mentioned, was that the worst side effect that's common is retention, but since I already know how to cath it's not a big deal.

Re: Botox sswinbu - Not sure if you have had the procedure yet or are still investigating but I have had botox twice now and have my third treatment towards the end of this month. My doctor recommends that I go every three months, however, I did try to go four months this time since I was still feeling the effects after three months. My main symptoms are urgency (and I would have frequency if I allowed myself) but very minimal pain. I LOVE botox. I don't think I realized how great it is until it started wearing off. I still have bad days but I have more good days than bad and my bad days are much more manageable. The first couple days you do really have to concentrate on voiding as your bladder muscles are pretty lazy but that goes away rather quickly. There is also quite a bit of urethral pain and some blood but that usually only lasts a day for me. I won't lie and say that the actual treatment is a breeze because it's not. It sucks. But my second treatment was better than my first and I have been told that each consecutive treatment gets easier. Please let me know if you have any questions - I'd be happy to answer! Best of luck to you!

I am scheduled to get Botox, but after reading everyone's threads I'm scared. I can't recall what ozone therapy exactly is, but my friend has had the shots for other things and swears by the injections. This is what I read, Frankenhauser injection, bladder insufflations of ozone have been very helpful. Has anyone had this done or heard about it?

You can read about ozone therapy on the quackwatch.org site at http://quackwatch.org/search/webglim...ozone+therapy+ My urologist, who retired several years ago, told me about the quackwatch site and said it's a very good resource. Donna

contents: Just started Ozone Therapy I'm going for my second instill of ozone tomorrow. The doctor I've been seeing for over 5 years has been using it mostly for back aches. The patients say its really helping tremendously with their pain. I had my first one 3 weeks ago and its uncomfortable. Recently, I have no bladder pain but I will wait to see if its actually the ozone helping me before I post it here. There is a tremendous amount of information on the web about ozone helping to heal the bladder. There are many studies, lectures and research you can read to verify its benefits. People often call "new" therapy's quackery but I did my own research and felt it could only help me. Do your own research and see if its worth trying. I will update my progress in months to come.
