contents: Re: Prolapse Due to Botox in Pelvic Floor? Originally posted by cellicl Looking at the site right now, thank you for the information! I was told a long time ago I had a little prolapse but I could not see it! I didn't really know what it meant! Now I actually see it, it is creepy to me and scary! I am sure it is from the botox, everything is so relaxed, I am just praying it can be reversed! The kegels don't seem to help right now because everything is sooo relaxed, I can barely do them! Just scary!!! If it makes you feel better, Christine Kent doesn't believe Kegels work, and a lot of women find they make them worse (whilst i know other women find they make them better--again, we are all individual!). but just offering reassurance that there are other ways to deal with it. i know, it's a really weird feeling. but hang in there. some women find a sea sponge acts as a natural pessary. i found a small tampon would do the trick (put oil on it if dryness). just to help lift things up to feel a bit better. the forum there is as wonderful as the forum here, so post away and someone will reply!

Re: Prolapse Due to Botox in Pelvic Floor? Thank you I really appreciate it! I on the site right now do you have to pay anything at first to get some exercises the free video did not work? What did you buy and do you feel it is worth it! After reading it may not help me because I had a partial hysterectomy years ago!

Re: Prolapse Due to Botox in Pelvic Floor? start ont eh forum, there is a ton of info there. i haven't been on the rest of the site for a while so not sure what is available. she does have a bit on youtube i think… there are women who have done really well there who have had hysterectomies. the main book describes the posture, but you can also get descriptions through the forum to start on it. i got the videos to work, maybe it's a flash thing? just t know she is very negative about surgery, partly as she sees a lot of women who have had very bad results with it. of course there are lots of women who have had no problems as well, but she is very open about what can happen. just know that. i don't rule it out in the future if i really needed it, i'm just glad i found a solution that works for me, for the moment at least
I was wondering how you're feeling since your Botox? I know you said it usually takes up to 5 days, so I was wondering how you're doing??
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? Thanks for asking, my2boys. Since the botox injections last Thursday, I have had what I can only describe as an itchy, stinging bladder cramping almost daily. I don't know if that's my bladder healing from the injections or if it's the injections actually working or what. I'm going to assume it's one or the other. It's a bit uncomfortable. I tried like heck not to take the Norco unless I really, really had to and ended up taking it 3x a day through the weekend. I haven't taken any since then, though. I'm treating that stuff like gold. Last night the crazy itchy stabbing hit me about 1:00 am and lasted till about 4:00 am. I was pretty miserable especially since I couldn't take anything. Ice packs seemed to aggravate it, so did a heating pad on Low. So, I just suffered through it. I couldn't take anything because I am having fasting blood work today (as part of my annual physical with my primary doctor) and couldn't eat or drink anything after 10 pm last night. To me, that meant no meds either. I didn't want it to skew the results of the blood work from any medication in my system. Thank goodness my appointment for the blood work is in an hour. I can't wait till it's over so I can just take my meds, drink a big glass of water and have some breakfast. Maybe that will help. I have not veered off the IC diet at all so I don't think this is a flare. The botox does seem to be working a little. There are times during the day when I can hold it for 2-3 1/2 hours. Before the botox, it was more like 1-1 1/2 hr tops. Also, when I have urgency now, I still have leaking sometimes, but not as bad as before the botox. I've been going 4-5 times during the night. My frequency always increases for about 4 hours after I take my blood pressure medicine and I don't know that even botox can do anything about that. My void totals since the injections are as follows: Sat 24 Sun 28 Mon 15 <-- for me, this is incredible Tue 22 Is this as good as it gets? I have no idea. The jury is still out. Maybe I have a bladder that's just slow to respond. I'm willing to give it some time before I can say it was a success or a bust since it's only been a week. My urologist's office hasn't called me yet to set up a follow-up appointment, but should be doing so sometime this week. Last edited by Bladderella; 11-12-2014, 02:15 AM.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? Well it looks like you had a good day there with only voiding 15 times. I have heard it takes some time to be effective, so it will be interesting to see if you continue to improve. Hopefully, you've gotten some water and food in you now, because I know if I can't drink water, my bladder gets very unhappy. I remember my hydro was at 2pm at the hospital and of course, nothing by mouth after midnight. By the time they took me, my bladder was freaking out! I do the same thing with narcotics, take them only if I have to. But remember, you need to take care of yourself, and if you need pain relief from the procedure, take your meds. Did they inject botox directly in or on your bladder? My neurologist wanted me to have 35 botox injections around my head last year (for migraines). I was going to do it, but my insurance was not cooperating, so I forgot about it. Well, am hopeful for you! Please keep us posted. 😊
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? So I get to the doctor's office for the nurse to draw blood and when she asks "how are you doing today?", I replied, "I am hungry and thirsty since I haven't had anything to eat or drink in the past 12 hours but that's ok, I've got a big jug of ice water waiting for me in the car when we're done here". She says, "What? You haven't had food or water? It was ok for you to have water and black coffee, just nothing else. " DUH!!!!! I totally misunderstood the instructions. So I caused my own self some of that grief last night because I could have at least had water and I didn't. Ohboy. The urologist wants to see me 6 weeks following the botox injections so my next appointment is the week before Christmas. Since he doesn't want to see me till then, I figure I should at least give my bladder 6 weeks to adjust to the injections. That seems reasonable to me so we'll see how everything looks and feels then. I got the injections inside the bladder, into the lining of the bladder, all the way around the inside.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? Oh geez, well you survived without the water, so that's good. Yeah, I would imagine you have to give it time to work. Similar to a hydrodistension. I noticed a big improvement around week 4 (and my uro agreed that's about average for most people who are going to benefit from it). Botox may be similar. I'll be looking for your updates!!

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I hope all goes well and that you see some improvement soon! Keep us posted.
Re: Bladderella...how are you? Bladderella, can you update us again? Hope you are well.

Re: Bladderella...how are you? It's been a month since I got the botox injections. Here are some things that have happened and that I've noticed so far: 1. My sense of urgency has definitely improved. I haven't had one incident of leakage since the botox and this was a real problem for me before. I'd be sitting in a chair, feel the urge to go, stand up to head for the bathroom, and as soon as I stood up, I started leaking. Between me and my puppy, we were doing a number on the carpet. I very rarely need to use incontinence aids now. 2. Frequency has improved. It's not great, but it's better. I was going on avg 25x a day (5-7 times at night) before the botox. Now it's about 15-18x a day (2-4 times at night). 3. My bladder seems to be more sensitive since the botox. I have a raw-like stinging sensation every day, usually all day from the moment I wake up. Sometimes I'll take some AZO and sometimes I'll take a couple of extended release acetaminophen (650mg) which seems to help. It also seems to calm down some after I eat a meal and drink water. The good news is, I have not needed to take any Norco for the past couple of weeks. 4. I have not decreased my water intake and have NOT strayed from the IC food list. I have stuck to the IC diet like a horsefly to flypaper. I have not had a flare since the botox! 5. I had a problem with constipation before the botox but recently I started taking the stool softener, Miralax. It has really helped. When I get backed up it's like the nerves in my bladder are stressed and the stinging worsens. It does subside some after I've had a bm, but it never completely goes away. 6. My heating pad no longer gives my bladder comfort. It seems to make things worse. I put the heating pad on my belly and my bladder does NOT like it. I get more relief now from ice packs that I put between my legs and up over my pubic bone. 7. I continue to take 3 hydroxyzine a day per my urologist. Even though I'm getting a little more sleep at night now, the meds make me very sleepy during the day. I was driving with my adult son the other day and we were at a stop light about a mile from home. All of a sudden I heard him shout "MOM!". I didn't realize that I'd nodded off while waiting for the light to turn green! Not good. I'll be bringing this up to the urologist when I see him in 2 weeks for my botox follow-up. It may be that I need to go back to taking only 2 hydroxyzine a day instead of 3. 8. I stopped taking the Renew Life probiotic for a while. It seemed like when I took it, the stinging feeling in my bladder worsened. 9. As my bladder is filling, the stinging is more pronounced. The good news is that now when I pee, I can count to 6-one thousand, sometimes 7. It used to be that I couldn't get past 4-one thousand so I'd say that my bladder is holding a little more urine these days. So.... would I do it again? I don't know yet. I'd like to give it at least 3-6 months before I feel I could honestly answer that question. My urologist and I believe that I have both Interstitial Cystitis and Overactive Bladder. The botox injections only address the Overactive Bladder part so there is still the IC part to deal with. Last edited by Bladderella; 12-06-2014, 09:09 AM.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I'm glad you've seen some improvement in your OAB symptoms. Decreasing the amount of times you get up during the night is huge. Did the doctor indicate when the stinging might subside? How scary about the nodding off while driving. I hate any medication that makes me drowsy (unless, of course, I'm planning on sleeping). I think there's a warning with the hydroxyzine not to drive or operate heavy machinery. You probably shouldn't be driving just taking 2. I must be really susceptible because half a dose put me out for 13 hours. It's interesting that the heating pad no longer is soothing. But glad the cold packs help. I hope with every day you notice more and more improvement.

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? Bladderella, you say that you now have a "stinging" sensation. I also see that you are now taking Miralax. Miralax contains polyethylene glycol, a chemical similar to Propylene glycol. Both of these chemicals cause my bladder to hurt and burn. I wonder if you could take something else for constipation for a few days (maybe magnesium?) to see if it is the Miralax that is causing the stinging. Just a thought. I so want you to feel better. Jo Ann

Re: Bladderella...how are you? Originally posted by joannharllee Bladderella, you say that you now have a "stinging" sensation. I also see that you are now taking Miralax. Miralax contains polyethylene glycol, a chemical similar to Propylene glycol. Both of these chemicals cause my bladder to hurt and burn. I wonder if you could take something else for constipation for a few days (maybe magnesium?) to see if it is the Miralax that is causing the stinging. Just a thought. I so want you to feel better. Jo Ann The stinging was there before I ever started using Miralax, so I don't believe Miralax is the cause. I really think most of it comes from being constipated or from having too much salt, i.e. chips. My bladder seems to have become more sensitive since the botox and whereas a handful of plain potato chips before was just fine, now it's too much. I was at the grocery store today and bought a bag of "No Salt" potato chips (I kinda laughed to myself and thought "what's the point?" when I saw the bag on the shelf but I figured I'd give 'em a try anyway.

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I had my 6-week follow-up with my urologist yesterday. I told him about my constipation and that I'm now taking Miralax every day. He said that was fine as constipation is a common problem for patients with IC/OAB. He explained that one theory is that the nerves in the bladder and intestines get mixed signals. When there is waste in the intestines, it puts pressure on the bladder because there isn't much space between those organs and the bladder goes bonkers. If I need to have a bowel movement, I usually have stabbing abdominal pain first. After the movement, the pain usually subsides, but then the itching sets in. smh My urologist was happy to hear that my leaking is gone and that the frequency has decreased. He said the botox "puts the bladder to sleep" so that it's not so active. He said the positive effects from the botox should last from 3-11 mos and that there is no danger in repeating the procedure if it's necessary. We also talked about the change the government has made in re-categorizing pain medication. He said hydrocodone can still be prescribed, but a doctor MUST see you in person first. Gone are the days that you could just call the doctor and have him call in a pain prescription for you. Unless there's a drastic change in my condition, I won't be seeing him again until after my daughter's wedding next June.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I had my 6-week follow-up with my urologist yesterday. He was pleased to hear that my leaking has stopped and that my frequency has decreased a little, which is exactly what the botox was supposed to do. The improvement would be even better if I wasn't taking blood pressure medication which makes me pee. It's like a tug of war in my bladder. Anyhow, he said the botox should last 3-11 mos. He assured me that there is no danger in repeating the procedure if we need to. I told him I've started taking Miralax daily because I am always constipated. He was fine with that and said constipation is a common problem among IC/OAB patients. He didn't have an answer for why I have itchy bladder except to say that one theory is that the nerves in the urethra and bladder get mixed signals so that the nerves in the bladder go bonkers. He told me to keep taking hydroxyzine 3x a day. Ice packs give me some temporary relief but nothing long lasting. I'm so itchy sometimes that it feels like I have ants in my pants. I also talked to him about the new re-classification of pain medication, specifically hydrocodone. He explained that the main difference now is that a doctor must see the patient in person first before prescribing it. There's no more requesting pain medication over the phone and then the doctor calling it in to the pharmacy, or having the doctor write a prescription that you pick up at the front desk. I can live with that. Unless there's a drastic change in my condition in the next 7 months, I won't see the urologist again until July... after my daughter's wedding. I don't know when the botox will wear off, but I've got my fingers crossed that it will last me until then.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I'm happy to hear that you've had some improvement- especially in regard to the leakage.

Re: Bladderella...how are you? Oh, one more thing I meant to add. I asked the doctor how my bladder looked to him when he was doing the botox injections and cystoscopy. It was the first time he'd ever seen my bladder with his own eyes so I wanted to know what his assessment was. He said there was no bleeding, no glomerulations, and no Hunner's ulcers. He said my bladder actually looked pretty healthy. I said, "What?? Seriously?!" Then he said, "don't be mislead. The surface of your bladder looked fine, but that doesn't mean there's nothing going on beneath the surface, and that's the part we can't see." The first time I had a cystoscopy in Jan 2014 (with a different urologist I ended up dumping for the one I'm seeing now)... I saw the inside of my bladder on the screen. It looked wounded with visible spider-web like splotches of blood everywhere. Seeing that with your own eyes, it's a bit unsettling. Back then, whenever I pee'd, there was visible blood in the toilet so I knew something not normal was going on. The 1st urologist kept saying, "this is the worse bladder I've ever seen!" (way to apply that comforting, bedside manner with a new patient, doc). Apparently, that's not the case now. My current urologist thinks the Cystoprotek is responsible for helping the bladder surface to heal. I've been taking Cystoprotek for 8 months not knowing if it was doing me any good or not. Well, now I know!
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? Wow- That's great to hear that your bladder surface looks so much better after taking Cystoprotek. Have you noticed any decrease in pain or irritation at all since you started the Cystoprotek?

Re: Bladderella...how are you? Originally posted by earthlady Wow- That's great to hear that your bladder surface looks so much better after taking Cystoprotek. Have you noticed any decrease in pain or irritation at all since you started the Cystoprotek? If I was only taking CystoProtek that would be an easy question to answer. CystoProtek is only part of my IC-fightin' arsenal. In addition to strictly following the IC diet ( I do NOT cheat), I take CystoProtek, Desert Harvest Aloe Vera, hydroxyzine, d-Mannose, glucosamine MSM, and Renew Life Probiotics. There is no way for me to really know which of those is responsible for lessening pain, irritation and inflammation. Probably all of it has helped in one way or another. Last edited by Bladderella; 12-20-2014, 03:51 AM.
signature: "When you gotta go, you gotta go!"

contents: Re: Bladderella...how are you? I guess you wouldn't really know if it was just one or the combination of all of them. It looks like a good combination though. I take a few of the ingredients in Cystoprotek separately and also the Desert Harvest Aloe, along hydroxyzine when needed for allergies and I found for me it is a very good combination.

Since nothing else much has worked, my urogyn suggested either botox or interstim. Interstim seems complicated, lots of surgery for mixed results at best. Botox might be ok, but I can't get past the potential need to self-cath if the retention is too much. Has anyone here had any success with botox? Doc says 5% have complications, but the things I've read say closer to 17-20%. My job is accommodating with my issue, and I don't have the option to quit work or not function. Any insight would be appreciated.

Re: Doc wants to try botox, but I'm not so sure. I haven't tried Botox, but if it were me and other treatments had been tried, I would go for Botox before Interstim. My reasoning is that even there are problems with Botox, it's not permanent. If the interstim has been implanted, it would require additional surgery to remove it. I learned how to catheterize years ago and have been glad on several occasions that I can do it. Just the fact that I know how is a comfort to me. Donna

contents: Ok, so my 77 year old MIL has had OAB for years. She just can't hold it and has frequent accidents. She's been wearing adult diapers for at least 5 years. Lately, it's getting worse, so she saw my urologist (well, she saw the PA in the office). This was about a month ago. He put her on Myrbetriq. She tried it for a month, but it didn't help. So, she went again yesterday. He told her since that drug didn't work, it's likely none of the OAB meds will help. So, he suggested either Botox or a spinal cord stimulator. Well, she already has a spinal cord stimulator connected to her neck for chronic pain due to bone spurs in her neck. She's had it for about 10 years and it does help some, although not great. She's fearful of the botox because she read the pamphlet and it said it could cause allergic reactions. She does seem to have allergic reactions to a lot of things. Has anyone heard of a spinal cord stimulator for either OAB or IC?? She would have two of them inside her if she did it

contents: I had Botox done last Friday, today is Thursday. My frequency has been cut in half. 20 times a day vs 40. Had my post-op visit today. The doc said my bladder tissue was in good shape but the underlying tissue is covered in scar tissue - all over the bladder, not just on one area. He did a hydrodistention along with the Botox. He's not sure the results are from the hydro or the Botox. He told me he will only do it no more than every 6 months, but he prefers to do it closer to a year. He said that's the Botox guidelines. I can't find anything. Not sure if he is telling me the truth. He tends to be on the conservative side. Wondering if it's time to find another uro. The sad thing is I totally trusted him. I've gotten a 'weird' vibe from him the last couple of times that I've been in. Not sure if I've frustrated him because I research things or if he's frustrated that he hasn't controlled my frequency with meds. Feeling a little out of control of the situation. I can't go back to 40x/day. It was hard to do anything except be in the house. I really need to have a life!
Re: Botox Frequency I've never had it but I was told it can be up to 6 months for the next treatment. I'm glad you've found some relief though!

I have suffered from IC for over 10 years and after trying everything I am now going to get Botox shots - I had a trigger shot which showed botox could reallt help but am nervous about side affects - has anyone had the shots?

Re: Botox shots I personally haven't tried that option, but have read notes from some who have found it helps. Please let me know how it works for you --- I'm sending positive vibes your way --- and hope it works for you. Donna

contents: Re: Botox shots Hi Melanieisu45, I am considering Botox shots too, but can you tell me what a trigger shot is. I've never heard of it. Please let us know how it works for you.

Re: Botox shots I believe the trigger shot I received was a mixture of Lidocaine and a steroid that they shoot into the bladder muscles - many women find great relief from the trigger shot and just get those every month but the doctor also tries the trigger shot first because she said 95% of people that get relief from trigger will get relief from botox - because botox is much more expensive and there are risks with botox she wanted to make sure it would work for me. I am actually getting another trigger shot while waiting for insurance to cover botox. The shots are quite painful - not going to lie I yelled quite a bit but the pain is only for the few minutes of each shot. Melanie
Re: Botox shots I had a terrible UTI after the botox shots and did catch it because I thought it was the pain from the procedure.

Hi all: Today I started participating in a Botox study. I don't know if I got Botox or Placebo, but I didn't think the injections were that bad, and I consider myself a wimp. It was more like a rough day at PT. She did about 10 or 15 injections on each side, but it was really hard to distinguish what was an injection from what was just regular pressure that you would feel whenever trigger point release pressure was put on those same muscles. If it was Botox the muscles should start to soften in 6 to 10 days, if not I suffer until PT starts up again in 4 weeks. I go back to the study doc in 2 weeks and in 4 weeks. No PT during the 4 week period. The study lasts 3 months and I guess usually the injections last anywhere from 3 months to 6 months for most people. I took 2 Clonazepam and 1 1/2 Percocet before she got started. I was only going to take 1 Percocet, but I managed to scare myself into taking another 1/2 . I go to a pain clinic for my prescriptions, but the study doc gave me enough Percocet (hopefully) to make it through until I can get to the pain clinic. I guess I just sit back and wait at this point. We'll see what happens next week.
signature: I was born with one ureta not attached to it's kidney, I've been poked and prodded for so long. I never had "private" parts. I had constant infections and took low dose preventive antibiotics into my teens. DX with IC July 1993 after seeing 3 different Uro's and finally switching Primary care doctors and affiliated hospitals. I have 2 beautiful daughters, the oldest has been in heaven since she was 14 when she was hit by a car The youngest is 20 now. She has had one UTI. She went into complete panic that she would end up like me. Now she has some idea of what the pain is like. Poor kid has been forced to know where every bathroom in Disney World is.

contents: Re: Botox Study Thank you for participating in a study. People who are willing to do this are heroic! And it's you who will help find a cure for us. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: Re: Botox Study Cia - I second that. You are super brave! Wow. I went to a another uro for another point of view and she was really into botox and wanted to do a cysto and urodynamics first. Not sure I can "bladder" more invasive procedures. I did the RTX trial long time ago and it was quite painful and tried liposomes instillation and that was painful (can't for the life of me understand why) , so all I can say is anyone who goes into a trial like you are, are doing us all a great favor. Botox does need more study for sure and you are undergoing something quite invasive not even knowing if you got it for real, so we all so appreciate the brave soul you are. Thank you so much.
signature: I got IC in 1970! I was not diagnosed until 1991. I've tried many drugs and therapies but I tend to only resort to drugs when in a flare because when I am not in a flare (from being good on diet), I suffer only from small bladder volume (like about 7 ozs.) and peeing will relieve the discomfort. When I am feeling relatively normal, I say to myself I am glad I am not on a drug. When I am in a flare, I say, why am I not on a drug! I've recently have been trying to solve my connective issue problems in general. I look to diet and herbs mostly unless it gets really bad. I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: Re: Botox Study I don't know whether it's brave or desperate It's funny, because my previous uro had suggested Botox and I told him no way, because I had heard so much negative stuff about it. My new doctor was highly recommended by my Physical Therapists (they treat all of the patients in the study) who I spend an hour and a half with every week, so I think I trusted her more before I even met her or found out about the study. I do thank you ladies, your kind words will give me strength IF I am dying in pain a few weeks from now...
signature: I was born with one ureta not attached to it's kidney, I've been poked and prodded for so long. I never had "private" parts. I had constant infections and took low dose preventive antibiotics into my teens. DX with IC July 1993 after seeing 3 different Uro's and finally switching Primary care doctors and affiliated hospitals. I have 2 beautiful daughters, the oldest has been in heaven since she was 14 when she was hit by a car The youngest is 20 now. She has had one UTI. She went into complete panic that she would end up like me. Now she has some idea of what the pain is like. Poor kid has been forced to know where every bathroom in Disney World is.

contents: Re: Botox Study Hie Hey proud of you, my other Uro suggested Botox but the moment he mentioned its still under trial, I wasn't sure whether I should do it but I was desperate, I haven't heard anyone with IC from Zimbabwe yet and the uro had not done here in zim so hey , waiting to here the outcome from your angle. Behind you always and thank you hugs and hugs and hugs

Re: Botox Study So...here is my follow up. I didn't really want to say too much while I was taking part in the study (because I would feel like an idiot if it turned out to be placebo, when in actuality that would have been fascinating) I thought all along that I had received Botox rather than Placebo, but I had no idea what to expect. The first 2 weeks were awesome once the pain from the injections went away. Week three a little less so, but tolerable. I had to skip seeing the doctor at the 4 week point because of Memorial Day but I would have loved to get back into PT. I was starting to get uncomfortable. At the 5 week point, I had my 3rd examination. For the study, I was asked if I thought I had Botox or Placebo. The doctor was asked as well. I think we both answered Placebo. I was so happy to start PT again. I was uncomfortable after the examination so getting right back into PT really helped. Once I started PT again I was feeling pretty good. At the 10 week point the pain suddenly came back. It just came back. Full blast. I have a friend who gets Botox injections in her face, and she knew that I was taking part in this study. She told me that as soon as she hits 2 1/2 months she can move her forehead again!~This made me pretty certain that despite my doubts I had received the Botox. The study should have ended at week 13 because of the one missed appointment but the doctor was on vacation so I had to wait another week. At week 14 I was examined before the "unveiling" I guessed Botox, the Doctor said Placebo and then changed her mind to Botox. She opened the envelope and sure enough it was Botox. I asked about the fact that it seemed to wear at at 10 weeks instead of 12 and she said that that is pretty common complaint but that they cant inject again prior to 12 weeks. Now I have to wait to see if my insurance will cover a round of injections. I'm keeping my fingers crossed because all in all it did seem to work well. I had no retention problems at all and since that was my biggest fear...

contents: I remember reading somewhere, either here or on another website while doing research on Botox, that there are certain areas of your bladder, if your doctor stays away from those areas are you less likely to have retention and the need to self cath. Anyone know what I'm talking about and know what those areas are? I have an appt on Mon, Aug 4, to talk about Botox.

My physical therapist recommended to me that I get botox in my pelvic floor muscles. She knows of one doctor who does it but it takes over 6 months to get an appointment. I've asked other doctors and they have no idea where to get it. I live in New England and my parents live in the Chicago area so those areas are most convenient to me, however I would be willing to travel if someone was really awesome at it.

Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Fedfan, I have a great doctor in Boston. Dr. Elkadry with Boston Urogynecology Associates. I just have botox in my pelvic floor muscles this Monday and she does it often for patients. That made me more comfortable because I know that she is conservative with the botox and I'm not just a "trial" case. It is important to continue PT after the botox treatment and she stresses that...those two things work hand in hand. I hope this helps!

Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Thanks for your reply--I actually was able to connect up with Dr. Elkadry on my own and am having injections in a couple weeks!! I've heard nothing but great things about her and am also gonna get PT afterwards.

Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Oh, that is great!!! She is amazing! I actually felt hopeful after seeing her for the first time. I'm feeling like I will have really good results after botox...too early to tell right now since it's only been a few days. Good luck in a couple of weeks. :-)

Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Hi all: I just signed on to get Pelvic Floor Botox (or a placebo) as part of a study that Dr Elkadry is participating in. I am going on a 2 week road trip to Florida in 2 weeks so I will not start the study until 4/28. I will have to give up my obturator internus muscle injections (that are working awesome) during the time that I am in the study (3 months). I also will have to skip PT for the 4 weeks immediately following the injections, (I guess I can have dry needling but no internal work). The things that I can't do are what I have been using as treatment and coping mechanisms right now, so I am a little bit adverse to giving them up, even for a short time. I am looking for any comments, advise, etc...I think I have pretty much read about every possible negative outcome that you can have from Botox, so I am looking for for more positive stuff...I know people are much more likely to write about the bad rather than the good, so I am aiming for some balance. Thank you in advance Cindy

All around I think it was worth it, and I also appreciated the opportunity to contribute to medical science. Dr. Elkadry is also really great. Let me know if you have any more questions.

contents: Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Hi Cia- I've now done two rounds of botox treatment (bigger dosage the second time). I was just as nervous as you are before but Dr. E is great! I did mine in the office and they wrote me a prescription for a Valium and percocet to take beforehand. (Maybe you should call them about that today, so you can get it over the weekend...?) The valium really helped with my nerves! hehe My situation is a little different because I wasn't part of a trial, so I was able to continue PT after the injections (thank goodness!!). Dr. E did evaluate me before to see how the muscles were feeling, etc. My first round I experienced no side effects...the second I experienced more. I think that is mainly due to the increase in dosage the second time. The first time was successful and I could definitely tell a difference in the muscles but it was just didn't last long enough. My second injection was on April 7th...and this week is the best I've felt in a long, long time!! I'm hopeful that it continues. I actually go back to Dr. E on Monday for a check-up. I'll be keeping you in my thoughts on Monday. :-) Keep us updated on how it goes.

Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area [QUOTE=amb46;649529 My first round I experienced no side effects...the second I experienced more. [/QUOTE] Ummm...which side effects did you have trouble with? (if you don't mind my asking) Please don't say retention. I hate retention! I get my pain meds from a pain clinic and I signed a contract saying that I wouldn't take any from anybody else. Dr E was supposed to talk to Dr V about how to handle that little problem. Also because I can't have my steriod injections while I do the study I have no appointment with the pain clinic to refill my meds. Dr E was supposed to call me after she spoke to Dr V, but she hasn't. That has me a little nervous because frankly I am a wimp when it comes to pain. Soooo... How bad was the pain during and after the injections? My tolerance is 1 percocet= I feel no pain. 2 percocet=I don't know my own name. I usually control pain with 1/4 to 1/2 percocet during flares, but I really have no idea what to expect after the botox. All the stories that I have heard are about retention or pain of injections, nothing about any day to day pain. I usually run between a 4 and an 8 on the pain scale, depending on when my last PT or injection was. Were you evaluated by your PT prior to the botox injection or did Dr E do the eval? Thanks for any help that you can give

The pain clinic is still going to take care of my prescriptions so I will be there bright and early next week when my doctor returns. Dr E gave me (hopefully) enough meds to last me until my doctor returns to the pain clinic. I guess I just sit back and wait at this point. We'll see what happens in 6 to 10 days.

contents: Re: Where to get pelvic floor botox? New England or Chicago area Hi Nomad: I am in a study so I don't know if I got Botox or Placebo, but I didn't think the injections were that bad. More like a rough day at PT. She did about 10 or 15 injections on each side, but it was really hard to distinguish what was an injection from what was just regular pressure that you would feel whenever trigger point release pressure was put on those same muscles. I go back in 2 weeks and in 4 weeks, at which point I can go back to PT. The study lasts 3 months and I guess usually the injections last anywhere from 3 months to 6 months for most people

contents: Hi, I've been considering bladder botox for a while now and finally agreed to it but I'm still really freaked out about it. I'm 25 and I've had overactive bladder/ic symptoms for over 5 years now. I've tried several medications, bladder retraining, and physiotherapy and nothing has worked. I've been reluctant to get botox because of the possibility of retention and no part of me was willing to self cath. But at this point I'm willing to take that risk if it means a reduction in my urgency and frequency which have been really severe lately. A couple weeks ago, they thought I had a uti and I was on macrobid for 7 days and cipro for 5 days but now things are worse than ever. I think the antibiotics may have actually caused an infection and I have to pee every 20 minutes and there is some burning. I was supposed to get the bladder botox last week but my urologist said to postpone it until my symptoms got better but they're not getting better. She's on vacation for the rest of the month so I don't know what to do. Would it be bad to go ahead and do the botox in the beginning of April even if my symptoms are still this bad? Or should I be requesting another round of urodynamics testing just to make sure my condition hasn't further progressed into something else. I just want relief as soon as possible and I don't want to keep putting it off, but then I've also read about people having it and things getting worse instead of better. Has anyone here received the treatment before and if so what was your result? Thanks.

Re: Bladder botox I'm sorry, I wish I could help you. I've only read about botox for ic, and you're right, often catheters are needed for a while afterwards. But if I was in enough discomfort, it would be worth it to feel better. Although I have no experience, since I've never been catheterized. I know many women do it themselves and say it isn't bad at all. I hope you get some better advice from others here. And I really hope you feel better soon.
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Bladder botox I haven't tried botox, but have seen reports that it does help in some instances, it doesn't always help, there have been instances when it temporarily made the situation worse --- however, it's important to remember that when someone has success with any specific treatment option, they are less likely to share the information than they are if they have negative results. If you decide to go for it, I hope it works well for you. I did learn to self cath many years ago and even though I haven't needed to for a long time, it's comforting to me to know I can do it if the need arises again. Warm healing thoughts, Donna

contents: Re: Bladder botox Is there anything else you could try before still? Instills, some meds or?... I feel that botox is really huge step and I am not sure it is always good idea.

Re: Bladder botox Originally posted by krist656 Is there anything else you could try before still? Instills, some meds or?... I feel that botox is really huge step and I am not sure it is always good idea. I have tried many options and my IC is not too bad most of the time. Hydrodistention is one thing that helps me, antispadmodics another, and I have had several different combinations instilled directly into my bladder. I suspect the diet helps me more than any other thing. Instillations I've had: Silver nitrate (rarely used any more), solu cortef, DMSO, heparin, all combined with a water solution. Usually they include lidocaine or Marcaine in the solution and I think Marcaine works best of the two. I self instilled heparin for several months. I had side effects from elmiron and had to stop taking it. I've actually tried quite a few things. Donna

contents: Re: Bladder botox I've tried dozens of medications over the years. I'm currently on mirabegron which doesn't seem to be doing anything. I'm also taking dmannose, uristatin, pyridium, and buscopan. Things are getting progressively worse and the last urine sample I have they said there was bacteria that's not normally in the urine and they didn't do a sensitivity test but suggested I start taking septra anyway, but my family doctor said not to take any antibiotics because whatever was present in the urine wouldn't cause the symptoms I'm having. The urgency gets so severe and constant that all I can do is take a sleeping pill and go to bed. I definitely want to do botox as soon as possible but don't know if my urologist will let me if things are this bad.

Re: Bladder botox Hi Donna I see you have had many hydrostentions as well as me. I was wondering have they always worked for you time and again. Everytime I have gotten one is usually did the trick in like a few days this time around its been 10 days and still no relief. Have you had them over again if did not help the first time. I am thinking of getting an installation first then trying again in a week or so with another hydro. Any info would be helpful jo jo Sure hop they find a real cure for this soooon we all can use it

contents: I feel like I only read negative stuff about Botox treatments. My dr suggested this if the oral meds failed (so far I failed Vesicare, Oxytrol, and had 2 weeks Toviaz and didn't like it). I am fortunate though that none of these meds caused any side effects. No Retention at all. My dr. says my bladder empties properly and that Botox should work well. And better yet I can get it for FREE! However, I get worried bc unlike the oral meds it's not irreversible! I worry about retention. She said out of hundreds of patients it's only happened 2x and they were older patients. Still concerns me. I am willing to try it since it's free but scared. I dont want to not pee just want to pee less! please anyone have positive feedback
signature: It's been 14 months since my symptoms started acting up and still no diagnosis. Even after my doctor performed a uro-dynamics test and stated, "your bladder is aware almost constantly of something going into it. It's highly sensitive and on the small side." I have been given no treatment options and it's frustrating. I failed most OAB meds and my doctor is not surprised because I don't clinically have OAB. So now I'm trying my best to be on a stricter diet, although I admit, it's hard and I do cheat slightly. I have no pain. Just a constant pressure that feels like I carry a 20lb weight on my bladder and lower abdomen. I have had ultrasounds too and all seem clear. I started LoEstrin 24 birth control in Feb 2014 and have had no period since and I don't know if this is helping or hurting. Looking for a solution to take away the pressure. If I need to urinate often then so be it. It sucks but the pressure is enough.

contents: Re: Botox and Retention? I have just had the Botox done. I was good, still in some pain for about a week. Then a couple days ago I couldn't void my bladder anymore. I thought maybe it was the meds I was on so I stopped them. I am 37 and as of today I now have to self catheter to void my bladder. I wasn't told this before my surgery, it wasn't my Dr. It was the nurse who explained the procedures and possible side effects. I saw the Dr. Today and she scanned my Bladder and I wasn't able to void 200cc. So they taught me how to cath myself. I was in so much pain before I went to dr because I was pushing so hard to go. The good news is I can go sometimes on my own and the pain is a lot better. I hate my disease and was diagnosed 2005 and at this point was willing to do anything to help. I hope in a few days or a week I won't have to cath anymore. I am new to the forum and this site. I wouldn't wish this disease on anyone. I am waiting to see how this works out. I had surgery 2 weeks ago, I just wanted to warn ppl. About side effects also want to keep ppl. With IC updated on how the Botox works out in the end. I am not willing to give up. I will keep you posted

I had surgery 2 weeks ago, I just wanted to warn ppl. About side effects also want to keep ppl. With IC updated on how the Botox works out in the end. I am not willing to give up. I will keep you posted I'm glad they taught you how to catheterize yourself. I think everyone with IC should know how --- it's never good to strain. I hope the botox will be an answer for you. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: Re: Botox and Retention? Thanks Donna, Self cathing isn't as bad as I thought it would be. I will keep posting on how the Botox process goes. Still in early stages. Hopefully everything will work out and I can be helpful to someone else. Thanks, Jaime

Hi there, I just had Botox injected in my pelvic floor muscles for PFD at the elevator ani area and after a week the bladder pain is worse than ever! The vagina is soft but now I have this bladder flare which is making me very depress... Not feeling for having sex which was the main reason for botoxing the vagina... My gyne is such a liar, he said it was impossible to get worse, the only thing that could happen was it didn't work. Just lies...I've read some Icers get relief after few weeks, but and all and all I feel this have been a completely fail. Why I did get worse? My bladder is detoxing the Botox and the chemical hurts me? I have PN instead and I should have done pudendal block? I don't know what to take for pain now. And I feel I've spoiled Christmas. I had a feeling before getting this done that something was not right, that i should run to my house.,,but I thought it was just fear. Back in time machines, anyone? Thanks for listening ladies.

contents: Hi, I have had severe IC,PFD, and Vulvodynia since I was 19, I'm 36 now which seems like an eternity. I live in NJ, and am looking for a list of doctors who do Botox for the pelvic floor in NJ. The only doctor I'm aware of is Dr Whitmore in PA, which would be a problem to get to because I would be having to drive myself, and I get lost very easily. That would mean I would have to take a cab or something which I can't afford. I never thought I would even consider Botox, but after this Thanksgiving yesterday when the pain level was an 11, and I couldn't even participate in the activities. I've just had it! If I can't get my life back to what it was before IC/PFD, then I at least want it to be a be a Better quality of life with less pain/urgency and frequency. I'm taking Lyrica, Baclofen, Hydroxyine, Clonopin, Xanax, Diazapam inserted vaginally, and Propananol. But, the effects only last for a few hours. I've tried Interstim, but had it removed because it seemed to have no effect, Myfofascial Release Trigger Point therapy with and without lidocaine injections, DMSO, Hydrodistension, CystoProtek, the IC Aloe pills, Elmiron, which also didn't seem to have any effect except hair loss, Biofeedback, and other bladder instillations which I can't remember the name of right now, but they were too painful, possibly because I hadn't yet learned how to handle having a catheter used, along with the stinging itself from the procedure. I have Medicare as I am on SSDI, I'm also trying to find out if this is covered at all, and the possible cost. Please anyone with ANY information let me know, I can be PMsg'd, I just REALLY need help and guidance because I want to make an informed decision, not an impulsivie one. Thanks.

I'm just wondering if anyone here has had botox injections into the bladder? My Uro is suggesting this but I'm a little worried I may be that 1 in 10 percent of people who may experience urine retention. Can anyone please tell their experience and give a me a little insight as to what to expect from this? I also do not like the fact I'll have to get it done every 6-8 months... My Uro quoted it would cost about $2000 for the procedure and I don't have that much money so I'm saving for this but I am still a little unsure about it. Also I'm in Vancouver Canada if it helps at all.

Re: Botox Injections Hi Melanie, I have not tried this but on the new guidelines, the have Botox quite far down the list, like towards last resorts after various things have failed like instillations, meds and tens. Have you tried all of these things first? I would definitely research it thoroughly first. If you decide to go forward, please keep us posted and I hope it goes well for you! Warm regards.
signature: Love is a fruit in season at all times, within reach of every hand. ~ The Blessed Mother Teresa Status: Diagnosed October 2006 via cystoscopy with hydrodistention. Max anesthetized bladder capacity only 250 cc's. Mast cells and pinpoint bleeding found. Remission for me means less pain for more days than not. Frequency is inevitable with a bladder this tiny! That is ok though. The difference between when I was diagnosed and now, is that I have embraced that fact. Me and the loo, we are one.

contents: Re: Botox Injections Hi ICKIRSTI, I have tried many things for the past year and a half and nothing seems to be working. I feel like I'm back at square one. My main symptoms are urgency and frequency. I have never had any pain. I had a hydrodistention done when I was first diagnosed but it did not help. I have tried installations which were working for a while but then I developed an allergy to the sodium bicarbonate in my instillation. It usually consists of Heparin, Lidocaine and Sodium Bicarbonate. I asked my Uro today if I could just get the instill without the Bicarbonate but he said the Bicarbonate helps the Lidocaine get in to the layers of the bladder better and that not having it in there would change the ph balance of the treatment making it not work as well. I did try using a catheter to drain out the instillation after an hour but still experienced a burning from the bicarbonate. The burning would last until the next day. I went on Elmiron for 8 months but developed severe diarrhea so I had to stop. I also tried Uracyst for a couple of months but it didn't do anything except burn a big hole in my bank account. But my Uro wants me to continue with the Uracyst anyways till I can afford the Botox to see if it helps. I am nervous about it because I'd rather try a different medication first. I tried to ask if I could try a few different medications I've noticed have helped other people on here but he did not want me to try them. So I guess I might have to do the Botox soon I'm not a big fan of invasive treatments but I just want to get better and not miss work any more. If I do have it done I will post on here again about the treatment and what happens! Thanks for the support the ICKIRSTI! Cheers, Mel

Re: Botox Injections Hi Melanie, So sorry to hear you are having such a rough time. I am a little concerned your doctor is not willing to try less invasive options while you are willing! I think there are a lot of other bladder instillations that could be tried and oral medicines as you had stated. Also Tens therapy and physical therapy. Inwould really considering readdressing that with him and if he does not listen perhaps seek a second opinion. The part that scares me personally with the Botox is that you risk the chance of needing to self cath for a period of time. Eek. I have posted the link below! Good Luck to you on your journey to greater health. Just know we are here for you and by all means feel better soon! IC Daily Fact #25 - Botox and the Bladder? It can help! - IC Awareness Month http://www.icawareness.org/ic-daily-fact-25-botox-and-the-bladder-it-can-help Botulinum toxin type A (BTX-A or Botox) is one of several experimental therapies currently in use for the treatment of IC, neurogenic and overactive bladder. Botox can be injected into the bladder wall or used as a bladder instillation. It is believed to block the sensory nerves in the bladder that triggers some of our

contents: Re: Botox Injections Originally posted by MelanieL I'm just wondering if anyone here has had botox injections into the bladder? My Uro is suggesting this but I'm a little worried I may be that 1 in 10 percent of people who may experience urine retention. Can anyone please tell their experience and give a me a little insight as to what to expect from this? I also do not like the fact I'll have to get it done every 6-8 months... My Uro quoted it would cost about $2000 for the procedure and I don't have that much money so I'm saving for this but I am still a little unsure about it. Also I'm in Vancouver Canada if it helps at all. My doctor talked about this today. Of course she said this would be a later option. I am not diagnosed w/ IC she still says it's OAB. I have to have a cystoscopy and urodynamics in January and at this point 2 OAB meds have failed and she is trying me on TOVIAZ she said next would be mybetriq and then after cystoscopy/urodynamics based on those results we can talk about BOTOX. I will be honest it scares me. At least with OAB oral meds if something goes wrong you stop taking them. With Botox you can't reverse it. When I mentioned retention she said in all her patients its only happened once. ANd the people that are more susceptible to it happening are those with really bad bladders or people with neurological disorders. She said I'm a good candidate and bc they are doing a trial study at their office I could get it for free. But again she said, this is a bit aways. She also mentioned insterim? But I also find this invasive. Yes some days my frequency is 30 min - 1hour but there are days I get by on 2 hours and I sleep pretty much through the night. I don't know I just think this is excessive for someone like me so I don't plan on going down this road unless my frequency becomes so out of control. I like my doctor but I do want IC considered. Last time she said that since I didn't experience any pelvic pain it couldn't be IC. Just so much to think about.
signature: It's been 14 months since my symptoms started acting up and still no diagnosis. Even after my doctor performed a uro-dynamics test and stated, "your bladder is aware almost constantly of something going into it. It's highly sensitive and on the small side." I have been given no treatment options and it's frustrating. I failed most OAB meds and my doctor is not surprised because I don't clinically have OAB. So now I'm trying my best to be on a stricter diet, although I admit, it's hard and I do cheat slightly. I have no pain. Just a constant pressure that feels like I carry a 20lb weight on my bladder and lower abdomen. I have had ultrasounds too and all seem clear. I started LoEstrin 24 birth control in Feb 2014 and have had no period since and I don't know if this is helping or hurting. Looking for a solution to take away the pressure. If I need to urinate often then so be it. It sucks but the pressure is enough.

contents: I suggest you read all you can about it. A Dr suggested doing to me, I already can't pee, I have severe urinary retention, she told I would have to wear a catheter for about a month, then they try to see if I could pee on my own. When I pressed her for info she couldn't give it and admitted she had only read about it. I ran away from her very quickly. Just because it was not right for doesn't mean it is not right for you, just make an informed decision and find out if your Dr actually has expirence doing it, and what to expect afterwards.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: I've read not to have it if you don't fully empty your bladder. As much as I hate it, I think I'd want a urodynamics done just to make sure that I'm emptying my bladder. When you go every 15 min, it's hard to tell if I hadn't emptied my bladder or if it's irritation from new urine entering the bladder.

I did have a urodynamics test, but they can tell if you are emptying completely with an ultrasound. You just go in with a full bladder, it doesn't have to be over full like when your preg. They use the ultrasound to measure how much urine you have in your bladder, you go pee and they measure how much urine is left in the bladder. If you have quite a bit left in the bladder they can get you to go again and measure it again. This test is a much easier test on you and your bladder, you my need a urodynamics test down the road to figure out why your bladder doesn't empty, but if all you need to know is if you empty the ultrasound is much easier. Lots of uro offices have a hand held ultrasound to do this with.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Thanks for the info on the ultrasound. I'm actually scheduled for one tomorrow. I'm having another Hydrodistention next week. I had such a great response to one in Feb. I was symptom free for at least 2 months and then things started up again very gradually. I'm now to the point if I drink anything, I'm in the bathroom every 10-15 min. I've gone over 40+ times a day. My uro keeps warning me you get diminishing results with each hydro. So, if I don't get enough relief from the hydro, he's suggesting botox. I want to do it by the end of the year since I have a $5000 deductible. I guess he's checking if I completely empty, just in case. (I'm already dreading the full bladder part of it.)

I just had Botox injections in my pelvic floor about a month ago, and it made my symptoms 10x worse. I go to the bathroom 20-30x day (I didn't have bad frequency before). My pain level went from a 7-8 daily pain to a 9-11 daily pain. I'm having to go to pain management now. A lady in my IC support group said she had the same procedure, and she was in complete agony until the Botox wore off in 9 months. Sorry to be a downer, but that's what I'm currently experiencing with Botox. Worst decision of my life.

I was told 10 to 20% of patients experience retention and must self-cath to pee for the first month or two. Since I am extremely sensitive to catheters (just one will flare me for a month) we decided botox would just not be an option for me. I understand the deductible situation.. ours is almost $6000. If it were me I would make sure the dr. is honest with the risks and has done a lot of them.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: Re: Botox Linda, I would ask your doc how many of these he or she has done, and what the outcomes have been. My uro suggested this to me last year if my round of instills didn't help. They have done many of these procedures. I'd seen a lot of comments about retention after the procedure, and I get retention from a lot of meds, so it was a concern. If I recall correctly, she said if the Botox is put in certain areas in particular, retention should not be a problem. She told me that retention will not occur if they avoid certain places in the bladder. (I wish I could remember the details, but I can't.) I know there are a lot of people who have been helped by the Botox, so it may still be an option. I would just recommend doing all your homework before making a decision.

Hi all, So I had 100 IU of Botox injected into my trigone six days ago. Theoretically, doing the trigone only (and not the detrusor muscle) drastically reduces the risk of retention...and now here I am with retention problems just starting. Yesterday I had 175cc residual in the morning & 120cc in the evening. This morning it felt even harder to go myself and I had 175cc residual again. So, my questions to those of you who experienced issues with retention after a Botox treatment: 1. Did it get worse once it started coming on? 2. Did it get better with time or did you have to wait until the Botox completely wore off? 3. How long did it take for the Botox to wear off? 4. If you had to self-cath, how did you deal with the constant irritation to your urethra? I am really freaked out right now. I haven't gotten any positives that I can tell yet, and while self-cathing is an easy for me to do technically - physically it is a nightmare and makes everything worse. Just hoping I can learn from how others dealt with this. Thanks so much for anything anyone can share. Addy (who is really wishing she had tried an interstim trial before this!!!) Last edited by adechka; 02-20-2013, 03:22 AM.
signature: Addy Symptom onset: 9/2010 Diagnosed: 11/2010 via urodynamics, voiding diary and symptoms Current Treatments: Botox to bladder trigone (not going well at the moment - retention issues with minimal to no symtomatic relief as of yet) Biofeedback IC Diet + gluten free Physical Therapy (since January 2011) Valium suppositories Xanax Tried & failed: tricyclics (severe retention), atarax (retention), gabapentin, Cymbalta, Lyrica, Trileptal, Topomax, Lamictal, Keppra, Vicodin, Methadone, hydrodistention (waited over a year before willing to try this), elimiron/lido/sb instills, Whitmore cocktail instills, Uracyst instills, PTNS, acupuncture, electroacupuncture, low-dose naltrexone, nerve blocks (impar ganglion, superior hypogastric plexus, left sided S2-S4, caudal), Botox to pelvic floor

contents: H Addy! Sorry to read about your situation. I'm in the opposite boat- looking to try botox, already did an interstim trial. The interstim trial was more painful and horrific than my fully-awake, non-anesthetized cystoscopy which only lasted about five minutes. The interstim trial procedure lasted two hours, was the most excruciatingly painful thing I've ever experienced, AND they were unable to even locate my bladder nerve. Please keep us updated on how you're doing with the botox, and I really hope someone who knows something responds to you! Sorry I couldn't help.
signature: IC symptoms since Dec. 12 2010 after confirmed bladder infection "never went away." Recently also diagnosed with PCOS, piriformis syndrome, and pelvic floor dysfunction, sinus issues. The doctor decided to do it with IV sedation instead of general anesthesia. I woke up during the procedure, I couldn't open my eyes or talk. The doctor knew I was feeling it and told me to relax. The pain was unbearable. My doctor felt terrible that this happened and promised it would never happen again. It was very traumatic for me. lisa

I still think there is great hope for each individual finding a path to healing and there are many.

contents: Originally posted by purpleviolet My uro told me sometimes she does botox only in pelvic floor muscles and not bladder if she thinks it is better. (avoids retention problem). Anyone consider this? Yes. Although I had both done and regret it. The bladder botox caused retention (almost 3 months now, although it's starting to wear off), whereas the pelvic floor botox relaxed my pelvic floor and provided 2-3 month of incredible relief (finally)! If/when I repeat botox, I will only be doing pelvic floor muscles and not the bladder again. Too bad bladder botox is FDA approved and pelvic floor botox is still considered experimental... left me with a huge hospital bill

I have had IC for 3 years. I deperately need to be a part of a group but I know NO ONE else with this disease. I have a strict diet, I take Celexa 20 mg, Elmiron 5 mg, prelief and uribel....all just to be able to get by but it doesnt prevent flares or make the urgency go away. I get bladder instillations monthly and some treatments I am not lie in bed for 24 hours in severe pain and then I can begin to feel better. I havent had an alcoholic beverage IN YEARS and I really miss my cranberry juice. What can anyone tell me about botox for my IC? Please let me know and if anyone wants to email me I will grateful love for you to subject line ICN [email protected]/
Hi. I am supposed to get botox for my OAB . 100 units. I learned to self cath in case of retention. Can I have anyone's stories positive or negative please. Thank u Cherie

Hi Cherie, I had botox done a few years back for my urethral syndrome and I.C. I didn't find it helped me. It didn't make any difference at all. The first couple of days I felt very irritated and uncomfortable as I have now come to expect after having a procedure carried out. I really hope it works for you though! Maybe it is more effective for OAB. In fact, I'd imagine it will be since it works by freezing the schincter muscle which is much more obviously involved in OAB than it is in my conditions...Good luck! Jem x

I only have problems with burning during flares, but do have pelvic pain. My biggest problem is urgency, and I am getting up several times per night. My uro suggested having botox, and since I'm in Canada where it is free and 10 minutes from a hospital that can do it, I am really interested. For those of you who have had it, did it really help with urgency and frequency? I really want to I am so tired of the feeling of having to pee tiny little bits bits 5-6 times per night and 10+ times per day!!!!

I am getting ready to start 10mg of vaginal valium suppositories and I have 2 questions, are you allowed to drink alcohol while on them or is it the same rule as with regular valium not to drink while on them can you use them as needed , do they cause withdrawel symptoms like regular valium I've was on another benzo drug orally for a month or so a long time ago and had horrible withdrawel symptoms so I want to be careful with this one I wasn't sure how much of the valium gets absorbed into the rest of your body Also my Dr gave me 5mg and I'm supposed to use 2 of them, do you think all that base will cause me a yeast infection , should I get them re-made into 1 suppository Thanks:-) Last edited by amesg31; 12-02-2009, 11:30 AM. Reason: wrong word
Check with your doctor about the drinking & vaginal valium, & how he wants you to take them. Regardless, alcohol is a big no-no for IC patients.

About withdrawal - I do not have this problem. I do not use them while I am on my period and I have no issues. Sometimes I forget too and don't have any symptoms. Also - if you are worried about taking two you could try to start with one. I was supposed to take 2 as well, but I find that 1 helps to releave my symptoms for 24 hours. If I take two, it's too much and just leaks back out. Of course, check with your doctor too, but just thought I'd chime in to give you some suggestions/ease your worries.
signature: Have had IC symptoms since childhood, but did not seek serious treatment or become diagnosed until I was in college. 24 yo F I've had a cystectomy with an Indiana Pouch. It was a success and has helped my symptoms and pain immensely.

contents: valium suppositories Hi Just saw my gyne who suspects with 95% certainty that I have IC, he botoxed my PC Muscle, prescribed valium suppositories (10mg) he said that 70% of the suppository goes right to the muscle vs I think he said 4% for the oral valium. I also was given elmiron. But I would not drink alcohol if u sincerely want to try and give ur bladder a rest- this with coffee I think are the worst culprits.

hi everyone, my doc just gave me actual valium pills (that are usually taken by mouth) and said to use them intravaginally. however, i've noticed that they don't disintegrate up there. does anyone have any ideas? were you guys given actual suppositiories? take care, kit

Mine faxed a script to a pharmacy he uses and they mailed me the actual suppositories on ice to my house. I don't know where you are, but if you cannot find a pharmacy to use I think the one I used ships all over. Let me know.

You can break up the pills. or crush them and mix with a lub you can use, you can then put in a syring with needle removed and insert. I find if I break the pill into 1/4 and insert the bits, then I us a replends a vaginal lub it helps the pill to disovle. I also ussally lie down for awhile to give the pills a chance before gravity pulls it out. Good luck this is a bit of trial and error to find what will work, you could search it on this site as I remember other's dealing with this.MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: I have just started using the suppositories that have been compounded with valium, and a few other ingrediants. My pharmacist spoke directly to my doctor, and together they decided on what they would consist of. So far they are doing a great job in controlling the bad vaginal burning I've had for three years. I also have been doing the stretches my physical therapist wants me to do at home, and together with the valium I feel like I'm doing so much better.

I take 10mg of valium orally, before sex, to relax the pelvic floor muscles. Without it, it is impossible, the muscles are so tight. Oral valium also helps with sex related anxiety. I think it is more a subconscious anxiety becuase of fear of pain, flares, utis, fissures, all related to sex. How to get rid of this is another subject. Still, I won't give up, and valium has helped me a lot. Vaginal dilators have helped me tremendously. I want to try valium vaginally but wasn't sure if it'll irritate my V V. If I do it, I'll crush the pill and mix it with a lube, or maybe put the powder on an empty clear gelating or veg capsule. Carol5555: I have a few questions for you: Did you have a Hydrodistantion or have to go under general anesthesia to have your pelvic floor botoxed? I alsways wanted to to this but doctors want to botox my blader and I think it is not necessary! Please share you experience with it. I will very much appreciate it! Thanks for the info on oral valium only being absorved 4% in the PC muscles....
signature: Laser cervical surgery after abnormal pap (moderate dysplasia) in 3/2004 UTIs since mid 2004 Recurrent yeast infections since mid 2005 IC dx 3/2006 V V dx 12/2006 Other conditions: IBS, allergies, dry eyes. Current IC medications: Elavil (since 12/06) 10 mg; Cysta Q (since 5/07) Rephresh acid jel every 3 days D-mannose, fish oil, Calcium Citrate, Multivitamins & garlic pills (sometimes) Probiotics Femdophilus or other brands Gy-Na-Tren for yeast infections (started using it as needed since 2010) Other Rx:Omeprazole 20mg; benadryl as needed, Restasis Past Meds and why I stopped them: Elmiron: March to September 2006: headaches Lyrica:2 weeks in 2006: headaches Cystoprotek: from Jan to April 2007: nausea Femcon Fe (2/07 to 10/07):caused spotting/bleeding BCP:Loestrin (since Ocotber 2007) to stop spotting for 2 months. V V worse. Stopped the pill in Nov 07. "Obstacles don't have to stop you. If you run into a wall, don't turn around and give up. Figure out how to climb it, go through it, or work around it"

contents: Hi No the botox was no big deal at all. I had myself scared to death about putting a needle up my vagina, but the pain was so minimal, atleast for me~it seems like there are varied results. Its been almost a month now, and I gotta tell ya my pain is still the same. I notice better days when I watch my diet. Do you have vulvar pain (vestibule)? He botoxed just my pc muscle because he felt it was referring pain to that area, but the pain is still there and Im out $1500. I guess with this stuff, ya try about everything- but for me personally I wish I would have gone to a warm, sunny beach with the $$ I would have felt better atleast!

I know how it feels to spend tons of money in things that do not work. But you did what you thought it was best for your health.... Sorry the botox didn't help...or maybe it didn't kick in yet? I have a friend that does it twice a year for few years already with gret success but if I'm not mistaken, it takes a few weeks to kick in and start to feel better. If your doctor is giving you valium, suppositories I assume you have tight or hipertonic pelvic floor muscles, or PFD. Are your muscles at least more relaxed after the botox??? Thanks for you feedback and I hope you feel better!!

contents: I just called my URO and asked if she could put me on this. Having sex with my husband just kills my bladder and I have sever IC FLARES. So has anybody that uses this see any diff. while having sex? DAIMIC65

You should give it try! Valium makes a big difference on pain during sex. I take 10 mg orally. I would suggest for you to perhaps try 5mg first 45 min before sex ( yes, you have to time it) if you dont wait 45 min you'll hurt bc the drug is not yet in your system. You dont want to wait too much either. Try a small amount first, see how you react to the medicine. Also bc you dont know how youll react to it and dont want to fall sleep! It can make you drowsy, but it takes the pre-sex anxiety and the pain during sex. I may try valium vaginally at some point just having a lot of yeast issues and dont want to mess things more. Talk to your doctor. Theres hope. See vaginal dilators too, they helped me so much!
