contents: well it was kind of backward because, he discovered he thought I had IC and referred me to an IC specialist. Almost certain I have the IC, had the urodynamics test done- OMG how awful, thought I was going to die it was so painful, has anyone had this test!! The cathetar was awful Catheters are way worse than botox! Anyway, it might take more time, but my bladder being problematic probably needs addressed before the benefit of botox would kick in I think, not sure what his reasoning was, but I would have maybe waited until I got some treatment for IC. The elmiron gives me headaches too

i know this thread is old, but if anyone reads my reply to this, please try to advise. i was just prescribed the Valium suppositories last week, and tried them yesterday. the first one didn't seem too bad, but by the second and third one, when i woke up early am, the base mixture of the suppository left my vaginal area very irritated and sore. The uro said i can use rectally instead, that it would help the whole area down there, just the same. I tried it rectally this a.m. seems better this way, but when i inserted it, less than 10 minutes i felt extreme urge to evacuate, and had to go. so the suppository wasn't in there very long. My question is, how long does the suppository need to remain in the rectum, to get the full benefit from it. Also i have been reading from some women that have used it vaginally have reported getting a yeast infection from it. Has anyone here experienced this as well. Thanks in advance to any advice anyone can offer.
signature: Dx 2005. Worsened w/preg in 94. Voiding 100 x. DMSO in 04= 7 weeks, worsened. Saw urogyno 05. Started heparin, kenalog lidocaine, helped a few hours. Referred to uro who implanted stim in 06. Trial great, w/99% improvement. Not seen same w/perm implant, leads were placed in rt. labia which caused me pain to set on therapeutic level. Leads were placed in butt w/trial stim, & not painful to turn up. Having revised 11-19, done while awake, so can ask where I feel pulses, so can be placed in butt rather than labia. W/revision, should be able to turn high & see therapeutic effect. Still need to see gyno for PFD, have terrible pain w/orgasm. On Valium supps. Helps some. IC & PFD make sex painful, hopefully stim & gyno will help. Previous meds: Urised= off market, Elmiron= caused gastro problems. OAB meds= retention. Hyoscomine- not help. Azo= makes worse. Current meds: Elival 50 mgs at night, helps w/nocturia. Loratab=pain Prozac= anxiety. Zocor=cholesterol, Fioricet= Migraines. Zantac=acid reflux. Trazadone=insomnia. Zofran&Phenergan=nausea. Atarax =pain relief. Diflucan=yeast infections. Supplements=Magnesium=migraines, Marshmallow root, not sure helping or not, but not hurting anything. Enzymes= digestion. Melatonin=insomnia. Stool softeners & Miralax =constipation. Acidophilius =gut. Gas pills. Bio-freeze=muscles. Prelief= bladder Chewable vitamin. Tylenol=pain. Rarely ibuprofen= intolerant to nsaids .On elimination diet since Aug Not seen help w/bladder, but w/gut. Good effect w/diet is have lost 30 lbs.

contents: Hi Sheryl ~ I have been using them for about two years now. I have never had a yeast infection from them. What exactly are they compounding the valium with? Sometimes the other ingredients can cause problems. Mine are Valium 10mg and Baclofen 2mg. Originally they had included Lidocaine. I found the Lidocaine was irritating me so they discontinued that. I know that using them rectally would cause me to have the same reaction that you had and I've never heard of them being used that way.

Thanks Dakota. She had prescribed them to use either vaginally or rectally, telling me that either way, would accomplish the same effect. After trying a couple more times yesterday, i have been able to have the suppository remain at least 20 minutes, so hoping I am getting the full benefit from it. It does seem to be helping some, I was able to go a whole hour yesterday with out urinating, which is a whole lot for me, I usually go to the bathroom every 6-10 minutes because the urgency and freq are so bad for me. It also seems to be helping a little with the pelvic pain. I do not know exactly what the base of the compound is. I have to go pick up the suppositories they owe me this afternoon, the were suppose to give me 90, but only had enough molds to make 26 of them. I will ask her then what the base of it is. Mine are 5mgs, to use 3-5 times a day, i have been using 3x so far. Thanks again for replying, have a great day

contents: Hi all, I see this thread is from a while ago but- I tried valium suppositories. I think they were somewhat helpful but I couldn't work out a good system. The compounded suppositories were five dollars each for me out of pocket, and I can't afford that, not even for one a day. I then tried baclofen suppositories from the same compounding pharmacy, and for whatever reason, they were not dissolving at all and they dried out my vagina and I had to stop. Then I tried using the plain pills and they didn't seem to absorb either (pink pills caused pink vaginal discharge, blech) and while I thought this was pretty easy, they started to irritate me and before two weeks caused what seemed to be a yeast infection, so I had to give up. :-( I have no idea what I could do differently to either get the actual suppositories to be cheaper or to get pills that dont' irritate me? I guess I could try rectally but I'm afraid that it wouldn't be close enough to my bladder to help, and I really need it to affect the bladder.
signature: IC symptoms since Dec. 12 2010 after confirmed bladder infection "never went away." Recently also diagnosed with PCOS, piriformis syndrome, and pelvic floor dysfunction, sinus issues. I was in great health before fall 2010. Current medications: citalopram 40 mg, valium 2.5 mgs a night, lidocaine patch (for back pain), Prelief Therapies tried or continuing: stretches, walks, baths, internal massage, physical therapy, IC diet, IF unit, heating pad, mindfulness based stress reduction classes, cognitive therapy, books on IC and pelvic pain... Drugs Tried: the urinary analgesics, 4 kinds of muscle relaxants (including in suppository form), hydroxyzine, 3 kinds of TCA's, lyrica, cymbalta, 6 months elmiron, vesicare, antibiotics, prednisone (after sinus surgery), NSAIDS... Supplements: over 20 herbs and supplements, including regimens from TCM practitioner and a naturopathic MD Intravesical/Surgical: 3 instills of Uracyst, 2 “rescue instills” lidocaine, bicarb, elmiron; 1 self-instill of marcaine, a steroid, bicarb and heparin; 1 hydrodistention and capsaicin instill (made me worse, still recovering two months later...) Other: Acupuncture treatments, magnets (LOL), Anti-Candida Diet (2 months), Wheat free diet, water pH

contents: i'm not using them anymore because they don't help me, but I did have the problem with them dissolving. however, there are different brands, so I would call around and ask different pharmacies what color their valium is. later someone else gave me them to take orally which also didn't work, but when I got it filled, it hink it was green. so that's what I would do. In order to get t hem to absorb better I also crushed them and put them with ky jelly or other things, but getting the one that just absorbed was much easier. I think it was green, but since it didn't work, I can't remember. I hope this helps. there are different generic brands. good luck

With botox. I thought I would share my story. After 10 years of being recommended to "try it" with many times told insurance wouldn't cover it, it doesn't help for pain, blah blah blah. So finally, at the end of my IC treatment, with my new MD we did a hydrodistension, some botox and a s/p cath to divert the urine since my urethra and I are not friends. Plus my last Uro I had before I moved suddenly cross country in Sept and I were going to try it. It sucks. I know people can have to cath but for me.....what happened was they put a small s/p tube in which got all blocked and came out some 3 days later. By then the botox is kicking in and I am peeing less and self cath more with lower self void volumes and more residual. I went to the ER and had a tiny little cath place and my god it gave me bladder spasms for the full 3 weeks I had it before they could replace the s/p tube. With my botox now a few mo old one woudl think it was wearing off. Not so fast. If I try to sit on the toilet no urine comes out (s/p cath) but my urethra opens and it spasms, then I might have a bowel movment. If I cap the s/p cath I last my usual hour and oh, have to pee. I can pee a tiny bit out of my urethra which hurts but then I drain the rest via s/p cath which causes spasm to. I know these were done under odd circumstances and why on earth I did it with the s/p cath is NO clue to me except I figured try and they were already in the OR doing a hydrodistension so why not. I will say b/c it was with another procedure it was covered. Happy to talk about it more. Melanie
signature: I am blessed to have an Indiana Pouch now IC since childhood, finally diagnosed at 31 yo PFD/Pelvic Pain,SI joint and LBP Low Back Pain s/p spinal fusion at 19 with more nastiness in back Hydrodistension 2001, 2005, 2011 w capsacian, 2011 with botox/ super pubic tube Interstim trial and removel 2002,C-Section 2007 and 2010, Post Partum Depression, post partum hypertension, Idopathic hypothalmic amenorrhea Radical Cystectomy with Indiana Pouch 2/3/12--->multiple complications and revision of ureters in pouch due to a giant ball of scar tissue in 8/12, occasional pylo, L kidney small and scarred, low flow Hysterectomy 2/3/12 w one ovary removed Dry Eyes L radical nephrectomy 1//3/14 after a long "what if " the conversation, kidney working 4 %, path said full of absesses Still have issues, still take meds. I have worked some, been doing pilates, just got a post masters in nursing, learning to pace myself. Learning things I can't do, learning things that I need to take a LONG time to master and regain myself, but mostly just so happy to be bladder free.:

contents: My Botox Experience I like reading others' experiences with botox. Mine has been fairly positive. I had it done in June and I definitely have less pain and discomfort and frequency. She didn't inject my urethra with botox so it still spasms and is painful. She felt if she injected my urethra i would be incontinent. The only thing negative i've experienced post botox was constipation. I just had a colonoscopy, and an intravaginal ultrasound. The colonoscopy went fine and nothing was found (thank goddess) but the ultrasound was EXTREMELY painful and uncomfortable. I have a feeling my uterus is tilted onto my colon and is causing the constipation. I have also felt more pressure in my pelvic area similar to my old IC symptoms --but different. I have not received the results of the ultrasound yet and probably won't until wednesday. i can't help but think there must be a connection between the botox in my bladder wall and my uterus changing position...but we'll see. Anyone else had this experience? Thanks Nyeema

Botox - My Experience I had botox injections in my bladder a week ago. I had it done under general anesthesia. Very easy experience, no residual pain or problems and I was easily able to pee afterwards. It's been a week now and I've noticed that the urgency has gone away, I am going less often (even when drinking coffee), and what used to feel like pins poking my bladder is now a dull discomfort. I can "hold it" for awhile and am trying to do some bladder training to re-stretch my bladder so that (maybe) I can go for much longer periods in between. I can still easily pee without straining (no retention). I've heard that things can continue to improve for up to two weeks and that the botox doesn't begin to wear off until after 90 days. I guess we'll see. At this point I will do it again with no hesitation. This has been wonderful for me!
thanks deedle for sharing!!

contents: I have had one round of botox injections to my pelvic floor muscles, which have helped with my progress in PT, but did not do much for my symptoms. I am now considering trying botox to the bladder. From what I have read people have pretty mixed results, but it does sometimes seem to desensitize the nerves? As that is my main issue - the chronic sensation of needing to go, I was wondering if anyone has had luck with that particular symptom? I have concerns about the retention issues it can cause (I seem to be highly sensitive to that side effect from anything), but truthfully I would rather cath myself regularly if it meant I could stop feeling this sensation all the time! It seems like the lesser of two evils. At this point I have tried so much, I feel like it is this or interstim, and it seems better to try this first as it is less invasive and somewhat lower risk. I haven't talked to my dr yet, but will be seeing her in two weeks, and want to go in with some sense of what I want to discuss with her and make sure I ask all the right questions. Anyway, any experiences, good or bad, would be welcome. Also, if anyone has any experience with botox to the superficial pelvic floor muscles (bulbospongiosus, ischiocavernosus, or tranverse perineal), I would love to hear about that. I had only the deeper muscles botoxed, but I have left sided nerve issues that seem to be localized in the peripheral branches of the pudendal nerve that innervate those muscles. I know from reading online that some people have gotten those muscles botoxed, but have not yet asked if they do it where I am seen. So again, any experiences would be welcome. Thanks,
signature: Addy Symptom onset: 9/2010 Diagnosed: 11/2010 via urodynamics, voiding diary and symptoms Current Treatments: Botox to bladder trigone (not going well at the moment - retention issues with minimal to no symtomatic relief as of yet) Biofeedback IC Diet + gluten free Physical Therapy (since January 2011) Valium suppositories Xanax Tried & failed: tricyclics (severe retention), atarax (retention), gabapentin, Cymbalta, Lyrica, Trileptal, Topomax, Lamictal, Keppra, Vicodin, Methadone, hydrodistention (waited over a year before willing to try this), elimiron/lido/sb instills, Whitmore cocktail instills, Uracyst instills, PTNS, acupuncture, electroacupuncture, low-dose naltrexone, nerve blocks (impar ganglion, superior hypogastric plexus, left sided S2-S4, caudal), Botox to pelvic floor

contents: I had botox to the bladder approximately five (5) weeks ago and could not feel better. I have been five (5) weeks now without any major bladder symptoms at all; I had pain, frequency and urgency. This has been absolutely amazing!!!! I do have to self-cath each night to make sure my bladder is completely empty but that's no big deal; I had to self-cath a few times a day during week 3 when the Botox came into full effect. Just don't need urine staying in the bladder too long and now just cathing to ensure there isn't any there. I am sure it's different for everyone but I will do this again in 6-9 months. Oh, and after surgery I was more sore than a cysto but not much. With a cysto I feel bad for a day or two and with botox it was more like four or five but mostly just very tender.

I was wondering Addy about the Botox to the pelvic floor muscles, this is what I am looking for more then for the bladder. Because in all honesty, my PFD is WAY worse right now and causing more issues. It's like the bad kid, or the bully always picking on the other kids on the block, so to speak. 8 times out of 10 my left side is the horrid side when I get my TPI's & PN Blocks, and the other 2 times my right side is as bad or almost as bad. I was hoping the InterStim would help calm my bladder so that my PFD could calm down. But the muscles just won't budge. They're frozen to the point that most days I walk with a cane, because my left side is so bad and so painful, and I live n the mountains, full of hills, and going up hill--- ouch. Did the Botox injections help relieve the muscle issues for you at all? Did they calm down and relax any? I'm just looking for anything that could help.

Did they tell you the effects could last 6-9 mons I was told 3 mons. I also used to get botox for migraines and it was every 3 mons. I did not do the bladder botox as the uro who wanted to do it when I questioned her about it admitted she had only read about it and I didn't want to be her guinea pig, bearing this in mind when she told me 3 mons she could have been way off base as she obviously didn't km=now much about it. I for one would like to know how long it lasts for you. thanks MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Botox I had Botox in the pelvic muscles in April and I find it has really helped. Just make sure you find a dr that is very experienced in doing this. I have gone out to NY to have this done. I have also had pudendal nerve blocks a few times and find that helps so much with pelvic and helpingback pain for me. Its two and half months already and I still have lasting Botox I hope it lasts longer then 3 months. I am really afraid to do the botox in the bladder I do not want to catherized myself I would rather do bladder installs and Hydro stention at this point. Accupt can also help settle things down I find. Thanks Jo Jo

I've had Botox in the bladder several times in different doses along with injections to the pelvic floor, I must admit that it was helpful at the time when my body was responding well to it. But like all good things, it lost some of its effect, but I don't regret going through with it because when it worked it worked pretty good.
Hi JoJo, would you mind sharing who did your injections in NY? I Minteresyed in trying this but only with a very experienced dr. Thanks, Claudia

botox Originally posted by Claudia1 Hi JoJo, would you mind sharing who did your injections in NY? I Minteresyed in trying this but only with a very experienced dr. Thanks, Claudia Hi Claudia, Sohoobgyn both Dr Dena Harris and Debra Cody . Dr Coady wrote the book "healing sexual pain" they are both terrific Dr Harris has helped me so much. One thing they do not take insurance you have to pay upfront and then you can send to your insurance company if you have out of network benefits. Well worth it. Good luck Jo Jo

I am at the end of my rope with my IC. The only things I have not tried are botox, cyclosporin and interstim. I wont do the cyclosporin after my dog got cancer from 6 months of the stuff (he was only 3 years old). My urologist does botox in the bladder, but says at least 30% of patients will have to cath themselves. And since I am the queen of side effects, I know I will be cathing. So it looks like interstim is next.... and my biggest symptom is pain, which interstim is not really for. But I cant find any doctor that will recommend anything else.
signature: I have basically done every treatment for IC except cyclosporine and botox. Everything has failed. I live on pain meds all day. My diet is so strict that I now have multiple vitamin deficiencies. I have also tried numerous alternative therapies. I would do a cystectomy, but doctors seem to think living in pain is better. Currently taking Synthroid, Elmiron, Percocet, Toradol, Desert Harvest aloe. Interstim done 4 times, what a waste. Also newly diagnosed with Hunners Ulcers on cysto. Now disabled due to IC. A body of life, a bladder of hell.

contents: Sorrel- I see this was from awhile ago. Did you have an interstim trial? I had one and they couldn't find my bladder nerve. No one even tells you that's a possibility. It took two hours and was excruciatingly painful. :-( It was a traumatic experience that I would avoid at all costs.

contents: I have been dealing with urinary frequency and mild pain / irritation in the urethra and perineal area for two years. The only thing that has every been found to be wrong with me is tight pelvic floor muscles. I have been trying physical therapy and medications for the last six months with no improvement at all. My pelvic floor muscles remain tight. My urologist has suggested Botox in the bladder as an option. I don't think it will help because I don't have urgency. The feeling of needing to urinate is near constant, but it is never sudden and strong. I don't really think Botox to the bladder will help and I am terrified of having to self cath. I asked the urologist about botox to the pelvic floor muscles, and he just dismissed it and said it doesn't work. I would still like to try the Botox to the pelvic floor muscles. I figure that the worst that can happen is that it doesn't work either. Can someone recommend a doctor in the Cleveland area (or anywhere in OH) that does Botox to the pelvic floor for men? I read that a doctor Barber at the Cleveland clinic does this for women (he is an OB/GYN).

I just had Botox done to my bladder last week. It was the small dose of 100 units (instead of the standard 200) because I had heard a lot of things about retention if you do 200 units. After a few days it got harder and harder to use the restroom. Thankfully I dont have retention, but I was wondering how long it takes until it becomes better? Do I have to wait 4-6 months for the botox to completely wear off before I have normal control of my bladder again or have other people felt better after a few weeks? If anyone can provide any feedback, I would greatly appreciate it. Not knowing what is happening to your body is scary.

Hi, I don't have IC, but I do have alot of urinary frequency. I don't have leakage though. I had 100 units of Botox in my bladder 4 days ago. My uro wanted to do 200 units, but I thought starting out with a smaller dose would be better because of so many people talking about retention issues. He also told me that I would notice a difference the next day. Well, I didn't. It is now day 4 and I am no better or worse. I called the uro's office and the nurse said that she would pass along the message to the uro to see if he wanted to add more units. For those of you who have had Botox, can you tell me please how many units you had and when it started working? I've read that it can take a few days to a week or even more, but my uro was sure that I would have noticed a difference by now. I'm thinking I should have let him do the 200 units to begin with. Any info would be appreciated. Thanks! Paula

Ok so, I didn't get any response to my thread about Botox. I went to my urologist's office again last Friday and had an additional 100 units of Botox (200 units total) since the 100 units he did a week prior did nothing. The procedure was ALOT more painful this time around. I was literally screaming and crying. My doctor didn't anything to console me and just left the room after it was over. I don't know if he just didn't know what to say or he didn't know what to do with me crying. Anyways, I have had no relief from my constant urge to urinate. Yesterday and today, I have had to strain alittle bit to urinate and the flow kept stopping and restarting. I hope I'm not going into retention! I don't know if I'm starting to get an infection or not. Like I said, I still have the constant feeling of needing to urinate, but there is no pain that would indicate a possible UTI. Maybe the feeling is from the straining to urinate....? I called the nurse and she prescribed some Cipro for me to take just in case I was getting an infection. I'd rather be safe than sorry. Has anyone else had experience with Botox?
So many people talk about botox. But is it really safe to have something like this? I saw so many girls with puffing cheeks and getting younger day by day. Is it really that safe?

contents: Botox in the pelvic area is great do it every three months. Helps so much for PVD don't know about bladder.

I haven't had Botox but talked to a girl who did. It took 4 weeks for her to work. It is helping with her frequency, urgency, and pain. Have you tried OAB meds or Interstim? (PS: It helped the girl for 13 months!)

contents: Originally posted by KristinKathryn Feeling any better yet? Kristin, The Botox did nothing for me. I got your inbox message on Friday and I replied. I'm not showing it in my Sent folder, so I'm guessing it didn't go through. I go back to PT on Wednesday and Friday of this week. Last Thursday was my first day. My PT told me to do Kegels (10 sets, 2 to 3 times a day) as homework. Well, I did some Thursday night and twice Friday. They made me worse, so I didn't do any for the rest of the weekend. I've been reading about them online and apparently there is conflicting information. Some PTs have you do them for a tight pelvic floor and some don't. My PT told me that my body needs to focus on the relaxing of the Kegel, not the squeezing part. I'm going to call her tomorrow and let her know that they made me worse. I'm hoping that she has other therapies and at home exercises for me to do. The place I go to specializes in PFD, so she should know what she is doing...right?? I will keep you updated on my progress. Paula

In fact I read a study somewhere that recommends avoiding Kegels for people with IC. I know for a fact Kegels exercises made me worse. I wish someone had told me earler so I would not have kept doing them.
Kegels send me into excruciating spasms. They cause severe pain and sometimes retention issues. Thought I was the only one that had issues with them.

In fact I read a study somewhere that recommends avoiding Kegels for people with IC. I know for a fact Kegels exercises made me worse. She does some external and internal massaging. We are also starting to use a dilators to help stretch me out. I'm doing alot of stretches at home now, as well. She said that I can keep using my heating pad, (I use it every night directly on my bladder to help me sleep) but I'm starting to think that the heat is further irritating my nerves. I'm going to try to sleep without it and see how it goes. Paula For me at least, streching is also making my IC worse. I dont know why, but I would be suprised if that was not the same for other people as well. So right now I dont do any streching and I dont do kegels. I do take really hot baths with I believe made a big difference for me. After I stopped the streching and kegels and started the hot baths, my urgency went away. I still have a lot of pain though, so I am planning to try out either Botox or instills next. Not sure what I should try first though.

Originally posted by lycrates For me at least, streching is also making my IC worse. I dont know why, but I would be suprised if that was not the same for other people as well. So right now I dont do any streching and I dont do kegels. I do take really hot baths with I believe made a big difference for me. After I stopped the streching and kegels and started the hot baths, my urgency went away. I still have a lot of pain though, so I am planning to try out either Botox or instills next. Not sure what I should try first though. I don't have pain, just the frequency. I have not been diagnosed with IC. I've had 3 urologists tell me that they don't think I have it. One urologist did a hydro/cysto and said that I didn't have it. I later saw on my medical records that he did see "diffuse glomerulations" present in my bladder. A different urologist told me that everyone has that and it is not an indication of IC. I tried 200 units of Botox in my bladder and it did nothing for me except make it a little harder to urinate. Botox does work well for alot of people. Some get it in their bladders and pelvic floor. I just had it in my bladder. After the Botox failed, I asked my doctor about physical therapy (he had never mentioned it to me or said that I have PFD). He told me that I definitely had PFD and physical therapy could help. (Thanks for telling me sooner doc!) My theory is to why the Botox didn't work for me nor any overactive bladder meds (except cause retention) is because its not really the inside of my bladder thats the problem. I think that my PFD has squeezed my organs and nerves so much that has irritated them. That's why I always feel the need to go. My physical therapist thinks my bladder is still playing a part in all of this, but I don't know. I'm on the IC diet and I cannot tell any difference in my frequency either way. If I had pain, then maybe it would be different....?? I'm going to stick with the diet and PT for now. It hasn't even been a month, yet. Also, there are some people that have good luck with the instills. Hope you find something that works for you! Paula
Paula~ How are you???

contents: I had botox to the bladder one year ago. It completely stopped the spasming that kept me running to the bathroom every 20 minutes. Now I only spasm when my bladder is full. It also significantly reduced the pain. I almost felt normal again. My doctor said it would last 9 months or so, but mine has lasted a year. I am now getting pain again so I have an appointment with the doctor to see about doing it again. Best decision I ever made! The other thing that has helped me significantly is going on a gluten and dairy free diet.

contents: I had it done a year ago. Insurance covered everything except my deductible.

I would love to hear some opinions on this. I am thinking of doing Botox to reduce the pain, but I am kinda of scared of the possibility of retention. So, exactly how bad is it when you get retention after Botox? And does it always happen or can you avoid it? What do others think?

I have had IC for over 10 years, previously I was have it stretched about every 4 years, I" would get 3 years of relief and then book in to have it done again in the 4th year, and that was fine. I had it again last year and it didn't work, so my urologist told me that she thinks it is time to try botox. I had it under a general anaesthetic (I have no idea how much), last Tuesday. I felt ok for a few days just a bit sore, but as of 2 days ago, I have severe retention, I can't sleep, it's uncomfortable to do anything. I feel like my bladder is about to burst and I sit on the toilet and push and just a little or nothing comes out. Has anyone had this procedure or can give any information or advice about what I am experiencing. Also I am becoming very frustrated, overwhelmed and depressed about this disease seems never ending. I am very active, I used to go to the gym, do regular dance classes, enjoy dressing up and now I can't do any of the things I enjoy. Has anyone had any procedures, medication or anything that has actually worked. Sorry so long winded....

Hi, Blondi50 ~ I'm so sorry you're having such issues after you think you've done something better for yourself. Is it possible that you have a UTI? Maybe you should take in a sample just to make sure. After procedures, we are so susceptible to infections. Another thought, if you're having such retention have you tried to cath yourself? At least that way you might get some relief. I don't have any experience with distentions or botox, but maybe it just takes a while to "work"??? Please don't hesitate to call your doctor and keep us posted. Hope this is a better day for you. , Ginger

contents: A Dr. wanted me to try botox, but she told me up front I would have to have a full time cath for 3-4 weeks and then intermittent self cath for as long as needed till I could pee again on my own. Botox is known to cause this problem I am quite surprised you weren`t told about this possibility before you were given the shots. You need to go to your Dr, uro now, you need to be cathed and either have it left in for a bit or taught how to cath yourself. Pushing hard is not good for you, and retaining is not good for you. Retaining also increase your risks in infection. good luck mg

contents: Mothergoose is right; straining is not good --- it usually will make it worse. If you haven;t learned to cath yourself, I suggest you call your doctor's office today and ask to learn ASAP. You may need to go to ER or a clinic to be catheterized until you can learn for yourself. In the meantime, sitting in warm water sometimes helps. It's worth a try. Donna

contents: Thank you so much everybody After my awful day yesterday, I called my Uro's office. The nurse/receptionist said that it sounds unusual (which I found strange after reading stories & conversations on this forum. She said it sounds like either I have an infection or the constipation is causing the bladder not to work properly, there was no mention of cath or self cathing. I have taken today off work and am taking a Chinese tea I usually take when I have bowels problems, which seems to be working but still no bladder relief. She told me to do this and has made an appointment for tomorrow afternoon to see the uro. I feel like I need to be emptied. Sorry to sound so ignorant by what is self cathing.??? I had a cath 10 years ago, as when this started I had an infection which caused inflamation on my bladder, a urologist gave me a diathermia and told me 2 weeks recovery, I had a contiki tour booked to Eurpoe 4 weeks after procedure (it was booked specificaaly 4 weeks after procedure as per uro's advice, he said 2 weeks recovery max but i gave it 4 weeks to be safe. After 2 weeks I ws still hurting, when I called him he told me I was being a hypercondriac and I would be fine, no need to postpone the trip. On the second day I couldn't walk, long story short I flew to cypress as I had a friend there and found that the procedure had given me an ulcer, Uro wouldn't speak to me when I called from OS. 4 days in hospital, cathedar & iv anti biotics. Ever since then, I have had IC, have never been told about self cathing. My uro didn't tell me anything about what to expect from botox leading up to or on the day of the op. She did sayshe had been putting it off as it was more invasive than the stretching. Thank u for all the advice, nice to know there is help out there and people experiencing similar things.

Ok this has gone on long enough. You need to go to the Uro or the ER, the bladder feeling full and stretched will not go away till you can empty your bladder. If I were you I would go to the ER as they will have a machine, small ultrasound machine which they can just run over your bladder and see how much urine is in your bladder, remember to tell them you have IC and IC bladder usually hold a smaller amount of urine than normal bladders, from being contracted due to scars. Even if the retention is not caused by the Botox (which it is known to cause), you will not get any relief till your bladder is emptied. Constipation would make it feel worse but would not stop you ability to pee. The longer you go without emptying your bladder the more you run the risk of getting a UTI, or urinary tract infection, the bladder needs to be flushed out on a continuous basis to not get infections. Also if your bladder just keeps filling and not emptying you run the risk of having your bladder burst, not fun. Self cathing means to be able to cathertize yourself to empty your bladder when you can pee on your own. It really does sound much worse than it is, it is very liberating to not have to be reliant on someone else to decided you need to be catherized, and then do it for you. In my experiences all while I am in extreme pain while they are getting everything ready and deciding if I need to be or not. Good luck keep us update on what you do. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Blondi ~ I agree with MG....you definitely need to get some help.....and consider changing doctors. After having IC for 10 years your doctor should make sure you are up to date with current treatments. Self cathing is so relieving....it's a tiny catheter inserted through your urethra and the urine is able to drain. If you're still at home you might try getting into the shower and allowing warm water to just run over you...relaxing it very important. Please get help if you're still not able to urinate. As MG said, keep us updated. Ginger

contents: Hello, Thank you for your posts. I went to work this morning & could barely sit on my seat, so after an important meeting I took myself to uro which is attached to the hospital. She could see I was in significant pain and took e in straight away. I had a scan and they said my bladder was holdng 120 mls, which the nurse said wasn't much & said it's not retention and no infection. I then saw my uro, she said that she only used 100 units so wasn't expecting these complications, however that my bladder has deteriorated and is so sensitive now that clearly 100 units of botox is enough to cause significant discomfort. She also said that I should have contacted her a lot earlier. Anyway she arranged self cathing for me, the nurse explained it and kind of showed me, they were very good. Uro said if it's not working in the next 2 days to call her and she will get me straight into the hospital for a cathedar. My first attempt was not so great, but I'm going to try again tonight, but my urethra is hurting a lot from my failed attempt. The good thing is that I spoke to a nurse that works in the industry, she said my uro is the best in our state & the only female, she is honest about IC, but also patient and commited and will try everything to help manage it as best as possible, she also said that many of the other uros don't really understand IC and do not manage it well, so that was reassuring. I'll keep you updated with my self cathing mission. Thank you again.

Hi there, I wanted to respond about the Botox and my personal experience. I think that Botox is actually a pretty effective treatment for many people, but has to be done right, meaning they have to know exactly where to inject the Botox within the bladder and the pelvic floor. If they inject a certain area in your bladder close to where your urethra connects to your bladder right at the entrance, no matter how much is used, the outcome will surely be a negative one in terms of having to self cath. But it wears off overtime. In my experience, the procedure itself is not extremely painful, but it wasn't brutal either. I always stayed over night for pain control. I never had any more than 250 cc's throughout my Pf & bladder all together. And the relief did not show up right away, it took some time to recover and then within a month I felt it helping. I hope that iT starts to help!

Has Botox helped with severe pain? I saw my uro yesterday and he said Botox was a possibility for for severe IC pain. I have tried every treatment, including a medtronic trial, and nothing has worked. In my exam yesterday he had to catheterize without and installation and I had retained alot of urine. Shortly after and today at nearly noon I am having nearly intolerable burning in my utethra I read that a percentage of people have to self-cath after botox, which I have never done, although I've had many bladder installations over the years. Any experience with pain relief at this point would I think outweigh having to self-cath, although I work a full-time job, with easy access to a bathroom. What are your thoughts, experience....Anthing info would be greatly appreciated! Thanks, Beth Originally posted by HopeWithin Hi there, I wanted to respond about the Botox and my personal experience. Hi All - firstly is this an Australian forum? I have a 10 year old daughter who has had UTI problems. Jan this year we met with a surgeon who said Botox into the bladder will help. He told us most only need 1 injection and on the rare occasion 2. My daughter had her 1st injection in Jan this year with great result until recently when the UTI's started to come back. She is now booked in for a 2nd injection. As I am going private it is $4,400 an injection. 1) Will she require this on an on going bases? 2) Is this done through the public system? Understand their maybe a wait.

Ok, I'm going to admit that this thread is scaring me just a bit. At my visit today with my uro-gynecologist, she suggested Botox. She is going to schedule me for a cysto w/hydro next week and wants to inject the Botox at the same time. She didn't mention anything about self cathing and its something I've never done. For me, right now, I'm leaning against the procedure.
signature: ---LeAnn Diagnosed on 9/23/08 via cysto with hydro SAHM to 2 wonderfully active boys Also have PTSD, depression & anxiety

contents: Hydro distentions is the only treatment so far that puts me in remission for around 5-6 months. Found a new uro gyno dr out of town and he taught me how to self cath to instill med. They really didn't last but a day or two. After the first week on my own, I ended up with a sever UTI....only one I have had since my diagnosis of IC....and it was brutal. So the dr had mentioned botox but I read about it and I told him that is a no go for me. I take Elavil and it really doesn't help the bladder but does help with the depression. After I took Levaquin antibiotic...I have absolutely no IC systems in two weeks. Is that not just the strangest thing. I am finally being sent to a pain management dr because my primary dr and my uro are not really comfortable giving pain medicine for chronic illness so maybe I can find a Dr that will help me control the pain. Two hydrols a year is just too costly. Botox is a no go for me. I did read where it kind of paralyses the bladder for a while. So I just refuse that. I do not want to take Elmiron due to stomach issues I already have. I also read only 30% of patients actually get relief

Tracy, I am so sorry to hear about your horrific experience with self instills. I can well imagine how it would cause the mother of UTI's!! I had a UTI recentently and the ARNP in my urologist's office suggested several possible causes: sex, which I only have occasionally and diarhhea, which I do have about every two weeks after I use that dreadful Magnesium Citrate for constipation. It always causes the runs, but I have tried so many other things for constipation, take Miralax and a probiotic daily, and it's not always enough. I was born with what the gastroenterologist calls a "tortuous" colon, and my father died from complications of colon cancer. At just turned 61 (EEK!) I have had three colonoscopies, and the last one revealed a number of adenomous polyps, none malignant. So constipation has been a lifelong struggle for me, and with the MS Contin the problem is magnified. I was considering Botox until I found out that my insurance had strict criteria and would not cover it at this time. I began taking gabapentin (neurontin generic) about 3 weeks ago, at 300mg twice daily. It appears to have helped the IC pain and burning, but today I am having some pain so I will add the third dose as prescribed. I tried to go up to three daily but I guess it was too soon, because I felt totally out of it! I am enable to tolerate Elavil and I have gone through all the instills as well as a failed trial with the Medtronic implant. I gave Elmiron two different year long goes, but I guess I'm not one of the lucky 30% that it helps. You might try the Gabapentin....it's cheap and seems to help nerve disorders, which I believe includes IC finally. Good luck to you and everyone with this miserable, debilitating disease....I pray they find the cause and a cure in our lieftime!! Hi, I don't have IC, but I do have alot of urinary frequency. I don't have leakage though. I had 100 units of Botox in my bladder 4 days ago. My uro wanted to do 200 units, but I thought starting out with a smaller dose would be better because of so many people talking about retention issues. He also told me that I would notice a difference the next day. Well, I didn't. It is now day 4 and I am no better or worse. I called the uro's office and the nurse said that she would pass along the message to the uro to see if he wanted to add more units. For those of you who have had Botox, can you tell me please how many units you had and when it started working? I've read that it can take a few days to a week or even more, but my uro was sure that I would have noticed a difference by now. I'm thinking I should have let him do the 200 units to begin with. Any info would be appreciated. Thanks! Paula

Ok so, I didn't get any response to my thread about Botox. I went to my urologist's office again last Friday and had an additional 100 units of Botox (200 units total) since the 100 units he did a week prior did nothing. The procedure was ALOT more painful this time around. I was literally screaming and crying. My doctor didn't anything to console me and just left the room after it was over. I don't know if he just didn't know what to say or he didn't know what to do with me crying. Anyways, I have had no relief from my constant urge to urinate. Yesterday and today, I have had to strain alittle bit to urinate and the flow kept stopping and restarting. I hope I'm not going into retention! I don't know if I'm starting to get an infection or not. Like I said, I still have the constant feeling of needing to urinate, but there is no pain that would indicate a possible UTI. Maybe the feeling is from the straining to urinate....? I called the nurse and she prescribed some Cipro for me to take just in case I was getting an infection. I'd rather be safe than sorry. Has anyone else had experience with Botox?

contents: I've been to all the doctors we all have seen here on the site. My PT referred me to Dr. David Karaba in Costa Mesa. Call the Pacific Center for Optimum Health in Costa Mesa, CA on 17th Street. Phone is (949) 873-5886. It's the best thing I ever did. No one has been sicker than I was after my diagnosis nearly 2 years ago. Now my bladder is completely healed and I'm out of pain! This caring, hope filled, cutting edge practice saved my life and Dr. Karaba may be able to save yours too. He coordinates Western medicine with integrative therapies that get to the root cause of the auto immune cases, IC diagnosis, allergies and the emotional pieces as well. His practice calms the inflammation to get your body back to health. WOW! What a difference. I don't have to live with IC! I wouldn't go anywhere else! Last edited by Daisysmuse; 09-18-2012, 09:46 AM.

I wouldn't go anywhere else! wondering what sort of treatment you received there? I would rather try something more holistic.

Anastacia... the first visit is over an hour...and subsequent visits are not rushed either. He listens and understands that IC is a complex condition that is different for each person with physical, autoimmune, hormonal and emotional puzzle pieces. It also involves body "patterning" where our bodies get used to responding in a certain way and the patterns can be difficult to break. This caring physician slowly and cautiously gets to the root of the causes for each person and unwinds the patterns. He coordinates Western medicine with his treatments, looks at all your medical records, ordering any labs that could have overlooked pieces. Then he makes a plan specificually for your case. It is complemented by acupuncture and NAET which is an allergen/sensititity elimination technique. There are no bladder distentions or drastic measures. Just personal attention to detail, designed to get you freed up and back to feeling better again. Very personal, very careful, very skilled. I did everything he told me to do as I was desperate and I'm pain free Does this help?

Hello everyone, My urologist suggested Botox injections on my recent visit. As I've been through every known treatment, including a Medtronic trial with no results. My main complaint with IC is constant severe pain with virtually no let up. I also have IBS (I was born with what the doctor calls a "tortuous" colon, Fibro, Degenerative Disc Disease (about to have surgery on my neck), Depression (who with this disease doesn't have a measure of this) and a few other odds and ends. I'm 60, about to turn 61, and have been diagnosed for over 10 years. Last year at this time my urologist said that Botox wasn't yet approved for the bladder, but now it is, and we are in the process of getting approval from the insurance company. I'm not really nervous about the actual injections, but I am worried about the possibility of having to self-cath, which I've never yet done. Any thoughts and experieces would be greatly appreciated...I am divorced and work full time, and I'm afraid if the Botox doesn't give me some pain relief I will have to try for disability. Thanks!

Good luck with the Botox! I did not have botox, but did self cath marcaine coctails for a while before I got my Interstim. It was scary at first, and I actually needed my husband's help (with the meds going in). Looking back, if I had bought a small self standing mirror, I could have done it myself. The first few times were frustrating but it gets easier with practice. Don't lose hope. Things can get better.

I have not tried Botox but my doctor is kind of pushing for me to try it. I don't have any success with other treatments either. He said that he sees a lot of success, and that the procedure is done under anesthesia. He also warned that in a very, very small percent of cases, people become incontinent either temporarily or (in even rarer instances - less than 1%) permanently. These cases are usually with people who have very severe bladder problems to start with anyway. I am afraid of the same thing - having to self cath. Just don't know if I could do it -eeek. I think I'm going to wait and see if some miracle remission is in the near future before I resort to the botox. Anyways that's my related story. I hope that you find a way to feel better! Best wishes.

contents: Well, my insurance turned the Botox down, so I'm not sure if I want to appeal or not. I was in the urologists office with a suspected, but really rare, UTI. It was positive for white blood cells and they send it on for a culture and gave me a script for Bactrim-DS. I've taken three total so far and some of the unbearable burning has been relieved. I guess that's what IC feels like with a bacterial infection. We're thinking about trying Neurontin in two weeks. As far as the Botox, I'll see what happens.
Mine failed to provide relief the first time but it did give me retention. At the end of my rope and wondering if a second try could offer benefit.
Did I miss something? What failed the first time?

contents: If it failed the first time I dont see why it would work the second time. In my opinion it might be better to try someting different, like Interstim or Bladder installations.

Has anyone had with this? Most of my symptoms seem concentrated at the base of the bladder and i have been told recently I have "trigonitis." Anyway, from what i have read online there is much less risk of retention if only the trigone and bladder neck are injected and not the detrusor muscle. I just had A second round of Botox to my pelvic floor, so it's not something I can pursue right away, it's just that I am getting to the point where my only remaining options are Botox to the bladder or an interstim trial. So, thought I would see if anyone on here has had this done. Thanks, Addy

signature: Arizonian

contents: Umm, I have never had a hydro, but from what I have read and been told by three different medical professionals is that the hydro can cause extreme pain. That coupled with botox I would think would be very painful. There is no way I would do both at the same time, I personally would never do a hydro after all I have read and been told. That is just me though. In any event, I would look into it and research it first. I wish you luck in whatever you decide to do.

I think I've earned my frequent Botox flyers miles... Hi there, To answer your question regarding Botox treatment into the pelvic floor and bladder, I've had it done 4 separate times over the span of a year and a half. Although my doctor does not "expand" my bladder or do a hydro at the same time when he goes in with the cystoscope, I have it done in the OR because it's not a pleasant experience to have that done awake with just a plain ole cysto. If it were me, I'd ask to do one treatment or the other because it would be hard to tell which one was helping you. Now I've had trigger point injections, not Botox but just steroids injected into my vaginal muscles awake with no anesthesia and that was quite painful. I'd much rather be asleep during any bladder or pelvic floor treatment. As far as Botox helping with my PFD, it has helped in the past quite a bit. The first two times I had it done I was amazed at the relief in terms Of pain that I had previously and spasm wise. But it doesn't come without side effects, retention is the biggest one of them all. If I were you I'd ask firmly the amount of units that your dr is injecting. I know from my experience and reading online that no more than 200 units should be used if you want to void harsh retention issues after the procedure. As far as the procedure goes, its not too bad. You go to sleep, it takes about 30-45 minutes usually for me. Then I wake up and feel pretty sore , bleeding is normal when you void... Feelin sore days after is normal too. It took me about 2-3 weeks to actually finally see a change in symptoms, but overall I was happy I did it. But the last two times it had it done, it had not worked as well, which I've been told that my body is most likely becoming immune to it. But that is the case for everyone. I hope this helped some, good luck.

30 min. hydro with Botox injections I had these 2 procedures done at the same time this past Monday under general. I was given a spinal then put on a Morphine drip so I felt no pain. I went home with a Rx for Percocet and only took one pill at home. My bladder has a bit more urgency but I have felt far better this week than I anticipated. So, for all the hype about the "pain", that is all hype. I say do it if nothing else has worked for you. Your pain can be controlled easily. I should feel results within 3 weeks. There is a 70% chance of success with the hydro according to my doctor's statistics. He is one of the few in the U.S. who do this procedure.
signature: Arizonian

contents: I wouldn't exactly call having Botox injections in your bladder a walk in the park... And refer to pain of the procedure as hype. The level of pain probably depends on how sensitive/ severe of a case your IC is. I know even with an IV drip of pain Meds after I can't barely move for almost a week. Everyone's different though.

Wondering how many you have had and did you have a major problem. Thanks for your help I have already had 3 prudential nerve injections and they were fine I was fully awake with no problem. Jo Jo

Botox Maybe your Doctor gave you to much Botox.

I HATE IC I just wish it would go away I am tired of dealing with this.. however since the Botox injections the pain level has decreased about 90%.. the only thing I use for pain at all is the valuim gel... hope you can get things under control...

contents: Botox procedure I am suffering from IC for 10 years. Some times it was better some, times worse. I did not have any remission for the past 18 months. I am not sure if I will be able to void normally after botox surgery and if botox will eliminate bladder pain and urgency. I have a bad experience to install catheter in my bladder before. It cause bladder inflammation even worse. Please advise if somebody had this procedure before and what is the result? Thank you very much. Happy Holidays to everybody!!!!
Out of curiosity.. how painful is the injection? Somehow I would imagine a needle in an IC inflamed bladder isn't a great idea..
signature: 27 year old student Had symptoms since I was 5 Been on forums since '07, knew I had this since then, received mild treatment without true diagnosis due to anxiety. Diagnosed by symptoms on 4/08/11. Had first horrible flare in 07, bad doctors, had to research IC myself to clue in onto what it was. After following the diet I went into remission. Big flare in 09 due to an untreated bladder infection. Went back into remission in 2012. Symptoms returned 4/1/15. All of my remissions have ended in April-May or September-October. It sounds like a connection to seasonal allergies to me.. Currently: Continuous Birth Control, vaginal valium (10/6/11), Elmiron (4/08/11), UTA (4/08/11), continuous Trimethoprim for infections (10/6/11), Heating pad, Benedryl, harsh diet What I know works: Benedryl, diet, birth control, UTA, valium, ditropan, soma What didn't work: Elavil, vistaril (12/22)

contents: personal experience I have had botox done & it has been quite helpful as far as any of the treatments that i have tried. I suffer from retention & although the first 2 weeks or so the botox causes a little more difficulty in voiding, it wears off & the relief outweighs this side effect. botox is not permanent... I know that everyone worries about retention & having to self cath for the rest of their lives with this treatment, but i think its important to remember that if done correctly, the treatment can be a success. I can't say that everyone should try this treatment, but im just simply saying that weighing the options a little further, and looking past the retention aspect, there can be quite a benefit from this. I say if you are debating on doing any sort of treatment because you "heard" and read and so on and so on, it might be a whole lot simplier if you just kept an open mind rather than completely wiping off botox from the list of things to "try"...just my two cents.
botox Have heard lot success stories re botox and OAB Anyone know if botox for IC? Are drs. doing it.
I don't know if this helps anyone, but I'm very sensitive to urine retention. I however haven't had it since the botox injections 2 and a half months to go. It's harder to empty though.

Botox injections I am scheduled to have the botox injections on thursday .. I am worried because lately I have had to have many different times of having foley caths in because I could not pee at all one time my bladder got so bad it had 900 cc's of fluid in it talk about severe kill me now pain.. but now they have thought me how to self cath and that does help at some points however the pain I feel on a daily basis is horrible right now they have me on valuim suppository gel which seems to help (most days.. BUT NOT TODAY ) other than that I am nervous about how my body will react to this medicine as I have weird reactions to alot of things.. such as when the doc did the last instill on me of lidocaine and sodium bicarobonate it sent me so far back and into so much pain..that is when the doc started the valuim.. so i am scared to death as to how I will react to the botox on thursday... praying it helps because honestly I AM DONE Last edited by ICNDonna; 11-14-2011, 02:08 AM.

botox injections My daughter has been suffering from IC for over 2 years. She has severe pelvic pain. She does not have urgency, but rather retention. At the beginning of August, she had Botox injections in her vaginal area. It HELPED! She had her 2nd round of injections last Monday. This has not been a cure-all. It has made it possible for her to function. She does have terrible opiate caused constipation and is on Amitiza and Relistor. It takes that combination to work. Thank goodness for them though because nothing else worked. She is on several medications, but there is another thing that is new to us that has helped - compounded creams. A pharmacist friend told me about them and sent information with me to her doctor. These are creams that helps with pain in her legs, arms, hips, and she also rubs it on her tummy where her bladder is. It does not have the side effects as medicine taken by mouth. I pray that this will help someone to find a little relief. I have been an avid reader for the past 2 years trying to find any ideas that might help.
Inoble, Thank you for posting this I hope your daughter continues to do well.

Hi everyone.. I have not been on lately because I am trying to keep myself busy.. I wanted to let everyone know that since my surgery on the 17th getting a Cystoscope and botox injections.. I am doing much better in many ways and worse in some.. I still have my bad flare days but ..... I have not had to take ANY pain meds for more than 1 reason.. first the doctor started me on VALUIM suppository gel.. it is AMAZING!!! It stops my flares with in 15 mins sometimes less. Also since the botox injections i have not had to use much of anything at all.. however I did end up with a cold right after surgery so I have been having problems controlling my bladder.. Yesterday I was down at my friends house where i have been spending alot of time..and I have to say she is the most understanding and loving friend I have. She understands that when I cough I pee myself.. which I find horribly embarrassing.. but you know what.. she gave me one of her brand new pair of underware and pair of pants and some of her pads (I dont have my periods anymore since my hystorectomy..been using my daughters pads) and she didnt even make me feel embarrassed or upset.. I was going to go home right in the middle of our card game but she is so sweet... well tomorrow I go for my first appt for pelvic floor therepy.. doc wants me to atleast get evaluated.. I am scared because I dont want the pain to start up again. I want to thank you all for standing by me through this horrible up and down battle I have been through.. I will let you know how tomorrow goes... and I hope all of you have friends like Renee.. and my bf who both understand and pass NO judgement...

contents: Hi All, Elmiron and good diet has eliminated most of the burning pain inside my bladder that used to torment me. My understanding is that the burning is what most urologist prescribe botox to treat, but my current uro wants to use botox to try to deal with a different symptom: My current problem is a chronic urge to urinate that goes from zero right after I urinate, to a mild pressure 10 minutes after I urinate, to an uncomfortable pressure 30 minutes after I urinate, to pain after about an hour. That pain can escalate to intolerable from one to three hours after I urinate. Under the best circumstances I often find it difficult to urinate, and impossible to urinate when I'm away from home (just can't get the muscles to relax enough--any tension at all is enough to keep me from urinating). My current urologist wants to inject my bladder with Botox in order to improve this symptom. Is it likely to do that? Is there anywhere I can find out more about this unusual procedure? I've gone ahead and scheduled the botox for eight days from today, simply because I haven't improved in a couple of years, and without improvement my life is terribly limited.

I've had progress from it. Not 100%, but better than before. Harder to empty, but worth it. I have a shy bladder, which makes it harder in public and sometimes I just wait till I get home to go. Did you end up getting the procedure done?

Botox I am scheduled for my botox injection surgery on thursday this coming week I am PRAYING it helps because right now I am to the point where life is unbareable and I am just about done.. good luck with yours I will let you know how mine goes

contents: Mothergoose, I get botox for migraines these days and can't raise my eyebrows much either! Georgecoolname, I'll be interested to see responses about this. I had no idea they do the botox now for the bladder. Definitely something I would consider because it works well for my migraine pain (every three months). As mothergoose said, once you get it, it doesn't wear off for a while. I'm not sure how the botox works on the bladder, but as for migraines, I was told the first two treatments may not work so well. It usually takes a few treatments to see good results. In my case the results were good right away. Of course the bladder is a different organ, so I'm not sure if it works in the same way. And I know they will give a specified amount. In cases where there's a problem of some sort, they reduce the amount during the next treatment.
signature: Frances Diagnosed: 2011 - have had symptoms for over 20 years. Diagnoses so far: Overactive Bladder, then IC, and now Myofacial Pelvic Pain Syndrome. Medicines: For IC am doing PT, and will be taking Gabapentin, Ativan, Tramadol and Ambien. Ice packs and drinking vanilla shakes help. Foods that cause a flare, tomatoes, yogurt, coffee, soda, all sports drinks, and most teas. Take Verapamil, Naproxen (as needed), Omeprazole, Frova (as needed) and receive Botox shots(every three months) for migraines. For allergies I use Claritin and Flonase. (Feel free to ask me any questions about meds.) Other conditions: Migraines, allergies, mild IBS. "If you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer." (Matthew 21:22)

contents: Toto I was told the same thing the first treatment for migraines may not work as well, I needed to consent to 2 treatments to have the first. Like you I noticed a difference right away, or within a couple of days. I had them every 3 mons for 2 years and now no longer need them to control migraines, I still get headaches but no where near as bad and not constant. If I don't get a good nights sleep, I will wake with a headache but if I get on it right away and get up and moving I can usually get it under control, I may have to take migraine meds thought out the day, never the max anymore and I may have to repeat pain meds, but it is livable. I had a migraine everyday for 5 years, the last 2 were much more manageable with the botox, and I am happy to know if they ever get that bad again I can go back to botox. My migraines were caused from sleep deprivation from IC, once I got on treatment before both conditions I have done much better. Georgecoolname: If you decided to go this route let us know how it works for you, if it made retention worse etc. Thanks MG

signature: Sandy Shadix

contents: Botox was the best Thing ever! I have had 4 injections of Botox in the last 2 years (yes before approved by FDA) my insurance totally covered it. It changed my life. Under general anesthesia, the doctor injected 30 times into the detrussor muscle in the bladder. The discomfort after was minimal compared to the IC pain. I had to cath my self only one time. I felt no pain, in fact didn't feel like I ever had to pee. Losing all sensation in the bladder makes it hard to pee, I had to bear down and use my stomach muscles but it was totally worth it. It lasts about 6 months or so then I would go again. Unfortunately, just went yesterday to get checked and my uro said that I might be too far gone for more Botox. I am having urodynamics done on the 16th to see just how scarred my bladder is. I am an endstage IC person. I would recommend botox for any IC patient.

contents: Did you do it? Just reading through this and am wondering if Georgecoolname had the botox injected? I saw a uro a few weeks ago who says I was a candidate, but after reading all the posts, I'm not sure I want to do it. He was not really wanting to answer a lot of questions. He's not my regular uro but the only one in the group that does botox. After going to the bathroom, there are times that I will go every 10 minutes, especially after drinking something. There are times at the movies that I just stand in the aisle because I know I will be up again in 10 min. and don't want to crawl over my husband again. I get such a "heavy pressure" like when I was 9 mo. pregnant and really, really had to go. I also have pudendal nerve pain. A lot of times it keeps me from exercising because afterwards I feel like someone has kicked me between the legs and I'm bruised and swollen. Not really sure what to do. It's covered by our insurance but we haven't meet the deductible and it would be about $2000. We're so late in the year I'm thinking about doing it in Jan., then if it has to be done again, the deductible will be met. Thoughts? Linda

My actual surgery experience Just got around to this guys, sorry. I got the surgery done August 30th. I'm happy with it. Things went smoothly that day, except I spasmed a lot at first (probably from being knocked out) and they cathed me before I left because I didn't have enough energy to make all that time to empty out my bladder. I was prepared to self-cath (as I'm sensitive to urine retention), but was able to go on my own that evening. Didn't take long for me to feel better and I still do. I don't have to go to the bathroom as often and it doesn't hurt as much. However, it is usually slow to empty, even to this day. Having a shy bladder makes it worse at times. I'm just happy I can still go and since it doesn't hurt as much, it's not so bad holding it if I have to. I usually wake up in the middle of the night to empty what I can't before going to bed, but that's no different than before. I'm curious on what it's going to be like when it wears off. I would get it again.
