contents: Merry Xmas everyone. I had 30 Botox injections into the bladder 13 days ago. I was in an unusual period of mild symptoms at the time (never lasts longer than 3-4 weeks). My symptoms got very bad again a week ago with a lot of pain and severe frequency (no infection). I was told it takes a couple of weeks for the Botox to work. Does anyone know if it can take longer than that? So far I have had no benefit from it at all. My urologist is off on Xmas holidays so I can't discuss it with him. Jenny
signature: Diagnosed with severe IC Feb 2008. Symptoms since 2004 C5/6 fusion 2004. Chronic pain in neck and arms C4/5/6/7 posterior neck decompression December 2012 Treatments tried for IC Hydrodistension - made me worse for 5 months DMSO installations (2) - second one caused so much pain that Dr decided not to do any more. Numerous oral medications for the bladder, none of which helped. 4 treatments of Botox injections - minimal success. Permanent Interstim implanted August 2012 - nil benefit so removed May 2013 Bladder nerve cauterization March 2014 and April 2014

contents: Hi, When I got my injections, I was told that it would take several weeks for injections to take place. Mine are just kicking in and I had injections in early November. Be patient. It was worse before it got better.
Thanks Dolphjoy. Jenny

contents: Any relief? Have you found any relief yet? I hope so for 2 reasons. I too have severe IC and know the pain, frustration and fear it causes. Secondly, I am getting 200 units of botox put into my bladder on Friday along with 100 in some tight pelvic floor areas. I have never tried botox so I am very anxious. My doctor says he has a 90% improvement rate with botox. How many units did they put inside your bladder? Did your doctor put botox into the bladder neck? I pray that you feel better regardless of your botox outcome.
signature: DX w/ IC in 2002. Received disability for IC in 04. Have severe IC. Hunner uclers, high frequency, moderate arthritis in lower back, severe Pelvic floor dysfunction. Anxiety due to changing amounts of pain meds. Hips misalignment (left leg 1/2" shorter). Past treatments: dmso and heparin instillations-in severe pain 24 hours afer emptying, hydrodistensions very painful because of ulcers, interstim twice--both failed due to infections at battery sight. PTNS-didn't reduce frequency. Botox jan 09 in bladder and pelvic floor and steroids placed on uclers. Burning pain 3 weeks after procedure but helped 30% with frequency and 50% with pain and spasms. Botox june 09 in bladder only and steriods injection into ulcers. Not as painful afterwards, put had to bear down to urinate which caused severe tightening of pelvic floor. Helped again with freq and pain. Tried physical therapy past 4 years. Has helped over time. On a regular basis I get deep tisssue massages, acupuncture and see a chiropractor. Had 3 caudal nerve blocks. Slightly improved PFD. Had Sacral nerve streriod injections on boh sides of buttocks. Helped with lower back pain, hip and buttock pain. Pudental nerve block 9-13-09. Severe pain 2 days following. No help with PFD. Steroid injections April 2010. Helped relieve some of the pain and frequency. Pelvic floor lidocaine injections May, August 2010. Noticeable relief of trigger point pain. Steroid injection into hunner ulcer August 2010. Very helpful. Current meds: percoset, ambien, trazedone, klonopin, flexeril and norethindrone (to control endometriosis). Probiotics and a high fiber/low sugar diet helps help with constipation. Meds that didn't work or had adverse side effects: elmiron, elavil, lyrica and several others. On a daily basis I walk 2-5 miles, pray and meditate. All of this gives me tremendous strength and energy in both my mind and body.

contents: Still no relief Still no improvement yet. The symptoms have fluctuated wildly over the past couple of weeks. A few days ago I had brief but severe food poisoning (diarrhoea, cramps, cold sweats and blacking out). It only lasted an hour but the intestinal pain seemed to affect my bladder and I ended up with the worst bladder pain I have ever had. At the same time the urgency became intense and I ended up spending all night sitting on a bucket in front of the tv as I couldn't stay off the toilet for more than 2 minutes, and taking morphine for the pain. 24 hours later I was back to my normal pain and frequency level, which as yet is no better than pre-Botox. I don't how much Botox was used because the hospital surgeon, not my specialist, performed the surgery. All I was told was that 30 injections were being applied to the bladder via a cystoscopy. I also was dreading the procedure, but as it turned out, it only caused a mild symptom increase in the days afterwards. My surgeon told me he has had a 60% success rate with Botox and has treated around 100 patients with it. So far it has only been 3 weeks so I'm trying to be patient as Dolphjoy has suggested.

contents: any improvement with botox? Hi jellyfish! Have you had any improvement since your last posting? I am going through all the botox posts as my new doctor, a urogynecologist at a university teaching hospital, is recommending botox combined with physical therapy. I hope you are feeling better - it sounds like you really went through the mill with pain.

Tried all "usual" IC treatments, Elmiron, Atarax, DMSO, heparin, lidocaine instillations. 2000- First hydrodistention by Dr. Daniel Curhan, classic IC glomerulations & mast cells. 2005 - 2nd hydrodistention by Dr. Christopher Payne, Stanford University; dx'd with "refractory" Interstitial Cystitis. 2006 - Dx'd with left pudendal neuralgia by Dr. Jerome Weiss, had two unguided nerve blocks, pain worsened. 2008 - Botox, unguided intravaginal blocks (*WARNING* risk of further damage) by Dr. Felicia Lane; PT with Julie Sarton. 2010 - PNE (Pudendal Nerve Entrapment) surgery, TG approach, by Dr. Michael Hibner. (FAILED) 2011- PNE surgery by Dr. A. Lee Dellon, frontal approach. (FAILED) Jan, 2012 - Dx'd with "centralized pain" by Dr. Joshua Prager, UCLA Pain Management. 3-day ketamine infusion, nearly pain-free!!! Bad news: Bladder flared as ketamine is known bladder irritant. Oct, 2012 - Pain pump trial on Dr. Prager's recommendation. Awesome results! Nov. 13, 2012 - Surgery to implant intrathecal pain pump, which has been a lifesaver! Success at last! Thank you Dr. Prager!

contents: Hi ICsmiles, A week after the Botox I started getting intense pain and frequency which lasted for most of January. I had to buy a bedpan to sit and watch tv on because I was too exhausted to keep getting up and down to the toilet. On 2/2 the pain started easing and by the 7/2 I was feeling as good as I ever do since first getting IC. I was able to have the odd alcoholic drink and to only get up around twice a night. That lasted about a month and then I could feel it all coming on again, but after a week it settled down. It has been ok for a few days - i.e. symptoms are not limiting my activities. As yet there is not really a distinct pattern of improvement post-Botox. In December I had a good month prior to the Botox, then a very bad month after Botox, then a good month. It is usual for me to have maybe 5 months bad, then up to one month good, then bad again. After more time has elapsed I will be able to weigh up whether I have had more good days/months overall than pre-Botox. What I hoped would happen, i.e. get much better and stay better for 6 months or so, doesn't seem to be the case. It may turn out that the flares last for less time and are less frequent, but still occur. I haven't ruled out having another Botox procedure mid-year if it turns out that there is any overall improvement by then.
signature: Diagnosed with severe IC Feb 2008. Symptoms since 2004 C5/6 fusion 2004. Chronic pain in neck and arms C4/5/6/7 posterior neck decompression December 2012 Treatments tried for IC Hydrodistension - made me worse for 5 months DMSO installations (2) - second one caused so much pain that Dr decided not to do any more. Numerous oral medications for the bladder, none of which helped. 4 treatments of Botox injections - minimal success. Permanent Interstim implanted August 2012 - nil benefit so removed May 2013 Bladder nerve cauterization March 2014 and April 2014

contents: thanks that was quick! Hi Jelly - thank you for such a fast response! I have been on ICN all afternoon and evening so i got it right away.... oh my gosh, the bedpan thing sounds so rough. I feel for you... I am not in the intense frequency mode anymore, which is weird... I guess because I have had IC for so long. I still go more than most people, but not up and down like a yo-yo. I lie on my back a lot because sitting hurts --and the pain doesn't change much after emptying my bladder, it is like my brain finally got it - the pain is constant so why bother to get out every drop every moment ... I think lately - the last year maybe? - the pudendal nerve pain has become so dominant, a deep constant hot poker with knives stuck on top of that, then the burny bladder pain is a kind of eerie dissonance blending it all together.... A veritable symphony of pain. how lovely. And maybe add some sound effects of muted screams in the background wow maybe there is some kind of creative expression that could come out of that, if I could only get myself motivated to get up and try to do it. (I make art when I am feeling better, but it has been a little while) But right now the heating pad and ice pack and pillows, with the laptop perched on my chest - that is my nirvana. Well, I am hoping that botox provides some relief = and it sounds like patience is part of it. Thanks for explaining how it has been going for you. We all seem to have such different experiences! No wonder there is so much confusion about IC and related conditions -- we all react differently and have different symptoms, so doctors and the medical community do not know what to make of it. You and I do have some similarities - I read your list in your signature, and I, too am one of those who could not tolerate DMSO, or the heparin / lidocaine instillations either. When everyone started raving about "rescue instillations" I thought maybe I didn't give it a good enough try, and OWIE ZOWIE - just one instill by my uro and I was down for over a week. Totally in flare hell. So, no more of those. let's hang in there!!! I promise to post my positives as well as all these sob stories about pain, okay?

contents: I'm having botox in my pelvic floor on Wedneday. My doc is doing it in the office. I've had enough anesthesia in the past few years and always end up with retention, so we decided to do it in the office. She plans on injecting about 300 units of botox. I'm very nervous about having this done while wide awake. Anyone with any tips or advice? Gigi
Gigi, how are you doing after your botox injection? I usually have 150 units put inside my bladder and it is done in the doctor office to. Deborah Bush

contents: I had botox injections under GA on the 18th December. 5 weeks later and I seem to be worse than ever. For the past couple of days I have had to take morphine for pain and have been going to the toilet every 10 minutes or so. My urologist has booked me back into hospital for a repeat procedure of the botox injections. I am really worried as so far the botox has caused a bit of difficulty with passing urine. Often I have to try several times. Also I have had nil relief from urgency, pain or frequency. The 2 most severe flare-ups I have had this month have been worse than anything I've experienced before. I am concerned more botox will just add to the retention problems and again do nothing to relieve the primary symptoms. It seems very soon to be having another 30 injections after only 5 weeks.

contents: I had botox injections several years ago for my IC, it helped with the pain, however I ended up having severe retention from it and I had to self cath for about 8 months until the botox wore off. I agree with you it does seem kinda soon to do another 30 injections after only 5 wks. I would deffinately call your doc and discuss this with him. Tell him your concerns. If it were me I would go with my gut instinct on this one. Dont do anything your not comfortable with, only you know how your body feels!!! I hope you feel better soon =)

Thanks for your reply steelerfan. I was supposed to have the second round of botox this Monday, but I've decided not to go ahead with it, for now at least. I went to another dr today who has given me a slow release patch of Buprenorphine to wear for pain relief. I was tested for a UTI as well. I might try the botox again in a few months but I'm in far too much pain at the moment to do something that has the potential to aggravate my symptoms.

contents: Botox repeat . . . Hi Jellyfish, My journey with botox is coming up - I am just waiting for the scheduling, hopefully later this month. It seems some people flare horribly after botox, and others sail through it - some flare and then get improvement. I am with you -- if you are in pain from your botox injections, it makes sense to wait longer before having more. Meanwhile, let us know if you start having any improvement. I am hopeful that you will. My experience so far with IC has been that I am on the disappointed side of every treatment - nothing has worked so far - so I am hopeful that botox will help.

contents: Hi jellyfish, i just read ur posts, i encourage you to think many many times before repeating the botox, i had it injected twice, once 100cc to the external sphincter to enhance the Bladder to work as i am on CIC self catheterization when it failed i injected 300cc in the bladder as apain management procedure my pain is worse, i ended now on taking 20-40mg morphine daily for the pain as i had to strain more for urinating, sometimes i used to urinate a little without the catheter, so think , iknow the Botox was a big sucess to some people, but u shall feel if ur one of them or not, Wish u all the best.

Hi everyone, Thanks for your input. I still have only had the one botox procedure, being the 18th December. After an excruciating January, I started to improve on about the 2nd February and have been pretty good since - i.e. the past 3 weeks. I don't know whether I have improved due to the Botox or whether it is just the normal course of going in and out of flare-ups. Over the past 1 1/2 years, the longest I have been out of a flare is about a month. If the current improvement lasts much longer then I may consider more Botox injections in 6 months and see if the same thing happens. Whether it is because of the Botox or not, it came at the right time as I was able to enjoy my birthday with a glass of french champagne and no ensuing torture. I'm enjoying being "normal" while it lasts! Jenny

Jenny

contents: Hi all, I am US Soldier stationed in South Korea, I was referred out to see a local urologist at a major hospital in South Korea. I had already tried many of the medications with little effect as well as heparin instillations. After doing a scope my doctor thought the best thing for me would be to have botox injected into my bladder. Luckily I dont have a severe case of IC my main problems are frequency and low bladder capacity. I get up 3+ times a night to pee. So on the 19th I got botox. The procedure was relatively easy (I was asleep), I was allowed to leave the hospital after I produced 300cc of urine, which took 3hours because I was hooked to an IV. I had some pain afterwards just when trying to pee, which abated within a day. I was given IV antibiotics before and after the surgery. I was also sent home with 10 days worth of antibiotics as well as an antiulcerative. its been about 5 days and so far I have noticed a decrease in my frequency and I can actually hold it for about 6 hours at night when I go to bed. The only thing that I am concerned about is I have to strain more to pee. So now instead of going every couple hours I actually have to make myself wait until my bladder is full, instead of automatically going every couple of hours. I was a little concerned about retaining urine, since I feel like I am not emptying my bladder completely. I have a follow up in a week so I will talk to my doctor about this when I go in, unless it gets worse and then I will call sooner. So if anyone has a similiar experience or can offer some advice on what kind of effects they had afterwards that would be great I want to make sure that this is normal.

How are you doing? Hi I just read your Botox post from Dec 21. Sorry I can't help much as I have not had Botox yet -- I am being scheduled for Botox in the muscles around my bladder as soon as possible, just waiting for the call. Then they are following up with PT - physical therapy - in those muscles. From what I have read, the fact that you did not have a major pain flare-up is good. How is your retention? Have you seen some improvement since then? Hope you are feeling better.

contents: Just found this in the news: It was a drug trial -- specifically for IC -- between using Bacillus Calmette-Guerin and Botulinum toxin-A (BTX-A) [Botox] for the effectiveness of treating IC. http://www.urotoday.com/3391/urotoda..._cystitis.html It's really techie-speak, but for those of you who are interested in Botox, it might give you more info. ~Beth
signature: I am not a physician, so please do not mistake any of my comments as medical advice. I am just a fellow sufferer sharing my experience with IC. Scheduled for Radical Cystectomy & Indiana Pouch, July 16, 2009 Hit with severe pelvic pain on 6/14/08 (7:05pm): GYN, MRI, CT, laparoscopy, colonoscopy: All Clear DX'ed with Severe IC: Findings during cystoscopy and hydro-distention on 9/11/08 showed: scattered areas of hemorrhage and glomerulizations, w/bloody efflux. Update: Had second cysto/hydro on 5/21/09: My uro said "there were thousands of lesions" all over my bladder. Current Meds: Bladder Instills (self-adm 3 x per week or PRN); MS Contin; Lortab (PRN); Zanaflex; Maxide; Estradiol Patch Other helpful remedies: IC Diet (very important!) especially no chemical additives or preservatives; Drink 1.5+ gal. of pH neutral water per day (I test my water frequently! This is very, very important!!); Prelief; Heating pad; and lots of hugs from my wonderful hubby! Meds I have tried, but could not take: Amitriptyline & Imipramine (both made me mentally nutzoid, gave me nightmares, and neither one touched the pain); Lyrica (didn't touch the pain either, plus it made me very nauseous after eating and then it triggered my IBS); Cymbalta (didn't touch the pain either and it made me horribly depressed--all I did was sleep and cry!); Percocet (did very little for the pain and gave me headaches); Discontinued using Sodium Bicarb & Kenalog in my instills (it burned really bad!). Discontinued Meds: Elmiron (replaced by Heparin in instills); Bentyl (antispasmodic and anticholinergic; Zanaflex works much better for me!); Valium Suppositories History: IBS (spastic colon) since 1982; Partial hysterectomy 1985--completed in 2002 with extensive adhesion removal (I think this surgery was my IC trigger!); Non-painful urinary frequency since 2002; No history of frequent UTI's; HPV and abnormal paps 1/07; Sporadic PFD

contents: Hello everyone, I have an appt with my Urologist in Feb., and the topic is going to be Botox treatments for my IC. She does this sort of therapy now, so I am eager to find out more info on it. Please, any of you that have tried this or heard of the success rate for relief, please let me know. Thank ya'll!
signature: As I think, so I shall be.... I have had IC for 4.5 years now. I also have fibro, sjogren's syndrome, and vulvadynia. I live in immense pain. I often daydream of life before this demon . When I feel good, I embrace it with all of my heart! I pray that someday I will feel 100% again, we all deserve it! Medications: Vicodin (as needed, so daily!) Percocet (morning & night) Klonopin (.5 mg at night) Zanaflex (2 mg each night) Thyroid med for low thyroid (hypo) leuko-stim Vitamin D (4000) Diagnosis: IC, CFS, Fibro (it is really bad it has taken so much from me and changed my life), Anxiety, Depression from it all, & Hypothyroid.

contents: Botox ashallIbe I would be interested in knowing which doctors are working with Botox for IC and Vulvodenia. I hope that you have wonderful results from this. Please let us know all about it!!!

Hi all: I have severe pain from my IC. Does botox help this? Sounds like it's for more just urgency and frequency (I have those too). Trying to find a doctor in Miami who will do Botox if I am a candidate; I'm in SEVERE pain. My regular Uro doesn't do Botox... Sara PS - I have a friend doing Botox for pain and he says it's helping him.

Hi Sara, There's a doctor in Miami who does botox injections for IC. His name is Dr.Gousse, his practice is at Jackson Memorial hospital and his number is: 305-243-6630. I plan on going to see him to get botox injections as my pian/frequency/urgency are unbearable. He is supposed to be the best IC specialist down here. Hope that helps

Dr. Gousse Dear stitches305, I am a patient of Dr. Gousse's also. He did not mention to me that he could do Botox injections at all and he knows that I failed every other IC treatment. He wants to do another hydro on me soon? Are you sure that he does Botox? Thanks! Sara

I just met with a new urogyn who does Botox for IC/PFD. She offered me the choice of doing in the hospital under IV sedation or trying it in her office while totally awake. She said she usually does it in the hospital, but thought I might be able to tolerate it in the office. To put it in perspective- I had natural childbirth, twice. So I think I can be tolerate it. I won't be having Botox in my bladder, just in the muscles surrounding it. I'd rather not deal with the hassle of going to the hospital and having IV sedation. Has anyone done Botox in the office before? thanks, Gigi

I had the surgery last Friday, no complications. He said if it works for me, I should notice in about a week. I'm keping my fingers crossed! I have an appt later on in the month, I am going to ask if my bladder looked any better than the last time I had my hydrodistention.
I fingers are crossed for you, too.

contents: I sure hope it helped!

contents: Update Well, it seems to have at least helped with the frequency/urgency, ESPECIALLY at night. I actually slept ALL NIGHT LONG WITHOUT HAVING TO PEE 2 nights in a row!!!! I haven't done that in over 3 years. I am in a great deal of pain at the moment, but I think that's the raging yeast infection that I can't get rid of. We'll see if it improves my pain. That is one reaon I wanted to have the Botox done ASAP- so I would know what my level of pain is when I see the pain specialist.

I saw on discovery health how it works.They did it in the neck of a guy with a spasm. Sometimes multiples injection are needed.If we knew how many it takes,that would be great. I wish it works well for you.
signature: Bentyl effects seems to wear out within weeks. I'm not using it much anymore.

contents: . Keeping you in my prayers that the botox will help and you will get great results. JJ

Is it possible? I just read an article on the use of botulinum toxin (Botox) for IC. Does anyone know anymore about this? anewday, if you read this, has the botox significantly lowered your pain since mid November? I can live with my level of frequency...it is the pain that is crippling for me...

signature: --------------------------------------------------- [SIZE="1"]1997 Lupus diagnosis 2002 Type II Diabetes 2007 Hashimoto's Thyroiditis/ Mixed connective tissue disease 2008 Interstitial cystitis/PFD

contents: Most insurances don't cover it b/c it is not FDA approved as a treatment and that sucks. It is considered experimental for IC etc. Maybe another reason it is not FDA approved is b/c the results are so inconsistent. I know one vial is like $250, depending on how much you want to use it...it can add up to thousands. If I ever do it, because it is an option, I'll make sure to have the insurance cover it. Doctors can play their part too, specially if you have tried several treatments and botox ends up working for you. That way the isnurance should cover it! appeal.....

contents: I had the injections yesterday into my pelvic muscles (not bladder) and my pelvic soreness is worse! Is this normal? Can it get worse before it gets better? Is heat or ice best for this? I have been using heating pad to try to calm muscles down. Could these injections have done damage and made things worse all together? Because it feels worse I have been afraid to sit even with my cushion. I have plans this weekend that require quite a bit of sitting...is it gonna effect any possible progress? Help! Would like to connect with others who have had this done.

contents: I'm having injections in the muscles next week, and my doctor said that I'll be pretty sore for the first few days, and maybe leaky.... Hope you start to feel better soon- keep us updated here
signature: I had major surgery for a genetic rectal prolapse in Aug.05 which I believe links directly to my new Dx of....PFD! March.08. I'm working with a great PT named Zelda- I think the world of her I have the same feelings for the team at the Kaiser Center for Pelvic Pain in SF- amazing people. I've had a cystoscopy, 2 cat scans, falied a potassium test and have had about 50 clean UA's. This is my hot water bottle- the fish. I love him! I'm also a big fan of my "Electric Fish," the TENS unit. Very helpful in a flare! **I'm getting Botox next week!** I take: Oxybutynin 10mg Elmiron 100mg 3x day Gabapentin 300mg 3x day Antihistamine, CystaQ, Bc, & Thyroid (125mcg) nightly I've also taken: Detrol LA Oxybutynin patch and lower dose pill Amitryptiline and Nortryptiline

contents: I had botox 4weeks ago now. The first few days lots of retetion as I mentioned in a previous post. I honestly havent felt any better. The pain is still there everyday. I was at the doc twice last week he said give it time and he was glad the retention part was done. I thought I would be feeling better. But..I am so sore the spasms are unbareable. I feel like I did before I was diagnosed. I can handle the pain for so long, then I just snap. I hate this condition it controls your life. I try to be brave everyday. But sometimes I just cant handle the pain. I want my life back. I have had 3 procedures with DMSO. I felt great immediately afterward but unfortunately didnt last very long. I dont mean to freak anyone out. The botox injections are supposed to be a miracle. But it just doesnt seem to be working for me. I dont even know whether to call the doc again or not I think he thinks I am crazy. But the pain I feel is real. I dont think there is anything worse than pain in your crotch area. Anyway thank you for listening. I just needed to get my mind off the pain. Again anniepie just take it easy and dont over do it.

How does botox help with pain in the bladder? I am just curious. I always thought botox was used to add bulk to an area( lips, face etc...). I still dont get how it can help with IC pain and frequency. Maybe it can help with someone who has severe incontinence, I can see that, but not with pain. Just curious?? Jen

Thank you for your concern. Quick update. It has now been 5 weeks. This week the pain as somewhat lessened. I didnt bother calling the doc. Because the discomfort has become bearable. Funnying thing though if I drink too much fluids in the day it flares up. I have been thinking about what Jen said about the botox and the bulk up look. OMG I have felt so bloated threw this whole recovery time. My stomach is usually quite flat but it is definately bloated. I cant even think about putting on a pair of jeans. I have worked out with weights for many years and since this procedure I just cant. Although I have become a cardio queen. Us women we are strong no matter the pain I try to exercise as much as possible. And actually it does make me feel better. I still wish that I felt better. I expected the botox to work really well especially having to pay for it. Thanks for your support.
Just a note, you are thinking of collagen that is used to "bulk up." Botox paralyzes tissue and muscles.

contents: update Hello fellow IC patients, As I have said I recieved botox in mid August I had a really hard time with it. Never really felt better after surgery until Mid October my husband and I went away for 3 days. I felt terrific. I did everything wrong too much coffee, too much wine and too much fun. I felt great. Then about 2 days after getting home I felt like I had a bladder infection. Of course as usual tests came up negative. I dont understand how quickly this disease comes and goes. The flare ups I mean cause normally. I eat and drink the same everyday. I dont normally drink more than 1 cup of coffee per day and very little alcohol if at all. Suddenly again today after 9days of feeling lousy today I am okay. This is so crazy. One week from now I am going to have DMSO TREATMENT. I am going under this time, previously I have had IV sedation. Good luck and God bless all of you who are having procedures and treatments. I wish you all the best and thank you for listening. IC HOPEFUL

My Experience I have had it in my pelvic muscles once before and I will have it again in November. I was sore for a while. It takes a while for it to kick in before I got real results with PT. Hope it gets better for you.

If you get botox in the crotch area and pelvic floor area, and you are "not supposed to apply any presssure on the site on the injections" like is says in a post up there. How in the world am I supposed to sit, drive, etc after the procedure? I'm thinking about botox and I know that at after teh injections, at the beginning you don't feel well, only after a few weeks (like when you have any surgery - so that part is, to me, very understandable) what I didn;t know was that you couldn't put any pressure on the area of the injections, now that is news to me. How is then that botox is also a treatment that can help vulvodynia? how is the post operatory for that???

contents: At my uro visit yesterday, he decided we will try Botox in my bladder. He is going to do a hydro/distention and inject the Botox in my bladder druing the procedure. He said he will inject the Botox into several spots on my bladder. He explained that it is not FDA approved but that he is very interested in and excited about this treatment and said that in Europe they are having very good success rates with it. At this point, I'll try anything! They are going to be calling me w/ a surgery date once they figure out who is going to pay for the Botox.
sorry for the double posting, don't know what happened...computer glitch I guess..anyway, mods, could you remove one of the threads?

contents: Hi, I have not posted in a long time but I am a 25 year old male with IC. Lately my frequency and Urgency has been really bad. My doctor wants to wait 3 months and if by that time it's still bad he wants me to talk to a female doctor he is in practice with who specializes in IC. He brought up how she has been doing Botox for IC. To anyone who has undergone it can they explain what's involved? reason I ask is 2 and half years ago I had the worst and most painful experience of my entire life when I under went a DMSO treatment. The amount of pain I was in was awful and my system was all messed up. Plus the catheter hurt me so badly that If I ever have to have anything in me I must be put to sleep. Any's thoughts who have had it done would be greatly appreciated.

I'm sorry you went through that horrible pain.I was the same when they did a DMSO to me.I made sure they put the cath in after I'm out.But since then I have learned to cath myself and put rescue instills in my bladder.I use a 10 fr it's really small and if you do it yourself you know where things hurt and where to go with it.Or stop and take a breather.Some places let you do it your self. Now to your question no I have not done Botox but I am thinking on doing it soon.I'm in the process on finding a URO that knows what IC is.Then trying new treatments.It really can be a struggle.I have two that want to put a Interstim in me.I don't think I'm ready for that yet.I've been in a flare for almost 2 yrs now. Anyway I seen your post and no one has answered yet.So I thought I would at least let you know on the cath for next time.I think that how they get the Botox in but I'm not sure
signature: Hugs Machele Diagnosed w/ IC Oct 98 via Hydro Migraines,IBS,VV,PFD,Endometriosis, insomnia,depression Disability in 99 very easy Hysterectomy in 2000 left in ovaries Right ovary out in March 03 Cysts Too many Hydro's to count Meds, Lortab Amitriptyline Up to 100mg Lorazapam. stopped useing.Trying benadryl for sleep Imitrex when needed plus injections if needed for migrains, Dance as if no one is watching.Laugh as much as you can.Live as if there's no tomorrow

contents: Is it possible that the botox put into the pelvic muscles can travel into the abdominal muscles? I mostly feel it when I do a lot of walking. That area just doesn't feel right. it's very sore and achy. I don't know if it's botox related or maybe part of PFD but since botox it feels weird in there.
signature: Facebook- Ann Stockmeyer Rachinger

contents: your transverse abdominus and other core muscles in your abdomen are linked to pelvic floor muscles - in that they are all part of a structural support system that steadies your torso when you do anything . It is conceivable that the botox has relaxed the pelvic floor so much that it isn't "pulling its weight" when it comes to supporting your regular movements, and thus perhaps your transverse and rectus abdominal and even your obliques are in overdrive mode. I don't think that the botox itself could have traveled to your abdominal muscles, but I don't know botox's capabilities... Do you know which muscles the botox was injected into?
signature: Onset of symptoms 1/04 after laproscopic appendectomy Diagnosed with prostatitis 3/04, again 6/04, again 9/04, went through various cycles of anti-biotics. Self diagnosed with PFD 6/05, received PT on and off for a year with good results but recurring symptoms. Self diagnosed with IC 2/07, officially diagnosed via potasium test 3/07 Current meds: Uracyst Benedryl Having great results with Uracyst Alex

contents: I don't know much from experience, but I was reading an article about cosmetic use of botox applied in the forehead wrinkles. One of the instructions is not to lower you head after the injections b/c the medication can travel to the eyelids, resulting in "eyelids droop". (Marieclaire magazine October 2008) That means the toxin could possibly migrate....however you should check with your doctor and mention that to him/her. See what they had to say. At any rate, pleae note that botox ears off and you may feel worse before you feel better. I hope that's the case. Take care.

contents: As a former plastic surgery nurse... it is possible for Botox to "migrate" but highly unlikely if you follow your docs precautionary instructions. I have a friend and another acquaintance that have done multiple Botox treatments, one for migraines. Neither have ever had complications.

I had these injections about two weeks ago now. One of the spots he injected was into the tail bone area since I have a lot of pain there and BMs make my pelvic pain worse. I have had excruciating pain in the tailbone area ever since. I called them to ask about it and he said maybe the needle going into the muscle stirred it up and give it a couple weeks. Well, here I am and still in agony! I am so scared! Does anyone know about nerve injuries at all? Is that what this could be? Do they heal on their own eventually? Really scared here.
signature: Facebook- Ann Stockmeyer Rachinger

contents: Anniepie, I am so sorry to hear you are feeling really bad. Please know that you are not alone. As I have said before I had botox treatment about 5 weeks ago now. Mine was in the bladder. I couldnt imagine having it in your tail bone. I havent felt really good since my treatment. I have had several ups and downs. But never complete relief. Last week was a really bad week. But the past 7 days have been bearable. Well today just got worse and worse. I dont mean to freak anyone out, about this treatment but.... I just cant handle the pain anymore. And the worst part is that my family gets the effects because my patients is zero. I really dont understand this illness. I hate having to deal with this daily. Believe me I try so hard to be positive and carry on about my day but sometimes I just want to snap!!!! Now I am trying to figure out what I did differently to cause this flare up. But my days are pretty much this same. I just hope someone out there is feeling some relief from this illness. Because today is a really bad day. The pressure I am feeling on my uretha is horrible. I am just glad that someone out there is listening to me ramble. It is so hard to communicate how I feel to my family. Actually I am just sick of being sick and I know they get scared when I feel bad. Anniepie hang in there and know that I am listening. And I understand what you are going through. I pray that this treatment works for you and relief comes soon.

I had a Botox injection in the bladder (trigone) and external urinary sphincter at the beginning of the summer. It took a couple of weeks to kick in, but I felt much improved for a few months. It wasn't a cure for me, but made life much more manageable. No hesitancy or retention either. I'm planning to go for another appointment soon.
signature: Clinical trial participants are urgently needed for research on IC/PBS to move forward. ICN Clinical Trials Page.

contents: cgirard, I am so glad to hear the botox treatment worked for you although short time the relieve must have been wonderful. I am just over 4 weeks and I had a horrible day of spasms a few days ago. I am a bit better now but I still dont feel like myself. I feel like I have to go to the bathroom even when I dont. I am on meds for frequency I take twice daily the doc suggested to take one. I am scared that it is going to get worse. If I stop meds. I really hope the full effects of the botox kick in soon because I dont have much faith in this treatment thus far. The recovery has been long. If anyone else has anymore info. on their progress it would be appreciated Thanks

I have had Botox injections several times for urge incontinence (UI) and my doctor injects it into the posterior (rear) wall of my bladder. This is typically where it is injected, because she uses a rigid scope and this is the only side the scope can reach. I have also have voiding if I empty completely that feels like a very severe bladder spasm. Next time I have it done, I'm considering asking her if she can somehow inject the Botox into the anterior wall (front) of my bladder to correct the UI and perhaps the voiding pain. Has anyone ever had Botox injections to the front wall of their bladder? Thanks,

Hi- I had the botox done 8/8 and still feel horrible. I am doing the PT and she did measure my tone and it has had a big affect with regards to that but the pain is driving me nuts. I was down to about 22 mg of morphine a day and now back up to 45 and still hurting. I am afraid to do the pt and at the same time want to give this 110%. Anyone have a similar experince?

Botox Botox does not "instantly" work. It took me about a month to begin feeling the full effects of the procedure. I do hope you feel better soon. I, too, was disappointed due to the fact noone explained to me that the effects were delayed, however it does help. Unfortunantly it only lasted a few months for me, but a few months is better than nothing! Best of wishes! Sheryle
signature: Prayerful blessings, Sheryle Good, Better, Best - Never let it rest... Until your Good is Better and your Better is Best!

contents: Oceandreamer, I had the botox injections 4 weeks ago. I did just babble on another thread. But I feel like crap too. First I had retention which was horrible. Now I just feel like I did before I was diagnosed. The spasms are unbearable. I have constant pain. I thought this was supposed to be some miracle treatment. One other thing I noticed the past few days was a bit of an odour down there is this normal? I know when I had the DMSO I had bad breath for awhile. I was just at the uroligist twice last week. He didnt seem concerned about my feeling the urgency to urinate and pressure on my bladder. I feel worse than I did before the treatment. I think he thinks I am crazy. But the pain just doesnt stop. I am doing my best to just carry on with work and family duties but this evening I just snapped. I dont even say anything about it to my family anymore because I am tried of listening to myself. I am sick of being sick. One more thing do you have a bloated feeling? I have no desire to wear anything form fitting. I am not happy with this botox crap at all. I really hope the medication kicks in soon. Thanks for listening. It feels better to know that I am not alone. I hope you feel better soon.

I had Botox 4 days ago and was told that I could have 3-5 days of flare and then in 7-10 days I would feel benefits. I am worried. I felt worse the first few days and despite the pain walked around the fair for a few hours. The next day I was worse. Then despite the usual home care I was constipated. I was unable to empty my bladder last night. I took a laxative and it helped but I am worried that 1) I over stressed the muscles and that might somehow affect the results and 2) I can already feel a difference in the muscles. It does feel a little different already. I am nervous. How does it feel different?

Where did you have Botox? I had mine in the pelvic floor area. My urogyn said that it would take several days for it to have full effect. It took me about 2 to 3 weeks before I would feel the effects. And he was right. It was in pain til then. Hope this helps.

I had mine in the pelvic floor - about 30. The first few day was a constant feeling like I had a lidocaine injection. I was having them in the vaginal wall. Now I sort feel like I did before the procedure with that feeling like I have a bowling ball in my pelvis except an occassional sharp pain and then hesitancy when I urinate. I have the same sort of numb feeling I would get after the lidocaine ointment I was using on the vulva and inner vaginal area. I was told the bowling ball feeling would go away and she said it would help her determine if the other pain- burning etc. was nerve pain or muscle pain. Is it a wake up and feel different kind of thing?

For me, after the waiting period had passed, it was sort of gradual. The pain from my vulvodyna (sp) area didn't subside. It wasn't until I had Pelvic PT and the doc rechecked me that I discovered the pain from those things were disminished. The flare pain was less intense.

Oceandreamer, Sounds like we are in similar situations. I recieved botox injections about 11 days ago now inside the bladder. It hasnt been a pleasant experience. I went about 6 days with this heavy feeling in my pelvic area before going to the doc. Did I mention my doc is on holidays? Anyway his nurses in office were very accomodating. I needed a catheder and I was quickly relieved of 2 cups of fluid. But the relieve was only temporary. Next day same thing. 3rd day Went to an on call uroligist. He suggested stay in catheder for about 5 days. we were to meet at hospital. Anyway didnt exactly work out because he had emergency surgery. He did call me later that night. Went back to nurses in my doc office next day she scanned me and I was down to less than 1 cup in my bladder. We did catheder anyway but she said it wasnt necessary. I am on the road to recovery. I still feel the heaviness in the pelvic area. which is still a discomfort not as bad as before but...still there. I have recieved several DMSO treatments which recovery was much faster. It worked terrific but for such a short time. This is such a horrible illness it really interferes with daily life. Its funny though when I am good. I feel so good. When I have a flare up watch out I am a different person. I just wanted you to know that by reading your post last week It gave my some comfort to know that the symtoms were so similar I need not panic. Please keep me posted with your progress. I am going to work all weekend even though I am not 100%. But it might take my mind off the discomfort. If I knew that recovery was this bad I might have tried another option before botox. Especially because the huge cost involved. And still feel crappy. Thanks for listening.

Some thoughts Sorry to hear you are going through this after botox. It is an experimental treatment with different outcomes. Botox can give you retention, I know some people take a medication that is given to patients with prostate cancer, (I don't know the name of the medication) but it it supposed to help you urinate. For those of you reading this post and thinking about about getting botox, I suggest you: 1- To have the procedure with a doctor that has experience on it (do not be a guinea pig ~ although the procedure is experimental so you are kind of a guinea pig by default) 2-The first time you get it, tell your doctor to take it slow. do not get it all over the place as you don't know how you'll react. **Just wondering if a Test can be made prior to the procedure? i.e. just a few injections the first time? see how you react, and then increase. Hope you feel better.

contents: Thank you for your reply. I am about17 dys post op now. Not feeling too bad. My doc has been on Holidays. But he is he best in our area. Like when I had my tonsils removed, OMG the first days were horrible! my throat hurt so much I had to take codeine...but later felt so much better. Just a comparison ;-)

contents: Botox Originally posted by mlzippy7 Has anyone done botox for IC without sedation just in the drs office?? Has anyone had to cath after getting botox? Thanks for the info!! Good Morning, Have had Botox twice now and do not need sedation. Had four needles. I go every 8 weeks and it has helped with sex but I still need to follow my diet and take my meds. I mainly have it done for my husband and I and our marriage. No cath either, might have a little incontinence, no big deal. You'll do fine. Good luck!
Oh my dr said he does a cystoscopy in order to do the botox. Is that how yours was done? All the drs do it so different it seems.

contents: Botox Guess they do do it differently. I have gone to a pain center for a year, had three nerve blocks and pudendal nerve block and nothing worked. Botox was my last hope, had hydrodistention, p/t etc. I guess if he wants to do it that way he does have his reasons and he probably will sedate you. I just have the shots and go home. Maybe the next time that's what he will do. Let me know how it goes and how you do. All the best.

I was told that they can numb you if you are having it in your bladder and that can be done in the office. My Dr. said it also depends on how many injections are being done. I had about 15 to each side of my pelvis floor not in the bladder itself. This was to help her determine how much of the pfd was causing my symptoms vs bladder itself. I was in a lot of pain after. But back to my version of normal after about 5 days.

Originally posted by Free Bird Good Morning, Have had Botox twice now and do not need sedation. Had four needles. I go every 8 weeks and it has helped with sex but I still need to follow my diet and take my meds. I mainly have it done for my husband and I and our marriage. No cath either, might have a little incontinence, no big deal. You'll do fine. Good luck! did you have it in the bladder or just Pelvic floor? I totally understand your reasons to do it...glad it works. I'm also thinking of botox with no sedation.... general anesthesia lowers the immune system among other things...(not to everybody though) so if it works for someone to have it under anesthesia, great! less pain.

contents: Botox Hi Angeles, I had the Botox in my pelvic floor where most of my pain is. I have mainly urethral pain and have had it for l3 years. Not much bladder pain, unless I really screw up my diet which I am really trying to stick to. My dr. used half a bottle of Botox B and that took the four shots. I know we are all diff. in our pain so we need diff. amounts. Right now it is trail and error. Hope you are doing well and all continue to improve. Found this in one of my books and it helps. The secret of health for both mind and body Is not to mourn for the past (I have lost a husband) not ot worry about the future, or not to anticipate troubles, but to live the present moment wisely and earnestly.

contents: I am going to jump right to the issue I need help with. I had my first treatment of botox done on March 2nd 2007. My docotor did a Cysto/hydro then the botox. The procedure worked and I went from having urge/frequency of 30 times a day and pain t0 no pain and peeing 8-12 times a day. Now my insurance paid for everything but the botox. Because he was doing a hydro to ? IC. This is what he put down for codes. "IC" Well now that I have researched all of this and found a way to get the botox reimbused with the help of some of you ladies on this site. I need help in knowing how your doctors do the procedure to just do the botox injections. This is Why. My doctor does not feel that Hydro's have any medical benifits. He doesn't feel it helps with the pain or urge/frequency. I read what alot of you women say and some of you do Hydros only to help with your symptoms. So because he feels hydros don't help with the IC. He will only do a cysto to inject the botox. Well my insurance will not pay for a cysto to do just botox. So now I know how to get the botox covered but the procedure is alot more money then the botox and I can't get the procedure covered. What I need to know is what do your doctors put in your charts and as codes for the reason he is going in as a cysto to do the botox injections. If we could come up with a reason for him going into my bladder other than for botox injections 6 months after he already did a cysto/hydro we will be golden. Or Could some of you lead me to places I can get stuff that is written to prove to him they Hydros do help the IC. He did all his training in Cleveland Medical Center. He is really good and had done and done out of his way for me many times. I am going on my first vacation trip at the end of September with my kids (disney) and I want to feel good for it and I want to have another treatment at the end of Aug so I will feel good. My doctor said he will do it if I can get the insurance to cover the procedure. Please if anyone has any ideas please e-mail me. Thank you P.S I have already been through the botox reimebursement hotline. I need to know how to get the insurance to pay for the procedure. Thanks

contents: Botox I need someone's help...Is it painful having the botox treatment done? I'm going to see my bladder today and want to discuss with him about the botox injections. I have been in severe pain now for three weeks, with one trip to the hospital and no relief in sight which is a little unusual since I do have days with no pain. I take percoset for the pain which I have to double up on and it is not helping me at all, I have to do this every 4 hrs. I've had Chronic IC now for 5yrs and my doctor is doing everything possible for me, whenever I suggest a new treatment that Ive read about he goes and checks it on his computer and agrees to try it. I've been on so many different kinds of oral medications and have had several surgeries done with no relief (my first one done 5yrs ago did make my IC go away for a year but then it came back) I'm so depressed and fed up with being in so much pain, and I really don't think my husband has any idea of how much pain I go through. Has anyone out there ever been prescribed Morphine for severe pain? I want to ask my doctor to change my pain meds but don't know what I can handle since he put my percoset up to the second strength and it made me sick so now I'm back down to the lowest dose. God I'm fed up. sorry...
Hi IC/IBS Hopeful I have Botox done for my bladder and my insurance pays for the botox. I have a cysto and the botox done in the doctor office. Deborah Bush
signature: I have IC, the IBS, Heartburn, High bloodpressure,thyrod problems, depression and clorties pain.

contents: I am sorry you are having difficulties and I know how you feel. I also have a wonderful doctor who goes out of his way for me. I will not lie to you, and it is different for everyone. for me the first time I had the botox I also had a hydrodistention done. It was my first time for both. My doctor did a hydro basically to prove to me that I had IC. I didn't want to believe it, he knew but wanted to prove it to me. well the outcome of the hydro gave us alot of info. My bladder could only be stretche with me asleep to 450 cc. while I am awake I can only hold 200cc (very small bladder) normal is 1000 to 1500 cc. He also told me in his words "your bladder is a bloody mess" I have hemerages every where. then he proceded to do 33 injections of botox using 100 units of botox. Yes, I was in extreme pain when I woke up. my doctor washed my bladder out with lidocane and pumped me with morphine to get the pain under control. After about 5 hours I was better and sent home. I had a rough 4 days after and then it began to heal. I went from peeing 30 times a day to 8to10. It lasted about 4 months like this and now the urge and pain is increasing again. but they say the more you have the longer and better you feel. I also think he will use 150units next time instead of 100 units. I hope this info helps you. Remember it is different for everyone.

contents: abdominal botox I really hope you are feeling better and are able to do the botox, because I think that it has a large place in treating ic pain. In my case last week was the first injections that I have had ,having mine done in the skin above the pubic line. The procedure was quick and painless and I do think it has helped me with the "constantly touching my bladder" viceral pain. I still need my oxycontin, but my pain is controlled now with the injections and the pain meds. I think docs know how to code the botox in a way to get it thru. BTW...My skin almost a week later where I had the botox done, is still purple and black. As for me I am too worried about retention issues to have it in my bladder. However I am so happy that we have it to add to the ic arcenal! Hope this helps someone!

contents: I am just after some advice on botox. I am very interested in trying the botox injections. Can anyone advise me on what sought of relief you may get from the procedure. my major concern is being unable to pee afterwards because having to catherterize scares me to death but I think I need to try something different. i have been on Elmiron and pain relief meds for almost six months and although they have helped a little I am starting to think I want to go for something else and take the next step. I dont have alot of frequency but a constant pain and feeling of infection and like all of us I am feed up. my doctor wants to try installations next but this also scares me because I have read you can get worse before better and with 3 small children and a job i dont think i can afford to get any worse. Im confused on where to go next. help.

botox injections I do Botox injection for urgency&freqency and was going 60 times a day and know I am going 6 to 7 times a day. I really never had problems with retation after having the botox done. They put the injection into the bladder wall and have 150 units done. I tried the interstim first without any luck and when I tryed the Botox I got 100% relief. signature: I have IC, the IBS, Heartburn, High bloodpressure,thyrod problems, depression and clorties pain.

contents: I am having the botox done again. My Dr. says he has grounds to do a cysto so that the insurance will cover the cysto. I will have the botox reimbursed by the botox reimbursement hotline. I am not sure if just having botox injections done is very painful. I have only had it done once and it was done along with a hydrodistention. I think the hydo is what caused all my pain. I will find out on Sept4 when we do just botox injections. Last time we did only 100 units. I did not have any retention. This time I am going to try to convince my Dr. to do 150 units. I feel the last time it helped me alot and loved the results but it only lasted about 4 months. I still had some spasms (not as many) but they were still there. I feel I need more units to control that. As far as the relief I had. With out it I can not wait long between voids. I feel (urge) to pee all the time. I have to ask myself is it real or is it just an urge. My bladder only holds 200cc so for me it doesn't take long to fill the bladder. If my bladder is fun I will spasm and can not wait at all because of the pain. With the botox I didn't have the urges inbetween voids. if I felt the urge and I was in the middle of something I could actually say to may self I will finish my task that I am doing then I will go pee. I had no cramping (spasms) unless I waited way to long and my bladder was very full and at this point it was the fact that my bladder is very small not the IC. So for me and my family botox made a huge difference. I think the comments from my family & co workers made the results more real for me. they were like wow you are feeling much better aren't you? and I would be like what? they would say well you are not running to the bathroom so much any more. and I would then be like ya your right!. As far as may family I didn't have to pull the car over anymore or say hurry home I have to pee. It was more relaxing. Once it started to wear off they could tell right away. I am back to franticlly finding bathrooms or always in the one at home. This is how the botox has impacted my life.

contents: RE: Getting botox paid for I was able to get my botox treatment paid for by doing research on my insurance company's website and finding their position paper on it. Turns out my condition ("Incontinence due to detrusor instability and/or neurogenic bladder not responsive to anticholinergics) was covered. However, it took several letters (two initial and two appeals) and six months to them to cover it. Be prepared for them to lose your information several times. One of their tactics to get people to give up!

botox for vaginal burning Has anyone gotten relief from vaginal burning from having botox shots? I know this sounds funny but I just do no want to feel my vagina anymore as it has been a source of pain for fourteen years. I am going to see a specialist at the enc of the month and I was just wondering what to expext. I have had five surgeries so my pain is due to a lot of nerve damage and I think I should be on lyrica or neurontin or something like that. Narcotics really do not work well for me for the burning pain. Please help me if you can. I feel like I do not know where to turn next Thanks

I had the botox treatment 3 times. My treatments were in my bladder not my vaginal walls. So I can not say if it will help. Botox freezes the muscels, it doesn't always take away the pain. I had good results the first time with urge and frequency and it lasted 4 months. the next 2 times was not so lucky, I also got a bad side affect the 2nd time it caused me not to be able to pee on my own. I have to straight cath everytime I have to pee. It has been almost a year with this problem. so be careful. I can only tell you what happen to me. I can't tell you what it will do for you.

contents: Botox into pelvic floor muscles Please don't be apprehensive about the Botox injections into your pelvic floor muscles. if you have a competent physician who knows what he/she is doing then you're in for a treat!!! I have had these same injections every 3-4 months for the last three years, and they have been an absolute blessing. I began with decompression surgeries (pain went from 8-9-10 down to 5-6-7), then with the Botox the pain went from 5-6-7 down to 1-2-3, and now with pelvic floor rehabilitation I am on my way to ZERO!!!!:woohoo::woohoo: Negative side effects usually include only slight discomfort at the injection sites for a brief period of time, and if your physician really knows what they're doing they will combine a local, along with a short acting corticosteroid (like Decadron) and the Botox. Hence, you will be quite comfortable. Also, if it is suggested that you have Botox into the bladder proper, then don't let your fear stop you, as the effects are temporary, and via Wallarien regeneration the dendritic growth of new synaptic connections will manifest within 3-4 months and any negatives will become history. Though I have heard of few negatives being that problematic. One woman found she needed to wear Poise pads for 3-4 days following injection into the bladder proper (hardly anything to become terribly concerned about!!) Take care and the very best of luck to you Karen Wiesner

I am told mine will be about 30. I am scheduled for 8/8/08. I have wonderful insurance but I think its usually by the vial not the injection. I would call your insurance and ask if you can buy the whole vial and make sure they understand this is for theraputic not palative care and that this is medically needed. Any insurance should cover if the Dr. write's it correctly. I ahve seen several posts to how this was done. You may need to appeal but it should be covered. Are you on FMLA? This is the law that protects your job if you need to care for you or a family member with a serious health condition? if you are this would help your case. Good luck! I have had several surgeries and did home and office instillations for years but am still nervous. Good Luck!!

Keep us posted on the results...I hope all goes well Botox has been a lfe saver for many. I think it is very important the doctor knows what he/she is doing and I woul start with less and the depending on the results, increase. I'm glad to hear people do well on it.

I went to a pt and she said my muscle tension was normal. How can this help me then? Please share My main symptoms are pelvic pain, urgency, nocturia, and vaginal pain.

Botox vs instills I have had Botox injected to the pelvic floor, bladder and sphincters all at one time (under anesthesia). I had 26 injections in all. It seemed a little "over-kill" at first but was an answer to prayer! I had some bleeding and a bit of retention for about 24 hours but I still say "go for it!" Just remember, Botox is a poison and it temporarily paralyzes so, you might need to wear some hefty pads for a few days. I had NO control over my bladder for about 3 days and everything started getting back to normal after that. I wish you the best!

Botox Hi I can't tell you about botox in the bladder,but I get botox in in forehead and kneck every three mos for migraines, only thing that has helpped for me. My Dr. said if I was going to try it once I needed to do it twice because it works better the secound time, and he was right. I was doing so well once that I cancelled my appointment to see if I could at least space out the injections, that was a mistake as it wore off the headaches came back. Then a mounth later I called to get an appointmnet for the shots and I had to wait another month, I can tell you it was a long two months. I can't say about the bladder, but it does work well for me for headaches. But you need to keep in mind once you get it, if it dosen't work or makes you worse you can't take it out of your body. contents: Originally posted by ABliske Today my uro said he knows a pain specialist who has been having good results with botox injected into the pelic floor. Has this helped any of you? My uro has injected it into people's bladders too, but says it's not as effective as when it's done in the pelvic floor. I don't even think I have PFD. I went to a pt and she said my muscle tension was normal. How can this help me then? Please share My main symptoms are pelvic pain, urgency, nocturia, and vaginal pain. The goal of Botox injections is to relax spastic muscles, so if you don't have tension, I'm not sure it would be of much help. However, maybe you do have PFD, and the PT missed it (this would be very likely if she didn't do an internal exam). It's probably worth getting a second opinion with another very experienced physical therapist; some of them notice things that others don't. Overall, Botox in the pelvic floor is a pretty simple procedure, and lower risk than bladder Botox (which can send you into retention). Put it this way: I am considering a pelvic floor botox injection, even though I don't have much pelvic floor tension. Another thing to ask your urologist is where he usually injects the Botox into the bladder -- the detrusor, the base, or the trigone. Most urologists inject the detrusor, because that's where overactive bladder patients need it. However, IC is different from OAB, and it seems that IC patients might benefit more from injection into the trigone and bladder base. For example, this is what Dr. Chris Smith does: How many actual shots are given during a treatment? One patient reported 25 separate BOTOX injections into her bladder muscle during a treatment. That seems extreme. (Dr. Smith) 20-30 injections. We need to spread toxin around the bladder, particularly the trigone and base of the bladder http://www.ic-network.com/guestlectures/smith.html

contents: Thanks cgirard! you have been very helpful in responding. No problem! I thought you always had to be under general anesthesia to receive botox injections, in the pelvic floor, etc. I know for the ones who get it in teh bladder go under general anesthesia. Actually, even for the bladder, only some doctors put you under general anesthesia and do a hydrodistension at the same time as the Botox injection. Others will just use local anesthesia (i.e. lidocaine in the bladder); all they do is fill the bladder with some saline and then inject the bladder. If you phone around to a few urologists you might find one who does it without general anesthesia.

contents: I think the above comment about "instillations" referred to the usual IC instillations (lidocaine, marcaine, heparin etc). I don't think it was a reference to botox instillations (that wounldn't be possible). Many doctors do instillations as a complementary treatment to any other IC treatment the patient is receiving. In the above case probbably the doctor wanted to combine botox injections + the usual IC instillations (lidocaine, marcaine, heparin, DMSO etc you name it) as part of a treatment, and it sounds like a good plan. The idea behind it is to help reduce or eliminate the pain signals in teh bladder via instillations in conjunction with another treatment: because pain creates/agravates pelvic floor dysfunction in most cases.

contents: botox for burning pain in vagina I have two questions that I hope someone can help me with. First I do not have a verified case of IC however I have had five pelvic floor recontructive surgeries and have so many symptoms like the rest of you lovely people. I have a feeling of constant pressure. pulling, and burning in the upper vaginal floor. Has anyone had botox injections that helped them with vaginal issues. I just do not want to feel my vagina--I know that sounds strange but this burning pain is not an infection and it has been with me for 14 years. Secondly does anyone know the exact location on the tibal ankle for acupunture to be applied. If somone has a picture of it that they could scan into an e-mail or a web page with it I would be most appreciative. Guess I have one more question--has anyone found cymbalta to be a great drug for pain. I took it once and it made me extremely tired combined with pain medication. Thank you all so much . It feels so good to be a part of this group because believe me most people do NOT get it. Blessings to all of you

Hi all, I have severe IC and the next idea from my urologist is to get Botox. But I feel like a I need to be prepared before I see her again with some of my own research! I don't feel like I suffer from an over active bladder! I was diagnosed with ulcers/inflammation in my bladder which ultimately reduced it's size and proper function. So should I be suggesting to get the injections in the base of the bladder rather than in the pelvic floor?? I have read though that this causes more problems with retention? Thnx

botox My IC dr had scheduled me for a hydro w/Botox injections into the bladder. He said it had helped reduce pain in many and also frequency. He also mentioned some people have retention. As I self-instill, I wasnt so worried about that as I have retention issues now from time to time where I need to self-cath for relief. My dr said it would be injected into the inside of the bladder. I wasnt aware that the shot were also available for PFD. It would be great to do both! Take care, Annette

contents: Botox shots during upcoming hydro I saw Dr Robert Evans in Greensboro NC last week (my IC dr). As my symptoms and pain arent improving w/tradition methods and treatment, we scheduled a hydrodistention for mid-July (this will be my 4th). He said they were doing trial of Botox injections during the hydro...did I want one? It was totally optional. He warned me that the main side effect was retention. I already do home instills, so have plenty of cathethers to take care of that problem if it occurs. I said "Yes, let's go for it!" He said it has helped reduce frequency in many patients, and reduce pain in about 1/3rd of the patients that have tried it. Not only that, but his hydros are better! I dont know what they do differently, but he's the only dr that's given a hydro that offered any relief! Seriously! He said their results were relief in over 60% of patients; most uros were around 30%! He said the Botox was going to shot into my bladder...didnt say exactly where and I didnt ask. I'll keep you posted after the procedure to let you know how it goes. I'm ready to try most anything. I would even consider the injections into the pelvic floor...he didnt mention that. PM if you'd like more details on this dr. Take care! Annette

Has anyone had any sucess in Botox. My first botox injection (42 injection overkill) I thought i would feel like a whole new women but i guess not the doctor i believe was new to botox do you think he could have done something wrong? or i just didnt respond to it .. I dont urinate as often like a use to but isnt it suppost to help with this severe pain?

contents: I would like to tell everyone my experience with my second round of botox. The first time my doctor injected 100units (one bottle) spread in 33 injections throughout the bladder he also did a hydrodistention. I went from peeing 30 times a day to 8to10 times a day and feeling great. It lasted 4 months then the symptoms returned. We repeated the botox and hydrodistention 6 months later. The doctor injected 180units this time in hopes to have the wonderful feeling last longer. This time he injected the botox then did the hydro. Last time he did the hydro first and then the botox but it was way to bloody he couldn't see very well to put the botox this is why the second time he did the oposite. This last time he was able to stretch the bladder to 400 the first time and 500 the second. This is more than the first hydro as he only got it to 450cc. The bladder looked better no cracks like last time but plenty of hemerages. The thought it was because they did the botox first so the bladder was more relaxed. I still had plenty of pain & nausea when I woke up. Things seem to go well until the next morning when I discoved I was only peeing 25 to 50 cc but peeing every 15 minutes. I called the doctors office and he suggested I cath myself and I did. I had 375cc left in me. You guessed it the botox made is to I can't pee on my own. It has been 6 weeks and I still have to cath my self 8 to 10 times a day. Funny thing is I have no urgency or pain between Which I think is great. The part I don't like is my bladder starts to spasm at 250cc. If there is more than 250cc in my bladder I can pee out the extra usually about 50cc my bladder will not stretch more than that and at that point I have such pain. Like I told the doctor if I had a big bladder I would have no problem with this. So I just wanted to tell my story. Will I do botox again? well when the symptoms wear off and the urge & frequency & Pain come back. I will probably do it again until they find some other treatment. Cathing is not as bad as it sounds. Lynn

contents: Question I was told by my doc that the Botox makes it harder for the bladder muscles to be controlled - so there might be incontinence...however, I've never had to be cathed unless it was for a medical procedure... is that something that has to be taught? I am wondering how botox can help IC? You would think it would make it harder for the bladder to work. Jen

Lynn, I agree I had my first injections in dec 2007 and for sure the self cath is a trade off but well worth not having the pain and urgency!!!! Im going for my second round today. it has poped its ugly head again my Dr gives me a full 300 although I am a man and my bladder only holds 75cc and I go CRAZY at that point . but Im all for the Botox!!!!!! makes life as normal as its going to be. BTW im a male I guess not many men get this problem.
I dont' think it would really work, but someone mentioned it to me. Thanks- Melanie

contents: Hello everyone, I am desperate to find something that will give me relief. My doctor mentioned about getting Botox injections in the bladder. Has anyone had this? Does it work? He mentioned that it may cause some urinary retention and I would have to self-cath myself many times throughout the day. Is this true? I am willing to travel anywhere to get this if I know it will help even a little bit. Thanks, Ashlee

Thursday Hi.. I will have had this experience coming up this Thursday afternoon. I hope I won't have to cath myself as I have to get on a plane the next day. Have you decided yet? I can let you know afterwards how it was for me, if you wish. I think there are others who have had more experience. In that case, I'm curious to find out their experiences. Thanks

Had my first shots, four, of Botox in my uretha and the sides of my vagina. Most of my pain is utrethal, with bladdar pain at times. I am on Opana for pain but it makes me so tired my dr. at the pain center is changing it next week. The Opana does work so I'm not anxious to try something new that might upset my stomach. I have had this mess for l3 yrs., right after my hysterctomy. I could deal with it if it was not affecting my urethra. The pain somedays is horrible. I have noticed that if there is a low coming in and the weather is rainy I hurt a lot more. Does anyone have that problem. I will try the Botox again in four months because there was some relielf. The pain of the shots is not too bad at all. I was hoping the Botox would take all the pain away and I wouildn't need pain meds. Found out diff. I'll be on pain meds forever with the Botox giving me about 50% relief. It's worth it. I have days I can wear jeans and that says something. So glad you all are here, if you don't have IC no one understands your pain and you can feel very alone. Thanks for being here and listening. Oh yeah, I stress, big time and believe that cause a lot of my pain.

and welcome to the ICN... I really am glad that the Botox is helping you. Oh boy do I know what you are talking about with stress. Stress is my #1 trigger. If I start worrying about something or start to feel anxious about a situation, my muscles begin to tighten and the next thing I know, my bladder begin to flare. I have also found that being cold does the same thing.

contents: Botox Hi, how was this procedure for you? Were you sedated at all? I'm going to have botox injections on in a couple of weeks. Any info would be great. Stephanie

I'ts not bad Hi Steph, No they did not put me to sleep, though at first I wanted them to. There really is no reason. It goes fast and the needles are very thin. There is a little pain, nothing we can't handle and a little bleeding. Wear a pad. That stops quickly. You will be numb which actually felt great. My dr. used only half the vial because of incontinence. I had no trouble with that at all. I'll have it again in 4 mths. and he will put the needles somewhere else down below. I also have PNE and spasms, it did cause a little bit more spasms but they stopped in a couple of days. I can wear jeans for awhile and have more sex which is a major problem for a lot of us. So I would say it helped about 50%. Hopefully it will even be better the next time. I never give us hope. Don't be afraid of this procedure there is nothing to it. Hope I helped, ask me any questions you need to. Will be thinking of you.
Keep us posted on how Botox works. Stress is also a big factor for me. This last weekend was proof of that.

contents: Botox: Insurance and Anesthesia I'm trying to answer two questions here at once. I would never have Botox without being under anesthesia; I had approximately 30 injections. The pain was so intense when I woke up from the anesthesia that I was kept on the IV for 22 hours afterward in order to have pain meds administered. Maybe I'm just a sissy, but I wouldn't think of having it any other way. As far as insurance: Ask your Doctor if he/she can do the hydrodistention with instill and while he's doing that (which insurance covers) if he could inject the botox. I have to pay out of my own pocket for the medication (botox) but it is well worth it. Hope this helps those of you who sent me questions! Have a great day!
signature: Prayerful blessings, Sheryle Good, Better, Best - Never let it rest... Until your Good is Better and your Better is Best!

contents: If you have BCBS FEP, Botox is a covered medical benefit. If you try to get a prior approval for the Botox, you will receive a denial letter because the FDA has listed Botox as a experimental treatment for IC. However, if your doctor bills for the Botox along with the other charges for the cysto, BCBS will cover the Botox.

Botox Payment I do have BXBS but we are HMO and they flatly denied payment. I appealed and they denied again. I appealed the second time and they turned me down the third time so I had to pay out of pocket.

contents: I had the botox injections done along with a hydrodistention and my insurance paid for it. I did have to pay the urologist a deposit of $500.00 and I did not get any of it back. I had injections and hydro done in the hospital in the outpatient department. My insurance paid 100% of the hospital expenses which were $16,000.00. I did not have good results with the botox injections and my pain levels were so intense. It took me 3 weeks to recover, I had to take Predinsone afterwards, there were days that I don't even remember, and it made it hard to urinate. I would never do it again, but I know others have had good results. We are all so different and what works for some does not work for others. I hope if you do get to have them they work well for you. Good Luck!!
