contents: Botox is diferent for everyone. The first time I had it done. It was great I went from peeing 30 times aday to 8 to 10 and had no urge or frequency inbetween, and I could hold more. It only lasted 4 months. The second time it made me go into retention so bad that I have to cath everytime I need to go pee. It did decrease the frequency and urge. It only lasted about 3 months then I started to have a lot of spasms and pain. They did it again to get me out of the pain but this time they did it while I was awake with no pain meds. I was in so much pain I was screaming. I had it done this last time on Dec 24th and I am already starting with pain and it hasn't even been 2 months. so like I said it is different for everyone. Lynn

contents: Botox Thanks for the info on Botox. I was recently in Calif. and had an unexpected trip to the hospital for what they thought was a stroke but thank goodness it wasn't. However, I was referred to a Uro there for my IC pain and he suggested I look into Research Testing for clinical trials as it was very expensive any other way. This wonderful Dr gave me samples and RX for Urelle. I had immediate relief the next day. I now take it 3 times a day and am pain free after 2 1/2 years. I still follow the IC diet as I'm afraid to get the pain started again. Has anyone taken Urelle? And if so were you able to add the questionable foods back? Also how did you arrive at the decision to use Botox as another option for pain. Thanks for your answers. Judith

I just had my Botox done on Friday in Miami, Fl. I had it done in the doctor office and I had 150 units put in my bladder wall. I just can not believe you are having 300 units done!!! You are ready to walk out of the office when it is done and it takes only 10 min. for doctor to put the Botox injection in you. I did a 3 year study on the Botox with Dr. GOUSSE in Miami, FL and he is really very get doctor to do Botox injection on people!!!! It has been over 4 years since I have been doing the Botox injection with Dr. GOUSSE. After the study I got my insurance to pay for my Botox and I was lucky that they payed for it. The Botox is working for my urgency&Freuqency. Good luck doing your Botox!!!! Deborah Bush

I can not pee on my own and have to cath. this makes the bladder much more sensitive when I stick the catheter in. I have lots of blood clots and it hurts when the catheter touches the sides of the bladder which causes more pain. Has anyone had this expericence? Lynn

I am scheduled to see another doctor for pain in January, I am also going to schedule some Biofeedback therapy and see if that help any. If this route doesn't work... I'm sorry the botox didn't work for you. From what I understand what your bladder looks like is not always an indication of how bad you feel. When I had my hydro the uro I was seeing at the time told me my bladder looked completely normal. Of course he told me this when I was coming to in the recovery room and totally lost it. I couldn't figure out how I could feel so horrible all the time and have a "normal" bladder. Well it turned out there were tons of mast cells everywhere and I did get the diagnois of IC. My current dr said he would have diagnoised me on symptoms alone because they are pretty bad at times. I kind of liken it to endo where you can have a little and have tons of pain or have a lot and have no pain. There just seems to be no rhyme or reason sometimes. I hope you find something that helps.

contents: Botox Botox injections basically "paralyze" the area for an undetermined time. Some get relief for up to a year, others, like myself only a couple of months. However, two months is better than nothing. My insurance will not pay for the procedure, so I worked out a deal with my doctor. He does a cystoscopy ( which my insurance DOES pay for) and I pay for the botox which he injects during the cysto procedure. There are ways to work around insurance.... Blessings, Sheryle

contents: my doc wants to inject my pelvic floor muscles with botox? I wanted to know if anyone had success or failures with this ...any feedback would be greatly appreciated .....

Botox of pelvic floor muscles Yes, I have had multiple injections of Botox into the muscles of my pelvic floor for pudendal neuropathy (which includes the symptoms of I.C.). I've been doing this every 12 weeks for the last two years with EXCELLENT results. Pain is diminished by 95-97% and this allows me to have pelvic floor rehabilitation (P.T.) which should, over time, allow me to discontinue the Botox and have a pelvic floor which is no longer painful & dysfunctional. It only takes about 15 minutes and the discomfort is minimal. GO FOR IT!!! Karen Wiesner :woohoo:

My former doctor (who just retired) was very leading edge in his treatments - when botox was - approved, I think - almost 3-4 years ago, he immediately suggested I get the treatment, even though I was relatively stable using prozac, instillations and birth control pills, and hate anethesia and the whole surgical route. So I did it, it was covered by insurance. I had a flareup immediately due to the procedure, but after that, it was like I was normal. I quit the prozac and instillation. For 2.5 months. We were expecting up to 6 months. And when it flared, it ws full force, and my doctor was out of town. His referral doctor did an instillation, which quieted it down, and recommended trying Detrol LA instead of prozac - which was he best advice I have gotten since the initial diagnosis 10 years ago! When my doctor came back I contacted him regarding doing the treatment again, and he shut me down - after the conversation, piecing comments together, it appeared that he did not actually file it as "botox" treatment, as the insurance would not have approved that, and they found out what he was doing after several procedures were performed, and threatened to cut him off. Meanwhile, I found that Detrol solved whatever problems I was having - at least until my estrogen started fluctuating heavily last year, but that is another story involving cycle tracking and Premarin. I would go back for botox in a heartbeat if it A) lasted longer B) was covered by insurance- because I've ony got about 10 years (now 50) before excess estrogen becomes an issue, per all the docs (including a radiologist for mammograms, and ob-gyn). And without estrogen, I am in constant flare....but I am also now with a much more conservative doctor, so he may not be willing to check this out either. Good luck to all of you, hope it works as well for you as it did for me terry

Hi!! Terry I am going to Miami, fl for Botox injection. They have been doing great for me and I have been doing them for over 4 years. My insurance is paying for the Botox treatment and it is done in is office. I am flying from Ohio Thru and coming home on Sun. Deborah Bush

contents: Botox Done on Oct 3rd This is my very first post on ICN so please excuse any mishaps! Octoberfarm, I am very interested in your post. I also recently tried botox. Specifically, on 10/03, my urologist performed a cystoscopy, botox injections into the pelvic floor, and physical therapy while I was asleep. The botox (and/or physical therapy) was successful in reducing the pain I would frequently feel in my butt and (sometimes) down the backs of my legs. Unfortunately, the symptoms of urgency and frequency have increased tenfold (increasing more and more each day). On 10/18, I went to my first post-op appt with my urologist and was basically crawling out of my skin with discomfort. He suggested taking 250 mg of magnessium twice per day to relax the pelvic floor naturally. He also suggested a sitz bath twice per day. I thought I might have a UTI (symptoms were severe at this point). The urine test came back negative though. On 10/22, I ended up in the ER. They did another urine test-this time it was positive! I was given a prescription for Cipro. After the ER, I headed back to my urologist's office. They did a "rescue" instillation. I took off of work on 10/23. 10/24 and 10/25, I felt a little bit better. This morning, however, I woke up miserable again. I returned to the urologist for another "rescue" instillation. They also think I may have a yeast infection & prescribed Diflucon. He also prescribed hydroxizine and amitriptyline, gave me a referral to someone who specializes in acupuncture, and brought up the Interstim (egads). I know that botox has been extremely helpful to some IC patients. My question is-has anyone else had an increase in urgency and frequency after having the botox injections? Please feel better Octoberfarm (and everyone else!!!) and let me know if there is anything I can do to help!

Hi Laura, It sounds like we're having a similar week...yesterday was my birthday and my boyfriend wanted to take me to dinner but I was too tired and out of it to go anywhere (he was sweet and brought it home). I took vicodin for the pain today, but it didn't help so now I'm at work on morphine. I wrote on another thread about how work is going - I asked my employer if I provided adequate documention, if I could work at home , but the owner refused and even hinted around that maybe doing something else where I could work at home would be better for me!) I had mentioned to them that I wrecked my car a few weeks ago and don't want to be driving on pain meds anymore. My employer wasn't very sympathetic, although if they put me in this new position, they said the hours could be flexible. I think they're doubting my abilities completely now,though. The pain is unbearable today - I'm on the 3rd type of antibiotic in 2 weeks now - 2nd uti in less than 2 weeks. The second antibiotic, after taking it for over 24 hours, I was told was resistant to this UTI and the doctor switched it. I feel completely toxic right now. Another doctor upped my dosage from 20 to 40 mg of lexapro a couple of days ago. He said my blood pressure is really high probably from anxiety and depression and they want to try that first before they give me blood pressure medication. I turned 39 yesterday, but have seriously been wondering how I'm going to make it another 20 years the way I'm going. A good life insurance policy for my daughter is looking very appealing at this point. On top of it all, yesterday my daughter said she wished I wasn't her Mom, that I was someone else's, because I'm "boring" (she's 5). Hard to explain to a 5 year old why you feel like s**t all the time and too tired to do much. Not to mention the danger of driving her around in my car...
signature: Current Medications: Lexapro 40mg Heparin Oxycodone Claritin Illnesses: Depression, Hep C, IC, High Blood Pressure, OCD IC Normal symptoms: constant urgency, pressure, pain, diet sensitive, frequency and very little capacity, extreme pain with sex, frequent UTI's, fatigue. Suzy

contents: Oh no Laura, I was really hoping the botox was going to help you! I am so sorry you're in so much pain and not getting any help with it. I can only imagine how frustrating this must be for you. If your pain is really bad I would call up the uro's office and tell them you need them to call the ER and tell them to expect you and what to give you so hopefully you can get in and out. My PCP is open Saturday mornings so maybe that is an option for you as well though I know not many dr's do this anymore. I had an ovarian cyst that was VERY painful one Saturday morning and went into the office before going to the ER and he gave me a shot of something in my butt that really helped take the edge off. I am just so upset for you that you are trying to find something to make you feel better and it makes you feel worse!
signature: Christine I have been diagnoised for 6 1/2 years now. I have taken a long break from the ICN but really miss helping out my fellow IC patients and want to get back into posting. 1st hydro 4/07 showed no visible signs of IC but tons of mast cells in all my biopsy samples which did prove IC. 2nd hydro 4/13/09 showed dark purple glomerulations and I had a capacity of 450 cc's. This hydro proved that my IC had progressed. I have tried every oral medication as well as rescue instills and DMSO. I have been lucky enough to see Dr Hanno, the top IC specialist in PA who has told me due to the fact that I have not responded to any "standard" treatments that I have a severe, end stage case of IC with a horrible quality of life (didn't have to tell me that last part!) Proud wifey of Shane, mommy to Griffin, and step-mom to Logan and Gage Also proud mom to the best Bullmastiff on earth, Claus

contents: I knew going in that there would be a 50/50 chance that it would work so I did know the risk. It is just so frustrating going though all of the pain for nothing and on top of it having to pay for it also. There is no telling how much this is going to cost me in the end. You have to pay for all of the pre-op blood work, ekg, etc. Then you have to pay for the nursing care, operating room, anesthiologist, and the list goes on and on. I know one thing for sure, I won't be a guinea pig again and have to pay for all of these cost without something working for sure. Everyone is getting rich off of our miserey. I think if they are trying things off label then they should not charge you for it. It should be in some kind of trial so the patients that get no help are not out so much money, especially when we can not even work. jlt-1103 I will pm you my phone number so we can compare our treatments. It sounds like you went through quite a bit also. I am so sorry. Life is just not fair sometimes. But hang in there.....we will all get through this together I am so grateful for each and every one of my friends here on this board. I would be lost without you

If not the bill you are going to get scares me. If you have to pay for it all out of pocket remember that as long as you are making decent payment to the hospital it will not hurt your credit. Also, cal the hospital and see if they can help you with this bill. Sometimes they will reduce their costs. I personally would pay my uro first because I need their care. I am sorry.

contents: Wow, I went back and read my posts since my botox treatments and I was pretty angry and upset. I guess that is what severe pain does to a person. I did go to my pain doctor and we got my medication changed to help the intense pain from the botox injections. I am still having the pain but I finally feel like we are starting to get it under control I am getting stronger each day and I am able to get around and do some things. I even cooked Thanksgiving dinner for 8 people. That was a major accomplishment compared to where I was 2 weeks ago so for that I am grateful. I am resting today because I have done so much the last few days that it put me back a little bit but that's o.k. because tomorrow is a new day. I want to thank all of you for your prayers, posts, and support during this trying time. I can't thank you all enough for all of your kind words and concern. You all got me through this tough time. Thanks again to everyone. You all are angels Love, Laura

contents: What helps? What has helped you with the pelvic pain? Do the suppositories work?
I was on them for a while.I took them to evacuate gas and pressure. It does help but it dosen't cure anything. Tip

About 18 months ago I had a TURP for chronic UTI. Since then I have had a combination of UI/SI, usually with daily accidents. Also have terrible spasms (pain level 9 to 9.5) if I void completely. About a month ago I was finally able to get botox treatment for this. Thought I would share my experience. (FYI, had a foley for 3 weeks!!!!! I was NOT happy!!!!!) The botox seems to be working, at least partially. I have not had a single episode of urge incontinence since. Have basically been dry. (I have a little SI, probably not much that can be done about it.) I do have to do a little cathing from time to time (because of retention), but it really hasn't been a problem. Since the botox, I have had a few episodes of pain, but they seem to be diminishing. So... I will call this a success at least as far as the UI/accidents. Being consistently dry for the first time in 18 months is a big plus! The jury is still out on the end-void spasms. If they continue, I may ask my doctor to try to trigone, if I get an opportunity to try this again.
I botox mainly used for incontinence? Thanks, Tracey

RE: Botox Tracey, The surgery I had left me with three problems: urge incontinence (UI), stress incontinence (SI), and spasms if I void completely. Botox won't fix stress incontinence, that's really a "mechanical" problem. For me, it did cure the UI, at least temporarily; however, I still cannot empty completely without the painful spasms. The pain level is about 9 - 9.5. BUT: I'm consistently dry for the first time in 18 months, so I'm happy. I can partially control the spasms by not voiding completely. Does this help?

contents: I don't have incontinence. I just have the urg/freq that has increased lately, especially at night.

Jenny - It did help my urgency/frequency CONSIDERABLY. My surgery caused changes to my anatomy that contributed to the UI. But the UI ITSELF was caused by urgency, which was also a byproduct of my surgery. (Kind of a catch-22!) Before the botox, I would void 4-8 times before lunch. Now I can usually wait until after lunch. Now that my bladder has been calmed, I can also hold more. Not physically, (botox does not increase the capacity) but because of a decrease in the sense of needing to void, so I can wait longer. Does this make sense? My bladder is fuller when I realize I need to go to the bathroom. For me, it helped a lot. Before, I would have wetting accidents nearly everyday. I haven't one since the botox. You really should discuss this with your doctor. If you have IC, it helps some. Another resource is the Texas ICU website: www.icutexas.com.

Tracey - I just noticed you are in Tennessee. My urologist is Harriette Scarpero at Vanderbilt. (I usually see her in Franklin, so I don't have to go all the way to Nashville.) She has been WONDERFUL in helping me deal with this problem. Also, the urology department at Vanderbilt has been doing research into urgency/incontinence and has been involved in several clinical trials using botox for this problem. If your insurance won't cover it, you might consider this. I don't know if they are still enrolling, but I could send you contact information and you could find out.

Hi and thanks for the response....how do I go about requesting this and will insurance cover? How is this procedure done and where? Thanks, Jenny
signature: -Jenny Symptoms: Urgency, frequency, urethra stinging/burning, pressure, bladder pain. Current Medications: Prelief, Pyridium, Loestrin FE 120, Probiotics. Unsuccessful Medications: Elmiron, Atarax, Paxil (off since July 07 after 12 years), Oxytrol Patch Diagnosed with IC in 2004, also have IBS and Endometriosis.

contents: RE: Process for botox Jenny (and all other readers!)- Forgive the length of this answer. I'm not a doctor, so I can only tell you about my procedure. 1. First and foremost: Ask your urologist. I cannot emphasize this enough. They are able to guide you better that I can. If your doctor is not familiar with using Botox in this manner, find one who is. 2. Botox is not approved by the FDA for bladder use. Using it this way is considered "off-label." It's not illegal; Doctors can prescribe medications for other than their intended usage. However, most insurance companies will not cover off-label usage of medications. 3. As most insurance companies will not cover it directly, most doctors assume it will not be covered. Therefore, do your own research. I was the one that figured out (for my condition) my insurance might cover it. I was able to find my insurance company's "Position Paper" on Botox. This is their statement of what they will cover and what they won't. In reading it myself, I found that they should cover it. However, it had to be pre-approved. 4. Check with Allergan, the maker of Botox. They have resources on writing letters, appeals, etc. I didn't know this at the time, so I had to figure this all out for myself. Here's one website: http://www.botoxmedical.com/Resources.aspx 5. Requesting coverage. You can ask for coverage yourself. However, it is much better to get your doctor to do it. Carries a lot more weight. Find all the information you can (studies, position papers, etc) and give all this information to them. Ask them to write the letter on their letterhead. Assume that your initial request will be denied. In other words, start planning any appeals before the initial letter is sent. Find out what the appeal process is, don't rely on your insurance booklet. Ask your insurance company directly. Keep records of any correspondence, including emails. In my case, the address in the booklet on where to send any appeals was WRONG. Any appeals sent to this address would have been ignored. Keep copies of everything you give, and request copies of everything your doctor sends. Good possibility that your insurance will lose it, at least once. In my case, the insurance company lost both the initial letter and the appeal. Everything had to be sent twice. Make sure your doctor is willing to do this. It took SIX MONTHS to get it approved. Don't give up! 6. The procedure itself. This varies depending the doctor, clinic/hospital, etc. Most procedures use about 300 units of Botox, injected about 10 units at a time. This means about 30 injections into your bladder. (In my case it 20.) Needless to say, without medication, this could be painful. My procedure was done in an operating room. I had my choice of either general ("put to sleep") or spinal. I had spinal, so I was awake. It was not painful. I did not feel the injections. However, the hydrodistention they must do to see the bladder was moderately uncomfortable. My doctor used a rigid cystoscope with a needle probe passed through the scope. A syringe containing the Botox was attached to the probe on the outside of the scope. She would press the needle into my bladder wall and inject a small amount of Botox. Vanderbilt has participated a couple of clinical trials for using Botox. One of the nurses there told me they have done this using only local anesthetic in the bladder and a flexible scope. As part of the trial they try to assess pain/discomfort and she said most people tolerated the procedure pretty well. Some don't feel anything, others just discomfort from the hydrodistention. Once again, this is a topic for your doctor. I know some urologists have done this as an office procedure, and if I have to have it done again, my doctor is going to consider doing this way. Would be a lot cheaper, the OR is expensive. I know this is really a lot to digest. Hope it helps.
Thank you for the response!! So did you have to be get these injections 20 separate times or just one time? -Jenny

contents: I was so excited to have botox done again since it helped me so much last time. This time the Dr. and I desided to do more botox since he had only done 100 units and it lasted only 4 months. So we were ready to do the hydro distention and botox. He did the botox injections first since last time he did the hydro first and I had so many cracks in my bladder that it caused so much bleeding he could see very well. Then he did the hydro. He was able to stretch my bladder a little more than last time. 400cc the first and 500cc the second. He took pictures this time since it didn't look as bad as last time. He said the pictures really doesn't show how bad my bladder really is and he thinks that is because he did the botox first which caused the muscels to relax perventing the cracking. now I am have trouble with retension the day after the surgery I was constantly peeing but only peeing 25cc to 75cc. I called my doctors office and he thought it could be because of the swelling. but said to cath to see if I have any retension. I sure did I had 375cc left in me and my bladder normally holds only 200cc awake. No wonder I was so uncomfortable. I have been cathing every 4 hours and getting 150 to 250 each time on top of peeing 75 to 150 before and in between. I have been cathing for 3 days and I now think I have a UTI. I am going to the ER to get checked since I have to work tomorrow and I can't miss any more work. My doctor doesn't want me to have more than 300 cc in my bladder at one time since I have such a small bladder he doesn't want it backing up into my kidneys. I am so upset the botox worked so well last time. If we do end up doing it again we will have to back off of how much botox we used. I am still hoping that the retention will go away on its own and it is just my bladder being lazy after surgery. Lynn
signature: IC/IBS Hopeful : I had my bladder removed on September 29, 2008 for me but I am also so happy I have no regrets

contents: It could still work. Retention after a procedure isn't uncommon. The first time I had a hydrodistention I had some retention for the first week. Hopefully when the swelling goes down a little the problem will disappear. Donna

contents: I am sorry you are dealing with all this. I know what you mean about the concern of getting a UTI from the cathing. Maybe you could take a prevenative antibiotic. I unfortunately am allergic to most antibitoics, or they mess up my stomach awful. I do instiallation at home to help with the IC, and I am always in fear of getting a UTI. Do you use the clean technique when you self cath? I make sure I use the betadine swabs ( Swab the area before putting the cath in). This may help to prevent a UTI somewhat better. I do hope you get to feeling better and hope the retention goes away soon. Jen
So sorry you are having this problem with retention. Hope your bladder 'settles down' & you can pee properly soon. Hugs x

this is the first i have heard of botox helping for IC, how does it work and has anyone had it help with severe urge/freqency is this a last step treatment my doctor has never mentioned it to me but at this point i am willing to try anything!!

Many ICers have been helped by Botox. It's becoming more popular now. The number of Urologists that do it is limited. It does cost and some Insurance Companies don't cover it but that is also changing. I hope some of the other ladies and gentlemen can give you their success experiences with Botox. The ICN has a discussion with a Doctor about Botox that may answer some of your questions. This was written up in 2003 so many things may have changed. Hopefully your Doctor can explain how he would do this in your particular case. http://www.ic-network.com/guestlectures/smith.html Good Luck, Kara Last edited by Kara29; 07-20-2007, 01:13 PM. contents: Botox paralyzes the muscle that it is injected in. There is 100 units in a bottel and you are usually charged per bottle. once the bottle is open after 24 hours it is no good so you are charged for the whole bottle. The price depends on the hospital. The mark up is very high. My insurance didn't pay for the botox but paid for the procedure. it paralyzes the muscle (bladder) so it stops spasming. it only paralyzes where the injection site is. my Dr. did 33 injections using 100units mixed with some other stuff. It lasted me 4 months (this was the first time) I was peeing 30 times a day and after botox 8 to 12 times a day. To me that was the best relief I have ever gotten. We are repeating the procedure Sept 4th. I would try it.

My insurance paid for the botox! The 1st and now the 2nd time. The only thing that wont be paid for fully is the surgrey itself. Persistence and being annoying calling everyday pays off. I thank my mom for that. My procedure is June 12th. I have regents exams that week so please pray for me. I need the prayers and i will certainly do the same in return. I will update as it gets closer and after the botox to update you. 16yrs old and i have to go through this...... I would have never thought..But thanks and im praying for everyone. Take care. Love Laura

Hi Laura, I'm so sorry that your so young and have to deal with this disease. I'm 46 and I have a hard enough time getting a grip on it, I can't even imagine having to go through it at 16! So I really feel for you. Good luck on your exams, I'm sure you'll do great! Kari Oh, and good luck with the Botox to, I hope it helps you.

contents: 200cc's He does 100cc's on either side of my pelvic area. But where im not actually sure. Dr. Moldwin said he also does it in his office too but im still so tender and in so much pain i wouldnt be able to handle that. He did a lil tiny internal exam and i was in tears after. Then i had to fly home which sucks. But i cant even walk because it sets everything off for weeks. But im hoping this works longer and cant wait to get it done again so i dont have to worry about the pain and hopefully be able to go back to school. Ive even started weight watchers because ive gained 30 pounds since ive been sick! In 4 weeks ive only lost 2.2 pounds. I cant eexercise and with the meds its hard. contents: pain I have severe muscles spasms in the pelvic muscles. My bladder has been doing great. No urgency or freq......only when i have an infection which is about every couple months. Ive been able to go back eating and drinking everything with no problem. The pelvic pain is just the worse thing in the world. My mom and dad are wondering.....what does the pain feel like to you guys?? My mom sometimes wonders if it feels like giving birth but i really dont know how to explain it. So how do you explain your pelvic pain as being???? To me its like getting hit in the pelviis with a baseball bat over and over again. AHHH! But thanks for all the prayers and help!!! Laura

What does IC pain feel like? Laura, To me giving birth was much easier than having IC pain. At least with giving birth the pain would eventually go away. IC does resemble being hit with a baseball bat or being kicked every 5 minutes in the pelvic area. (Your dad can probably relate more to the being kicked part!). Best of luck on your regents. Sandy
thanks Thanks so much. I just cant wait to get it done and over with especially the regents. Laura

contents: 4 Days!!! Four more days until the botox. Im so excited because i am in sooo much pain and cannot wait to get relief. My insurance has covered the botox we only had to pay 29$ for 2 viles 100cc's!!!!! It was shipped to our house so we have to bring it on a plane with us to Long Island. Dr. Moldwin was like WOW i cannot believe it was covered. But it helps a great deal and i thank my mother for that. She would call the insurance company pretty much everyday and sometimes maybe twice a day. I guess they got so annoyed they covered it LOL! Does anyone notice that if you walk too far or fast that you have major pelvic pain afterwards?? If i run around with the dog....sit on a chair too llong ( a hard one) or walk too far or fast i get sooo much pain. I had to go into school for a lab practical and it seemed i had to walk a mile just to get to the classroom (science wing). Then i felt the pain just like that.....then the next day its 10x's worse but then the day after that (today---Friday) im much better. It was hard for me to go into school. I was so scared and i havent seen my friends in so long....even sadder part was when i was sitting outside the classroom waiting for my teacher to come out from the class.....bell rang.....friends and people i knew walked by and never said hey how are ya. At first i thought of it as a blessing because i havent seen them in so long and was nervous...but then when i was talking to my counsler/social worker...it really sunk in....i realized it was very upsetting but hey what are you gonna do about it...They ask what is wrong with me but nobody knows what IC is so they think its nothing....they arent smart enough to know what PFD is so i guess they think its nothing...Had to vent sorry this is so looong!! But if i were to go back to school in september....and i had to walk so much would my pelvic pain come back again and create problems where i would have to start homeschoool again??? My mom suggested doing the botox every 3months..depending how long the next one lasts....but im so iffy about it. My dad WANTS me to go back...but he has no idea what the pain is like...sometimes i just feel like breaking his pelvis so he knows!! But im excited about the upcoming week....hoping the botox goes well and that im not too drugged to take my regents exams!!! Please pray for me. I will update either when i get home after the botox( fly in and out the same day) or the day after to let yyou know how everything goes... Thank you so much for the support and prayers. Its wonderful to have a group of people who knows what im going through..without it i have no idea where i would be. Thanks again and sorry its so looooong!!! Laura

Hi Laura I am so happy that your insurance is paying for the botox. There is one thing I would do is take something for the burning pain. I did not have any problem about walking. I will be praying for you and did let us know what happens. Deborah Bush

contents: oh boy Lets see ladies....everything went good yesterday.....i was stuck on the jetblue plane for 3hrs because of storms and we could only fly 10,000ft or we werent allowed to leave so i saw lighting when we were up and was like yep im shutting the window lol....then we dropped from 10,000ft to 8,000 during a bad bad turblance...but after we got out of scranton PA everything was good...........as for today im in a ton of pain and had to take the global regents and hardly could do that but got through it.....i think i did good..... dr moldwin stretched my pelvic muscles as well as putting the botox in....didnt know he was going to stretch them till after the surgrey....that is what made the pain worse....but in a week or so ill be better... and will update you all again...thank you for the continuing support and prayers... Is it just me or are my veins stupid??? i have five holes on both my arms until they got an IV in my hand its swollen and hurts but nothing compared to the awful pelvic pain today...that IV hurt so bad! i was about ready to break down and cry while i was waiting three hours on the plane for it to leave i was so ****** and in soo much pain im just glad we got home....we took and earlier flight cuz i was done early and which was good cuz the one were supposed to go on got cancelled!! Aye!!!!! But i will update aagain and thank you!! Laura

contents: ................................. He did an exam right before with some sedation i still felt it so sedation my butt LOL....he told my mom yesterday he punctured six holes on each side of my pelvis where i hurt the most...plus he stetched my muslces out as well which hurt.....better day today but it still kills a bit!! All they told me to do was to take painpills constantly to get me zonked out.....but other then that they cant do anything else for me so i have to wait for the pain to go away which usually takes me a week then the botox kicks in....cant wait for that!! Laura

IC Pain IC pain for me can only be described as being in labor, I find myself doing the breathing as though I'm having contractions. I've had IC for 5years now and I've been on everything out there. I had a one year period where the IC just went away I had no pain, and no other problems what so ever but now it seems that the IC is getting worse. I've been in serious pain since Friday at 1am and usually the pain lets up once in a while but this is a constant pain it feels like I actuall have a baby kicking me that's how bad my bladder is contracting, I'm scared to go to the doctors because I feel something else might be going on as well. Is it worth while discussing BOTOX with my doctor, I'm at the point where I just don't seem to care anymore because nothing is helping me. Missy

over 3 weeks....not so good I am having trouble going to the bathroom so i have to push but im told not to because it hurts the pelvic muscles which i know so i have not yet felt any relief this time. Dr. Moldwin put me on flomax so that it would help me go to the bathroom and not push. He says its not from the botox but i had the same thing happen to me after the 1st botox but it wasnt bad i just thought it was from cymbalta which the dr. thought was also the problem. Well after the 2nd round it got worse and he still says its not from the botox! Its weird!!! i still think it is. But he said the only way that would happen is if i would put it into your bladder but he doesnt and says never will do that to anyone. So im confused...depressed and upset that it is not working this time. I believe its not working because im pushing which is not allowing the muscles to heal. So hopefully the flomax will take affect in the next few weeks and ill see if my muscles have calmed down. Hope it does. The next thing is the interstim so i dont know!! By the way.....when my mom and i were stuck on jet blue for 3 hours on the runway....the next day we EACH got a 100 dollar voucher! which will be used to fly back to NYC to see Dr. moldwin again. But its sooo confusing you work on one thing and another problem occurs. My bladder was doing great no burning urgency freq. nothing and now im having trouble starting sometimes and my flow is weak and have so much urine left in my bladder afterwards it sucks!! But i will check back again in a week or so to let you know how things are going. have a great 4th of july!! Laura

Flow problems Laura, I haven't tried botox but after having a bladder sling put in back in November, I have had "flow" problems. I take Flomax AND Valium and it does seem to help. If you can self-cath, that is the best way to relieve the pressure. I use a pediatric catheter because the larger ones cause more spasms. I use an 8 fr. I work so I can either take the Valium or pain pills - not both. Both makes me a little loopy. . .OK a lot loopy!!! The Valium, according to Dr. Robert Evans here in NC relaxes the pelvic muscles. If you can't self cath, I would go to a local doctor and ask them to show you how. It is a pain in the butt to do but once you get used to it. . .it is no big deal. I too "pushed" and it does cause a lot more discomfort - some good - some bad. Best of luck. Sandy

thanks I am taking valium 3 times a day plus the flomax. Dr. Moldwin doesnt believe that it is my bladder that is causing this but my pelvic muscles are so tight around my bladdder that its making it hard for me to pee. I will never self caf. that just would make it worse for me. I can pee i just cant empty my bladder so i either have to sit there till it comes out or go to the bathroom 10 times. But im hoping with taking the valium 3 times a day and the flomax which i start taking two tonight that it will somehow help. By the way... what mg. are you on for valium?? Dr. moldwin has me on 2mg so 6mg a day...is that too little or is that right?? Although he put me on 2mg for some reason. Thanks again!!!!!!!! Laura

I was on 3 mg once a day. But I cath. It's not as bad as you would think!!! In fact when I do cath, I drain everything out and then I put in lidocaine, heparin and sodium bicarb which is wonderful. It numbs the bladder so I don't have to deal with the pain.I have really bad spasms too. .. that is what stops my urine flow. So did you stop taking the Cymbalta? That was another thing that my doc said would/could cause retention. But after being off of that for a few days caused some major mood swings - worse than PMS! and then I would just yell, "No one understands this pain!!!!" The yelling didn't help but it's just one of those I can't take it anymore pity parties. The Flomax should help. They told me to take it at night too but I found that I had to get up a lot during the night to go to the bathroom so I started taking it during the day. He has you on 2 pills at night? That seems like a lot.

yeep I was taken off the cymbalta and yes i had MAJOR mood swings....i was ****** at everyone and when there werent any bagles and my mom wouldnt go out and get any i turned into this monster it was stupid and nuts!! But thats all over with so im glad im off the cymbalta and the affects are gone.. I started with one flomax for 3 days then two after that and i do get up to go to the bathroom once every night. It isnt even 1 mg its less then that so i dont think that is a lot im not sure?!? How much are you on???? Laura
Hi Laura I take Flomax and I take .5mg once a day. I think it does help me. I will go either Dec or Jan for another Botox treatment in Miami. I hope you are doing okay. Deborah Bush

Well thanks again! Laura

Flomax issue Hi Laura, I am new to these boards but have suffered from constant bladder/urethral pressure, and constant urgency/freguency/feeling of never being empty etc. for over a year...I was recently diagnosed by Dr. Moldwin as having IC due to a very thin bladder lining he saw during my hydrodistension..Anyway, at my appointment on Friday, Dr. Moldwin prescribed an 8 week course of Flomax as part of my therapy..I was told to take 2 tablets at night...Sure enough, I had the same reaction as you mentioned...I had get up to pee 3 times that night and I think the medicine really tightened my muscles as hardly anything came out, tho I felt like I had to go badly...(Usually I get up approx once at night and have a pretty normal flow amount.) Anyway, when I woke up in the morning I had TERRIBLE bladder pain..I've had it before, but not like this...The pain dispelled a bit after getting up, moving around and taking some Vicodin and Valium...also took a warm bath..Anyway the next night I tried skipping the Flomax. I slept through till dawn and there was no real bladder pain when I woke up, so it must have been the Flomax the night before.... Does Flomax increase urgency/frequency or maybe it does at first and then it settles down?? If it does increase it, it's not the drug for me as it would only be exacerbating my main problem... anyway just wanted to let you know I had the same reaction on the 1st night I took Flomax...! Regards, Kathy

Wishing you luck Hi Laura, It's Kathy again (the new member)....I also wanted to add that I really sympathize with all you are going through at such a young age...I am a mom of 3 kids..My oldest child is almost your age...I find it very hard to take care of my kids and do all the things that everyone need me to do while suffering from my symptoms...Also I sure understand that no one else "gets it"! My husband and my mom try hard to understand but unless you feel it yourself it's hard for them to understand how bad it really is...I know what you mean about it being difficult to explain to people..altho I/C seems to afflict many of us, it doesn't get much media attention, so people don't understand it or PFD, which I also have...(I have been pushing to get that last drop of "pee" out since I was about 18 (and that was many years ago!) I didn't realize I was hurting my muscles by doing that until I read Dr. Moldwin's writings on the internet!...Anyway I wanted to wish you best of luck in your recovery and hope all goes well for you! Dr. Moldwin is supposed to be the best from all I've heard, and he is easy to talk to! Good luck, Kathy

Thank You! IC is very hard to explain and my dad seems to be the only one that doesnt understand it....my mom had such bladder problems when i was younger to the point where she thinks she might of had IC....So she has had BAD bladder infections with pelvic pain so she most likely knows what it is like. And as for the pushing....im still doing it!Its just a habit now but i have to do it in order to empty or i will just feel like crap. So we'll see how well the flomax works and hopefully the pelvic muscles will get better. Im off to take a bath just like dr. moldwin orders...he says 3 times a day....but what if you work and cant do that many..just came to my mind LOL????? I ddont know it doesnt seem to help me that much. But thanks again for everything! Laura

botox not working... Botox is not working this time....dr. moldwin doubled my valium....he said no more botox...you can get immuned to it after a while so there is no point in doing it again......so he wants to do the the pain block injection....so we have to call him back next week...i already did this injection once..he doesnt want to put me out again bcuz i have been put out too many...but there is no waaaaaaaaaaaaay im doing that without being put out even though he will numb it....OUCH!!!! But i will update next week when i speak to him again..thanks!! Laura

Hi Laura I am going to have a pain block on thru. for my clitorus pain. I am hope that it will work for me. How much Flomax are you taking? It is really driving me up the wall waiting till last Aug to have this surgery done to see if it is cancer. I want all of you to pray for me!!! Deborah Bush

contents: The reason they want Flomax to be taken at night is due to the side effects. It can cause orthostatic hypotension, which is dizziness and faintness caused by fluction of the blood pressure due to changing position from lying, sitting, and standing up. ads Originally posted by NTHottie14 I take .8mg of flomax a day.....i take it at night but my "hesitation" is in the morning and daytime so i am going to ask if i can take it in the morning instead. As for your surgrey i will be praying for you. It must me hard to be told its cancer and have to wait so long. I will be praying that everything goes well. Good luck with everything!! Laura

flomax I knew that was the reason they gave it to me at night....my dad had to take it and fainted the day after so they took him off....but i was figuring once you got used to it maybe you dont have to take it at night??? I have not had any problem with the medication...but who knows!! Thanks! Laura

My IC is so out of control. I am trying to eliminate things hoping something will prove to have been a trigger. What are your thoughts on OsCal or calcium supplements in general? I was taking iron because of anemia and have stopped that too. Anyone know if iron is a problem? I was taking a product with glucosimine and hyluronica acid and have stopped that too. What do you think Is turkey bad because of the triptophan? I had ribs last night which was a big mistake. Still in pain after 3 percocet. I felt red meat was a problem. Are we just supposed to stick to fish and chicken?
Please let me know. I'm just trying to put some information together for my appeal letter. Did anyone by any chance have Blue Cross? Thank you!

Melbel, If you don't get any information on the board, you might contact all of the support group leaders in CA. They might be able to ask amongst their groups or facilitate getting some information your way. Support group leaders are wonderful people even if you don't attend their groups. Despite living with IC, they devote their valuable time and limited energy to running a group. They are extremely generous people, so don't be shy just call or or e-mail, and ask if they have any advice for you. I think the link to Jill Osborne's post is the most up to date list: http://www.ic-network.org/forum/showthread.php?t=30094&highlight=support+groups Good for you appealing, despite going thru IC and Botox treatments! Perhaps if you get a yes, it will be one step forward for the IC community. ads

I have federal blue cross blue shield. When I had botox injections done back in March, blue cross blue shield paid for the cysto and the botox. The secret with BCBS is to have your doc document in your chart that you have rectal pain. BCBS will not cover botox for IC or for PFD but they will cover it for rectal pain. I'm not kidding. Even if your prior approval is denied, BCBS will cover the Botox if your doc states that you have rectal pain. My prior approval was denied but during pre-op, my doc made a note in my chart stating I had rectal pain and the botox was paid for. The appeal process with BCBS is long and frustrating but the outcome is totally worth it.
This board is amazing how quickly it connects people with information, which empowers people with their fight against IC. ads

My Insurance Did NOT Cover the Botox but my Doctor did something so that I did not have to pay for it. Not sure what or how but luckily I did not have to pay for it. Should I have done it a 2nd time I would have had to pay for it. My Insurance may have changed since this was a few years ago that I did it. My Doctor must have written it up the correct way. He also put down that he was doing a cysto and some other scar tissue things so maybe that was the key to mine. I have BCBS as well. Kara

contents: Can you go to the employer who pays for the insurance. Sometimes companies will help out when a cliam is denied because they are paying big bucks for the insurance. Get a copy of you policy or perhaps it is online and read on how they are getting by with rejecting it or if you can find a reason they should cover it. Let the insurance company know you are going to go full blown thru the full appeals process and you aren't going away. While, it may not be said out loud, you will be sayint it with your assertive actions. Start getting any medical journal articles/research on Botox. This website may be a good place, you doctor may be able to help and look for the closest medical library. I am sure you must fill out a form to appeal, fax it even though they say you can only send it and make sure you attach many pages of doctor notes and medical research to back up the medical nec. Carbon copy the employer too. Then send the same large document registered mail with signature required; the post office can help with the details. This is just a start. It is a lot of money, so a strong appeal is in order. ads

contents: Maybe by listing it this way, you doc was able to get it approved thru insurance. There are a lot of ways docs help patients with insurance. He maybe doing you a huge favor. Just ask him right before the procedure and if you feel uncomfortable in anyway just back out. You don't have to go ahead with the procedure because as a patient you have rights. It is your body. The stomach muscles can play a role in PFD and visceral pain, which may be referring pain to other pelvic areas. Just don't proceed until you feel comfortable with the situation. ads

contents: botox injection Hi everyone ...I am sorry I didn't post last night. I was in a lot of pain..more like major discomfort after the botox,and was told to sit with an ice pack last night and as much as I could today. Apparently,youre right it is easier for a pain doc to get botox approved for other uses in the body that a reg doc would not be able to. How it was...I guess they use some kind of anesthetic along with the botox .Basically,the nurse pinched my skin really tight while wiggling it as he administered it. It was not at all painful..like a good pinch. He did it in two different areas to the left an right of top of pubic line.Unfortunately, I did not get any in the center which is where my worst pain is. I am literally black, blue and purple where it was applied. I had to wait in the waiting room for awhile after because it makes you kind of woozy. Last night and today I have been diligent about the cold packs. We shall see what happens it it works. The skin is numb where the botox is and maybe after the bruising goes down I will be able to see some benefit. Next time though, I will ask for it dead center of my pubic line. Otherwise, I feel absolutely fine, now sickness or anything with the botox. Thanks for your replies

contents: Hi Well i start with a little about my self i suffer from chronic IC no tablets work or cystistat im 27 i live in England im married with a great husband 7 yr old and got 5 month baby. The hospital called today and the next step for me is botox injections im to go in for a day put to sleep have the injection then go home in the evenings leading up to having it done a nurse will teach me how to catherterise myself incase i get retention. Im scared for lots of reasons will it work whats the worst that can happen to me my life is pretty miserable . So i really like to hear of people who have any information good or bad any advice or useful links that can tell me more . Also i cant seem to find many people who have had this in England. Thanks Emma x Last edited by emma26; 05-26-2007, 07:20 PM. Reason: spelling mistake

Hi Emma, I'm so sorry to hear that you are having such a horrible quality of life. I feel for you. I am in the same position, I wish my doctor understood that. I have had IC since i was 12 and I'm 21 now. Nothing works, so my doctor finally decided I could do surgery but when he did the last cysto he found that my IC has progressed a lot and now the ulcers are completely covering my bladder, and I'm bleeding out of the ulcers constantly. I was so and confused I didn't know what else to do. I don't think he did either. I was actually looking forward to surgery as sad as that sounds. I thought that it would help me. He doesn't want to attempt to do any other types of surgery because I am the youngest patient in the whole practicead so he thinks I have too much of a life ahead of me to go through surgery so young. I just don't know what to do. He said I could try to do Botox with a different doctor two hours away because he doesn't do it himself. I am not exactly sure what they do or how long you will be in pain for or if the insurance company will cover it. I'm lost. I hope this works for you. Please let me know how it all works out, maybe it will help me make a decision on what to do from here. Good luck. I will be thinking about you. Meghan

Hi I've had IC since 1979 and had botox in December as a last resort I had to pay $714 just for the botox. It took awhile to work, but got steadily better every day for about 3 weeks. It lasted about 2 more weeks and then just seemed to wear off. I'm not going for a second treatment. Can't afford it for only a few weeks relief. Dr. says it does work for alot of people though. I didn't have any complications from the surgery. It did burn more for a few days after and I felt I could just sit on the toilet all day long. I hope it works for you. Let me know how you make out.

I had botox done March 2 2007. for the first time. I had tried many other things including the interstim. nothing has helped the bladder except the botox. It has been 4 months today and I am having a flair as we speak I hope the botox is not wearing off yet. I had urge and frequency and pain. I peed 28 to 30 times a day. 2 to 3 weeks after a cysto/hydro with botox. I was down to 8 to 12 times a day and feeling great. I would go for it. I was very scared also. but I would do it again for the relief I had. They say the more times you have it the better it feels and longer it lasts. My insurance will not cover it so I am not sure if I will be able to do it again. I wish it was the insurance people going through what we do. maybe things would move much faster.

contents: After seeing six uro's/urogyn's in less than a year, I have decided that it is a waste of my time to find a good uro. My family practitioner is amazing. She is the only doc who listens to me, and she is willing to try just about anything to help me feel better. I'm impressed because she has taken the time to learn about IC. I recently started on Avinza. I had my first pain free day in over a year. :woohoo: I think my dose needs to be increased or I need a different break through pain med because my current dose of Avinza isn't working as well as it used to. After starting on Avinza I was able to stop both of my sleep meds so I'm no longer a complete zombie during the day. I still have a female uro I see occasionally. She can't help me because I have tried all of the treatments she is familiar with, but she can write prescriptions for my rescue instill meds. I have lost faith in the medical profession after everything I have been through in the past 11 months. I decided not to seek any new treatments for IC because I can't handle the emotional rollercoaster. I think my family practitioner and I can figure out the right combination of meds with some more trial and error.

I just met a lady that Botox for her Fibro. She had been using a cane & was told she would be in a wheel chair one day. She had a Neurologist use Botox on her forehead & head & she is PAIN FREE. The doctor is in Clearwater, Fl. I suggest that any of you who have Fibro look into it in your area! She was out playing soccer with her grandson yesterday. Kathi

contents: Has any had problems with urinary retention after botox therapy for UI? I understand that 25-30% of patients have at least some retention, and in some cases must self cath for awhile. Any ideas on how many? Thanks

I can only speak for myself, but yes I have retention after Botox. I had Botox in early March and am still having to catherize a good deal. I don't think I'm going to have that treatment again since I got no relief this time. I have also had two infections since this, probably from catherizing.
signature: Judi W.

contents: How exactly is botox suppose to help with IC? I know this is a rather newer treatment for IC, and was just curious. I would read up on it and do a ton of research on it and see what this procedure entails before going through with it though if I were you. Also make sure that it is reversible just incase things could go wrong or get worse. Especially find out if the urinary retention thing is only temporary. Look at the whole big picture first before doing it and of course discuss it with your doctor first and see what he/she says and all the pro's and con's. Jen

RE: Urinary Retention FYI, I don't suffer from IC... I came to this forum because of UI and bladder spasms (due to surgery), which many IC sufferers have. I'm not sure if botox helps IC directly. It may "calm" overactivity due to IC. From the research that I have done, and from responses in this and other forums, it appears that upwards 30% of people that have this procedure will have some level of retention.

Hi! Judy W. How many botox treatment has your doctor done? I was in a 3 year trail study with Dr. Gousse down in Miami, Fl. I was his first patient and it works for me. He puts 15 injections inside the bladder wall. He uses 150 uints. I started the botox back in April 2003. The first three times I was put to sleep and know it is done in his office. Deborah Bush
signature: I have IC, the IBS, Heartburn, High bloodpressure,thyrod problems, depression and clorties pain.

contents: botox I have had botox injections every six months since the fall of 2002. I only had a major problem with retention the first time. The 1st time I had 200 units injected into the bladder, now I never have more then 100. You can always ask you uro to start with a smaller dose and move up if it helps and doesn't cause retention. Injecting the urethra actually helps make it easier to empty my bladder. I wouldn't have it done without having both the blader and urethra injected.
Urinary Retention Daffie - Three questions: (1) The first time you had it done, how long did the retention last? (2) How long does 100 units last? (3)And why your urethra? Was it spasming as well?

I have only had two botox treatments - one last July which gave me relief from spasms and helped the frequency, but I did have retention. He used 200 units both times. I don't think he injected my urethra. Also, I was put to sleep both times which always causes some type of problems for me. I have asked him to do it in the office but he has not done that yet.

contents: Re: Uab Actually, no. My local Urologist and my Infectious Diseases doctor suggested Vanderbilt. They set up the original appointment. I chose the doctor, Harriette Scarpero. Is Dr. Keith who you have seen? Did you like him? Vanderbilt has several physicians that do this. They are also conducting a study to determine the efficacy of botox for UI. It may not be accepting patients anymore. They use "local" anesthetic, i.e., a lidocaine solution instilled in the bladder for about 15 minutes. My doctor has suggested either general or spinal. I always opt for spinal if possible. Thanks for the reply. Last edited by macsmaker; 05-15-2007, 10:04 PM. Reason: Add a few comments.

I started seeing Dr. Lloyd in 1990. He was one of the first urologists in the country to treat IC. He is very good. The past two years I have been seeing a young Urologist in Montgomery, Dr. William Moore. He is not as experienced with IC but is learning and is willing to work with you and pretty much lets you make the decision on the course of treatment and medication. If I have to have my bladder removed, I will go to Dr. Lloyd because he is the best on that - in Alabama anyway. Good luck on your Botox.

contents: I had Botox done in my bladder three weeks ago and still no relief. Things seem to be going downhill. I have more pain in my bladder and urethra than I did before all this and I still have the same constant frequency and urgency than I did before. I know he said sometimes on some people it just doesn't work. But if that's the case, then what next? I am at a loss. I feel like I have tried everything and nothing seems to work. I live off Oxycodone, which helps the pain a bit but for some reason the codeine keeps me awake so I can't sleep at night. And other than that, I have to take Ambien almost every night. My quality of life seems to be rapidly deteriorating and I am getting more and more hopeless. People tell me I have been such a trooper but I am feel as though I am beginning to falter.

I am sorry that the botox has not worked. Have you considered the interstim for freguenct and urgency? It is not for pain. I was also wonering if you had tried Elmiron, hydroxizine, urised, elavil? I hope you are feeling better.

I had a hydro distention and botox done on March 2nd and feel great. It was my first hydo and my bladder the doctor said was a bloody mess. He said the cracks and hemerages where all over. I was in a lot of pain when I woke up and did take pain killers for 2 days later. About a week and a half later my bladder felt great. I went from peeing 30 times a day to 8. contents: botox I have had botox injections many times. At first just the bladder but now I get injections in the urethra, pelvic floor, vestibule glands, etc. I loved the effects with just the bladder but really believe it helps more with urethral pain and pelvic pain then the bladder. Before I started getting my urethra injected I couldn't hardly stand a catheter now it doesn't bother me at all the use one during flares. I would suggest you order prelubricated catheters in pediatric sizes before the procedure. Daffie

For those of you who have had multiple botox injections done and are successful. Do you have a hydro distention done each time or do they just do botox. This is a question that my dr and I are trying to decide for next time. I have had it only once. my bladder is small awake I can only hold 200cc at the most. I had good results this first time. frequencey went from 30 to 8. I am happy with this. It did take a good 3 weeks to heal since my bladder has a lot of cracks in it but I will do it again. I would like everything done the same way just so I have good results again. I am afraid to do just botox incase for me I need the both for good results. any advice from you all thanks Lynn

contents: So my botox experience was okay, but overall didn't make a huge difference. I'm thinking ahead to the next time I have it done since the amount of Botox used and number of injections can make a difference. Has anyone had experience with this? Did you see further improvement the second time around? He also said Interstim might be an option for me. However, a few months ago, he told me he wasn't a fan of Interstim in general. Now he is thinking that perhaps Interstim might be a viable option for me because urgency is really my problem and Interstim treats that. I understand his thinking, but at the same time, I've heard more negatives than positives when it comes to Interstim.

I just had my Botox done on Thursday and I seem to have had the same experience. Not much difference since then and I thought it was supposed to be a rather immediate effect. I am still in pain though, I suppose because my urethra is sp damn sensitive. But how long is it supposed to take before the Botox really makes a difference? I know the next step is Interstim but that just scares me. I don't want anything implanted in my body and I too have heard many negatives. My problem is also mainly frequency and urgency.

Myca, You are in my head! Ha! It takes about two weeks until you feel the full effects of Botox. My doctor used a very small amount to see how I would respond to it. I'm going to do it again when this wears off and use more botox with more injections. My gut tells me that Interstim is bad. I hear way more negatives than positives so I'm scared and want to see the Botox play out. What annoys me is that my doctor was against it at first. I cannot believe this is the best they can do. It sucks. I hope you feel better in another week.

Thank you I appreciate the response. It has just been a rough couple of days, and I am still in quite a bit of pain. And I'm just getting frustrated that it hasn't started working yet. I suppose I thought it would be an immediate effect, and I would be able to sleep. My doc was also hesitant, and didn't really want to do it. It took forever to get him to order the Botox and schedule the procedure. I am also quite afraid of the Interstim. I have always been hesitant to do that because it seems like SUCH an invasive procedure, and it seems so risky. But I think if this Botox doesn't work that that will be my last resort. And that is not something I am really psyched about.

The Interstim will not help with the pain. Botox injections might. The Botox clinical trial results have not been published yet so I can't tell you how many people experienced pain relief from the injections.

To be honest, if it even helps with the frequency or the urgency, that will be enough. That is my main problem and it is mostly at night so I tend to not sleep much which is very frustrasting and tends to disrupt my days.

I have actually never had any pain. My problem is urgency/frequency. I've spoken with people who say Interstim ruined their bodies. I just can't go there. I know there is the preliminary test and all, but it still scares me. I have been sleeping much better with the Botoz so that is something. It's a much deeper sleep.

Has your doc mentioned the URGERNT PC device? Treatments involve sticking a needle into the muscles near the ankle for 30 minutes once a week for 6 to 12 weeks. You will have to go to your doc's office to get the treatments. Studies have shown that the Urgent PC helps with frequency and urgency.
No, I've never heard of it. I will definitely check it out. Thanks.
I misspelled the device's name in my last post. It should be spelled Urgent PC. Here is a link for the website. http://www.uroplasty.com/UPC_US.asp

I just had my Botox done on Thursday. Not much difference since then and I thought it was supposed to be a rather immediate effect. I am still voiding several times during the night and have pain in my urethra because it's so damn sensitive. How long is it supposed to take before the Botox really makes a difference?
Botox can take up to 2 weeks to work.
Have you had success with it since you had it done? Or did you not see much of a difference?
I didn't get any relief from the Botox injections. I am probably going to start on CyA or CellCept at the end of May.
What is that? I have not heard of those. Are they pills or injections?

CellCept and CyA are immunosuppressants. They are available in pill or capsules. CellCept is being studied in a phase 3 clinical trial for IC. Immunosuppressants can be dangerous as they affect the function of your immune system. CyA can cause kidney failure. ICLori has experience with taking CyA if you want to talk to an IC patient who has tried CellCept or CyA. Most docs will only try CyA or CellCept in refractory IC patients. I am eligible for the CellCept study but I will not enter the clinical trial because the closest trial center would be a 24 hour drive for me.

I lost my botox insurance appeal and I am so upset. The botox is the only thing that has helped me. I feel like stuffing all that paperwork in those insurances peoples mouthes. How can they play god and decide you can't get a treatment when it helps. They would rather pay for all the other expensive treatments that don't even work as well like instills and my bladder being removed. aaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhhhh I am so mad

contents: Some insurance companies will not approve Botox injections because they are not FDA approved for IC. I know that the military will approve the Botox injections if you have rectal pain documented in your medical records. The military has the strictest rules for approving drugs that I have ever seen. Some docs have contacted various insurance companies to ask what medical conditions qualify for insurance coverage of Botox. Other docs use the Botox patient assistance program. The patient assistance program works with your doc and your insurance company to get the Botox injections approved. To learn more about the patient assistance program visit www.botoxmedical.com.
Hi! The no. for that web site for the botox is 1-800-442-6869. I do the botox and my insurance pays for the botox. I went through the Botox Avantage Program. Deborah Bush

contents: I had the Botox injections on Wednesday morning. The surgery was a complete disaster. I was supposed to have an epidural but I ended with a spinal because the anesthesiologist was late and she didn’t have time to do an epidural. The epidural was for pain control, it was supposed to be left in until Thursday afternoon. The spinal wore off about an hour after the surgery and I was in soo much pain. My doctor ordered 2mg of morphine every two hours. There was no way that 2mg of morphine was going to touch my pain. As the spinal was wearing off, I apparently had a pseudoseizure. I have never seen someone have a seizure so how could I fake one? My doc then ordered a psychiatry consult. WHAT! The nurses in the recovery room claim that I threatened them and that I was a danger to myself and others. I should have known that my doc never believed me. He was just using me so that he could learn how to do the Botox injections. I also woke up with an ice pack condom in my vagina. I had to beg for over an hour to get it taken out. The ice pack did help with the burning but it also caused horrible cramps. I couldn’t pee and I asked the nurses to cath me to help relieve some of the pressure. The nurses said my doc didn’t order a straight cath or a Foley cath so I was going to have to wait until I could pee on my own. I took a LoFric out of my bag and cathed myself. I never thought that I would appreciate having a catheter inserted seeing how they normally hurt to insert. After a room on the pediatrics ward was ready for me, I was transferred upstairs. I was escorted by a security guard the entire way. The security guard was ordered to stay outside of my room for over an hour after I arrived on the ward. I also had a nurse technician in my room at all times. I was not allowed to leave the room, open up the drapes to look outside, or try to use the bathroom by myself. I was so humiliated. My doctor ordered Lortab 5’s for pain control once I got to my room. I take stronger pain meds than that on a regular basis. Once I asked for pain meds, it was over three hours before I was offered the first dose of Lortab. I went to use the bathroom and when I looked at my lower abdomen and vagina, I had black and purple bruises in the shape of a handprint all over down there. I don’t know how they got there, but bruises were not there before the surgery. As soon as I got back to my bed, I just started to cry. I have been through hell the past year and a half and now my doc thinks I’m crazy and I’m being denied pain meds. I also think I might have been raped while I was in the hospital. I’m bruised and swollen and I have weird discharge coming from my vagina. My birthday is on Sunday but I’m not in the mood to celebrate. I thought that the Botox would give me some relief from my symptoms but now I feel like I have been brutally raped and beaten. My regular pain meds are not helping with the pain. I tried calling my regular doc today and her office staff said she didn’t have time to see me. I don’t want to go to the ER because I would have to wait for hours just to be told there is nothing they can do for me.

OMG I WAS HORRIFIED READING YOUR STORY!!!! I really dont know what to think other than you are not crazy!!! I dont know much about botox but something about your story doesnt make sense to me. If I were you I would get a lawyer immediatly. There shouldnt be bruses down by your vagina. I just dont get it. I feel so bad for you I want to cry! I will be praying for you. I hope you get taken care of soon!!!!!! I would go to the hospital if I were you. ((HUGS)) Rachel

I have an appointment with my regular doc on Monday afternoon. I ended up with an Rx for Methadone. I just looked up the drug interactions and the Methadone interacts with several of my other meds. I will probably end up going to the ER to get an Rx for something else. I'm just so angry right now I don't feel like dealing with the doc in the ER.

Jeepers! I am horrified as a health care professional reading this. I am so sorry. You shouldn't have bruises in that area. The security guard and "sitter thing" --oh, I don't think so. If you were coming out of anesthesia of any sort, how can you fake anything???? Not only that, the overdistended bladder thing is a violation of the Nurse Practice Act as well as a bunch of other things. I take Methadone with pretty good success. I also wanted to say that you can certainly make anonymous complaints to your state's health department, board of nursing and medical quality assurance. Not knowing all the details, I know you can most certainly do that and share everything that happened without giving your name. Many prayers headed your way. Stay strong, sweetie. Hugs, Barb

contents: I also think you should go to an ER at a different hospital so they can examine you. I know you have been through a nightmare, but there are some instances when people go berserk from anesthesia. My father had to be physically restrained when he was waking up from anesthesia --- he was actually trying to attack everyone in sight, including my mother. We could hear him screaming; he thought they were all trying to kill him. He was fine once he was fully awake, but they did have a guard for him until then. When he finally was awake and rational, he didn't recall any of it and wondered why "that guy" was standing at his door watching him. I hope you feel better very soon. Sending gentle hugs, Donna

contents: Just wanted to let you know that Donna is right -- it's less likely to go beserk from a spinal anesthetic than a general, but it can happen if you have a reaction to the anesthetic because some has flowed up to the brain during the infusion. It's because there are stages of anesthesia -- stage one is when you're just going to sleep, stage two is when your nerves actually turn off, stage three is surgical anesthesia (where they try to keep you during the operation), and stage four is danger zone, where death can occur (they try to avoid this one, obviously). Stage two is the most dangerous period, because as your nerves turn off, the inhibitory mechanisms shut off -- this can lead to vomiting, thrashing around, shouting out, choking on the breathing tube, etc -- and most anesthesiologists use drugs to speed you through this stage. However, when you wake up, if the anesthesiologist has miscalculated dosages even slightly, you go through the stages backwards (from three to two to one to awake). So, when you hit stage two in recovery, you can shout out, flail around, swing at nurses, and the like -- but you won't usually remember it because you'll fall asleep again to pass through stage one and then wake up for good. Recovery rooms are full of this stuff, and your nurses should have known what was going on. I have vague memories of a quick stage two passage after my last surgery -- I didn't thrash, but I have vague recollections of shouting out some unintelligible things before sleeping for a while. So, it seems what you had was a fairly normal, if extreme, reaction to something they gave you in the OR. If that happened, it is possible you don't remember slots of time during your surgery as well... which is when the bruising incident could have occured. Definitely see a doc at another hospital...
